name: Student Organizations & Involvement
description: Student Organizations and Involvement at Florida State University is your direct connection to getting involved on campus and supports the mission of the Department of Student Engagement! You can find a student organization to join, learn more about event planning, and explore new and exciting activities to attend on campus! Our office includes over 650 recognized student organizations, Market Wednesdays, the Involvement Fairs, Garnet & Gold Tailgates, Leadership Awards Night, LeadFSU, Homecoming, The Big Event Tallahassee, Relay For Life, and Dance Marathon. Your FSU experience starts here!
summary: Student Organizations and Involvement is your direct connection to getting involved on campus. You can find a student organization to join, find everything you need to know about event planning, find new and exciting activities to attend and more!

name: Office of Fraternity and Sorority Life
description:
Mission Statement

The mission of the Office of Fraternity and Sorority Life is to enhance the quality of student life at The Florida State University by empowering members of the Greek community through advising with a developmental approach, educating with purpose, communicating with stakeholders, and cultivating collective partnerships.

 

Vision Statement

We advocate for the expression of ritual through the shared values of leadership, scholarship, service, and brotherhood/sisterhood within the Greek community.
summary: We work with 56 social fraternities and sororities at Florida State University. These values-based organizations promote leadership, scholarship, brotherhood/sisterhood and service.  With 30 fraternities and 26 sororities.

name: Interfraternity Council
description:
We, the Interfraternity Council (IFC) exist to advance conditions conducive to the success of its member chapters and to promote the progress of the fraternity experience. With this aim, the IFC actively encourages interfraternalism and collaboration with other fraternity/sorority governing councils and student governance bodies, to assist in the betterment of our members, and the student body as a whole.

 

The purpose of the IFC shall be to:
a. Provide a sovereign peer governance structure for its member chapters;
b. Enforce and hold member chapters accountable to the IFC By-Laws and other governing documents;
c. Develop policies and procedures to provide safe and healthy membership experiences;
d. Develop policies and procedures to provide safe and healthy social experiences;
e. Advocate for appropriate levels of university financial and staffing support;
f. Promote the interests of its member chapters;
g. Promote the interests of men’s fraternities in general
h. Promote the interests of Florida State University
i. Promote mutual cooperation between its member chapters;
j. Promote mutual cooperation between the IFC, fellow governing councils, and student organizations at Florida State University; and
k. Promote mutual cooperation between the IFC and Florida State University, its students, faculty, staff, and the local community.
summary: To be the governing body over all fraternities that are member and associate member organizations of the Interfraternity Council (IFC) on Florida State University's campus.

name: Multicultural Greek Council
description:
In order to promote the further advancement of Greek Letter Societies, by encouraging diversity among their members and taking full advantage of the various talents in all segments of culture and society, to advocate greater solidarity in the Florida State University Greek Community, and to nurture intellectual, emotional, and social development.

 

Unify and organize groups that seek to promote diversity and cohesion on campus and in the community.
Encourage cooperation and harmony among member organizations.
Provide educational programs and service the needs and wants for member organizations.
Take an active role in community service activities.
Serve as a liaison between the university authorities and member organizations in matters of common interest.
Act as a judicial body for violations of The Florida State University Student Code of Conduct and the MGC Constitution.
summary: A part of the Greek Life Community which encourages diversity among their members in order to promote the student experience and advancement of Greek Letter Societies. Members are able to thrive academically, and increase their potential.

name: Panhellenic Association
description:
Panhellenic was founded at Florida State in 1904 with the mission of empowering women, a purpose that still guides us today. Serving as the governing body for the 17 NPC member organizations at FSU, Panhellenic continues to foster leadership, scholarship, service, and sisterhood. As outlined in our mission, we emphasize community service through events like LineDance, uphold academic excellence with GPA requirements, study hours, and educational seminars, encourage leadership across campus, and promote lifelong friendships through the bonds of sisterhood.

 

We, as Undergraduate members of women's fraternities, stand for good scholarship, for guarding of good health, for maintenance of fine standards, and for serving, to the best of our ability, our college community. Cooperation for furthering fraternity life, in harmony with its best possibilities, is the ideal that shall guide our fraternity activities.

We, as Fraternity Women, stand for service through the development of character inspired by the close contact and deep friendship of individual fraternity and Panhellenic life. The opportunity for wide and wise human service, through mutual respect and helpfulness, is the tenet by which we strive to live.
summary: The Panhellenic Association serves as the governing body for the 17 NPC member organizations at Florida State. Panhellenic is dedicated to pursuing our four core values of scholarship, service, leadership, and sisterhood.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description:
Unanimity of thought and action as far as possible in the conduct of Greek letter collegiate fraternities and sororities, and to consider problems of mutual interest to its member organizations.

The purpose of the National PanHellenic Council is:
To promote an atmosphere of mutual respect; support a high academic and scholastic standard and assist member organizations in upholding their fundamental purposes and ideals
To act as a governing body for the member and affiliate organizations
To enhance the positive relationships among the members of the National PanHellenic Council.
summary: Unanimity of thought and action as far as possible in the conduct of Greek letter collegiate fraternities and sororities, and to consider problems of mutual interest to its member organizations.

name: Campus Recreation
description:
Find What Moves You. 

Campus Recreation offers a diverse selection of recreational programs and services for FSU students, faculty, and staff.  Our programs include:
Fitness
Aquatics
Intramural Sports
Sport Clubs
Outdoor Pursuits
FSU Challenge

Enjoy our programs and services at 7 Campus Rec facilities:
Dr. Bobby E. Leach Student Recreation Center
Fitness & Movement Clinic in the Health & Wellness Center
Rec SportsPlex
Main Campus Fields
Westside Courts
Tully Gymnasium
FSU Reservation Waterfront Park
summary: Find What Moves You.  Campus Recreation offers fitness & wellness, competitive sports, and outdoor adventure programs for the FSU community at 7 recreational facilities across campus.

name: Kappa Alpha Order
description: Kappa Alpha Order seeks to create a lifetime experience which centers on reverence to God, duty, honor, character and gentlemanly conduct.
summary: Kappa Alpha Order seeks to create a lifetime experience which centers on reverence to God, duty, honor, character and gentlemanly conduct.

name: Kappa Sigma
description: Kappa Sigma (ΚΣ), commonly nicknamed Kappa Sig, is an international fraternity with almost 300 active chapters and colonies in North America. Kappa Sigma has initiated more than 250,000 men on college campuses throughout the United States and Canada. Today, the Fraternity has over 175,000 living alumni and more than 15,000 undergraduate members. It is one of the leaders of all American fraternities in terms of pledges and new initiates per year, service hours, and philanthropic donations. Its endowment fund, founded in 1919 is the oldest college fraternity foundation and has donated more than $4.5 million to undergrads since 1948.
summary: Kappa Sigma (ΚΣ), commonly nicknamed Kappa Sig, is an international fraternity with almost 300 active chapters and colonies in North America. Kappa Sigma has initiated more than 250,000 men on college campuses throughout the United States and Canada.
comment: Organizaiton has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

description:
Founded in 1909, focusing on personal character develpoment through leadership, scholarship, community sevice, and positive social experiences.

 

To prepare and encourage collegiate men of good character, high ethics, and noble ideals to contribute positively to the world in which they live

name: Phi Gamma Delta
description:
Phi Gamma Delta exists to promote lifelong friendships, to reaffirm high ethical standards and values, and to foster personal development in the pursuit of excellence. Phi Gamma Delta is committed to providing opportunities to each brother to develop responsibility, leadership, scholarship and social skills to become a fully contributing member of society.
Cheers for Charity

 

To promote lifelong friendships and achieve scholarship.
summary: Phi Gamma Delta exists to promote lifelong friendship, to reaffirm high ethical standards and values, and to foster personal development in the pursuit of excellence. We're founded on the values of Friendship, Knowledge, Service, Morality, and Excellence
comment: Organizaiton has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Phi Kappa Psi
description: A national fraternity dedicated to the service of others.  Phi Psi provides leadership opportunities within the Chapter, the classroom, and in the community.  The Phi Kappa Psi Foundation supports members academically by annually offering over $350,000 in scholarships and grants to members of the Fraternity.  Upon joining Phi Kappa Psi, members form lifelong bonds that never die.
summary: A national fraternity dedicated to the service of others.  Phi Psi provides leadership opportunities within the Chapter, the classroom, and in the community.  The Phi Kappa Psi Foundation supports members academically by keeping all members accountable.

name: Pi Kappa Alpha
description:
A fraternity on campus aiding the community and the Florida State University campus.

We are a social organization on campus striving to help members become better members to society.
summary: A fraternity on campus helping the FSU community through multiple endevours. We are a social organization on campus striving to help young men become better members of society.

name: Pi Lambda Phi
description:
Contrary to popular belief, all fraternities are not the same. We pride
ourselves on our diversity. Our brothers aren't all stamped from the
same mold. Through our unique strengths, we achieve common goals. We excel with academics (providing scholarships), intramural sports, and building leadership. Our motto,
"Not Four Years, But a Lifetime," expresses our commitment to staying
involved long after graduation.

 

Our Florida State chapter, designated FL Epsilon Lambda, was founded in 1996. Pi Lambda Phi can help you get the most of your college experience with leadership
challenges, community service, academics, interpersonal skills, and
social activities. Our philanthropy, Linedance, is the top Greek fundraiser on campus and benefits the Elimination of Prejudice.
summary: Contrary to popular belief, all fraternities are not the same. We pride ourselves on our diversity. Our brothers aren't all stamped from the same mold. Through our unique strengths, we achieve common goals.

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
description:
The mission of Sigma Alpha Epsilon is to promote the highest standards of friendship, scholarship, and service for our members based upon the ideals set forth by our Founders and as specifically enunciated in our creed.

The mission of the Florida Beta chapter of Sigma Alpha Epsilon is to aid in the development of character in our members and to promote a strong and lasting bond between the brothers by rigorous planning for the future of our chapter in; educating our brothers and alumni on all aspects of the fraternity including ritual and post graduation commitments and the researching of the national fraternity, the chapter here at Florida State and the surrounding community on a holistic level.
summary: The mission of Sigma Alpha Epsilon is to promote the highest standards of friendship, scholarship, and service for our members based upon the ideals set forth by our Founders and as specifically enunciated in our creed.

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
description: Tau Kappa Epsilon (ΤΚΕ or Teke) is a college fraternity founded on January 10, 1899 at Illinois Wesleyan University with chapters in the United States, and Canada, and affiliation with a German fraternity system known as the Corps of the Weinheimer Senioren Convent (WSC). With 290 active TKE chapters and colonies throughout the United States and Canada, TKE is the largest college social fraternity in terms of number of active groups. The colors of the fraternity are the shade of red known as cherry, and gray. Of the national fraternities founded prior to 1920, TKE is one of the few fraternities that never had a discrimination clause in its membership requirements to prevent membership of men because of their race, color, or creed. Our Lambda Iota Chapter at Florida State University was founded in 1968, and ever since, we have been on a mission to aid men in their personal, moral and social development for life.
summary: Tau Kappa Epsilon is the largest social fraternity in the US, founded on January 10, 1899 at Illinois Wesleyan University. We stand not for wealth, rank or honor but for personal worth and character. TKE hopes to create better men for a better world.
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: alpha Kappa Delta Phi International Sorority, Inc.
description:
alpha Kappa Delta Phi International Sorority, Inc. was established on February 7, 1990 at the University of California, Berkeley by 14 dedicated women. It has since grown to over 55 chapters throughout the United States and Canada, making it the largest, fastest growing, and only internationally recognized organization of its kind.

    The purpose of the sorority is to promote sisterhood, scholarship, leadership, and Asian-awareness in the university and community while encouraging the expression of the individual.

     The Florida State University Associate Chapter of alpha Kappa Delta Phi was established in the Spring of 2001. We are soon to celebrate our 22-year anniversary on campus and in the Tallahassee community. aKDPhi at FSU is a full member of the Multicultural Greek Council as well as the Asian American Student Union, and is heavily involved in both.

 

Quick facts:
National Philanthropy: Our national philanthropy is Breast Cancer Awareness. We donate all of our fundraising money to help the cause via the aKDPhi International Foundation.

Colors: Purple & White

Motto: "Timeless Friendship Through Sisterhood"

Flower: Iris

Stone: Diamond

Mascot: Phoenix
summary: alpha Kappa Delta Phi International Sorority, Inc. was established on February 7, 1990 at the University of California, Berkeley by 14 dedicated women. It has since grown to over 60 chapters throughout the United States

name: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Inc.
description:
Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Inc. is a Latina founded, 501(c)(7), national sorority who aims to achieve professional development, academic excellence, and graduation of all its members; an organization dedicated to community service to their local university communities with an emphasis on the Hispanic/Latino population.

The Purpose of Kappa Delta Chi is to promote the values of Unity, Honesty, Integrity and Leadership among women at colleges and universities. Through numerous service events, sisterhood retreats and academic and cultural workshops, sister's learn more about themselves and each other. They sprout into impacting leaders and help guide the next generation to success. KDChi is a non-traditional sorority full of diverse members and eager young ladies ready to make a difference in their communities. With your sisters, anything is possible.
summary: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Inc. is a Latina founded, 501(c)(7), national sorority who aims to achieve professional development, academic excellence, and graduation of all its members; an organization dedicated to community service to their local
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Incorporated
description:
The year 1975 saw the birth of what started as a vision - an idea of an organization, a sisterhood that would cater to the needs of Latinas and the universal woman. Thanks to the tremendous strides made by our seventeen founding mothers, this dream was realized, and took form as the entity. Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Incorporated, the first Latina sorority in the nation.

There were several causes leading to the realization that such an organization was necessary. Traditionally, the role of the Latina woman was that of maintaining the family institution and falling into the realm of the Machismo stigma. As the Latino migration to the United States increased, so did the emergence of independent Latina women, eager to be at the forefront of an era of a new educational, political and social consciousness. In the early 70's, colleges and universities experienced an influx of Latino enrollment. With this growth, the need for support groups and outreach programs were at an all time high, primarily for the low percentage of Latina women in higher education institutions.

In 1975, Kean University in Union, New Jersey introduced and began to implement bilingual studies, as well as a Latino and Caribbean studies department. Women of Latin descent were now able to embark in Higher Education even if English was not their native language. Although this enabled and facilitated the educational advancement of the Latina woman, a huge void was left to be filled - still missing was support and equality; a constant reminder to these women that they were still the minority, and that their struggle for equality on every level had just begun. It was at this university, in this historical time period, that a group of women convened to discuss the formation of a sorority for Latina women. Its focus would be to actively integrate itself into the social, political and community service arena that other students had been involved with. Together, as a united front they could compete, collaborate and assist with any student run programs, thus making their voices as loud and profound as the majority voice; their concerns equally as important.

Lambda Theta Alpha was recognized at Kean University as the First Latina Sorority founded in the United States. This organization was the first to recognize the need for a support system, thus creating one. The desire to progress, dedication to their community, and the aspiration for the advancement of the Latina women marked the beginning of Lambda Theta Alpha. It allowed the independent Latina woman of a new era to have a sense of belonging and with that, achieve her highest potential. It would define a new role for the Latina woman, one with education, goals and vision in hopes of great success.

Now, 46 years later, we have seen the outcome of such an organization. Lambda Theta Alpha has contributed to the advancement of all people by helping to produce educated women of many races, ethnicities, and creeds. These women are political leaders, doctors, lawyers, teachers, accountants, etc. LTA has also paved the way for many other Latina organizations, which would later pursue the empowering movement that we set the trend for. From 1975 to date, Lambda Theta Alpha has upheld its purpose and mission, each day realizing the Founder's dream - that it may serve as a quintessential trailblazer for all women.
summary: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc. is the FIRST and LARGEST Latin sorority in the nation, founded in 1975. Our colors are Burgundy and Gray and our principles are Unity, Love and Respect.

name: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc
description: In 1975, there were no Latino fraternities in existence in the United States. The Greek-letter organizations of the time primarily catered to Anglo and African-American students and graduates. Lambda’s founders, as men of vision, realized there was a need to unite the Latino students, develop their leadership skills, impart upon them the value of an education, and instill in them a commitment to their community and culture. The traditional student club would not suffice to accomplish such lofty goals. Hence, Latino unity and brotherhood would be achieved through a long recognized institution – the fraternity. This newest addition to the Greek system would be the first in the nation, by identity and by name: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Incorporated. The ideals of this fraternity include academic excellence, brotherhood, leadership, Latino unity, and service. On a daily basis, our brothers live up to these ideals, as set forth by fourteen young Latino men decades ago. At the undergraduate level, our brothers are active within the Fraternity and in other student organizations. At the professional level, among our ranks you will find attorneys, doctors, and engineers. Our brothers are affecting policy and change as elected officials serving on City Councils, Boards of Education, U.S. House of Representatives, and the U.S. Senate. In short, Lambdas are contributing and excelling in every facet of human endeavor. "En La Union Esta La Fuerza."
summary: On December 1, 1975, Lambda Theta Phi was founded on the campus of Kean College in Union, New Jersey.  Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. is recognized as the first and largest Latino Greek lettered organization in the United States.

name: Phi Iota Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Since 1931 Phi Iota Alpha has set precedents for Latino Greek leadership in the global community. Through the past 94 years, Phi Iota Alpha has cultivated and inspired Latino men nationwide to become leaders of their communities across Latin America and the United States. Open to all men, this distinguished brotherhood includes many professionals and former presidents of Latin American countries. Today, Phi Iota Alpha, the oldest Latino fraternity in existence, continues to lead as the preeminent Latino fraternal organization with chapters throughout the United States.

Our goal is to provide intensive educational, cultural and social activities geared towards the promotion of Latin American culture and empowerment to the Latino community. Our purpose is to unify Latinos and other ethnic groups, and we are also dedicated to the abolishing of ignorance, prejudice, and racism against and between different ethnic groups.
summary: Since 1931 Phi Iota Alpha has set precedents for Latino Greek leadership in the global community. Through the past 89 years, Phi Iota Alpha has cultivated and inspired Latino men nationwide to become leaders of their communities across Latin America.

name: Sigma Lambda Beta International Fraternity
description: The Rho Alpha Chapter of Sigma Lambda Beta International Fraternity strives for academic excellence, diversity, and service to The Florida State University and Leon County communities. We are the largest and fastest growing multi-cultural fraternity in the nation. Our pillars of Brotherhood, Scholarship, Community Service, and Cultural Awareness drive us to better ourselves, maintain self-respect and encourage tolerance for the views of others.
summary: The Rho Alpha Chapter of Sigma Lambda Beta International Fraternity is the nations largest and fastest growing international fraternity. We strive for academic excellence, diversity and service to the community through our brotherhood.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated
description:
By uniting individual efforts, Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated has built a strong, cohesive organization that positively impacts the lives of families and communities. The Sorority's success is based on the depth of commitment, vision and confidence of its members. Membership into Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated is awarded to women who will promote and extend its policies and programs. In return, the Sorority is a conduit through which individuals can grow and develop, personally and professionally, while simultaneously providing "Service to All Mankind."

Chapters throughout Alpha Kappa Alpha's international network are implementing the Signature Program Initiatives with activities dedicated to: positively impacting the lives of girls in grades six through eight; encouraging personal fitness and healthy life styles; ending hunger, preserving the environment and empowering women; expanding the current programs related to wealth building and allocation of resources, home ownership, estate planning, support and empowerment of female owned businesses and urban enterprise zones; addressing gender equality issues; and leadership training.

January 15, 2008 marked the Centennial Celebration of the founding of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated. The Lovely Ladies of the Zeta Omicron Chapter are thrilled to be a part of history and celebrate the 100th year of preserving the legacy of their founders' vision for Alpha Kappa Alpha originally in 1908. Still 100 years later, and for many more years to come, Alpha Kappa Alpha women pride themselves in being of service to all mankind.

 

The purpose of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated is to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women in order to improve the social stature, to maintain a progressive interest in college life, and to be of service to all mankind.  We as college women, being aware of the increasing complexity of the women's problems, especially those of Negro Women, and realizing the necessity of forming an organization for the purpose of studying and solving such problems, do therefore organize this Sorority in order to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, improve the social stature of race, promote unity and friendship among college women, and keep alive the alumnae, and interest in college life, and progressive movement emanating there form.
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated has built a strong, cohesive organization that positively impacts the lives of families and communities. The Sorority's success is based on the depth of Sisterhood, Scholarship, and "Service to All Mankind".

name: The Iota Delta Chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
description: Alpha Phi Alpha, the first intercollegiate Greek-letter fraternity established for African American Men, was founded on December 4, 1906™ at Cornell University in Ithaca, New York by seven college men who recognized the need for a strong bond of brotherhood among African descendants in this country.
summary: The Iota Delta Chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc., seated at Florida State University, chartered on Friday, April 5th, 1974, is a pillar of excellence, leadership, and service, upholding the fraternity’s legacy.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Incorporated
description:
Kappa Alpha Psi is a social fraternity which prides itself in training for leadership and is geared toward achieving in every field of human endeavor. The founders of the fraternity sought a formula that would immediately raise the sights of black collegians and stimulate them to accomplishments higher than they might have imagined.

Our Objectives:

To unite college men of culture, patriotism and honor in a bond of fraternity.
To encourage honorable achievement in every field of human endeavor.
To promote the spiritual, social, intellectual and moral welfare of members.
To assist the aims and purpose of college and universities.
To inspire to service in the public interest.


Today, on the campus of the Florida State University the dream that was realized in the year of 1911 continues to flourish. One of our annual and longest running philanthropies is Kappa Christmas. The event features various performances and entertainment throughout the evening with the goal of raising funds and collecting toys for local families who are unable to have a Christmas of their own though the Marine's Toys for Tots Foundation.
summary: Kappa Alpha Psi is a social fraternity which prides itself in training for leadership and is geared toward achieving in every field of human endeavor.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Omega Psi Phi Fraternity,Inc. was to be composed of men of like attainment and of the same ideals of fellowship, scholarship, and manhood. In the Fall of 1967, nine men came together to establish their unity as AfricanAmerican at Florida State University, a predominately white school. These strong young men wanted to form a group that would exemplify their bond on campus. After much deliberation, they decided it would be best to become an extension of a powerful movement that was already in progress and that movement was Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Incorporated. With the guidance of the Chi Omega and Upsilon Psi chapters, Chi Theta chapter was born. As a result of their undying patience, love, time, and affection, The Chi Theta chapter was the first black Greek organization at Florida State University. This was a major step for the university and Omega, as this served as a gateway for other AfricanAmerican fraternities and sororities to develop at FSU. Officially chartered on August 1, 1970, these distinguished men that formed Chi Theta's were dubbed "The Super Fine Nine".

Since Chi Theta's inception, the morals and ideals of Omega has been instilled in all that followed these pioneers of truth and justice. The chapter strived to perpetuate the standards set by our four founding fathers by participating in Achievement Week, Social Action, Talent Hunt, Scholarship Attainment and other key community projects. The members of Chi Theta have continued to spread Omega's influence in the surrounding communities and on campus. Omega Men are consistently involved in activities that mold the climate of the campus such as student government being elected to senate positions, as well as the President's Committee. Most importantly, Omega Men have always taken a strong and active role in the Black Student Union which serves as the centerpiece of AfricanAmerican affairs on campus.

With now 35 years of hard work and due diligence, Chi Theta has been awarded the highest honor by the Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Incorporated by being named the National Undergraduate Chapter of the Year for 2002, 2003, and 2004. Chi Theta will continue to to the utmost to exemplify our four cardinal principles of MANHOOD, SCHOLARSHIP, PERSEVERANCE, AND UPLIFT.

Philanthropies and Community Service include:
"Dog Days of Summer Book Scholarship"
Purple Passion Scholarship Banquet
Elite 8 Basketball Tournament
Tribute to Black Women
Social Action Seminars
NAACP and United Negro College Fund Donations
Red Cross Disaster Relief and Blood Drives
and many more
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. was to be composed of men of like attainment and of the same ideals of fellowship, scholarship, and manhood.  In the Fall of 1967, nine men came together to establish their unity as African-Americans at Florida State.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated
description:
Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. was founded at Howard University in Washington, D.C., January 9, 1914, by three young African American male students. The founders, Honorable A. Langston Taylor, Honorable Leonard F. Morse, and Honorable Charles I. Brown, wanted to organize a Greek letter fraternity that would truly exemplify the ideals of brotherhood, scholarship, and service.

From its inception, the Founders also conceived Phi Beta Sigma as a mechanism to deliver services to the general community. Rather than gaining skills to be utilized exclusively for themselves and their immediate families, the founders of Phi Beta Sigma held a deep conviction that they should return their newly acquired skills to the communities from which they had come. This deep conviction was mirrored in the Fraternity's motto, "Culture For Service and Service For Humanity".

 

This fraternity was formed to develop and promote the ideals of brotherhood, scholarship, and service. In that we hold as our motto, "culture for service, and service to humanity".

 

The “Mighty” Mu Epsilon Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc was chartered on March 7, 1980 by Bro. Kenneth "KC" Colebrooke and Maurice Parris. The Brothers of the Mighty Mu Epsilon Chapter have since then sought to serve Florida State University and the Tallahassee Community in many ways.

Our many involvements include:

 

LEONES 

PTOT Club

NPHC Hall of Fame 

Progressive Black Men, Inc.

Big Brother Little Brother Mentorship Program, Inc.

Renegade Leadership Honor Society

Mr. Black Student Union

National Society of Black Engineers

IEEE

Order of Omega

African Student Association

Garnet & Gold Key

Caribbean Student Association

Orientation Leader
NAACP Executive Board
S.O.A.R. Board
NPHC Executive Council
Black Student Union
CARE Ambassador
Resident Assistant
Marching 100
Phi Eta Sigma
Seminole Torchbearers
Senate President
Homecoming Court
Gubernatorial Fellow
Lambda Alpha Epsilon

The Mighty Mu Epsilon Chapter is famously known for some of Florida's Largest Parties with Splashdown and Goombay.

As brothers of the Mighty Mu Epsilon Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma we are the Fraternity's most valuable resource and strength. We are the primary means by which the objectives of Phi Beta Sigma will be achieved. In order to accomplish the objectives of the Fraternity, it is essential that we effectively embody "Culture For Service and Service For Humanity" and promote brotherhood, scholarship and service.
summary: The Mu Epsilon Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated.

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc
description:
Opportunity to Serve

Through volunteerism in the community and on campus, Zeta women develop the self-worth and pride that comes from serving others and positively contributing to society.

Professional Development

Zeta women develop leadership skills by attending seminars, holding officer
and committee positions within the chapter; and serving as a role models
for the Sorority's youth affiliates.

Personal Development

Membership in Zeta Phi Beta Sorority is a living and learning experience for
women with shared values and objectives. You will have the opportunity to be
involved in a wide range of activities where you can use your unique talents and
knowledge to contribute to the chapter and your community.

Sisterhood

Zeta Phi Beta encourages young women to learn and grow in the spirit of sisterly love and friendship during college and beyond. Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. was founded on January 16, 1920, on the campus of Howard University by five women who had the dream of an organization that focused more on Scholastic rather than physical appearance and social status. Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. has always been at the forefront of endeavors, being the first NPHC sorority with a chapter in Africa and the first to participate in the Human Genome Project. Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. continues to make strides towards excellence and meeting the needs of its surrounding community through such projects as ZHOPE (Zetas Helping Other People Excel) and Storks Nest, designed to promote prenatal care and healthy living during pregnancy.
summary: Opportunity to Serve Through volunteerism in the community and on campus, Zeta women develop the self worth and pride that comes from serving others and making a positive contribution to society. Professional Development Zeta women develop leadership

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description: Alpha Chi Omega is a fraternal organization open to all women. Alpha Chi offers a lifetime of friendship, leadership, responsibility, community service, and support. Alpha Chi's main goals are to promote our five criteria: leadership, personal development, financial responsibility, character, and scholarship. Furthermore, our women strive to raise awareness and funds for victims of domestic violence. We support the local Refuge House and the Alpha Chi Omega Foundation.
summary: Alpha Chi Omega is a fraternal organization open to all women. Alpha Chi offers a lifetime of friendship, leadership, responsibility, community service, and support. Alpha Chi's main goals are to promote our five criteria.

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description: Founded on May 15th, 1851 at Wesleyan Female College in Macon, Georgia, Alpha Delta Pi holds the distinction of being the first secret society for women and the mother of today's sorority system. Alpha Delta Pi was founded on the principles of sisterhood, scholarship, leadership, and service to others, as exemplified with the motto "We Live For Each Other." These principles still guide the policies and programs of the chapter today. The Iota chapter of Alpha Delta Pi supports the Ronald McDonald House as their international philanthropy, and directly supports the Ronald McDonald House here in Tallahassee.
summary: Alpha Delta Pi was the first secret society for women, founded on the principles of sisterhood, scholarship, leadership, and service to others. Alpha Delta Pi's international philanthropy is Ronald McDonald House Charities.

name: Alpha Gamma Delta
description:
On May 30th, 1904, 11 inspired women came together at Syracuse University to create Alpha Gamma Delta. A part of the Syracuse triad, Alpha Gamma Delta is now an International women's fraternity with chapters all over the continental United States and in Canada. The Gamma Beta chapter at Florida State University was officially chartered in 1925, making it one of the oldest chapters at Florida State University. 

Bound by our Purpose, the women of Alpha Gamma Delta strive to live up to the high ideals that our founders set forth. We encourage our members to hold themselves to a higher standard than the average college woman. As Alpha Gams, we push one another to excel in the areas of academics, leadership, service, and above all, sisterhood. We value diversity of thoughts, passions, and ideals and are always striving to continue expanding our horizons as members of the Florida State community.

Through community service and awareness efforts, there’s no doubt that Alpha Gams are impacting the world. In the past year of philanthropic efforts, Gamma Beta was named one of the top fundraising Alpha Gam chapters overall for our philanthropic focus, Fighting Hunger, and the Alpha Gamma Delta Foundation. Alpha Gams can also be spotted contributing to many other organizations on campus, such as Dance Marathon, Relay for Life, The FSU Marching Chiefs, Hillel, Women in Pre-Law Society, Advertising Club, Homecoming Executive Council & Committee, Panhellenic Executive Board, the Honors Program, and SGA, just to name a few.
summary: Inspired by the vision and values defined by our Founders, Alpha Gamma Delta cherishes the lifelong opportunity to share sisterhood. We challenge our members to lead lives of significance that positively impact their communities.

name: Chi Omega Sorority
description:
Throughout Chi Omega's long and proud history, our six purposes have guided the direction of every chapter and brought our members unequaled opportunities for personal growth and development. These purposes are Friendship, High Standards of Personnel, Sincere Learning and Credible Scholarship, Participation in Campus Activities, Career Development, and Community Service.  Chi Omega Fraternity is the largest women's fraternal organization in the world with over 182 collegiate chapters nationwide. Our sorority was founded April 5, 1895 at the University of Arkansas and our much beloved Florida State University Gamma Chapter of Chi Omega was colonized in 1904 and later chartered on May 7, 1908. 


More commonly known as "Chi O", our organization's colors are cardinal and straw, our mascot is the owl, our symbol is the skull and crossbones and our flower is the white carnation. Our philanthropic events benefit the Make-A-Wish Foundation, an organization dedicated to granting the wishes of children with life-threatening medical illnesses.


Some fun facts about Chi Omega: The Longmire building at FSU is named in honor of a Chi Omega. Chi Omegas can be found among Student Government, professional executive boards, honor and leadership societies, FSU Cheerleaders and Golden Girls, as well as orientation leaders and peer instructors.
summary: Sisters inspired by our values who serve the world while keeping Chi Omega ever at heart.

name: Delta Delta Delta
description:
Delta Delta Delta is a Panhellenic sorority at Florida State University. Tri Delta's Sincerely Yours campaign, Dodgeball Tournament, and Delta House of Pancakes (DHOP) philanthropy events benefit St. Jude Children's Research Hospital.

Written in 1888 by Sarah Ida Shaw, Boston

THE PURPOSE OF DELTA DELTA DELTA shall be to establish a perpetual bond of friendship among its members, to develop a stronger and more womanly character, to broaden the moral and intellectual life, and to assist its members in every possible way.

IT SHALL ALSO BE THE PURPOSE OF DELTA DELTA DELTA to promote and develop mutually beneficial relationships between the Fraternity and the colleges and universities where the Fraternity has established chapters, to develop qualities of unselfish leadership among its members, and to encourage them to assume, with integrity and devotion to moral and democratic principles, the highest responsibilities of college women.
summary: Delta Delta Delta is a Panhellenic sorority at Florida State University. Tri Delta's Sincerely Yours campaign, Dodgeball Tournament, and Delta House of Pancakes (DHOP) philanthropy events benefit St. Jude Children's Research Hospital.

name: Delta Gamma
description:
Delta Gamma offers to women of all ages a rich heritage, a continuity based on sound and tested principles of personal integrity, personal responsibilty and intellectual honesty.

Delta Gamma's primary purpose is to create an environment for its members in which lasting friendships are established and in which members find the processes, the experiences, and the disciplines whcih will stimulate clear thought. Its aim is to foster an atmosphere in which women willl develop a deeper love and consideration for mankind, a more profound understanding of the purpose of life, and a basic wisdom upon which to build their lives.

Delta Gamma's purpose is accomplished through planned collegiate and alumnae programs which provide intellectual motivation, the opportunity for graciosness in daily living, and community awareness that will enrich the lives of members and instill in them a respect for the values to be gained from te Fraternity.

A Fraternity that donates through community service and fundraising to Service for Sight and Sight Conservation.
summary: Delta Gamma offers to women of all ages a rich heritage, a continuity based on sound and tested principles of personal integrity, personal responsibility and intellectual honesty.

name: Delta Zeta
description: Delta Zeta Sorority is a national organization founded on October 24, 1902, at Miami University in Oxford, Ohio. With a commitment to sisterhood, scholarship, service, and leadership, Delta Zeta empowers women to lead lives of purpose and impact. The organization promotes personal development through academic support, philanthropic initiatives, and leadership opportunities. Delta Zeta’s national philanthropy focuses on speech and hearing, supporting organizations such as the Starkey Hearing Foundation and the American Society for Deaf Children.
summary: Delta Zeta's strive to acheive excellence in academics, community service, and sisterhood.

name: Gamma Phi Beta
description: Founded in 1874, Gamma Phi Beta builds confident women of character who celebrate sisterhood and enduring friendships long after college graduation. Dedicated to building strong girls, we support the non-profit organization Girls on the Run to empower and mentor young girls.
summary: Gamma Phi Beta inspires the highest type of womanhood through education, service to humanity, and social means. Each member strives to live by and uphold the four core values we were founded upon: Love, Labor, Learning, and Loyalty.

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description:
510 on West Park is far more than just a house. To over 200 women, it is our home away from home; a place where we can fully be ourselves, a place to be loved and supported, and a place where there is infinite room for growth. For many, Theta has gotten us through our hardest points not only in college, but in life. At the end of the day, encouragement and empowerment from your sisters can really be all it takes. It is not everyday that you find a group of women who support you, push you past every boundary, and care for you when you need it most. It is these kinds of sisterhood that will truly last a lifetime.

Established on October 18, 1924, the Beta Nu Chapter of Kappa Alpha Theta at Florida State University has been standing strong for almost 100 years. The legacy that we now strive to continue began in 1870 by a few remarkably driven women with one main principal in mind; empowering leading women. Here at Theta, we push each other to be the best version of ourselves by encouraging excellence in academics, personal growth, social development, and service within our very own chapter philanthropy, CASA (Court Appointed Special Advocates).
summary: 510 on West Park is far more than just a house. To over 200 women, it is our home away from home; a place where we can fully be ourselves, a place to be loved and supported, and a place where there is infinite room for growth.

name: Kappa Delta Sorority
description:
Kappa Delta Sorority was founded on October 23, 1897 at Longwood College in Farmville, Virginia. Florida State University's Kappa Alpha Chapter of Kappa Delta was founded on November 11, 1904 and was the first sorority on campus. Locally, our annual philanthropies are a 5K Color Run and Lip Sync Competition which raises money for Prevent Child Abuse of America and Children's Home Society. Our colors are pearl white and olive green, and our mascots are the teddy bear, the nautilus shell, the dagger, and the katydid.

The purpose of Kappa Delta Sorority is to promote true friendship among the college girls of our country by inculcating into their hearts and lives those principles of truth, of honor, of duty, without which there can be no true friendship.
summary: Kappa Delta Sorority was founded on October 23, 1897 at Longwood College in Farmville, Virginia. Florida State University's Kappa Alpha Chapter of Kappa Delta was founded on November 11, 1904 and was the first sorority on campus.

name: Kappa Kappa Gamma
description: Kappa Kappa Gamma is an organization of women, which seeks for every member throughout her life bonds of friendship, mutual support, opportunities for self growth, respect for intellectual development, and an understanding of and an allegiance to positive ethical principles. We seek genuine, long-lasting relationships built on a foundation of honesty, trust, loyalty and a personal commitment to delivering on our promises. We are a women's Fraternity that unites women through membership in a close bond of friendship. Scholarship or intellectual development is our first priority. Kappa Kappa Gamma encourages members to seek opportunities for self growth in a variety of interests in the chapter, campus, and in the community. Kappa members believe that philanthropy or mutual helpfulness is of significant importance. Kappa's philanthropic focus is mental health and well-being. Through our philanthropic efforts at the Epsilon Zeta chapter, we donate funds and serve our local community via the National Alliance on Mental Illness Tallahassee chapter. Internationally, we also support one another through various relief funds, and grants dedicated to supporting our kappa sisters across the world.
summary: Kappa Kappa Gamma is an organization of women, which seeks genuine, long-lasting relationships built on a foundation of honesty, trust, loyalty and a personal commitment to delivering on our promises.

name: Phi Mu Fraternity
description:
Phi Mu was founded in 1852 at Wesleyan College in Macon, Georgia. It began as the Philomathean Society, a literary society, and is the second oldest secret society for women. The Philomathean Society became Phi Mu Fraternity in 1904. Because the word 'sorority' was not commonly used when Phi Mu was chartered, its proper name is Phi Mu Fraternity even though it is a women's organization.

All Phi Mus have different college experiences, but we all share a Bond of Sisterhood. We all believe in the ideals of our Creed: Love, Honor, and Truth. Each Phi Mu who gives time as an active member, officer, or volunteer, contributes to the continued success of Phi Mu Fraternity.
summary: Phi Mu was founded in 1852 at Wesleyan College in Macon, Georgia. It began as the Philomathean Society, a literary society, and is the second-oldest secret society for women. The Philomathean Society then became Phi Mu Fraternity in 1904.

name: Pi Beta Phi
description: Pi Beta Phi Fraternity is committed to becoming a premier organization for women by providing lifelong enrichment to its members and contributing to the betterment of society.
summary: Pi Beta Phi Fraternity is committed to becoming a premier organization for women by providing lifelong enrichment to its members and contributing to the betterment of society.

name: Sigma Delta Tau
description:
Founded at the prestigious Cornell University, Sigma Delta Tau is a lifelong sisterhood. Our mission is to enrich the collegiate experience for women of similar ideals, build lasting friendships, and foster personal growth. SDT helps each member reach her fullest potential by providing scholastic, philanthropic, leadership, and social opportunities. While our sisters are highly involved on campus, above all, they embody the values and morals of Sigma Delta Tau. We look forward to extending to new sisters FSU's brightest torch: Sigma Delta Tau.

 

The Gamma Lambda chapter has greatly evolved over the years. We pride ourselves on our genuine sisterhood and ideal of quality over quantity, for once a sister enters the room both are forever changed for the better. FSU Sig Delts come from all over and form lasting friendships. SDT's can be found living together on and off-campus, sitting next to each other in classes, sharing lunch, cheering on the Seminoles at Doak Stadium, and attending Greek functions together. Our sorority strives to uphold the ideals of our founders while bettering our college experience and our community.
summary: Founded at the prestigious Cornell University, Sigma Delta Tau is a lifelong sisterhood. Our mission is to enrich the collegiate experience for women of similar ideals, to build lasting friendships, and to foster personal growth.

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
description:
Zeta Tau Alpha was founded October 15, 1898 by nine women at the State Female Normal School in Farmville, Virginia. Only 14-15 years of age, these young women desired permanence to their friendships and hoped to perpetuate their sisterhood long after college. With 171 chapters nationwide, Beta Gamma is the chapter here at Florida State and was the first ZTA chapter in Florida.

 

The mission of Zeta Tau Alpha is to make a difference in the lives of our membership by developing the potential of each individual through visionary programming which emphasizes leadership development, service to others, academic success and continued personal growth for women with a commitment to friendship and the future based on the values and traditions of our past. We stay true to our values through our national philanthropy, Breast Cancer Education and Awareness. Through partnerships with the American Cancer Society, the NFL, and Bright Pink, our chapter personally raised over $200,000 in the 2022-2023 year. We were recognized as the highest fundraising chapter in the nation. We also received an award for Highly Commendable Academic Achievement, Recruitment Excellence, the Crown Chapter Award, and a Province President Special Recognition award. As recipients of the 2016 Silver Award, one of the highest recognitions from ZTA's International Office, we are always striving to create the best experience for our members.
summary: Zeta Tau Alpha was founded October 15, 1898 by nine women at the State Female Normal School in Farmville, Virginia. Only 15 years of age, these young women desired permanence to their friendships and hoped to perpetuate their sisterhood after college.

name: Baseball Club
description:
A student run non-varsity intercollegiate baseball team. We play in division 1 NCBA in the spring. We have seen tremendous success as of late, appearing in 4 of the last 6 NCBA Division 1 World Series tournaments and winning the National Championship in 2022.

 

The purpose of the Baseball Club at FSU is to bring together students from Florida State who enjoy the sport and want to play competitively.

The objectives of the Baseball Club at FSU is to compete in competitive environments and to promote baseball around the Florida State campus.
summary: A student-run, competitive (yet recreational) baseball club.  We compete each spring in the NCBA, and we travel to various universities to play against other clubs.  For more info about our league, visit clubbaseball.org

name: The Bowling Club at Florida State University
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be to promote the sport of bowling, to encourage student activity and recreation, and to provide and sponsor bowling teams to represent Florida State University in bowling matches, tournaments and exhibitions.

FSU Intercollegiate Bowling Club

 

Purpose: Provide another competitive club to Florida State and also allow students to learn more about the sport and have a good time doing it.

 

If you would like to join the Bowling Club, contact Cade Rogers at jcr22a@fsu.edu
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to promote the sport of bowling, to encourage student activity and recreation, and to provide and sponsor bowling teams to represent Florida State University in bowling matches, tournaments, and exhibitions.

name: Climbing Club at FSU
description:
We exist to provide a safe and secure environment for climbers of all skill levels to practice and advance. We also provide instruction in a variety of climbing techniques in hopes of our members representing Florida State University at outdoor crags and competitions across the nation.

Climbing Club at FSU calls for meetings when needed, usually on Landis Green. Announcements will be made on Instagram and GroupMe. We also hold Climb Nights every THURSDAY at Alchemy from 7-10 PM. If you want to confirm any of our meetings or events, please contact us through Instagram, GroupMe, or email.

Membership is open to all current Florida State University students who are interested in climbing. 

Information about our local indoor climbing gym, Alchemy Climbing, is available on their website.

Contacts:
Email climbingclubfsu@gmail.com
Instagram @fsuclimbingclub
GroupMe Climbing Club @ FSU


For more information, check out our LinkTree
summary: We exist to provide a safe and secure environment for climbers of all skill levels to practice and advance. We also provide instruction in a variety of climbing techniques in hopes of our members representing Florida State University at competitions.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Rowing Club at Florida State University
description:
The purpose of our team is to provided a safe, fun and competitive environment in which the students of Florida State University can learn and enjoy the sport of rowing. We practice at Lake Talquin 5:30-7:30 PM Mon-Fri. Our team focuses on the "sweep" style of rowing which involves only one oar. Most of our boats are 4+ or 8+ which means 4 rowers and a coxswain or 8 rowers and a coxswain. Coxswains are typically tiny but mighty people in charge of steering and giving instructions to their boat.

We are a competitive team, placing our Novice Women's 4+ 3rd at Nationals 2018, and our Varsity Women's 4+ 4th at Nationals 2022. Our Novice Women’s 4 got 1st place at Regionals in 2022. At Regionals 2024 both our Women’s Pair and Men’s Lightweight 4+ got 3rd place. At Regionals 2025 our Lightweight Varsity Women's 4+ placed in 1st place. We race 5k in the fall and 2k in the spring. We are looking for athletic individuals or those tough enough to make up the difference. Do you have what it takes?

No university student may be denied membership on the basis of race, creed, religion, gender, age, sexual orientation, national origin, martial status, parental status, disability, or the inability to pay dues.
summary: The objective of the Rowing Team shall be to serve the Florida State University, and its community as an athletic outlet, both recreational and competitive in nature, in the sport of Rowing.

name: Cuong Nhu Martial Art Club
description: Cuong Nhu ("hard soft" in Vietnamese) incorporates the best elements of several martial arts styles. It incorporates techniques from Shotokan Karate, Judo, Wing Chung, T'ai Chi Chuan, Akido, Boxing and Vovinam (A Vietnamese martial art). Master Ngo Dong, an expert in Vovinam (Vietnamese martial arts) and a black belt in Judo and Shotokan, founded the Cuong Nhu style of martial arts in 1965 in Hue, Vietnam.
summary: Cuong Nhu, which means "hard soft" in Vietnamese, incorporates the best elements of several martial arts styles. It incorporates techniques from Shotokan Karate, Judo, Wing Chung, T'ai Chi Chuan, Akido, Boxing and Vovinam (A Vietnamese martial art).

name: Equestrian Club at FSU
description:
Our Club is dedicated to serving our campus and the Tallahassee community by providing valuable chances for giving back service and the opportunity for horseshowing on the collegiate level. 


 

Our members enjoy all the perks of our club such as discounted lesson prices, official team merchandise, friendship, community service, and contacts with fellow equestrians in the Tallahassee area.

 

Our Club has been widely-recognized as the best of its kind as we have previously won Sport Club of the Year three times and are frequent contenders in the National Horse Shows.
summary: The Equestrian Club at FSU is a Sport Club that accepts both competitive and non-competitive members.  We offer three different disciplines: hunt seat, western, and dressage, which each compete at horse shows against other colleges.

name: Softball Club at FSU
description:
Softball Club at FSU is a student-led team for athletes with fastpitch experience who want to compete at a high level. We practice twice a week, travel throughout the season to face other universities, and hold tryouts at the start of each semester.

 

Some quick facts about us:

  • Compete in the NCSA D1 South Atlantic Conference

  • Back-to-back World Series appearances (4th in 2025, 5th in 2024)

  • Involved in fundraising, community service, and team bonding year-round

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Tz9oBflSkkc

Check out our video feature! It gives a glimpse of who we are as a team. If you love softball, making new friends, and competing at a high level, come try out and join us!
summary: Club Softball at FSU is a student-led team for those with fastpitch softball experience who want to compete at a high level. We practice twice a week, travel throughout the season to play other universities, and hold tryouts at the start of each semester

name: Fencing Club
description: The Fencing Club at FSU exists to encourage the growth and development of fencing as a sport as well as to disseminate information about fencing on the FSU campus and within its supporting community. Whether you are new or returning to the sport, we hope to provide a healthy community for all fencers alike. This organization shall strive to uphold the sense of fair play and the level of proficiency necessary to achieve our purpose as our members venture out into the competitive scene.
summary: The Fencing Club exists to encourage the development of fencing as a sport and to spread fencing knowledge within the FSU community. Whether you are new or returning to the sport, we hope to provide a safe and healthy community for all fencers alike.
comment: Organization has not turned in the Recognition Application for 2010-2011

name: Field Hockey Club at FSU
description:
To play, learn, and promote the sport of field hockey, while complying to University, local, state, and federal laws.

Sports club designed to assist individuals in learning and progressing within the game of field hockey.

 

Promote the sport of field hockey through not only students but also the state of Florida. Very open atmosphere for learning the sport, or continuing previous experience in various capacities. Always welcoming new players (experienced or not)! Guys or Girls
summary: To play, learn, and promote the sport of field hockey, while complying to University, local, state, and federal laws.Sports club designed to assist individuals in learning and progressing within the game of field hockey.

name: Gymnastics Club at FSU
description: The purpose of this club is to provide a competitive gymnastics opportunity to students at Florida State. The club serves to bring gymnasts together to form a great competitive team to compete in NAIGC meets against other clubs around the nation. Practice is one and a half hours twice a week at International Gold Gymnastics.
summary: The purpose of this club is to provide a competitive gymnastics opportunity to students at Florida State. The club serves to bring gymnasts together to form a great competitive team to compete in NAIGC meets against other clubs.

name: Men's Ice Hockey Club at FSU
description:
The purpose of the Men's Ice Hockey Club at FSU is to promote excellence and increase awareness in all areas of
FSU competition.

Visit our website: https://www.fsuhockey.com

Visit our Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/fsuhockeyclub/?hl=en
summary: The purpose of the Men's Ice Hockey Club at FSU is to promote excellence and increase awareness in all areas of FSU competition and to provide an outlet for men's ice hockey players at FSU to continue to participate in competitive ice hockey.

name: Men's Lacrosse Club
description:
The purpose of this organization is to provide Florida State University with a competitive men's lacrosse team at the club level. We strive to excel in the sport of lacrosse in club competition while representing the University to our fullest ability.

Men's Lacrosse at FSU is a part of the MCLA (Men's Collegiate Lacrosse Association). We compete on a level much like Division I lacrosse, and compete for a spot in the National Championship Tournament at the end of the spring season. We play and are held to the same restrictions as programs of Division I caliber. Our goal, as a team, is to win the National tournament held in Round Rock, Texas.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide Florida State University with a competitive men's lacrosse team at the club level. We strive to excel in the sport of lacrosse in club competition while representing the University to our fullest

name: Men's Rugby at FSU
description:
The Rugby Football Club at FSU seeks to foster an environment in which our student athletes can develop into men of character through hard work and community service. In all of our activities we will represent Florida State University, the City of Tallahassee, and the State of Florida honorably. 


The goal of the Men's Rugby club at FSU is to provide a competitive atmosphere for athletes willing to learn and play the game of rugby. We currently compete in the Florida Collegiate Conference, Division-1 AA, which includes FSU, UCF, UNF, FAU, USF, UF, and FIU. Every year we compete against each team at least once, then go to playoffs based on our team's standing. After each game, it's tradition that the home team hosts a social where you get the opportunity to meet and network with people from all over the world!

Overall, rugby has contributed to our well being so much that we feel compelled to share this sport with everyone. Our program not only gives students the opportunity to learn a new sport and stay fit, but to emerge themselves in a new culture, meet new friends, and enhance their college experience entirely. This is why it's our mission to expand and share this experience with as many students at Florida State University.
summary: The Rugby Football Club at FSU seeks to foster an environment in which our student athletes can develop into men of character through hard work and community service. In all of our activities we will represent Florida State University.

name: Men's Soccer Club at FSU
description:
The Men's Soccer Club at FSU is made up of Florida State University students, faculty, and staff. The Men's Soccer Club participates in soccer related events in order to promote the sport of soccer throughout the university campus, the City of Tallahassee, and the State of Florida.

The Men's Soccer Club is affiliated with NIRSA (National Intramural and Recreational Sports Association), and competes in the Southeast Collegiate Soccer Alliance. We also host and travel to matches with soccer teams affiliated with other organizations such as the NAIA, NCAA, USSF, FYSA, and the ALeague.

The Men's Soccer Club at FSU also participates in tournaments throughout the US, including the NIRSA National Championship tournament held yearly in November.
summary: The Men's Soccer Club at FSU is made up of FSU students, faculty, and staff. The Men's Soccer Club participates in soccer related events in order to promote the sport of soccer throughout the university campus and the city of Tallahassee.

name: Men's Ultimate Frisbee
description:
We are the competitive Men's Ultimate Frisbee Club team at FSU and we compete against the best collegiate teams in the country. We also encourage students of all skill levels to participate in all of our events.

The purpose of Men's Ultimate Frisbee at Florida State is to spread the game of ultimate at FSU and in Tallahassee; and to represent FSU at events locally and nationally. An emphasis shall be put on competitive but spirited play in the intercollegiate USA Ultimate college series.

If you're interested, join our groupme! https://groupme.com/join_group/70106527/xj2ylWg9
summary: We are the competitive Men's Ultimate Frisbee Club team at FSU and we compete against the best collegiate teams in the country. We also encourage students of all skill levels to participate in all of our events.

name: Men's Water Polo Club
description: We play competitive water polo with other colleges and universities across the nation. During the Fall semester, we compete in the Collegiate Water Polo Association's Florida Division for a chance to compete at Nationals. During the Spring semester, we participate in tournaments throughout the Southeast. Men's Club Water Polo at Florida State offers students the chance to play water polo at a competitive level, from beginners to experienced athletes.
summary: Play competitive water polo amongst other schools and universities; We are in the Collegiate Water Polo Association's Florida Division and participate in other tournaments in the southeast.

name: Roller Hockey Club at FSU
description: Our club has about 10 members as we compete in the National Collegiate Roller Hockey Association as a member of the South East Collegiate Roller Hockey League. We have been a highly competitive team in our history, attending nationals the past three years, and are poised for another nationals run. We were the only undefeated team in the country in 2019-2020, finishing 18-0 and winning the SECRHL division. We compete in tournaments throughout the southeast region, including Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee. We are looking to recruit new players and grow as a team this season, feel free to reach out to our social media page to contact us!
summary: We are a college roller hockey team, as members of the NCRHA and SECRHL conference. We travel to four tournaments in a single season, followed by both regional and national tournaments.

name: Seminole Scuba Club
description:
SSC is a community filled with members who love to dive, snorkel, or simply be in the water. Our goal is to provide students the ability to safely and consistently dive recreationally, while also planning diving events, socials, community service activities, fundraisers, as well as promoting diving proficiency.


We are a community-based organization specifically oriented towards student divers, snorkelers, and other interested parties. Don't know how to dive? No worries, come out to one of our meetings and learn how you can start your diving journey right here in Tallahassee!

We coordinate and conduct community services for waterways around the county, such as semesterly cleanup efforts at local beaches and springs/sinks, as well as provide an educational format promoting diver proficiency, as well as safety. Major SCUBA equipment is provided through local licensed dive shops and donations from alumni over the years.

Being a part of SSC is more than going on fun dives. It is about sharing your passion for diving, snorkeling, or simply being in blue waters with others who feel the same exact way!
summary: To provide students the ability to safely and consistently dive recreationally, while also planning diving events, socials, community service activities, fundraisers, as well as promoting diving proficiency.

name: Swim Club at FSU
description:
Swim Club is a recreational club that also competes on a national level with other swim clubs around the country. We practice multiple times a week and typically travel 5-6 times a year to participate in swim meets. Our practices involve stroke drills, technique development exercises, and various workouts focused on building endurance, speed, and strength.

Practices are 6-8pm on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday! All practices are at the Leach Pool!
summary: Swim Club is one of FSU's 36 Sport Clubs. We are a co-ed team that actively practices together throughout the year, and travels to compete with swim clubs on the national level.  We are open to swimmers of all levels of swimming ability and experience.

name: Table Tennis Club at FSU
description:
Competitive and recreational club which meets at the Leach center
Purpose:
- Promote table tennis at FSU through practice, events and tournaments
- Foster growth in collegiate table tennis at FSU
- Foster community and well-being
summary:
Competitive and recreational club which meets at the Leach center
Purpose: 
- Promote table tennis at FSU through practice, events and tournaments
- Foster growth in collegiate table tennis at FSU
- Foster community and well-being

name: Wakeboard Club at FSU
description:
The purpose of the Florida State Wakeboard Club/Team is to provide an opportunity for Florida State students to improve their skills in wakeboarding and/or wakeskating.

The objective of Florida State Wakeboard Club/Team is to be a registered sports club, open to all students that wish to improve their skills behind the boat or wakeboarding complex.
summary: The purpose of the Florida State Wakeboard Club/Team is to provide an opportunity for Florida State students to improve their skills in wakeboarding and/or wakeskating.The objective of Florida State Wakeboard Club/Team is to be a registered sports club,

name: Weightlifting Club
description:
The Weightlifting Club provides a supportive atmosphere for students to strength train. We utilize compound barbell movements, especially the squat, the deadlift, and the bench press. Some members also perform Olympic lifts. Our first goal is safety and proficiency for members within the movements they perform. Our second goal is to facilitate an understanding of progressive overload training.

The club officially meets once a week to lift and share knowledge about technique, form, and progress. Early each semester, new lifters are given a great deal of feedback and supervision, as well as program that aligns with their goals. The club competes in powerlifting in the USAPL federation. As a competition approaches, the team will arrange for interested participants to train in such a way that performance at the meet is optimized. In addition to regular training and competition, we participate in and host clinics and community events that promote our training style.

Message the club through Nole Central, Instagram, or stop by Capital City Barbell during our scheduled training times to become part of the strongest student organization at FSU.
summary: The Weightlifting Club is an organization focused on strength training.  Whether you've never been in a gym, or you've been pumping iron all your life, you will find resources and support to develop and improve upon safe, effective lifting techniques
comment: Organizaiton has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Women's Club Soccer at Florida State University
description:
The mission of the Women's Club Soccer team is to allow for a competitive playing field for females wishing to further pursue soccer at the club level.

Our purpose is to create an athletic environment that is conducive to team building, as well as providing an opportunity for women to continue to play soccer at a high level, while still being able to enjoy their college experience. 

We travel through out the southeast to play in tournaments as well as hosting games and one tournament in the spring.
summary: The mission of Women's Club Soccer at FSU is to foster a competitive environment for women seeking to continue playing soccer at the club level. We aim to promote individual growth and team unity through a healthy, supportive, and empowering atmosphere.

name: Women's Rugby Club at FSU
description:
The goal of Women’s Rugby Club at FSU is to teach and competitively play the sport of women's rugby, as well as creating a welcoming environment for individuals to grow and create lifelong friendships.

We are an inclusive group that hopes to teach others about the great sport of rugby and play competitively.  The Women's Rugby Club at FSU competes against other schools as a Division 1 team and is a member of USA Collegiate Rugby. We play teams such as UCF and UF, but also often have the opportunity to play teams from other states both at home and away.

We practice three times a week, where players will learn the skills necessary to play. There will even be opportunities to play games and tournaments on select weekends. People of all backgrounds are encouraged to join us, including all levels of experience (even if you have never even seen a rugby ball or heard of the sport before!). Members will be encouraged to participate in fundraising, traveling, volunteering, team socials, and other team-oriented events.
summary: Our purpose is to compete in the ever growing sport of women's rugby against other schools as a Division I team and member of the USA National Rugby League. This is a full-contact sport, and all skill levels are highly encouraged to play.

name: Women's Club Volleyball at FSU
description: The Women’s Club Volleyball team at Florida State University brings together students who have years of volleyball experience and are looking to continue playing competitively in a fun, team environment. We hold 2-3 practices a week and have other required activities such as leadership series, community service, and fundraising. We travel to tournaments on the weekends, usually 3 to 4 times a semester. Tryouts are at the beginning of each semester and we welcome everyone to come out!
summary: To promote sportsmanship, camaraderie, and excellence through the practice and competitive play of indoor volleyball around the state and country. The women's volleyball club travels to other colleges to compete in tournaments.

name: Women's Water Polo Club
description:
We would like to offer the female students at Florida State University the opportunity to learn, participate, and compete in the sport of water polo at the intercollegiate level. As members of the Collegiate Water Polo Association we compete against teams such as the University of Florida, University of Central Florida, Emory University, University of Alabama, and Georgia Institute of Technology and have opportunities to travel to these schools as well. 

 

2023, 2024, and 2025 Regional Champions!
summary: We would like to offer the female students at Florida State University the opportunity to learn, participate, and compete in the sport of water polo at the intercollegiate level.

name: Wrestling Club at FSU
description:
An organization for all FSU students, faculty, and staff regardless of skill level to learn and practice folkstyle wrestling, with opportunities to compete against other club wrestlers from across the nation.

Our club actively participates in competitions, supports local wrestling at the youth level, gives back through community service, and collaborates with other club teams to improve our wrestling and create strong bonds between the members of our teams that extend off the mat.

The Wrestling Club at FSU is a member of the NCWA (National College Wrestling Association), which is the organization that provides club teams across the nation opportunities to compete, and is the only organization at the college level that uses folkstyle rules for women's competition.
summary: An organization for all FSU students, faculty, and staff regardless of skill level to learn and practice folkstyle wrestling, with opportunities to compete against other club wrestlers from across the nation.

name: AcaBelles
description:
The AcaBelles are Florida State University's only soprano-alto a cappella group. Since being founded in 2000, these women have been redefining female a cappella with their fierce choreography, killer vocals, and innovative arrangements. The 18 singers come from various backgrounds and individual styles to create a unique blend and commanding presence in the Tallahassee a cappella community. The group recently achieved international success with their cover of a certain regal chart-topper, catching the eyes and ears of CNN, Good Morning America, and Lorde herself.
