Since then, the 'Belles have traveled to numerous gigs, placed 3rd in 2024 ICCA Quarterfinals and raised funds to record a variety of albums, EPs, and singles, including Bloom, Phases, Wavelength, Seamless, Rebel, Dimensions, and Pretty Hurts now available on Spotify, iTunes, Apple Music, and Loudr.  

The purpose of AcaBelles is to promote soprano-alto a cappella singing, and to raise awareness of a cappella throughout the Florida State University and its surrounding areas.
summary: Founded in 2000, AcaBelles is Florida State University's premiere soprano-alto a cappella ensemble.  We perform our own arrangements of popular music around campus and the community, travel on tours, and participate in international competitions.

name: Zeta Beta Tau Fraternity
description:
The mission of Zeta Beta Tau Fraternity (ZBT) is to foster and develop in its membership the tenets of its Credo: Intellectual Awareness, Social Responsibility, Integrity and Brotherly Love, in order to prepare its members for positions of leadership and service within their communities.

Mindful of its founding in 1898 as the Nation’s first Jewish Fraternity, ZBT will preserve and cultivate its relationships within the Jewish community. Since 1954, ZBT has been committed to its policy of non-sectarian Brotherhood, and values the diversity of its membership. ZBT will recruit and initiate men of good character, regardless of religion, race or creed who are accepting of these principles.
summary: The mission of Zeta Beta Tau Fraternity (ZBT) is to foster and develop in its membership the tenets of its Credo: Intellectual Awareness, Social Responsibility, Integrity and Brotherly Love, in order to prepare its members for positions of leadership.

name: Advertising Club at FSU
description:
First off, join our Facebook page here!

The club is an organization that has guest speakers, trips around the country, information sessions, and networking that allows students to get a knowledge-base of the advertising industry outside of school.

The organization's purpose is to provide members with information, experience and networking skills that will allow them to succeed in the advertising industry.

Please refer to our Linktree for all of our other links!
summary: FSU Advertising Club provides members with insight into the advertising industry. You'll also get the opportunity to network with talented professionals, build your portfolio, score internships, and travel to cities such as New York, Chicago, & Boston.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: All-Night Yahtzee
description:
All-Night Yahtzee was created in the fall of 1998 by Brian Haverkate, motivated by his ambition and passion for music. Thus, All-Night Yahtzee was born, and was recognized as the first aCappella group in Florida State University's history. The group held its first concert in the spring of 1999, and thus, aCappella music was born at FSU.

Since our beginning, we have continued to evolve and flourish into one of the oldest and most notable groups in the nation. All-Night Yahtzee has attended many competitions and conventions, including NACC, SoJam, BOSS, Betsy, and even ICCA, the international aCappella competition. We have received many awards at these competitions. They stand as a testament to our hard work, and guidelines to elevate our sound and look. Aside from our competition presence, we pride ourselves on also remaining present in the recording world. We have produced many albums and singles, and in the summer of 2019, we released our new album, Reign. The album was nominated for 3 CARA awards.

Outside of competitions and recording, All-Night Yahtzee hosts a series of concerts and events around campus, as well as gigging throughout the community. We tour Florida every winter and spring, stopping at all our favorite cities and many of our hometowns. Aside from all the craziness of traveling, we were also featured on the Pitch-Perfect reality TV series, Sing It On on Pop Network.

Our members come from different places, musical backgrounds and interests, but when blended together we bring great music, entertainment, and a great time. All-Night Yahtzee holds annual Fall Auditions at the very beginning of the semester, and will occasionally hold spring auditions. We welcome everyone! Follow us on Facebook and Instagram to stay up to date!
summary: All-Night Yahtzee is Florida State's premier co-ed a cappella group.  Every year, this singing group rehearses, competes, and travels all over the country in order to spread their love for music and for a cappella.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta
description:
Alpha Epsilon Delta is the National Health Preprofessional Honor Society and service organization dedicated to the encouragement and recognition of excellence in preprofessional health scholarship, including medicine, dentistry, veterinary, and others. The Society welcomes ALL students engaged in the pursuit of a professional healthcare career. AED offers opportunities for intellectual and professional development, provides a forum for students with common interests, and extends a program of service to benefit the college/university community.

 

Our goal is to provide the interested pre-health student with as much exposure as possible to various aspects of medicine to ensure his/her understanding of what a career in medicine entails. Alpha Epsilon Delta is a pre-health honor society for students interested in the field of medicine and overall good health. Club benefits include highly qualified guest speakers, Dean's Day, community-centered service events, special event teams, tutoring, and more.

 

To join Alpha Epsilon Delta, head over to our website aedfsu.com and click the join page. Read through the page and join our Canvas, GroupMe, and fill out the application link. We'd love to have you!
summary: Alpha Epsilon Delta is a National Health Preprofessional Honor Society and service organization. We are dedicated to the encouragement and recognition of excellence in preprofessional health scholarship, including medicine, dentistry, veterinary, etc.
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description:
The Beta Psi Chapter of Alpha Kappa Psi at The Florida State University is a concentration of motivated and success-oriented business students. We have the common objectives of professional preparation, development, and success.


Members are prepared to excel in “real world” business by providing opportunities to develop leadership, organization, time management, and speaking skills. Through networking, resume writing, career assistance, and professional workshops, members are continually building their business skills and furthering individual growth.

Alpha Kappa Psi. the largest and oldest coed Professional Business Fraternity, presents essential tools necessary to enrich the professional and social aspects of college life outside of the classroom. Membership in the Beta Psi Chapter provides valuable benefits and personal growth, both professionally and socially. Community Service work with Heart Walk, Relay for Life, and the local community is also a large focus for the Brother members.
summary: The Beta Psi Chapter of Alpha Kappa Psi at The Florida State University is a concentration of motivated and success-oriented business and non-business students. We have the common objectives of professional preparation, development, and success.

name: Alpha Chi Sigma
description:
Purely professional organizations have their place, and Alpha Chi Sigma constantly encourages its members to become active in these organizations. Social fraternities also offer much to students. But only a professional fraternity – and Alpha Chi Sigma is the only national professional chemistry fraternity in the United States – can provide the college student and the professional in chemistry and related fields with the interests and bonds of a fraternity.

The purpose of Alpha Chi Sigma is reflected in the Three Objects of the Fraternity, which express the true ideals of all its members:

  1. To bind its members with a tie of true and lasting friendship.
  2. To strive for the advancement of chemistry both as a science and as a profession.
  3. To aid its members by every honorable means in the attainment of their ambitions as chemists throughout their mortal lives.

Alpha Chi Sigma is one of a kind. Following the original concept for a fraternity as an academic organization, we are the only professional chemistry fraternity. More than 100 years old with more than 70,000 members, we bring together men and women pursuing a wide variety of chemistry-related careers.

Our past is prestigious. Not only are many of our members world-class scientists and engineers, but we sponsor a variety of scholarships and awards. Notable ones are the ACS Award in Pure Chemistry and the AIChE Award in Chemical Engineering. With our involvement in other chemistry and chemical engineering professional organizations, our members are a bridge between these various organizations.

Collegiate members benefit from a local group to not only help with their studies but also projects and activities that teach the roles of leadership and management. We also provide an opportunity to create lifelong friendships with others interested in chemistry. Sometimes it's just nice to walk into a room and be able to tell a chemistry joke.

As a Professional member, further opportunities for meeting other members can help with your employment, hiring and relaxation. Our Professional members appreciate meeting others involved in chemistry outside of their workplaces and often outside of their field of work.

Membership is for life.
summary: Following the original concept for a fraternity as an academic organization, we are the only professional chemistry fraternity. We bring together men and women pursuing a wide variety of chemistry-related careers.
comment: Organization has not turned in the Recognition Application for 2010-211.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
Alpha Phi Omega is a National Co-Ed Service Fraternity whose three main principals are Leadership, Friendship, and Service.  Unlike other fraternities, Alpha Phi Omega does not have a house on campus and places an emphasis upon service: for the chapter, campus, community, and country. We also have a strict no-hazing policy, one that is upheld by our national board. The Iota Rho chapter at Florida State was founded in 1951 and reinvigorated in 1989 and has since remained consistently active on the campus. We began with simple projects, like installing bike racks and hosting a textbook exchange for students. Since then, our service has grown to all reaches of Tallahassee, with some projects reaching a national scale. Some projects include:

Menstrual hygiene tabling - advocating for the homeless population that is often left without hygiene products. We ask for both monetary and material donations. 

Litchgate - Assisting a local garden in maintenance and development.

Food recovery - Collecting, recording, and delivering food waste from various locations on campus to in-need populations.

Dog walks and dog washes.

 

We host socials throughout the semester, ranging from pool parties, bonfires, and retreats, to general hangouts like getting food or playing IM sports. While service is a huge aspect of this organization, we also ensure our members are given the opportunity to become lifetime friends. 

One unique aspect of this organization is the power given to every individual. If there is a cause you want to support or an idea you want to share, you can. You can make your own projects, even when just beginning in the organization. Every member is a leader at all times. 

 

The purpose of this fraternity shall be to assemble college students in a national service fraternity in the fellowship of principles derived from the Scout Oath and Scout Law of the Boy Scouts of America; to develop leadership, to promote friendship, and to provide service to humanity; and to further the freedom that is our national, educational, and intellectual heritage.
summary: Alpha Phi Omega is a National Co-Ed Service Fraternity whose three main principals are Leadership, Friendship, and Service.  Unlike other fraternities, Alpha Phi Omega has no house on campus, no hazing of any kind, and emphasizes service at all times.

name: American Association of Textile Chemists and Colorists
description:
The American Association of Textile Chemists and Colorists FSU chapter is a part of the national AATCC organization. The club addresses the interests of students related to the retail industry and the fashion industry overall. Members receive exclusive information on internships and industry professionals with the help of the national AATCC organization resources. The student organization provides personal and professional growth opportunities for students of all majors. When possible, the club goes on field trips to local retailers, has guests speakers at general body meetings, and members have the opportunity to aid in the planning of an on-campus fashion show each semester. ​ 

Philanthropies we love and support:
The BIG Event
Dress For Success
summary: Students mostly with majors in fields that will be in fashion, apparel, textiles and retail professions. AATCC hosts an on campus fashion show each semester, prepares members for their professional careers, and aids in personal and professional growth.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: American Choral Directors Association
description:
The student chapter of ACDA provides all interested FSU students the opportunity to deepen and broaden their understanding of the choral art. ACDA members come from the ranks of both undergraduate and graduate students, music and non-music majors alike. They include future music educators, current church musicians, professional vocalists, composers, and music enthusiasts.

ACDA is a national nonprofit organization whose goal is to promote excellence in choral music. ACDA is a group of choral professionals who find joy in the performance, composition, publishing, research, and teaching of choral music.

ACDA strives to elevate the role of choral music in society from the smallest towns to the largest cities across America.
summary: The student chapter of ACDA provides all interested FSU students the opportunity to deepen and broaden their understanding of the choral art.  ACDA members come from the ranks of both undergraduate and graduate students, music and non-music majors alike.
comment: Organization has not turned in the Recognition Application for 2010-2011.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide professional enrichment and social support to all undergraduate students in the Department of Chemical and Biomedical Engineering at the FAMU-FSU College of Engineering. All activities and functions of the American Institute of Chemical Engineers Student Chapter shall be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.
summary:
The national professional organization by and for chemical engineers for the professional development of students in preparation for careers in chemical and biomedical engineering. 
To join contact AIChE President Nina Mihajlov at nm19n@fsu.edu

name: American Medical Association/ Florida Medical Association
description:
The mission of the American Medical Association (AMA) is to promote the art and science of medicine and the betterment of public health through leadership, excellence, integrity, and ethical behavior.  AMA strives to be an essential part of the professional life of every physician and to help doctors help patients by uniting physicians nationwide to work on the most important professional and public health issues. The Florida Medical Association (FMA) and the Capital Medical Society (CMS) mirror these values, with further focus on issues pertinent to the state of Florida and the city of Tallahassee.  Together, the AMA, FMA and CMS play an active role in shaping the future of medicine.

The AMA, FMA, and CMS offer an amazing opportunity for medical students to join a network of health professionals and resources to help them excel as physicians, public health practitioners, and leaders within their communities.  As a member of these organizations, students are given the tools to be kept informed of current and pressing health issues and policies.  Members also enjoy numerous opportunities to win scholarship money and honors.  Several times a year, students are able to travel to regional and national AMA/FMA conferences and partake as voting delegates, award recipients or simply as inquisitive members.  Through these conferences, students are able to interact with fellow medical students and prominent physicians from all across the nation and learn about important health care topics and how individual medical students can make a difference. 
FSU College of Medicine’s AMA/FMA/CMS chapter offers many opportunities for students to get involved on campus, in Tallahassee and on the state and national levels.  With the State Capitol and FMA headquarters nearby, students are able to personally intermingle with State legislators, sit in on Senate committee meetings and attend receptions and dinners with influential physicians and lawmakers.  In addition, with 7 campuses and a voting delegate at each campus, FSU COM has the largest voting power in the State, giving us a great edge at national AMA meetings.  All students are encouraged to submit resolutions at these meetings.  FSU COM’s AMA/FMA/CMS chapter is also active in community service events, including the Annual Cover the Uninsured Week and AMA National Projects- these projects change yearly with past headlines being Anti-Tobacco Education and Get-Healthy Project. And of course, FSU COM’s AMA/FMA/CMS chapter holds monthly lunch meetings at the College of Medicine with local physicians and leaders as guest speakers.
For more information on how to get involved today, please contact one of your AMA/FMA/CMS officers!!
summary: The AMA-MSS is dedicated to representing medical students, improving medical education, developing leadership, and promoting activism for the health of America.

name: American Medical Student Association, FSUCOM Chapter
description:
To be committed to the improvement of health care and health care delivery to all people; to promote the active improvement of medical education; to involve its members in the social, moral and ethical obligations of the profession of medicine; to assist in the improvement and understanding of world health problems; to contribute to the welfare of all members, including premedical students, medical students, interns, residents and post M.D./D.O. trainees; to advance the profession of medicine; to work to ensure that medicine reflects the diversity of society, with diversity including but not limited to differences in age, culture, race/ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender and disability.

AMSA is an organzation devoted to students interest in medicine. It is up-to-date with bringing current medical issues to tomorrow's doctors. We are concerned with students health and student life. Ultimately AMSA's goal is to take action as students and become stronger advocates for where we believe medicine should be going. AMSA sponsors national primary care week, healthcare speakers, skit night, and more.
summary: To be committed to the improvement of health care and health care delivery to all people; to promote the active improvement of medical education; to involve its members in the social, moral and ethical obligations of the profession of medicine.

name: American Medical Women's Association
description:
The American Medical Women's Association (AMWA) is a national organization that is dedicated to promoting women's health, improving the professional development and personal wellbeing of its members, and increasing the influence of women in all aspects of the medical profession.

American Medical Women's Association is a national organization designed to address the needs of female medical professionals and female patients. At the FSU College of Medicine, this group serves two purposes. First, it is a source of support for female medical students. Women pose unique challenges as physicians as they attempt to balance family and medicine. AMWA is here to provide support and advice. Second, we serve as a source of information about the special needs of female patient populations. Topics we are presently addressing at FSU COM are the health and well-being of medical students, violence against women, and professional development.
summary: AMWA is a national organization that is dedicated to promoting women's health, improving the professional development and personal wellbeing of its members, and increasing the influence of women in all aspects of healthcare.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers at FAMU-FSU College of Engineering
description: The ASME Student Section is dedicated to the professional and technical development of future mechanical engineers. Our mission is to bridge the gap between the classroom and the industry by promoting the exchange of engineering theory, providing direct networking opportunities with top firms, and fostering a collaborative community of peers. We empower students to build their professional identity and launch successful careers within the global ASME network.
summary: ASME is a non-profit membership organization that enables collaboration, knowledge sharing, career enrichment, and skills development, toward a goal of helping the global engineering community develop solutions to benefit lives and livelihoods.

name: Anthropology Society at FSU
description:
ASFSU aims to promote student involvement in anthropology through sponsoring regular workshops, supporting student participation in volunteer work, internship, and fieldwork and extracurricular activities emphasizing the anthropological perspective.

The purpose of this organization shall be threefold:
1. To further professional interest in anthropology by affording a common organization for those interested in the field.
2. To strengthen student and professional training through academic experiences other than those of the classroom and laboratory.
3. To advance the status of anthropology as a cultural and practical discipline for study and investigation.
summary: The Anthropology Society at Florida State University (ASFSU) is a student-driven organization currently affiliated with the Lambda Alpha National Honor Society.

name: Student Government Association Congress of Graduate Students
description: In accordance to the Student Government Constitution, COGS is a funding body of the Student Government Association and has been granted its own set of administrative rules and procedures. In other words, the COGS assembly is solely responsible for allocating all of the A&S fees it receives from graduate students each year.
summary: The Congress of Graduate Students (COGS) is the official representative body of all post-baccalaureate special, masters, specialist, professional, and doctoral students at Florida State.

name: Women's Law Symposium
description: WLS is dedicated to promoting the status of women in the legal community. WLS shall provide a forum for the discussion of issues concerning legal education and provide programs to advance and assist students throughout their law school career, including networking and leadership opportunities. WLS strives to actively participate in charitable and voluntary causes in the community.
summary: WLS is dedicated to promoting the status of women in the legal community. WLS shall provide a forum for the discussion of issues concerning legal education and provide programs to advance and assist students throughout their law school career.

name: Women Student Union
description:
The purpose of WSU is:

  1. To develop a greater awareness in the Florida State University community of the nature of women's problems and their relationship to the economic, social, and political nature of the American Society.
  2. To promote the growth of a positive approach towards women's concerns in the Florida State University community.
  3. To develop programs, services, and activities that will reach out to women.
  4. To build a spirit of camaraderie among women so that a feeling of strength and solidarity can be fostered.
  5. To actively confront sexual discrimination in the Florida State University.
  6. To lobby the University Administration regarding policy and security issue that affect women.

 

Mission Statement:

The Women Student Union fosters the growth of women personally, professionally and politically. The WSU will celebrate the power existing within every woman to promote self-autonomy, denounce all limitations, educate on systems of oppression and advocate inclusivity within a diverse community. WSU facilitates a network among organizations, departments, and services to embrace, engage and empower.
summary: The Women Student Union is an identity-based group within Student Government whose overall mission is to unite women on the common purpose of individual self-growth.

name: Women in Business
description:
Women in Business at Florida State University aims to promote professional enrichment and education among FSU's most ambitious students.

 

The Florida State University College of Business Student Leadership Council began the Women in Business organization to both promote an awareness of the dynamic role of women in today's business environment, and establish a forum for developing a critical discourse of that role. The organization serves as a developmental education and networking tool for students, faculty, staff, and business professionals alike.
summary: Women in Business at Florida State University aims to promote professional enrichment and education among FSU's most ambitious students.

name: Women in Accounting Organization at FSU
description:
Hello and welcome to our page! At Women in Accounting, our commitment is offering the chance to network and gain insights into the diverse opportunities within the accounting field, while fostering a positive and collaborative environment.

 

We work to bring you networking opportunities with professionals from leading accounting firms. Join us at our meetings on Tuesday evenings at 7:00 P.M. throughout the Fall and Spring semesters! Our meetings include all types of activities such as panel discussions, informative sessions, and interactive events with professionals from various accounting firms. It's a fantastic chance to not only gain knowledge but also to have relaxed and open conversations with recruiters and industry professionals.

 

We're genuinely thrilled to invite you to be a part of our community. Everyone, including finance majors and men, is not just welcome but highly encouraged to attend. We look forward to seeing you!
summary: The FSU College of Business chapter of Women in Accounting was organized during the Spring 2009 semester to address the unique challenges facing women entering the accounting field.

name: Wilderness Medical Society
description: The national Wilderness Medical Society aims to advance healthcare, research, and education related to wilderness medicine. This is a student interest group through the College of Medicine and COGS that seeks to do the same at FSU.
summary: The national Wilderness Medical Society aims to advance healthcare, research, and education related to wilderness medicine. This is a student interest group through the College of Medicine and COGS that seeks to do the same at FSU.

name: Art History Association
description: The Art History Association (AHA) brings graduate students, university faculty, and academics together in professional and social settings in order to further enhance the learning experience at Florida State. Our organization achieves this mission by offering professional lectures, symposia, luncheons, field trips to various museums and galleries, and support for graduate students.
summary: The graduate Art History Association, or AHA, provides its members with a means for interacting with other students, faculty, and professionals in the field.

name: Student Art Therapy Association at Florida State University
description: The Student Art Therapy Association (SATA) at FSU is an organization connected to the graduate level art therapy program in the Department of Art Education. As an organization, we participate in volunteer activities that bring art therapy into the community, promote self care of our members, attend and participate in the annual AATA national conference, and host an educational workshop in the spring semester with a guest speaker in the field of art therapy.
summary: The Student Art Therapy Association (SATA) is an organization connected to the graduate level art therapy program in the Department of Art Education. As an organization, we participate in volunteer activities that bring art therapy into the community.

name: Asian American Student Union
description:
Established in 1996, the Asian American Student Union was created to better represent the Asian American community at Florida State University and to spread awareness about Asian heritage and culture.

The AASU serves to promote and provide for the social welfare of the Asian American student body at Florida State University. It functions as an educational and cultural learning instrument for the FSU community by providing cultural programs and materials, which promote awareness of and appreciation for the various and distinct Asian cultures. AASU advocates for the growth of civic engagement and involvement with our members as well as students at FSU. The AASU shall serve as the nucleus of a network to improve the opportunities and living conditions of the Asian community. To this end, the AASU shall represent all registered Asian student organizations on campus, coordinate available resources, and serve as a catalyst in protecting the rights of the Asian student body. To educate students and promote the Asian cultures through seminars, workshops, guest speakers, and social events and to also promote unity within the Asian cultures
summary: Established in 1996, the Asian American Student Union was created to better represent the Asian American community at Florida State University, spread awareness about Asian heritage and culture, and engage students in social events to build community.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description:
The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) is a campus-based, not-for-profit organization under the umbrella SGA agency of the Asian American Student Union. VSA is committed to display to its community the preserved culture and heritage adopted from our ancestors, creating cultural awareness and educating those who are interested in learning our customs, practices, and interests.
 
Not only does our organization create and stimulate awareness of the transforming Vietnamese-American cultural identity, but we also foster friendship, good will and community among our members of all racial and ethnic backgrounds in Florida State University.

The Vietnamese Student Association will voice our message by tackling any social barriers that may hinder us from displaying our culture and heritage with pride, at the same time, show appreciation of the "American" in Vietnamese "American" through community service, social activities, programs, and vast networking of friends, family, and peers.

This organization was created in order to: create and stimulate awareness of the transforming Vietnamese-American cultural identity to its members and community, educate and empower our community through various programs and activities to create conscious awareness, aid in community service alongside the other affiliations of the Asian American Student Union in any way this organization is able to.
summary: The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) is an affiliate under the Asian American Student Union (AASU) and is an organization representing Vietnamese culture at Florida State University!

name: associationoflatinomedicalstudents
description:
LMSA is organized primarily for providing support and guidance to Latino, Hispanic, and all medical students in FSUCOM and FSU undergraduates.  This organization also aims to raise awareness about latin culture and medicine amongst the latin community. 

(1) providing support and guidance to Latino medical students in FSUCOM;
(2) providing education programs for Latino snd non-latino medical students in the FSUCOM;
(3) promoting biomedical and health services research, including cultural competence and language issues, for members of the Latino community and to address the health care needs of this community;
(4) developing interest in Latino health policy research by Latino and non-latino medical students at FSUCOM;
(5) encouraging Latino college students to pursue careers in medicine and health care services which address the health care needs of members of Hispanic community
(6) the conduct of any other activities related to the above-stated
purposes, including the promotion of charitable, religious, educational and scientific purposes in Latino community
(7) provide support to undergraduate students who have interest in a future medical career.
summary: LMSA is organized primarily for providing support and guidance to Latino, Hispanic, and all medical students in FSUCOM and FSU undergraduates. This organization also aims to raise awareness about latino culture and medicine amongst the latino community.

name: Baptist Collegiate Ministry
description:
The FSU Baptist Collegiate Ministry is a ministry of the Florida Baptist Convention and is a registered student organization under the Interfaith Council at Florida State University. By God’s grace we have been making disciples on campus since 1925 right beside our local church partners.

We work right beside the local church to make disciples on the campus of Florida State University and beyond, and to help our students pursue Jesus along side a community of believers. This mission is accomplished through our weekly meetings, Tribes, Bible studies, and a variety of on-campus events. All college students are welcome.
summary: The FSU Baptist Collegiate Ministry works right beside the local church to make disciples on the campus of Florida State University and beyond, and to help our students pursue Jesus along side a community of believers.

name: The Belly Dancers at Florida State
description:
The Belly Dancers at Florida State, a recognized student organization, provides service to the University and local community by hosting FREE belly dance classes and by performing all over Tallahassee. 

The FSU Belly Dancers strive to promote cultural diversity, self-esteem and good health through the art of belly dance.

We offer free beginner and intermediate level belly dance classes, no experience necessary! Students of the class are taught a full choreographed dance which can be performed at the showcase. Those who wish to do so are given the opportunity to audition for the performance troupe. For more info find FSU Belly Dancers on Facebook and Instagram. 

The FSU Belly Dancers performance troupe is the face of the organization. The troupe performs at shows and events on campus and around the community. To book us for your event, fill out the performance request form at https://docs.google.com/forms/d/169VZ739ttlIix-oA3Q2ALCe3mJQp7AszBL3o21OC3sU/viewform 

For more information email BellyDancersFloridaState@gmail.com.
summary: Promoting cultural diversity, self-esteem, and good health through the art of belly dance. Visit us on Facebook and Instagram @FSUBellyDancers

name: Best Buddies
description: Best Buddies is an international non-profit organization that focuses on forming friendships with people with and without intellectual and developmental disabilities, as well as providing opportunities for leadership and jobs. Here at FSU, we foster a positive environment based first and foremost on inclusion. Each month, our members participate in fun and exciting events, such as our Field Day, Talent Show, Holiday party and much more! Additionally, we host a Gala in the fall and a Friendship Walk in the spring. This is a fantastic organization to join if you are looking to meet awesome people and create life-long friendships. If you want to become a member or have questions please email fsubestbuddies@gmail.com.
summary: The mission of Best Buddies is to establish a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment and leadership development for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: Beta Alpha Psi - Beta Rho Chapter
description:
Beta Alpha Psi recognizes academic excellence and complements members' formal education through networking among students, faculty, and professionals. Our Beta Rho chapter at Florida State holds professional events with numerous accounting firms like the Big 4, RSM, and Grant Thornton. We are also proud of our continued community service, tutoring, socials, and professional development activities that foster lifelong growth, an appreciation for service, and ethical conduct.

For more information: contact our President, Jack Tinkler, at jbt22a@fsu.edu
summary: Beta Alpha Psi recognizes academic excellence and complements members' formal education by providing interaction among students, faculty, and professionals, while fostering lifelong growth, appreciation for service, and ethical conduct.

name: The Beta Chapter of Kappa Psi Psi Healthcare Sorority, Incorporated
shortName: Kappa Psi Psi Healthcare Sorority, Inc.
description:
Kappa Psi Psi Healthcare Sorority, Inc. is a professional Greek letter organization that fosters professionalism, networking, scholarship, and service. Kappa Psi Psi Healthcare Sorority, Inc. achieves these facets through the promotion of health awareness, community service as well as providing seminars on and off campus.

 

The purpose of Kappa Psi Psi Healthcare Sorority, Inc. is to conduct sisterhood in the organization, advance the health releated professions: educationally, sisterly, and socially, as well as instill diligence and high ideals among its members.
summary: Kappa Psi Psi is a professional healthcare sorority that fosters networking, scholarship and service. Kappa Psi Psi achieves these facets through the promotion of health awareness, community service as well as providing seminars on and off campus.

name: Black Graduate Student Association
description:
The Black Graduate Student Association (BGSA) is a student organization aimed at addressing the needs of black graduate students and providing opportunities for mentorship, service and building relationships between its members and other students and organizations.

Our Purpose

The purpose of the Black Graduate Student Association is to provide opportunities for social, service and professional involvement for minority graduate students at Florida State University. We also aim to create interdependent relationships between black graduate, law and medical students as well as undergraduate students at Florida State University

Our Motto

“To Empower, Enrich and Enlighten the Graduate Experience”

Our History

The Black Graduate Student Association (BGSA) was reestablished in the fall semester of 1997 at the Florida State University (FSU)

Our Mission

To Empower the graduate experience through community outreach and professional workshops, enabling our members to build relationships with the community and promote excellence in educational instruction, research and presentation of graduate work.

To Enrich the graduate experience through activities and events designed to create synergy and strengthen liaisons with administrators, faculty, other student organizations and the African-American community at large.

To Enlighten the graduate experience through undergraduate outreach, ultimately assisting in increasing the enrolment of minority students in Graduate programs at Florida State University.

Membership

Diversity strengthens the cohesion at FSU and enhances the university’s quality of life. The BGSA is committed to diversity and assuring a climate at FSU that values and respects the worth and dignity of all persons. Furthermore, BGSA membership, programs, services, and activities are open to all regardless of age, color, creed, disability, gender, national origin, race, religion, or sexual orientation.

Email: BGSAatFSU@gmail.com
summary: The Black Graduate Student Association (BGSA) is a student organization aimed at addressing the needs of black graduate students and providing opportunities for mentorship, service and building relationships between its members and other students.

name: The Black Law Students Association, College Student Division at FSU
description:
The Black Law Student Association, College Student Division is an organization whose focus is to ensure that all students interested in law are being adequately informed, fostered, and empowered with the necessary tools to be successful in Law School.

The purpose of the organization shall be to utilize the collective resources of its members and chapters to:

I. Articulate and promote the professional needs and goals of pre-law students (including but not limited to minorities);

II. Foster and encourage professional competence;

III. Focus upon the relationship of the pre-law student, law student, and Attorney to the American legal structure;

IV. Instill in the pre-law student, law student and Attorney a greater awareness and commitment to the needs of minority communities;

V. Encourage minority pre-law and law students to pursue careers in the judiciary; and

VI. Do all things necessary and appropriate to accomplish these purposes.
summary: The Pre-Law Division of the Black Law Students Association is an organization whose focus is to ensure that all students interested in law are being adequately informed, fostered, and empowered with the necessary tools to be successful in Law School.

name: The Black Student Union
description:
The Black Student Union seeks to develop unity among the Black students so that jointly and strongly they can express concerns and problems faced on campus. The Black Student Union attempts to create awareness to the total university community about issues relating to Black culture through its sponsorship and involvement in programs on campus. By assuming this role, we hope to bring about Black awareness to through planning and participating in Political, academic, and cultural activities. Through our future growth and development we know that Black Students will be understood. The Black Student Union is the official voice and representative on issues concerning Black students.
 
The Black Student Union is a Student Government Agency that offers Black students a form of identity, a social life and functions as a vehicle of communication to the faculty and administration of the Florida State University.

 

The BSU shall serve primarily to promote and provide for the social welfare of the Black Student Body at the Florida State University.  The BSU shall be limited neither to the aforementioned objectives nor to the Florida State University in scope.  The BSU shall also strive to provide and promote economic, political and academic enrichment through its activities, and it shall serve as a catalyst to the enlightenment of the Florida State University community to the distinct and proud cultural heritage of Black people in America as well as on the African continent. Finally, it shall be a source of edification of the Black experience at Florida State University.
summary: The Black Student Union seeks to develop unity among the Black students so that jointly and strongly they can express concerns and problems faced on campus. The Black Student Union attempts to create awareness to the total university community about

name: Florida State University Business Review
description:
Founded in 2000, the Business Review is a legal journal of the Florida State University College of Law.

 

The Business Review is designed to provide a scholarly forum for contemporary legal discourse and to address the issues and concerns transforming the business law community. In doing so, the Business Review offers its readers a unique perspective of the various topics under the rubric of business, and the ways in which the mechanisms of business both influence and are influenced by the law. The Business Review is published once per year, is led by its Editorial Board, and is guided by its Faculty Advisors and the College of Law administration.

 

The Business Review publishes articles by professors, judges, and practitioners, as well as Notes written by second- and third-year law students. Each piece selected for publication in the Business Review is subjected to a rigorous editorial process designed to sharpen and strengthen substance and tone.
summary: The Business Review is a legal journal of the Florida State University College of Law and is designed to provide a scholarly forum for contemporary legal discourse and to address the issues and concerns transforming the business law community.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Cru
description:
Cru exists to be a spiritual resource and community for students at FSU. We aim to help students experience true satisfaction through Jesus. 

 

The purpose of Cru at Florida State University is to help people experience true satisfaction through Christ.
summary:
Cru is a campus ministry with the vision of being a spiritual resource to the campus.

The purpose of Cru at Florida State University is to help students and faculty grow spiritually and connect in community.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Caribbean Student Association
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be to provide both Caribbean and other students with an active voice on campus in matters concerning Caribbean students at Florida State University.  The Association provides social, cultural, and educational activities for the FSU and Tallahassee communities. The Association is open to people born in the Caribbean, people of Caribbean ancestry, and people who are interested in Caribbean culture and affairs.

The purpose of this organization shall be to provide both Caribbean and other students with an active voice on campus in matters concerning Caribbean students at Florida State University. As an organization CSA aims to bring cultural awareness to the campus of Florida State, as well as build future Caribbean leaders that can be effective leaders of the local and global community. Through different events The Caribbean Student Association brings people from all walks of life together in celebration of the diverse and lively Caribbean culture following our motto, "Caribbean By Nature, United By Choice".
summary: The Caribbean Student Association brings people from all walks of life together in celebration of the diverse and lively Caribbean culture following our motto, "Caribbean By Nature, United By Choice".

name: Catholic Student Union
description: Catholic Student Union is an organization of university students who come together to strengthen, inquire about, and share their faith through community service, spiritual growth, and friendship. CSU is a community of students who are interested in learning more about the Catholic faith!
summary: Catholic Student Union is an organization of university students who come together to share their faith through community service, spiritual growth, and friendship. A community of students who are interested in learning more about the Catholic faith!

name: ChiAlpha Campus Ministry
description:
We are a community of students at Florida State University who passionately follow Jesus. Our mission is to be Christ's Ambassadors- to introduce students to the person of Jesus and be an expression of His likeness on our campus. We reconcile students to Christ through Spirit-Filled communities of prayer, worship, fellowship, discipleship, and mission to transform the university, the marketplace, and the world. You can find us tabling on campus throughout the week or at our weekly Monday night service!

 

We also have men's and women's Bible studies throughout the week.

We'd love to connect with you!

​

​

"We are therefore Christ’s ambassadors, as though God were making his appeal through us. We implore you on Christ’s behalf: Be reconciled to God." 

2 Corinthians 5:20
summary: We’re a Christian campus ministry on universities all around the world. We exist to reach the unreached, empower believers, and transform the university with the love of Christ. No matter your background, there a place for you here!
comment: Organizaiton has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Chinese American Student Organization
description:
The Chinese American Student Organization aims to provide a community for Chinese-American and other interested students on campus by hosting a variety of cultural events and activities. 

By being active in the FSU community, taking part in and creating cultural activities, as well as gathering resources for language studies, the Chinese American Student Organization will encourage the discourse between Chinese and American culture.
summary: To promote and organize social as well as cultural activities for incoming and present members of the Chinese American Student Organization, as well as the FSU community at large.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association
description: The main aim of this organization is to encourage cultural interaction and friendship among all individuals and offer services to its registered members. Additionally, it endeavors to facilitate the exchange of information between Chinese students studying abroad and China, thereby providing opportunities and benefits to its members. This student organization is dedicated to promoting social, intellectual, and cultural activities for Chinese students and scholars at Florida State University, with a mission to promote cultural interaction and friendship for all, as well as provide services for its registered members.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote friendship and cultural interaction for all people and to provide services for its registered members. It will also strive to provide opportunities and benefits for its members by facilitating the exchange
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: Christian Legal Society
description:
The mission of the Chapter is to maintain a vibrant Christian law student fellowship on the Florida State University College of Law campus, which enables its members, individually and as a group, to love the Lord with their whole beings--hearts, souls, and minds--and to love their neighbors as themselves. (Matthew 22:37-40).

 

In striving to accomplish this mission, the Chapter shall be guided by the ten purposes set forth in Art. I of the national CLS bylaws and by the LSM Threefold Ministry Model, which entails three interrelated activities to be carried out by the Chapter:


- Cultivating spiritual growth through communal prayer, fellowship, and worship; learning to share one's faith; and devotional study of the Bible and classic Christian works.


- Showing the love of Christ to the campus community and the community at large by proclaiming the gospel in word and in deed, such as through a life of integrity and charitable good works; as Martin Luther put it, "to be as Christ to our neighbor.


- Addressing the question, "What does it mean to be a Christian in law?" that is, learning to submit every aspect of one's calling in the legal profession to the Lordship of Jesus Christ.
summary: The mission of the Chapter is to maintain a vibrant Christian law student fellowship on the FSU College of Law campus to enable its members, individually and as a group, to love the Lord with their whole beings and to love their neighbors as themselves.

name: Christian Medical Association
description: The goal of the Christian Medical Association is to aid Christian medical students, physicians, and all others pursuing the health profession to gain mutual strength and encouragement through meeting with each other. The goals of the Christian Medical Association include emphasizing the essential duty and privilege of every Christian to take a definite role and interest in world wide evangelism and working to be a positive witness of God, our Father, and our Savior, Jesus Christ to those around us.
summary: The goal of the Christian Medical Association is to aid Christian medical students, physicians, and all others pursuing the health profession to gain mutual strength and encouragement through meeting with each other.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Circle K International
description:
Service, Leadership & Fellowship

Circle K International is home to university student leaders with a lifelong commitment to community service worldwide. We strive to promote service, leadership, and fellowship in everything we do. Our motto: "Live to Serve, Love to Serve!" 

The FSU chapter of Circle K International is a student-led organization dedicated to serving Florida State University and the surrounding Tallahassee community. We work towards making a positive impact at the local, district, and international levels through meaningful service and experiences. 

Executive Board for the 2025-2026 Academic Year

- President - presidentcki@gmail.com
 - Vice President - vicepresidentcki@gmail.com
 - Secretary - secretarycki@gmail.com
 - Treasurer - circlektreasurer@gmail.com
- Editor - editorcki@gmail.com

Meeting Information

Meetings are held every Tuesday, 6-7PM, in The Globe Room 2500. Follow us on Instagram to stay up-to-date with upcoming events!

Stop by and see us! It’s never too late to join.

 

Social Media

Instagram @FSU_CKI

Facebook "Circle K International (FSU CKI)"

Twitter @fsu_cki

 

Promotional Video
summary: Circle K International (CKI) is a collegiate service organization involved in a wide array of activities on a local and global scale. We work hand-in-hand with our partnering clubs under Kiwanis International.

name: College Democrats at FSU
description:
Established in 1954; branching off from the FSU Political Union, FSU's very first and only political organization on campus at the time, the FSU College Democrats are the official representation of the Democratic Party on campus.

FSU College Democrats seeks to advocate and educate the student body on the ideals and goals of the Democratic Party. In addition our organization seeks to Get Out the Vote and provide Voter Registration opportunities consistently across campus. The FSU College Dems main goals are to facilitate our members with as many opportunities as possible; internships, conferences, training workshops and campaign experience.

It's never to late to become a member! Follow us on Instagram to stay up-to-date on when/where we are meeting.

We meet every Tuesday @ 7 pm. New members are always welcome!
summary: Follow us on Instagram, or send an email to fsdems@gmail.com to receive updates. The purpose of our organization is to educate, organize and involve FSU students in the Democratic Party! We meet every Tuesday at 7 PM, more info is in our flare!

name: Ecology and Evolution  Research Discussion Group at Florida State University
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be to provide a forum for the ecology and evolution area of Biology.

This group facilitates the discussion of graduate students' research and supports faculty and graduate student interactions to build community and address any potential issues.
summary: This group provides a weekly forum for biology graduate students to engage in open and interactive discussion of their research ideas and accomplishments with their peers

name: Emergency Medicine Interest Group
description:
FSU Emergency Medicine Interest Group was founded in the spring of 2002 by students interested in emergency medicine. EMIG strives to educate current medical students about the residency possibilities and practice of emergency medicine.

EMIG has hosted intubation clinics, suture clinics, lectures on the practice of emergency medicine, participated in FSU community health fairs by doing blood pressure checks and heart screenings, sponsored student observations in Tallahassee Memorial Hospital's Bixler Emergency Center, and helped send members to national conferences hosted by the American Academy of Emergency Medicine, American College of Emergency Physicians, and the Emergency Medicine Resident's Association. Future goals include arranging EMS ambulance ride-alongs, additional clinics, Q&A panels featuring local emergency physicians, and continued participation at national conferences.

Membership in EMIG is open to any FSU College of Medicine student interested in learning more about the field of emergency medicine as a specialty. Feel free to contact any of the officers for any further information!
summary: EMIG strives to provide students exposure to the field of Emergency Medicine and educate students about Emergency Medicine residency possibilities through guest speakers, Emergency Medicine shadowing opportunities, national conferences, and other events.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Entertainment, Arts, & Sports Law Society at FSU
description: The Entertainment, Arts, and Sports Law Society (EASL) at FSU Law aims to promote and support interest in the current issues affecting entertainment, arts, and sports law, and to establish and cultivate networks within this specialized legal community. EASL provides interested students of FSU Law access to information regarding professional development in these ever-changing industries. EASL also serves as a conduit between students with an interest in the specified legal areas and professionals within those industries.
summary: EASL is a law school organization designed to promote interest in the current issues affecting entertainment, arts, and sports law, and to cultivate networks within this specialized legal community for the purpose of providing professional opportunities.

name: Environmental Law Society
description: The Environmental Law Society (ELS) is a student organization for anyone interested in environmental law. ELS provides a forum to address key environmental law issues on the local, state and federal levels, as well as functioning as a group available to participate in local environmental cleanups and fund raisers. ELS provides its members with opportunities to travel to national environmental law conferences and to make contact with state and local environmental employers through pro bono and internship opportunities.
summary: The Environmental Law Society provides a forum to address key environmental law issues, pro bono and internship opportunities, and practitioner contacts to assist our members  transition into the professional network of environmental attorneys.

name: Family Medicine Interest Group (FMIG)
description: The purpose of the FMIG is to educate and promote family medicine at Florida State University College of Medicine. FMIG currently provides speakers with topics of interest relating to Family Medicine, organizes a Wine and Cheese Social to allow medical students to meet local Family Physicians, organizes and implements TAR WARS which is an antismoking campaign sponsored by the American Academy of Family Physicians (AAFP), organizes an annual health fair that will be in conjunction with FSU Homecoming/FSUCares 5K, and provide interested medical students with the opportunity to attend the AAFP national conference and other state conferences. We recruit speakers to discuss current medical issues and residencies.
summary: The purpose of the FMIG is to educate and promote family medicine at Florida State University College of Medicine.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Collegiate Merchandising Association
description:
Founded in 1987, the Collegiate Merchandising Association is Florida State University’s oldest and largest fashion club. CMA is open to all majors and helps students prepare for a successful transition into the retail industry through presentations, activities, trips, and guest speakers. The Collegiate Merchandising Association is affiliated with Clutch Magazine- the university’s oldest fashion publication.

"The goal of the Collegiate Merchandising Association is to promote the Florida State University Retail, Merchandising and Product Development and Retail Entrepreneurship programs and to provide students with a valuable foundation of information and skills needed to facilitate their professional development. Most of all, we aim to be a resource for students and give them the ability to network, meet new people, and have fun while achieving these goals."

CLUTCH Magazine: your essential accessory! Brought to you by FSU's Collegiate Merchandising Association.

Our mission at CLUTCH Magazine is to provide FSU with the best of international trends and Tallahassee culture. All content is planned and executed by FSU students, for students. The hard work of the CLUTCH team is provided sans paycheck, allowing CLUTCH to be distributed for free!
summary: CMA is an organization that seeks to provide students with an interest in fashion and opportunities to learn more about the retail and fashion industry by bringing in guest speakers, organizing networking events, and hosting social events.

name: Por Colombia
description:
Por Colombia is a nationally recognized student organization created to promote and provide for the social welfare of the Colombian-American student body at Florida State University. It will function as an educational and cultural instrument for the FSU community, promoting Colombia's positive attributes and rich cultural heritage.

 

Our goal is to foster unity by developing a network among Colombian students and encourage student participation in the promotion of Latin America's cultural expression. Por Colombia helps bring together the Colombian community in FSU and promotes cultural awareness.

Everybody is welcome to join!
summary: Por Colombia is an FSU student organization created to promote and provide for the social welfare of the Colombian-American student body at the Florida State University. It will function as an educational and cultural instrument for the FSU community.

name: Corazón Dancers
description:
We are a culturally diverse group of students who share latin dance as a common interest if not passion. We teach latin dances on campus as well as throughout Tallahassee. We are also involved with community service opportunities around the city. We host workshops as well as perform at various events throughout Florida. We focus on becoming well rounded Salsa dancers through performance, social dancing, and teaching.

 

To promote a continual love and appreciation for Latin dance.
summary: We are a culturally diverse group of students who share a passion for Latin Dance. We teach and perform Latin dances on campus as well as throughout Tallahassee. We are also involved with community service opportunities around the city.

name: Dance Marathon at Florida State University
description:
Dance Marathon at Florida State University is a year-long effort that culminates into an annual event where more than 1,800 students stay awake and on their feet to raise money and awareness for our local beneficiaries. Dance Marathon at FSU is an FSU tradition, where the students and faculty rally year-round to raise awareness and funds for those children who need it most. Over the past 29 years, Dance Marathon at FSU has raised over $21 million for our local Children’s Miracle Network Hospital and the FSU College of Medicine. In 2024, DM at FSU raised a grand total of $1,240,058.81 For the Kids. These funds make the difference in providing the medical care, treatment, and research needed at our local Children’s Miracle Network Hospital and the FSU College of Medicine.

If you're interested in getting more involved with Dance Marathon at FSU, please go to our website at dmfsu.org or email our Executive Director, Darcy Sullivan, dmfsu.executivedirector@gmail.com
summary: Dance Marathon at Florida State University is a year-long effort that culminates into an annual event where more than 1,800 students stay awake and on their feet to raise money and awareness for Children's Miracle Network & FSU College of Medicine.

name: Delta Alpha Chi Christian Sorority
description:
Interested in rushing Delta Alpha Chi? Register for rush here!!

 

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSehSwFEjh5-OpaIgGYOsoaRbrsbCtUtUP98fPRJjf4E3NMVOg/viewformhttps://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd5YCXnKvYXXDbS1VqRJNyOUm0LGvyxvJWKUEZ89H8O7kwGaQ/viewform?usp=header

 

Delta Alpha Chi is a Christian sorority rooted in Christ, Character, and Community. We are a sisterhood of women who strive to keep Christ at the center of everything we do, seeking to live and love as Jesus did. Through prayer, fellowship, and service, we grow together in our faith while encouraging one another in every season of life. Our heart for service is reflected through our philanthropies, including The Little Event and Dance Marathon, where we intentionally serve and support those in our community.

Delta Alpha Chi is more than a sorority—it is a place to belong. It is a sisterhood where you are known, supported, and valued. Our chapter provides opportunities to build meaningful relationships while growing spiritually through weekly chapter meetings, Bible studies, and intentional conversations. Alongside these moments, we create lifelong memories through events like Bid Day, Retreat, Big/Little Reveal, Socials, Formal, and so much more.

Above all, Delta Alpha Chi is a welcoming and supportive community where women are encouraged to grow in confidence, leadership, and faith. We are committed to fostering an environment of love, respect, and inclusion, and we celebrate the uniqueness of every woman who joins our sisterhood.

Delta Alpha Chi does not discriminate based on race, creed, color, sex, religion, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, or any other legally protected status.
summary: Interested in rushing a sorority and looking for Christian community? Think Delta Alpha Chi! We are a sisterhood of Christian women that aim to live and love just as Jesus would by encouraging one another through our faith journey and college itself!

name: Delta Nu Zeta
description: Delta Nu Zeta is a group where all women can find a sense of sisterhood, even if they do not feel that they fit the mold or expectations of a traditional sorority. We strive to promote the intellectual, social, and personal development of all members. We accomplish this by working to bring out the best in all our members, to promote the welfare of our local community, to promote academic excellence, and to forge life-long bonds between sisters. Delta Nu Zeta is a sisterhood in which all women, regardless of race, religion, or socioeconomic background, can feel at home. We pride ourselves on our diversity and inclusion to all who have a passion for charity and are looking to find their sisterhood at Florida State University.
summary: We are a social-service sorority that excels in the promotion of academics, leadership, service, and personal development. We pride ourself on our group service projects in the community, a cohesive social network, and academic support for all sisters.

name: Delta Sigma Pi Business Fraternity
description:
Delta Sigma Pi is a professional fraternity, organized to foster the study of business in universities; to encourage scholarship, social activity and the association of students for their mutual advancement by research and practice; to promote closer affiliation between the commercial world and students of commerce, and to further a higher standard of commercial ethics and culture and the civic and commercial welfare of the community.

In addition to the series of Professional, Community Service, and Social events hosted, Delta Sigma Pi also offers corporate tours and leadership conferences that will give students a leg up on others in terms of networking and leadership development.

Please Contact  svp.gammalambda@gmail.com if interested.
summary: Delta Sigma Pi offers students majoring in business, economics, hospitality, sports management, and retail merchandising the opportunity to grow professionally, network effectively, and build strong bonds that will last far beyond their college years.

name: Omega Phi Beta Sorority, Inc.
description:
The purpose of OPBSI is to Serve and Educate people of diverse backgrounds through sisterhood, leadership, and guidance. As positive role models, we promote unity of all cultures, focus on the empowerment of our gender and raise the standards of excellence in our academic, social, and personal endeavors.

OPBSI envisions a world without oppression, where empowered women celebrate the essence of womanhood and act as resources in the advancement of their communities. We celebrate the success of the women of our past, recognize the accomplishments of women today, and promote to uplift future generations.

All in all, we are a sisterhood with an intimate network of mutual support, serving as an outlet for positive self-expression and individual growth for our members. If you are interested in becoming a hardworking community leader while establishing a strong bond of sisterhood, obtaining support in academic, personal and professional realms, as well as networking capabilities, please contact us. We are interested in women who share our passion for struggle and creating a new standard for women in the professional and academic worlds as well as in our communities.
summary: Omega Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated is an organization like no other, embracing all shades of women, with a strong focus on education, professionalism, service, and diversity. Although our orientation is Latina, we are open to women of all cultures.

name: Dominican Student Association
description:
Established in the 2004-2005 academic year, the Dominican Student Association at Florida State University has made it its objective to share the powerful culture so beloved by its members.

By implementing Dominican culture both on and off campus, the FSU community will be able to experience an ethnically diverse campus through working with and conducting events that span the cultural, social, and educational spectrums.
summary: The purpose of the Dominican Student Association is to spread our culture into the broader FSU community.
comment: Organization has not turned in the Recognition Application for 2010-2011

name: The Florida State's Chapter of DREAM Incorporated
description:
The Florida States Chapter of DREAM Incorporated is a community service-based organization established on November 16, 2006, on Florida State University’s campus. Founded from Martin Luther King Jr's ideologies, DREAM’s mission is to act as a medium to bring all minorities together and uphold Dr. Martin Luther King Jr’s dream to progress as one. DREAM believes that by experiencing different cultures and ethnicities, we will be able to foster a true unity. 
DREAM fulfills our mission statement by holding bi-weekly community service opportunities, creating events for students to give back to the surrounding Tallahassee community, and by always striving to create an understanding, warm environment for minorities on Florida State’s campus.
summary:
"Life's most persistent and urgent question is, 'what are you doing for others?'"

DREAM's mission is to serve as a medium in bringing all minorities together in an effort to uphold Dr. Martin Luther King Jr's dream to progress as one.

name: Economics Club at Florida State University
description:
The purpose of Economics Club is to further advance economic ideas, research, and opportunities for our members. We offer fascinating talks from our faculty and neighboring economic research centers to build a network of bright economists.

Come to our meetings for research opportunities, current event discussions, fun economics games and events, outreach to feeder schools, and building your social network. Anyone interested in Economics is welcome!
summary: The purpose of Econ Club is to further advance economic ideas, research, and opportunities for our members. We offer fascinating talks from our faculty and neighboring economic research centers to build a network of bright economists.

name: FAMU-FSU Engineers Without Borders
description:
YOU DO NOT HAVE TO BE AN ENGINEER MAJOR TO JOIN!!!

FAMU-FSU Engineers Without Borders is a non-profit humanitarian organization established to partner with developing communities worldwide in order to improve their quality of life. This partnership involves the implementation of sustainable engineering projects, while involving and training internationally responsible engineers and engineering students.
summary: FAMU-FSU Engineers Without Borders is a non-profit humanitarian organization established to partner with developing communities worldwide in order to improve their quality of life.

name: Every Nation Campus
description: We host a weekly college night, fun hangouts, guys and girls small groups, and one on one meetings in order to make disciples. Our goal is to help you find community, promote personal growth, and to develop your ability to lead well, so that you fulfill your destiny and be empowered.
summary: We are campus ministry that exists to build a Jesus-centered, empowering, and socially responsible family of students on campus.

name: Filipino Student Association
description:
Our mission is to promote and increase awareness of the Filipino culture to the student body and the Tallahassee community. We provide opportunities to enhance the understanding of our nation’s history and culture, while educating those who are unfamiliar with the beauty of our Filipino heritage. We seek to define, preserve and continue a strong foundation of our cultural values. We hope that through our events, activities and actions, we can define a higher appreciation for diversity and promote greater awareness at FSU.


Special activities include: Parol Ball, Kapatid-Kuya-Ate-Ading Mentorship Program (KKAAMP), Barrio Fiesta, Indak Cultural Dance Troupe, FSA Sports, Big Bend Filipino Association, etc...
summary: Our mission is to be a welcoming space to Filipino students. As well as promote and increase awareness of the Filipino culture and history to the student body and the Tallahassee community through workshops, dance, and sports.

name: Financial Management Association at FSU
description: FMA is no longer active and has been absorbed by The Finance Society.
summary: The mission of the FMA is to broaden the common interests between academicians and practitioners, provide opportunities for professional interaction between and among academicians, practitioners, and students, and to promote finance education.

name: Florida Public Relations Association
description:
The FSU Student Chapter of FPRA is a campus-wide organization of future public relations professionals. FPRA has meetings, events and workshops every other week where speakers discuss PR niches, industries and companies. These speakers give our members insight into the professional world of communications, with tips and tricks on how to be the best PR pro. FPRA members also get the chance to attend a networking trip in the fall and spring. 

The Florida Public Relations Association is a statewide organization of more than 1,200 public relations professionals dedicated to advancing the profession of PR and enhancing the professional development of its members. (https://www.fpra.org/) FSU’s student chapter works alongside the FPRA Capital Chapter. (https://fpra-capital.org/) 

To join, please fill out the membership form at the beginning of the Fall or Spring semester, which can be found in our Instagram bio. This is also where the link for the Spring 2026 NYC Networking Trip application can be found. 

Meetings are held bi-weekly on Tuesdays at 6:30 p.m. Please follow us on Instagram at @FPRAstudent_tlh for details on meeting dates, locations and themes.
summary: FPRA is for future public relations, digital marketing, and communications professionals. FPRA has bi-weekly meetings, events, and workshops where speakers discuss PR niches, industries, and companies.

name: Florida State University Law Review
description:
Florida State University Law Review: Providing a forum for contemporary legal discourse. 

TO PROVIDE A FORUM FOR CONTEMPORARY LEGAL DISCOURSE, WE MUST:

ADHERE TO THE HIGHEST STANDARDS OF ANALYSIS, ACCURACY, AND TIMELINESS IN PUBLISHING THE LAW REVIEW . . .

DISTINGUISH THE COLLEGE OF LAW AS A LEADING INSTITUTION, KNOWN FOR ITS SUPPORT OF SCHOLARSHIP AND ACADEMIC
PURSUIT . . .

ATTRACT THE COLLEGE OF LAW’S FINEST LEGAL COMMUNICATORS BY ENCOURAGING INDEPENDENT LEGAL THOUGHT, OFFERING POSITIONS OF RESPONSIBILITY, AND MAINTAINING A TRADITION OF SERVICE TO OUR COLLEGE . . .

INFORM OUR PROFESSION ABOUT THE COMPELLING ISSUES OF OUR TIME BY PROVIDING A FORUM FOR STUDENT SCHOLARS, PRACTITIONERS, AND LEGAL EDUCATORS . . .

OFFER OUR PROFESSION A SELECTION OF ARTICLES BALANCED BETWEEN THE THEORETICAL AND PRACTICAL . . .

UPHOLD THE HONOR AND DECORUM OF OUR PROFESSION BY CHAMPIONING THE HIGHEST STANDARDS OF CONDUCT . . .

STAND TOGETHER AS ADVOCATES OF QUALITY LEGAL EDUCATION, STRIVING TO IMPROVE THE REPUTATIONS OF OUR LAW REVIEW AND OF OUR SCHOOL . . .

INSPIRE COOPERATION, COMMITMENT, AND CAMARADERIE AMONG OUR MEMBERSHIP AND THE COLLEGE OF LAW AT LARGE . . .

SAFEGUARD AN INSTITUTIONAL MEMORY AND TRADITION SO THAT THOSE WHO FOLLOW WILL BENEFIT FROM OUR EXPERIENCE . . .
summary: Providing a forum for contemporary legal discourse.

name: Flying High Circus Club at FSU
description:
1. We uphold the highest standards of the FSU Circus.

2. Encourage fellowship and camaraderie among circus performers and production crew.

3. Promote interest in circus as a wholesome, family entertainment and art form.

4. Present traditional 3 Ring American Circus performances; represent FSU Circus locally and nationally.
summary: We uphold the highest standards of the FSU Circus through the encouragement of fellowship and camaraderie.

name: German Club
description:
Hallo zusammen!

German Club is making a come back for the 2025 year!

Our goal is to promote the German language as well as provide a space to learn about and appreciate German-speaking cultures. All levels of speakers and those interested in German-speaking cultures are welcome.

Join the GroupMe for updates and feel free to reach out with any questions!

Hope to see you there! Bis dann!
summary: Our goal is to promote the German language as well as provide a space to learn about and appreciate German-speaking cultures. All levels of speakers and those interested in German-speaking cultures are welcome.
