name: University Gospel Choir at Florida State University
description:
The Florida State University Gospel Choir was established in 1973. Rooted in the rich musical traditions of the African American church, the choir is dedicated to celebrating, preserving, and promoting the unique art form of gospel music, blending both traditional and contemporary styles. Through powerful, uplifting performances, the ensemble seeks to inspire audiences with messages of faith, hope, redemption, and love.

More than just a choral ensemble, the FSU Gospel Choir is a community. The gospel choir seeks to welcome and embrace people from all backgrounds, nationalities, beliefs, and experiences. It is our goal to provide a space where students can form deep and meaningful relationships through a shared love of gospel music. Whether someone desires to celebrate African American musical heritage, explore the gospel tradition, seek spiritual fulfillment through song, or simply find belonging through the joy of singing, the Gospel Choir offers an environment of support, encouragement, and unity. It is a place where voices come together not only in harmony but in fellowship, strengthening bonds that extend beyond rehearsals and performances.

Gospel Choir is committed to inclusivity and diversity, ensuring that no individual is denied membership. The choir fosters a culture of love, peace, and mutual respect, where all members can grow both musically and personally while being part of something greater than themselves.

In addition to its on-campus performances, the FSU Gospel Choir actively engages with the broader community through local, regional, and national events, using music to build connections and uplift others. Its student-led structure provides opportunities for leadership, collaboration, and service, empowering members to take an active role in decision-making and event planning. By creating an environment where students can engage deeply with gospel music’s history, styles, and techniques, the FSU Gospel Choir contributes significantly to the cultural and artistic diversity of the College of Music and Florida State University as a whole.

Rehearsals are held every Tuesday and some Thursdays at 4:45 PM in Kuersteiner Music Building Room 240.
summary: The FSU Gospel Choir, founded in 1973, celebrates and preserves gospel music rooted in African American traditions. More than a choir, it fosters community, inclusivity, and spiritual connection. Open to all, it uplifts audiences and builds relationships

name: Graduate Women in STEM
description:
GWIS aims to create a diverse and inclusive environment that values and celebrates women from different backgrounds and experiences and provide a safe space for people of all gender identities. Our goal is to help re-shape societal perceptions to create a future where women are equally represented and valued in all STEM fields. We aim to inspire, encourage, and empower women to reach their full potentials and make meaningful contributions to STEM knowledge and innovation.

GWIS hosts a variety of community building and outreach events during the year including hosting guest speakers from different institutions; advising and tutoring undergraduate and high school students; holding movie nights; and partnering with organizations in the Tallahassee community. GWIS is also proud to host the annual Art in STEM competition in collaboration with the FSU College of Arts and Sciences and the FSU Libraries Office of Digital Research and Scholarship. Come join us for our 11th year of Art in STEM in April 2025!
summary: GWIS aims to provide a supportive and inclusive community that promotes professional development, leadership, and opportunities for women in STEM graduate programs during their time at FSU and beyond.

name: Haitian Cultural Club
description:
To unify the Haitian population in the
Tallahassee community in order to:
a) Create a channel of communication between the Haitian students, the university and the community.
b) Act as a unifying and collective body for all Haitian students.
c) Develop, implement, intensify, and perpetuate policies, programs and public relations initiatives that will identify and address conflicting challenges to students in education, cultural and political arenas on campus and throughout the community.
d) Foster the enrichment of the students experience by providing a broad spectrum of entertainment, Cultural and other activities.
e) Promote the Haitian culture and serve as a medium of support to all interested students.

Develop, implement, intensify, and perpetuate policies, programs and public relations initiatives that will identify and address conflicting challenges to students in educational, cultural and political arenas on campus and throughout the community.

Some activities we partake in are:

  • "Translation Project" where we work as translators to migrant farmworkers so they can receive medical assistance.
  • HCC Week, a week dedicated to celebrating our culture by having events all week long. 
  • Creole Lessons, students teach community members Haitian creole or immigrants English. 
  • Collect donations for the new Haitian Museum that is being created in Miami.
    Dance and/or cook for Caribbean Carnival.
  • Sponsor Route de Malpassee Ganthier, a school in Port Au Prince, Haiti, with Centre D'Action pour le Development (CAD)

 

The purpose of the Haitian Cultural is to act as a unifying body for all Haitian-Americans and emigrated Haitians in the Tallahassee Area. We are also enabled to share and promote Haitian Culture every way possible. Whether it's through dialogue, music, dance, food, or books our intentions are to exploit this rich and magnificent culture.
summary: To unify and educate the Haitian population (and others interested in the Haitian culture) in the Tallahassee community.

name: Health Law Society
description: HLS offers members the opportunity to engage in discussions with respected professionals in the health law community, providing valuable insights into potential career paths. While many health law students aim for traditional careers, others are interested in exploring alternative options. Our objective is to showcase a variety of job opportunities through speakers and panels. We also aim to deepen members' understanding of healthcare and legal issues by inviting professors and local law firms to delve into the latest topics in health law. Periodically, HLS collaborates with other student organizations to expand student access and awareness through joint events. These gatherings foster connections among students who share similar career aspirations, facilitating valuable networking opportunities.
summary:
We organize or participate in a variety of events on FSU campus and in Tallahassee, including round tables where law students and med students can discuss specific areas of health law and policy.
 
To foster discussion at the Flordia State
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Hillel at FSU
description: Hillel at FSU is the foundation for Jewish life at Florida State University. We strive to enrich the lives of undergraduate and graduate students religiously, culturally, and socially. Some examples of common programs include bagel brunches, high holidays, lunch and learns, community service projects, and weekly Shabbat dinners. Follow us on social media @hillelatfsu to stay up to date.
summary: Hillel at FSU is the foundation for Jewish life on FSU's campus. Hillel sponsors activities that enrich students religiously, culturally, and socially.

name: Hispanic/Latine Student Union
description:
HOW TO JOIN US/BECOME A MEMBER: The Hispanic Latinx Student Union (HLSU) is an agency under the Student Government Association (SGA). Each year, we are allocated funds by SGA to host events and activities to educate the student body about our diverse community. Our events are open to ALL FSU students and faculty members, NO SIGN-UP OR MEMBERSHIP REQUIRED! To become an "official member" and receive newsletter updates, you simply just need to click the "Request Membership" button above!

Stay up to date with our events VIA Instagram: @HLSU_FSU

Suscribe to our Weekly Newsletter!

The Hispanic/Latine Student Union shall serve as an institution dedicated to gathering information in regards to trends, necessities and achievements of the Hispanic/Latine community. It shall serve as a nucleus for a network with other national institutes and organizations to improve the opportunities and living conditions of the Hispanic/Latine community. The Hispanic/Latine Student Union will function as an educational and cultural learning instrument for students, faculty and staff. The Agency shall provide a centralized coordination of resources and services for the Hispanic/Latine community. It shall be open and accessible to everyone, thus providing a liaison between Florida State University and the Tallahassee community.
summary: The Hispanic/Latine Student Union prides itself on being a place where student can feel at home and share their culture. We are a Familia full of fun energy, culture and most importantly Seminole Spirit.

name: History Graduate Student Association
description: HGSA is the representative organization for graduate students studying history. It acts as a liaison between faculty and graduate students, provides opportunities for professional development, and supports community through social events. Additionally, HGSA regularly hosts a conference for graduate students to gain experience in presenting and discussing their scholarship.
summary: HGSA is the representative organization for graduate students studying history. It acts as a liaison between faculty and graduate students, provides opportunities for professional development, and supports community through social events.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Honors Student Association
description:
HSA aims to support and enrich the honors student experience by unifying the honors community and promoting students’ social, academic, and professional development through events, affiliated organization support, and the sharing of relevant information and opportunities. Membership is open to all honors students. 

 

To join, follow these steps: 

1. Scroll to the "forms" section on this page 

2. Complete the HSA membership application form based on when you began your time in the university honors program 

 

Benefits of being an HSA member include:
- Access to our social events  
- Access to food at our social events (beginning fall 2025, membership is required to have access to food and drinks)
- Access to HSA merchandise  
- No-cost access to HSA-affiliated groups/organizations
- Ability to start an HSA-affiliated group
- Access to our flare group, including smaller social, professional, and academic group chats

To stay up to date, follow our Instagram @fsuhsa
summary: HSA aims to support and enrich the honors student experience by unifying the honors community and promoting students’ social, academic, and professional development. Membership in HSA is available to all students in the University Honors Program.

name: Horn Studio of FSU
description:
The organization is for horn players of all levels to participate in musical and educational opportunities that relate to their instrument.

 

The purpose of the Horn Studio of FSU is to unite horn players and enthusiasts for the continued development of musical and educational growth.
summary: The organization is for horn players of all levels to participate in musical and educational opportunities that relate to their instrument.
comment: Organization has not turned in the Recognition Application for 2010-2011

name: Indian Students Association of Tallahassee
description:
To promote Indian Culture and to help Indian Students on campus

The purpose of the association is to preserve the Indian culture at FSU through individual and group activities; and to be actively involved in assisting new incoming Indian students.
All activities and functions of the Indian Students Association of Tallahassee shall be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.

INSAT organizes a number of events for the Indian student community at FSU. Some of the events that we've organized are: Indian Classical Music concerts, Fresher's welcomes, An annual cultural event called the Glimpses of India, Potlucks and Cricket Tournaments. We also collaborate with the International Center to organize events.
summary: To promote Indian Culture and to help students get an insight about it. The purpose of the association is to preserve the Indian culture at FSU through individual and group activities; and to be actively involved in assisting new incoming Indian students
comment: Organization has not turned in the Recognition Application for 2010-2011

name: Institute of Industrial & Systems Engineers
description: IISE is the global association of productivity and efficiency professionals specializing in industrial engineering, healthcare, ergonomics and other related professions. IISE, the world's largest professional society dedicated solely to the support of the profession, is an international, nonprofit association that provides leadership for the application, education, training, research, and development of industrial and systems engineering.
summary: An Engineering student organization committed to developing professional, leadership, academic, and social networks that openly educates and promotes the Industrial Engineering profession to all students regardless of their disciplines.

name: Intellectual Property Law Society
description: The Intellectual Property Law Society (IPLS) is an organization of law students interested in patent, copyright, trademark, and trade secret law. Our mission is to encourage and facilitate student excellence in the field of intellectual property through 1) maintaining a network of interaction between students and local practitioners and alumni, 2) creating awareness of intellectual property trends that have a state, national, and global impact, and 3) helping students with and without technical backgrounds find a niche within one of the many growing trends within intellectual property law.
summary: The Intellectual Property Law Society (IPLS) is an organization of law students interested in the field of intellectual property law.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Inter Residence Hall Council
description: The mission of the Inter-Residence Hall Council is to engage students in the on-campus living experience through leadership development, community involvement, and peer advocacy. Inter-Residence Hall Council (IRHC) is an affiliate of both University Housing and the Student Government Association. IRHC establishes and governs Hall Councils and collaborates to build community and enhance the experience of living on-campus through hall programming, advocating for the needs of residents, and service in the FSU community and beyond.
summary: The mission of the Inter-Residence Hall Council, as an affiliate of both University Housing and SGA, is to engage students in the on-campus living experience through leadership development, community involvement, and peer advocacy.

name: Interior Design Student Organization
description:
MEMBERSHIP BENEFITS
• Networking with other students and local design professionals
• Opportunities to participate in social, networking, and workshopping events on-campus
• Awards and competitions
• Leadership opportunities and training
• Feature IDSO appellation on business cards and IDSO membership on a resume.

IDSO is a valuable resource for interior design students, providing them with a platform to develop their skills, build their portfolios, and connect with other professionals in the industry. If you are interested in joining IDSO, please email us for more information (fsuidso@gmail.com).
summary: The Interior Design Student Organization provides opportunities for students to participate in competitions, workshops, and stay up to date on the latest opportunities and advancements within the Interior Design field.

name: International Law Students Association
description: ILSA promotes the study and profession of international law. The organization promotes the growth of international law through its members and explores the international aspects of issues impacting our community including environmental issues, human rights, business, trade, investment and more. ILSA works to connect with the community to procure speakers and information regarding various pertinent international legal topics of interest or concern. The organization is dedicated to enhancing the education of members on international legal topics. Further, ILSA is dedicated to creating opportunities to network and get involved with international legal professionals.
summary: ILSA promotes the study of international legal topics. The organization promotes the growth of international law through its members and explores the international aspects of issues impacting our community.

name: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship
description:
To establish and advance witnessing communities on colleges and universities that grow in love for God, God's word, God's people of every ethnicity and culture, and God's purposes in the world.

A campus ministry (not affiliated with a church). Weekly bible studies offered to students living on and off campus, weekly worship meeting, opportunities to serve in the greater Tallahassee area as well as here on campus. We also host several retreats and conferences to challenge and equip students to live as Christians on campus.
summary: To establish and advance witnessing communities on colleges and universities that grow in love for God, God's word, God's people of every ethnicity and culture, and God's purposes in the world. A campus ministry (not affiliated with a church).

name: Japanese Animation Club of Tallahassee
description:
The purpose of JACT is to promote Japanese culture through exposing members to current trends in popular media, particularly animation, but not limited to graphic novels, video games, music and so on. By using Japanese popular culture, we unite the diverse student body through a common interest. This, we believe, will foster goodwill between the students of the Florida State University, members of the community, and the unique Japanese culture.

We are FSU's only anime club, and we host FreeCon yearly!

Join our Discord to keep in touch: discord.gg/MK7BKQzHKy
summary: Our purpose is to promote Japanese culture through exposing members to current trends in popular media, such as animation, graphic novels, video games, music and more. We intend to unite the diverse student body through a common interest.

name: Japanese Student Association
description:
The Japanese Student Association seeks to allow Florida State University students with connections to Japanese culture, heritage, and language to form a network of shared interests and cultural knowledge, as well as to share this knowledge with the student body.

JSA will cooperate with the wider Japanese and Japanese-American communities to promote awareness of Japan’s traditions, celebrations, food, art, and language throughout both FSU’s campus and the Tallahassee area. Those looking to learn and promote the language and culture will find in JSA a body of resources and support for this endeavor.
summary: The Japanese Student Association seeks to provide all students with connections to Japanese culture, heritage, and language. Our mission is to promote awareness of Japanese culture and traditions among students on campus and in the Tallahassee community.

name: Jewish Law Students Association
description: As members of the JLSA, we focus on the importance of connection and community in Judaism. We strive to serve the Seminole Law community as a support system and a source of unity for both Jewish and non-Jewish students to learn and socialize together while also promoting an environment of tolerance for students of all faith traditions. We welcome students of all backgrounds, all levels of Judaism (or lack thereof) and representatives of all those in the greater Tallahassee legal profession.
summary: JLSA was formed for the enrichment of Jewish culture at FSU Law. JLSA exists so that its members can comfortably embrace and celebrate their culture and heritage as well as help to provide resources for those seeking Jewish opportunities in Tallahassee.
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: Journal of Land Use and Environmental Law
description:
To provide a forum for advancing contemporary jurisprudence of the land, air, and waters, we must:
- Maintain the highest standards of analysis, accuracy, and timeliness in publishing the Journal;
- Encourage leading legal educators and practitioners to publish their articles in the Journal;
- Distinguish the College of Law as a leading institution, known for its support of scholarship and academic pursuit in the fields of environmental and land use law;
- Attract the premier legal scholars interested in environmental and land use issues by encouraging independent legal thought, offering positions of responsibility and opportunities for publication, and maintaining a tradition of service to our college;
- Inform our future profession about the compelling environmental and land use issues by providing a forum for legal educators, practitioners, and student scholars;
- Offer our future profession a broad, diverse spectrum of articles balanced between the theoretical and the practical;
- Promote esprit de corps among our membership and the College of Law at large; and
- Safeguard our institutional memory and tradition so that those who follow will benefit from our experience.

ORGANIZATIONAL PURPOSE
The Journal is formed exclusively for literary and educational purposes within the meaning section 301(d)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954. The Journal shall not carry on any activities not permitted to be carried on by an organization exempt from taxation under section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code or engage in any activities or exercise any powers that are not in furtherance of the purpose of the Journal.

PUBLICATION CHARACTER
The Journal shall publish, at least biannually, a scholarly legal publication intended to serve policymakers, legislators, and the legal and academic communities.
summary: Advancing contemporary jurisprudence of the land, air, and waters.

name: Journal of Transnational Law & Policy
description: The Journal shall be a student publication dedicated to research and publishing of transnational law and policy issues. The Journal shall be published annually by its members. The journal also strives to bring speakers on emerging international law issues for the benefit of our members and the law school, as well as the entire student body at the Florida State University. The speaker series, which includes the Lillich Lecture on International Human Rights, also attracts many distinguished guests from Tallahassee and surrounding areas. Furthermore, the Journal enriches our members' law school education by advancing editing and writing skills. We also offer students a forum to publish scholarly work.
summary: The Journal shall be a student publication dedicated to research and publishing of transnational law and policy issues. The Journal shall be published annually by its members.

name: Korean American Student Association
description: The Korean American Student Association is an all-inclusive organization for students interested in expanding their knowledge of Korean culture. The monthly events we host highlight different aspects of Korean culture, such as language, pop culture, and arts, for members to learn and socialize with each other.
summary: The Korean American Student Association (KASA) aims to organize social and cultural activities to educate and promote Korean culture among FSU students.

name: The French Club at FSU
description:
Bonjour à tous!
 
Rejoignez-nous cette année 2025-26 pour célébrer la culture francophone.
Le Club français vous offre un environnement convivial dans lequel vous pouvez pratiquer tous les niveaux de Français et participer aux événements culturels. Le Club Français promeut la compréhension, l'étude et l'appréciation de la langue française et des cultures francophones.
 
Nous espérons vous voir bientôt !.
 
Hello everyone!

Join us this year 2025-26 to celebrate francophone culture.
The French Club offers a friendly environment where you can practice all levels of French and participate in cultural events. We promote the understanding, study, and appreciation of the French language and Francophone cultures.

We hope to see you soon! À bientôt!

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

President: Neil Iles (nai24@fsu.edu)
Vice President: Danielle Selevan (ds22q@fsu.edu)

Reach out if you have any questions at frenchatfsu@gmail.com or on our social media!
summary:
Bonjour à tous !!

We are group of undergraduate and graduate students of all levels of French who get together to practice French and celebrate Francophone culture. All students interested in French or francophone cultures are welcome!
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Lady Spirithunters
description:
*Our recruitment process begins early Fall & Spring

Lady Spirithunters is an organization dedicated to spreading spirit and tradition at Florida State University. We are a registered organization at FSU, and works very closely with both athletics and the university community.

We are a non-Greek, non-religious, non-profit organization that strives to achieve a single goal: to promote and spread Seminole spirit and traditions by bridging the present with both the past and the future. Lady Spirithunters enables each individual to make personal contributions to the development of traditions.

Lady Spirithunters is committed to community service, engaging in social events, and fundraising for our philanthropy, Camp Kesem, at FSU! 

Involvement includes, but is not limited to:

  • Face painting at varsity sporting events
  • Buddy bags for all sports
  • Homecoming
  • Intramural Sports
  • Bigs & Littles
  • Socials and date functions
  • Disney/Universal Trip
  • Community Service
  • Formal and Semiformal
  • Dance Marathon
  • Relay For Life
  • Hayride

Follow us on social media!

Instagram: @ladyspirithunters
summary: Lady Spirithunters is an organization dedicated to spreading FSU spirit and supporting the Florida State and Tallahassee community. We are known for painting war stripes at all varsity sporting events!

name: Lambda Alpha Epsilon - Professional Criminal Justice Association
description:
Our organization is a community of Criminal Justice professionals and students who come together to support each other's career and personal growth. We believe in staying well-rounded by engaging in various activities such as community service, intramural sports, and social events. Our social events are of two types: recreational socials, which promote brotherhood and teamwork, and Criminology-related socials, which provide a platform for our members to learn and interact with different aspects of the Criminal Justice field. Each year, we participate in two conferences, a regional conference during the Fall and a national conference during the Spring, where we compete in mock crime scenes, academic testing, pistol shooting, and physical agility. We are proud to welcome all interested students, regardless of their major or career choice. Come join us in our pursuit of excellence in the Criminal Justice field!

 

Lambda Chapter shall uphold the following objectives of the Grand Chapter of the American Criminal Justice Association Lambda Alpha Epsilon as stated in the National Bylaws:

  1. Improve Criminal Justice through educational activities.
  2. Foster professionalism in law enforcement personnel and agencies.
  3. Promote professional, academic, and public awareness of criminal justice issues.
  4. Encourage the establishment and expansion of higher education and professional training in criminal justice.
  5. Provide a unified voice for professionals in and students of the criminal justice.
  6. Promote high standards of ethical conduct, professional training, and higher education within the criminal justice field.

If you are interested in joining, contact our Recruitment officer Kyah Williams, at fsulae.recruitment@gmail.com

LAE Recruitment video
summary: Our organization is a community of Criminal Justice professionals and students who come together to support each other's career and personal growth. We believe in staying well-rounded by engaging in various events catering to members' interests.

name: Lambda Phi Epsilon International Fraternity, Inc.
description:
The Florida State University Associate Chapter of Lambda Phi Epsilon International Fraternity, Inc.
The FIRST and ONLY Asian-Interest but not Asian exclusive fraternity on campus.
summary: Lambda Phi Epsilon is an Asian-interest fraternity. Our mission is to guide men on a lifelong discovery of authenticity and personal growth.
comment: Cannot complete the process becasue of SAC/Greek Life policy. Will re-recognize as Summit until further notice.

name: Level Dance Company
description:
Level Dance Company is a student-run, performance based dance team that offers opportunities for all styles of dance, specializing in contemporary, jazz, and hip-hop.

We showcase our talent at various events across campus such as Homecoming Live, Dance Marathon, basketball games, and others. In addition, we have a spring showcase of student choreography.
summary: Level Dance Company is a student-run dance organization that offers opportunities for all styles of dance, specializing in contemporary, jazz, and hip-hop. We participate in various events across campus, as well as our own end-of-year showcase.

name: Multicultural Association of Pre-Medical Students
description: Our mission is to enhance the recruitment of culturally diverse students into healthcare fields, and to help members become successful candidates for professional health and medical programs. MAPS develops its members to become successful matriculants into professional schools as well as qualified healthcare providers in the future. This is accomplished through several academic, clinical, and community service opportunities. This includes food and clothing drives, services with elderly care facilities, mentoring with college and pre-college students, facilitated tutoring and study groups, academic advising, MCAT/GRE preparation courses, Physician partnership opportunities, a medical assistant program, employment opportunities, and professional enhancement workshops.
summary: To enhance the recruitment of culturally diverse students into healthcare fields, helping members become successful candidates for professional health and medical programs. MAPS develops its members to become successful matriculants into medical school.
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: Trial Team of the College of Law
shortName: FSU College of Law Trial Team
description:
The College of Law Trial Team consists of an elite group of law students trained in the art of trial-level litigation. Each year, team members represent the Florida State University College of Law in competitions against other students from law schools across the nation. At these competitions, team members present cases before judge and jury. Cases encompass a wide range of topics in both the civil and criminal arenas. Case topics have included insurance fraud, homicide, intentional infliction of emotional distress, negligence and wrongful death.

The Florida State University College of Law Trial Team invites you to explore its proud history and current success in hopes that you, too, will catch the fever and join our ranks.
summary: The Trial Team consists of an elite group of law students trained in the art of trial-level litigation. Each year, team members represent the FSU College of Law in competitions against other students from law schools across the nation.

name: Moot Court Team at FSU College of Law
description: Excellence in Appellate Advocacy.  The Moot Court team competes in approximately 16 state and national appellate advocacy competitions. We also perform an exhibition oral argument before the Florida Supreme Court, and we select our members by having an intramural competition for law students each spring.
summary: Excellence in Appellate Advocacy. We compete in approximately 16 appellate advocacy competitions each year. We also perform an exhibition oral argument before the Florida Supreme Court and select our members during an intramural competition each spring.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Music Theory Society
description: The Music Theory Society fosters the development of all aspects of the discipline of music theory, including research, writing, and teaching. It also provides a forum for the music theory community to present new research and participate in workshops, social events, and other aspects of professional development.
summary: The goals of the Music Theory Society include fostering the development of all aspects of the discipline of music theory, including research and teaching.

name: National Association for Business Economics at FSU
description:
Our mission is to prepare all traditional and applied economics majors and minors for the professional world that they will meet after they graduate. NABE hosts bi-weekly meetings featuring guest speakers with backgrounds in economics. Members of our chapter will have the opportunity to form a network of peers, develop their professional skills, and attend two NABE conferences each year.

The Florida State University Chapter of the National Association for Business Economics chapter's goals include:

- Stimulating interest in the economics major and graduate programs in economics

- Providing students with the opportunity to travel to two N.A.B.E. conferences each year where members may enjoy networking opportunities, meet the industry's top leaders, and stay up to date on relevant economic issues

- Provide professional development for students intending to enter the economics career
summary: Our mission is to prepare all traditional and applied economics majors and minors for the professional world that they will encounter after they graduate through our bi-weekly meetings, conference trips, guest speakers, and networking events.

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
description:
The National Residence Hall Honorary (NRHH) represents the top 1% of student leaders living on campus as Florida State University’s only Residence Hall Honors Society.

 

The Garnet and Gold Chapter of NRHH leads initiatives that support community service, recognition, leadership development, and scholastic achievement. Members serve to recognize, develop, and encourage outstanding leadership within the residential halls of FSU. 

 

FSU's NRHH represents the South Atlantic Affiliate of College and University Residence Halls (SAACURH) at the regional level and the National Association of College and University Residence Halls (NACURH) at the national level. 

LINK TO SWAK-U CAREPACKAGES
summary: The National Residence Hall Honorary (NRHH) is the Residence Hall Honors Society at Florida State University. The Garnet and Gold Chapter serves as FSU's affiliate of the National Association of College and University Residence Halls (NACURH).
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description:
Minority Engineering Organization

To increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.
summary:
The NSBE mission is 

"To increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community."

name: Neuroscience Graduate Student Association
description:
The NGSA will provide an avenue for academic, professional, and social activities to the graduate students in the interdisciplinary Program in Neuroscience at Florida State University. Our program includes faculty and students across campus from the departments of Biological Science, Mathematics, and Psychology; the College of Medicine; the Institute of Molecular Biophysics; and the National High Magnetic Field Laboratory.  The main goals of this organization will be to facilitate academic and professional development of its members, and to promote collegiality and social interaction among the students and faculty of the Program in Neuroscience.


NGSA represents all graduate students in the program of neuroscience at Florida State University. Our goal is to foster an interactive group of researchers and assist the community when needed.
summary: The NGSA will provide an avenue for academic, professional, and social activities to the graduate students in the interdisciplinary Program in Neuroscience at Florida State University.

name: Obstetrics and Gynecology Interest Group
description: To inform medical students at all stages of training about the rewards and opportunities available as an obstetrician/gynecologist, and to connect them to the educational, community service, and legislative activities of the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists. This organization informs medical students about the field of obstetrics and gynecology and dually serves to provide exposure in preparation for residency training programs. We host speakers and seminars that allow students to interact with obstetricians/gynecologists and gain a realistic view as to what this field of medicine entails.
summary: To inform medical students at all stages of training about the rewards and opportunities available as an obstetrician/gynecologist and to provide exposure to the field of OB/GYN in preparation for making a decision on committing to medical residencies.

name: Pre-Physical Therapy/Occupational Therapy at FSU
description:
The Pre-PT/OT Club at FSU is an effort between the Pre-Physical/Occupational therapy students of Florida State University to assist one another in preparation for graduate school. The two professions are similar yet very unique in their own ways. The club provides a means by which Pre-Physical/Occupational Therapy students can get to know one another and help each other with future planning, class scheduling, GRE preparation, and the physical/occupational therapy school applications. We bring in a range of guest speakers from physical and occupational therapists, current physical/occupational therapy school students, and physical/occupational therapy school recruiters in order to help students understand the profession, choose which route to take, and gain knowledge of the tedious physical/occupational therapy school admission process. 

The desire of the club and its members is to:

1) Help members get a better understanding of the different types of professions available and what it takes to become a physical/occupational therapist.

2) Direct students in their selection of classes in consideration of the prerequisites and recommended electives of the specific physical/occupational therapy institutions.

3) Advise students on preparation for the GRE and give suggestions on the timeline of applications with their personal statement, recommendations, work experience, etc.

To stay updated about meeting dates and all club updates, please join our Facebook page! (FSU's Pre PT/OT Club)
summary: The Pre-PT/OT Club at FSU is an effort between the Pre-Physical/Occupational Therapy students of Florida State University to assist one another in preparation for graduate school.

name: Order of Omega
description:
Order of Omega is an honor society that recognizes members of the Greek community who embody high standards in the areas of scholarship, leadership, and involvement within their respective organizations, as well as within the Greek, campus, and local community.

 

The Omega Epsilon Chapter of Order of Omega was established on Florida State University’s campus in the Fall Semester of 1998. This chapter unites Greek Life at FSU by holding representation from all four Greek councils. We use our common bond as Greek leaders to better our campus, our community, and ourselves. Omega Epsilon is a chapter that encourages cooperation, while enhancing professional and social development between its members, for the betterment of the Florida State University.
summary: Order of Omega is an honor society that recognizes members of the Greek community who embody high standards within their respective organizations, as well as within the Greek, campus, and local community.

name: OUTlaw at Florida State University College of Law
description: OUTLaw exists at the Florida State College of Law to provide a platform for all students to learn about the legal issues facing lesbians, gays, bisexuals, transgender people, and others. OUTLaw sponsors events throughout the year to increase awareness of issues and to foster discussion of the current state of LGBTQ+ law at the national, state, and local levels. OUTLaw is committed to working with allied organizations on campus to better the Florida State community and ensure that LGBTQ+ students are treated with the same respect as the student body as a whole. We also exist to help students connect with the LGBTQ+ community in Tallahassee. We welcome and encourage all law students to join, regardless of orientation, gender, and expression.
summary: OUTLaw seeks to provide a platform for all students to  learn about the legal issues facing the LGBTQ+ community. OUTLaw sponsors events throughout the year to create an inclusive social environment and increase awareness of issues facing the community.

name: Pre-Pharmacy Informational Leadership and Learning Society
description:
The Pre-Pharmacy Club is a student organization created by six Florida State University students in the fall of 2003 for undergraduates who plan on pursuing careers in pharmacy. The goal of the club is to provide resources and opportunities to introduce undergraduates to the world of pharmacy. In addition, the club seeks to help its members become the most qualified applicants for pharmacy school.

The organization also seeks to encourage community involvement and to present members with opportunities to serve the community through volunteer service. By adding depth to members understanding of pharmacy, the Pre-Pharmacy Club constantly strives to make its members the best prepared and most informed applicants for the pharmacy program.
summary: The goal of the club is to provide resources and opportunities to introduce undergraduates to the world of pharmacy. To promote the field of pharmacy as a valid and exciting field of medicine in which to pursue.

name: Pediatric Interest Group at FSU
description: The purpose of the Pediatric Interest Group (PIG) is to perform service to the Tallahassee pediatric community and to promote the field of pediatrics at Florida State University (FSU). Our members are able to fulfill our purpose through hands-on experience and exposure to careers in pediatrics by shadowing local physicians, participating in health fairs, and receiving education through guest lectures. PIG is also committed to serving the greater pediatric community through its continued participation with Dance Marathon, as well as state camps such as Camp Boggy Creek and the Florida Camp for Children and Youth with Diabetes.
summary: The purpose of the Pediatric Interest Group (PIG) is to perform service to the Tallahassee pediatric community and to promote the field of pediatrics at Florida State University (FSU).
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Iranian Student Association
description: The Iranian Student Association at Florida State University is committed to increasing awareness of Iran's vibrant history and culture and its people through social and cultural activities. Furthermore, we offer a place of community for Iranian students from abroad and among the diaspora and for all students interested in learning and connecting with Iranian people.
summary: The Iranian Student Association (ISA) at Florida State University is committed to increasing awareness of Iranian culture and history among the FSU student body, and offers a place of community for Iranian and interested non-Iranian students.

name: Phi Sigma Pi
description: Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity is a gender-inclusive organization dedicated to: promoting lifelong learning, inspiring Members to lead, and cultivating lasting fraternal bonds, while always conducting our lives with honor. We consecrate ourselves to a life of social service with the goal of improving humanity through our principles: Scholarship, Leadership, and Fellowship.
summary: Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity is a gender-inclusive fraternity open to undergraduate students. Our purpose is to bring together some the best students at FSU in encouraging the three ideals of: Scholarship, Leadership, and Fellowship.

name: Philosophy Graduate Student Association
description:
PGSA is an association for all graduate students in the philosophy program at Florida State University.

The goals of PGSA are:

1.  To foster interest in philosophy and philosophical issues within the academic community of Florida State University,
2.  To contribute to the life of the philosophical community at large,
3.  To represent the FSU Department of Philosophy in both Florida State University and the philosophical community at large,
4.  To improve the quality of graduate philosophical work in both teaching and research, and
5.  To serve in an advisory capacity as a liaison between the graduate students of the FSU Department of Philosophy and its faculty, staff, and administration.
summary: PGSA is an association for all graduate students in the philosophy program at Florida State University, designed to foster interest in philosophy and philosophical issues within the academic community of Florida State University.

name: Political Science Graduate Student Association
description: The Political Science Graduate Student Association serves as 1) the main forum for graduate students to voice their opinions about department matters, 2) a resource for helping graduate students to successfully complete their academic degrees and, 3) the central coordinator of social and community service events for the graduate department.
summary: The Political Science Graduate Student Association is the official student organization of the Political Science graduate program at Florida State University.

name: Brazilian Student Association at FSU
description: The Brazilian Student Association at Florida State University (FSU) is a dynamic community fostering cultural exchange and support among Brazilian students and those interested in Brazilian culture. Through events like barbecues, soccer matches, professional development workshops, and Portuguese classes, the association promotes understanding of Brazilian traditions. It also serves as a support network for Brazilian students adjusting to life in the United States, offering guidance and resources. In essence, the association creates a welcoming environment for cultural exchange and personal growth at FSU.
summary:
This club welcomes anyone with an interest in the Portuguese language and Brazilian culture. Stay connected with us for updates on social media!

Instagram: @brasa_fsu

name: Pre-Physician Assistant Society
description:
This society was established to bring together students who are interested in becoming Physician Assistants. We provide valuable insight into the profession by inviting guest speakers and providing quality information, helping students with their journey starting in undergraduate school, up until starting PA school. Guest speakers include a variety of medical professionals, admission counselors, career center liaisons, and others from various programs. 

The purpose of this organization is to educate students and the community about the physician assistant profession as well as to provide opportunities that prepare students for entry into PA school.

Meetings will be held every other Tuesday at 7 pm in COM1200 and Zoom!

** Check our social media for dates and further information.
summary: This society was established to bring students interested in becoming Physician Assistants together. We provide valuable insight about the profession by inviting guest speakers and providing quality information, helping students with their journey.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Pre Dental Society at FSU
description:
The Pre-Dental Society aims to provide resources for FSU students who desire to pursue a career in dentistry. Our objective is to familiarize students with all aspects of the field of dentistry and provide resources to strengthen their applications.

 

We are one of the largest student-led pre-dental organizations in the United States, and we offer a wide array of resources to help you succeed in your dental career goals. FSU Pre-Dental Society conducts various workshops and events to collaborate with your fellow pre-dental students and help improve manual dexterity, interview skills, application entry, and knowledge of dental tools and procedures. Our organization also hosts many social events, volunteering opportunities, and fundraising efforts. 

 

Many dental schools from around the country come either in-person or virtually to our school to give details about their program and discuss what their admissions board is looking for in an applicant. These presentations are highly coveted opportunities to learn more about the schools you are applying to and to network with members of the admissions committee and current dental school students.

 

We hope to see you join this year!

 

Benefits of the Pre-Dental Society:

  • Different dental schools visit our society to speak about the application process and how to become a stronger applicant.
  • Workshops that are performed by dental students their first year of dental school. These workshops help members become familiar with dental procedures and improve manual dexterity, as well as other workshops to assist you with your application.
  • Socials that allow members to network with like-minded students, create lifelong friendships and study partners.
  • Volunteer work that allows members to give back to the community.
  • Constant communication with current dental students that help us all to be competitive students for the application process.
summary: The Pre-Dental Society aims to provide resources for FSU students who desire to pursue a career in dentistry. Our objective is to familiarize students with all aspects of the field of dentistry and provide resources to strengthen their applications.

name: UKirk Tallahassee
description:
UKirk Tallahassee is a ministry of the Presbyterian Church (USA) for college students in the Tallahassee area. We seek to foster a loving community of welcome and inclusion as we strive to live as faithful followers of Jesus Christ. We are part of a larger network of PC(USA) campus ministries (called UKirk Ministries) that seeks to reach, teach, and love college students no matter who they are or what they believe. 

We also operate a non-profit coffee shop called Calvin's Coffee House in our basement. We hope Calvin's and UKirk will be a space where all people feel welcomed, loved, and affirmed.
summary: The purpose of UKirk Tallahassee is to foster a loving community of welcome and inclusion as we strive to live as faithful followers of Jesus Christ.

name: Pride Student Union
description: The Pride Student Union at FSU provides services and programs to address the needs of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, questioning, asexual and intersex students and to increase the awareness of issues pertinent to them within the Florida State University and Tallahassee communities. Further, The Pride Student Union shall endeavor to create a supportive and healthy environment for the welfare of all students regardless of their sexual orientation or gender identity. Affiliates include Generation Action, an organization advocating for reproductive justice on campus, providing the Pride Student Union with a free sexual and reproductive health pantry.
summary: The Pride Student Union at FSU provides services and programs to address the needs of LGBTQ+ students and to increase the awareness of issues pertinent to them within the Florida State University and the greater Tallahassee community.

name: Progressive Black Men, Inc.
description:
Progressive Black Men is a brotherhood whose purpose is to reconstruct and redefine the image of the Black male and uplift people of African descent.

  Progressive Black Men, Inc. shall carry out its purpose through community service, volunteer work, humanitarian aid and the development of high standards of personal integrity and moral character. With events like Tale of Two Charities, Last Supper, and other philanthropic events we strive to better our community one person at a time.
summary: Progressive Black Men is a brotherhood whose purpose is to reconstruct and redefine the image of the Black male and uplift people of African descent.

name: Master of Public Health Student Association at FSU
shortName: Master of Public Health Student Association
description:
The Master of Public Health Student Association focuses on current graduate students and students in the Master’s of Public Health Program at Florida State University. This student association provides opportunities and the tools necessary for our graduates to be successful through participation in internships, education of various public-health related topics, community service projects, and peer involvement.

The Master of Public Health Student Association's mission is to increase awareness of public health-related concerns and policies as well as facilitate and enhance the health status of Florida State University students, faculty, and its surrounding populations. With the involvement of Florida State University’s graduate students within the association, our goals are to create a professional networking experience for our students through mentorships, volunteer experiences in the community, monthly seminars and other opportunities to gain a comprehensive public health perspective in the program.

Join us on Nole Central and Instagram to stay up-to-date on MPHSA-related events: https://www.instagram.com/fsumphsa/
summary: This student association provides opportunities and the tools necessary for our graduates to be successful through participation in internships, education of various public-health related topics, community service projects, and peer involvement.
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: Public Interest Law Students Association
description: PILSA is to be an inclusive organization - inclusive of all students who express curiosity about public interest as well as all fields/aspects of this realm of law.  This broad legal area is about helping those who need it most; protecting victims’ rights and serving the State; advocating for children, veterans, survivors of attacks and trauma, elderly and/or disabled citizens, policies to the benefit of society’s vulnerable, and the environment; working within government to protect the integrity and accessibility of state and federal laws; and championing the ideal of Equal Justice Under Law.  Thus, students face great opportunities throughout school to serve in the public interest and it is the intent of PILSA to help inspire, educate, and equip graduates to utilize their legal skills and passions to work towards a more just and equitable society for all.
summary:
Justice, Service, Community

It is the mission of PILSA to serve students interested in, committed to, and passionate about public interest law and helping better their society.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Puerto Rican Student Association
description:
The Puerto Rican Student Association - also known as PRSA - at Florida State University was founded with the purpose of improving the quality of life for all Puerto Rican and other students at FSU, as well as to promote the Puerto Rican culture through social, educational, political, and service activities.

Our mission is to be a safe space and community for all of our members regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, identity, sexuality, or abilities. We envision a PRSA for all - we welcome everyone who wants to learn more about our beautiful and diverse island, its culture, and its people.
summary: PRSA at Florida State University is an association dedicated to educating people on the history, culture, and current events of the island. We strive to foster an environment of inclusivity and family, within those who identify or appreciate our culture.
comment: Organization has not turned in the Recognition Application for 2010-2011

name: The Real Estate Society at FSU
description:
II. The purposes of the organization are to:

a. Create a social environment for professional development.

b. Provide opportunities for members to advance their knowledge of the real estate industry through professional activities and contact with practitioners. 

c. Provide opportunities for social contacts and mutual encouragement of the members toward achievement of their career goals.  The purpose of the Real Estate Society is to provide opportunities for members to enhance their knowledge of the Real Estate industry through firsthand activities and contact with Real Estate professionals in a variety of fields.

RES meetings are generally held every week or so during the fall and spring semesters. Each meeting will typically feature a professional speaker (in such a field as appraisal, development, lending, law, investment, brokerage, consulting, design, or construction), a professional development workshop, or a social event.  Each meeting will last approximately one hour and is usually followed with a social activity.

Corporate Tours are organized by the Society each semester. These trips are designed not only to allow the members to gain firsthand knowledge from respected professionals in the industry, but also to help introduce members to potential employers. In recent semesters, the Society has traveled to Tampa, Orlando, Jacksonville, Atlanta, Charlotte, New York, and Miami to visit major developers, property managers, fund advisors, appraisers, REITs, brokers, leasing agents, and more. The locations and travel dates of each trip will be determined during RES meetings early in the semester.
summary: The Real Estate Society at FSU fosters career growth through social events, industry exposure, and networking with professionals via meetings, workshops, and corporate tours.

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description: The organization was founded by the Presbyterian Church in America (PCA) in order to provide community and opportunities for growth to college and university students based upon Scripture. The primary and ultimate goal of this organization is to reach students and equip them to serve their fellow students, FSU, and the world around them. This will be carried out through a weekly meeting on Tuesday evenings for worship through fellowship with each other, preaching from the Bible, and music. RUF also offers other avenues for involvement including weekly community groups, one-on-one meetings with the campus minister, and a variety of fellowship and service activities throughout the semester.
summary: The organization was founded by the Presbyterian Church in America in order to provide an in-depth ministry to college and university students based upon the authority of the Word of God in every area of life.

name: Relay For Life at Florida State University
description:
Relay for Life of Florida State University strives to build a community of college students participating in a local, regional, and national cancer prevention effort to honor, support, and recognize individuals within our campus community who have been affected by cancer. 

 

Funds raised by Relay for Life of Florida State University are directed to the American Cancer Society, which funds cancer research, prevention, and detection efforts, provides support services for patients and their caregivers, and strengthens preventive efforts throughout Florida and the United States.

 

We all have a reason why we Relay.  Join us today! 💜

Keep in touch with us on Instagram at @fsurelayforlife!
summary:
Relay For Life unites students, faculty, and organizations across FSU in the fight against cancer. Benefiting the American Cancer Society, fundraising culminates in a 12-hour main event on campus in the spring. 
Celebrate, remember, and fight back! 🎗️💜

name: Reverb FSU
description: The purpose of our tenor-bass a cappella group is to promote a cappella music to Tallahassee, its surrounding areas, all of Florida and the surrounding states. We want to spread a love of music and the arts through our organization.
summary: The purpose of our tenor-bass a cappella group is to promote a cappella music to Tallahassee, its surrounding areas, all of Florida and the surrounding states. We want to spread a love of music and the arts through our organization.
comment: Organization has not turned in the Recognition Application for 2010-2011

name: Student Christian Life at FSU
description:
As Student Christian Life at FSU, we are committed to serving the FSU campus by doing our part in furthering the work of the kingdom of God. We pray and aim to accomplish this task by promoting the spiritual growth of the university's students and faculty in Jesus Christ.

As Student Christian Life at FSU, we are a group of believers in God and Jesus Christ. As we spend our time learning on the FSU campus, we want to do our best to encourage and assist all students. During the semester, we schedule a variety of weekly gatherings including Bible studies, hangouts, recreational activities, small groups, retreats, campouts, and a weekly worship service. Our main objective in all that we do is to provide students and faculty with a place to connect and learn about Jesus Christ.

 

The purpose of SCL is to bring college students to Jesus Christ and prepare them for a lifetime of service. (Matthew 28:19-20)
summary: As Student Christian Life at FSU we are committed to serving the FSU campus to do our part to further the work of the kingdom of God, promoting the spiritual growth of students and faculty in Jesus Christ.

name: Seminole Dance Force Club
description:
Seminole Dance Force Club (SDF) was created in 2001 as a competition and performance dance team at Florida State University and has become a nationally ranked collegiate team throughout the years. Practicing 3 to 4 times a week, team members develop their technique with a diverse repertoire in jazz, hip-hop, contemporary, and ballet by dancing at many on-campus events, attending overnight camps and conventions and by competing annually in NDA National Collegiate Dance Competition---ranking in nationally in both jazz and hip-hop. The team has performed at many of FSU's events such as Homecoming Live (Florida State's Homecoming Pep Rally), the Homecoming Parade, Dance Marathon, Relay for Life, Market Wednesday, while volunteering in the Tallahassee community and supporting other sport clubs.

SDF is a self-run organization, promoting dance as an industry. Every year we put together our very own showcase where members of the team are given the opportunity to showcase their own talent. This team gives a dancer many great opportunities and many ways to grow as a dancer and a person. From costume design, music selection and editing, business, choreography, and coaching, members are provided an opportunity to expand their interests and talents in dance.
summary:
Seminole Dance Force Club (SDF) is the Official Competitive Sports Club Dance Team at Florida State University.
Facebook: Seminole Dance Force
Instagram: @seminoledanceforce

name: SISTUHS, Incorporated at Florida State University
description:
Our purpose is to foster an environment amongst ourselves that is conducive to learning our history and the role that we have played and continue to play in the development of this and other nations. We aim to provide moral and educational guidance to other young women of color. We encourage and promote spiritual growth. We aim to develop local leadership to respond to community needs, particularly those of People of Color. We promote a positive image of Women of Color and anything that positively enhances the survival and well being of African American Women; providing a higher standard of living for our Nubian Kings, Queens and entire community.

 

Our purpose is to provide help and support in response to the community needs. Some of the other programs we host include:

Adopt a Street, Brevard Street (Monthly)
Herstory (Annually)
Tutoring and Mentoring
Womens Retreat (Annually)
Bible Study
Voters Registration
Home Leadership
Male Appreciation
summary: SISTUHS, Incorporated aims to improve the social disposition of underrepresented populations through community development, education, and the provision of other social services in our respective communities.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Society for Human Resource Management at FSU
description:
The Society for Human Resource Management at FSU is a professional student organization dedicated to helping students grow professionally and preparing them for successful careers in Human Resources and related fields.

We provide the opportunity for students to attend corporation tours, network with industry professionals, find professional mentorships, make connections with peers, and gain exposure to internships and full time positions.

Our organization welcomes all students interested in the Human Resource industry and values a diversity in majors, backgrounds, and experience!
summary: Society for Human Resource Management at FSU is an organization for students who want to build their business acumen through exposure to pertinent career and human resource topics.

name: Society for Musicology
description: The purpose of the Society for Musicology is to foster and promote academic excellence among musicology students, acclimate new students to the musicology graduate program, create a positive learning environment for all students, and promote a sense of community among students and faculty.  The Society strives to build relationships between music and its sister disciplines, and holds events each year that encourage dialogue between scholars from Florida State University and other institutions.
summary: The purpose of the Society for Musicology is to foster and promote academic excellence among musicology students, acclimate new students to the musicology graduate program, create a positive learning environment for all students, and promote a sense of
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: Society of Automotive Engineers
description:
FAMU-FSU SAE is a multidisciplinary organization open to all students. Members have the unique opportunity to apply their classroom knowledge and personal experiences to research, design, and manufacture a fully functional vehicle. In recent years our team has competed against universities from around the world in Alabama, New York, California, Nebraska, and Michigan.

There are three major components to the judging and success of our team:

Dynamic Testing (Hill Climb, Suspension, Auto-cross, skid pad, acceleration)

Static Events (Design, Cost, Technical Inspection, Project Management)

Business Presentation (Cost Report, ROI, Marketing)

Join today and develop your skills in entrepreneurship, engineering, project management, and professional development.
summary: FF Racing is the official motorsport team of Florida State University and Florida A&M University. Sanctioned under the Society of Automotive Engineers, FF-Racing competes in Baja and Formula SAE competitions against universities from around the world.

name: Society of Composers, Inc. at FSU
description:
This organization will be dedicated to the promotion of student composers, the performance of their works, as well as the understanding and dissemination of contemporary music.

We sponsor guest speakers and performers, hold concerts of members' music, workshops on orchestration and instrumentation, and presentations of each other's works.
summary: This organization is dedicated to the promotion, performance and dissemination of student compositions, as well as the understanding and dissemination of contemporary music.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers at FAMU-FSU
description:
SHPE is the leading social-technical organization whose primary function is to enhance and achieve the fullest potential of Hispanics and under served minorities in engineering, math, technology and science.

SHPE is here to empower the hispanic community through encouraging our members to realize their fullest potential. Our members are then able to make and impact to the world around them through STEM awareness, access, support, and development. SHPE's vision is a world where Hispanics are highly valued and are allowed to be influential leading innovators, scientists, mathematicians, and engineers. Our values include familia, service, education, and resilience. We embrace our diverse cultures and communities, which enable us to adapt, thrive, and persist with optimism.

We fulfill our mission by increasing educational opportunities; promoting professional and personal growth; carry out our social responsibility to be involved in education, business and government issues; enhancing pride within our organization; and reinforcing our reputation as a vital Hispanic organization.

Visit our website!
summary:
SHPE is the leading social-technical organization whose primary function is to enhance and achieve the potential of Hispanics in engineering, math and science.

We will fulfill our mission by increasing educational opportunities and promoting diversity.

name: FAMU-FSU Society of Women Engineers
description:
SWE is a non-profit organization for people interested in engineering and supporting women in engineering. SWE is the driving force that establishes engineering as a highly desirable career aspiration for women. SWE empowers women to succeed and advance in those aspirations and be recognized for their life-changing contributions and achievements as engineers and leaders.

The purpose of this organization is to further the objectives of SWE by stimulating women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expanding the image of the engineering profession as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrating the value of diversity.


SWE aims to inform the public of the qualifications of women engineers and the opportunities open to them. SWE serves as a center of information on women in engineering and encourages women engineers to attain high levels of educational and professional achievement.
summary: SWE is a non-profit organization for people interested in engineering and supporting women in engineering. SWE is the driving force that establishes engineering as a highly desirable career aspiration for women.
comment: Organizaiton has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Sociology Graduate Student Union
description:
The Sociology Graduate Student Union (SGSU) is an organization led by, and for, the graduate students in the Department of Sociology at Florida State University. SGSU represents sociology graduate students in professional and departmental policy matters and promotes a supportive and collaborative culture among graduate students.

The purpose of SGSU is as follows:

  1. To represent sociology graduate students in professional and departmental matters including, but not limited to, representation in departmental policy decisions and appeals processes.
  2. To further develop the quality of graduate education and overall learning environment within the Sociology Department.
  3. To facilitate a supportive and collaborative culture among Sociology graduate students.
  4. To establish personal contact between members, professional sociologists and professionals in related disciplines.
  5. To acquaint members with the educational, economic, and cultural opportunities within the university and community.
  6. To facilitate a graduate student culture of service to the Sociology Department, Florida State University, and surrounding communities.


SGSU is open to full-time and part-time FSU sociology graduate students.
summary: SGSU is an organization that represents sociology graduate students in professional and departmental matters and promotes a supportive culture among graduate students.
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: Organization for Religion Graduate Students
description: This organization provides an extra-departmental forum for the variety of students in the religion graduate program. It seeks to promote a scholarly and professional atmosphere for students by organizing and hosting an annual symposium
summary: This organization provides an extra-departmental forum for the variety of students in the religion graduate program to come together. It seeks to promote a scholarly and professional atmosphere for students by organizing and hosting an annual symposium.

name: Student Bar Association
description: SBA serves to further the legal, academic, and social interests of its members and of FSU Law.  We host regular social events for students of the law school, hold mixers with local attorneys, help coordinate events with other law school student organizations, and work to improve the local Tallahassee community. Additionally, we represent the ABA-Law Student Division at the annual nation conference.
summary: SBA serves to further the legal, academic, and social interests of its members and of FSU Law.

name: Student Dietetic Association
description:
To serve as a resource base that will provide members with career guidance, internship resources and contacts, community service and volunteer work.

Our organization meets once a month. We have guest speakers of local dietitians as well as faculty. We sponsor/chair a 5k annually, and we also try to do one fundraiser a year. We like to give our members a firm range of things for them to do after college as well as contacts in the business world.

We aim to get our members more involved in the community, specifically in the field of nutrition and dietetics, in order for them to gain valuable experience and connections, as well as begin to narrow down what path they would like to follow after graduation. Our instagram is @fsusda so follow that and join our GroupMe for all meeting and event notifications! https://groupme.com/join_group/107187783/CD0CA7Qn
summary: SDA is for dietetic majors or pre-majors who wish to meet others in their major, meet local professionals in the field, and serve the community in ways ranging from teaching about nutrition and volunteering at the local food pantry. IG: fsusda

name: Student Interest Group In Neurology
description:
The purpose of SIGN is to educate and promote neurological medicine at Florida State University (FSU). This will be done primarily via meetings in which Neurological physicians and residents participate in discussions and provide information concerning current issues in Neurological Medicine.

Student Interest Group in Neurology (SIGN) is an organization based at the College of Medicine. Our primary focus is to increase student knowledge of both clinical and research applications of neuroscience. This will be be achieved through lectures bringing renowned experts in neurology, psychiatry, and neuroscience research, as well as through outreach programs.

SIGN will also facilitate physician shadowing and tours with neurologists, neurosurgeons, psychiatrists, and research laboratories.
summary: The purpose of SIGN is to educate and promote neurological medicine at FSU. This will be done primarily via meetings in which physicians, residents, and patients participate in discussions and provide information concerning the field of Neurology.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: College of Business Student Leadership Council
description:
The Student Leadership Council will enrich the College of Business environment and represent the entire College of Business student body interest. The Student Leadership Council will be composed of 40 highly motivated, discipline-specific business students who meet regularly to facilitate communications and further their goals of the College of Business and the College of Business Board of Governors.

Serving as a subcommittee under the Board of Governors, the Student Leadership Council shall consist of the following members: President, Executive Vice President, Vice President of Operations, Vice President of Programming,  Vice President of Communications and Development. The College of Business Dean and an Executive Committee Advisor will be linked with the council as a means of providing necessary input. The council will be made up of declared business students.
summary: The Student Leadership Council will enrich the College of Business environment and represent the entire College of Business student body interest. The Student Leadership Council will be composed of highly motivated, discipline specific business students.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Student National Medical Association
description:
In 1964 the organization was established to help improve recruitment and retention of students of color, to increase the numbers of minority physicians, and to improve access to health care in underserved communities.

Nationally, the Student National Medical Association is one of the largest and oldest organizations dedicated to empowering minority students with the tools necessary to become excellent physicians.


Keeping with the national mission, the SNMA at the Florida State University College of Medicine was established by medical students to continue these goals in the Tallahassee, FL community. In our short history at the FSUCOM, SNMA has participated in various community health fairs, cohosted residency workshops, and attended many regional and national conferences.

We have formed strong and lasting relationships with the National Kidney Foundation through the Kidney Early Evaluation Program. In addition we have furnished our members with the resources that will help them attain their individual goals.
