As a new addition to the SNMA family, FSUCOM plans to incorporate the national vision, our schools mission, as well as our individual dreams for the betterment of our community. fsu_snma@yahoo.com
summary: This national organization was established to help improve recruitment and retention of students of color, to increase the numbers of minority physicians, and to improve access to health care in underserved communities.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Students Interested in Global Health
description: Students Interested in Global Health (SIGH) is a medical student-led organization that exists to increase awareness of global health needs, connect medical students to healthcare providers serving around the world, and immerse medical students in global healthcare service opportunities. SIGH strives toward these endeavors through Lunch & Learns with guest speakers who have served in medical missions, opportunities for medical service overseas, and volunteering through the Medical Student Refugee Assistance Program (MSRAP). In addition, SIGH pursues creative ways to understand cultural differences and honor the diverse cultures and backgrounds of the FSU COM faculty and student body. Ultimately, SIGH seeks to cultivate a life-long commitment to serving others through medicine in countries around the world with the talents and training received as physicians.
summary: SIGH is a medical student-led organization that aims to cultivate passion for global medicine, connect medical students to global health care providers, and develop a life-long commitment to serving others through medicine in countries around the world.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Healthy Noles
description:
As peer educators, we work to increase the general wellness of FSU students and faculty. By using FSU’s Nine Dimensions of Wellness as a blueprint for our cause, we can facilitate both fun and education on campus. 
We focus on stress management, sleep education, nutrition, body image, alcohol and drug use, sexual health education, fitness, hazing prevention, and overall wellness. Healthy Noles serves the community through the following:
  • Campus events and outreach
  • Hosting workshops
  • Providing classroom presentations
  • Developing health materials and passive campaigns
  • Serving as health advocates
summary: Healthy Noles is an organization of trained student peer health educators who strive to educate the FSU community on several wellness-related issues.

name: Surgery Interest Group
description:
The purpose of the Surgery Interest Group is to allow students to experience and gain interest in the surgical field through shadowing current physicians, learning about the career paths, and understanding/practicing the skills needed in the operating room. The organization gives each student the opportunity to work with current surgeons and to participate in clinics aimed at suturing, etiquette, and residency / educational experience.

This organization sponsors a yearly suturing workshop as well as hosting a variety of physicians in the community and from outside areas to speak about their profession.
summary: The purpose of the Surgery Interest Group is to allow students to experience and gain interest in the surgical field through shadowing current physicians, learning about the career paths, and understanding/practicing the skills needed.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Tau Beta Sigma - Alpha Omega
description:
Tau Beta Sigma National Honorary Band Sorority provides service to collegiate bands, encourages the advancement of women in the music profession, and promotes and enriches an appreciation of band music through recognition, leadership development, and education of its members.

 

1. To encourage musical interest and abilities in promoting band work among students.
2. To help band members adjust to the university environment.
3. To provide a pleasant social experience for every participant within the band program.
4. To cooperate with other musical organizations.
5. To promote a better relationship with band participants.
summary: Tau Beta Sigma National Honorary Band Sorority provides service to collegiate bands, encourages the advancement of women in the music profession, and promotes and enriches an appreciation of band music through recognition, leadership development...
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: The Bhakti Yoga Club
description:
The Bhakti Yoga organization is dedicated to exploring the timeless wisdom of ancient scriptures and the transformative power of mantra meditation. Open to all, regardless of religious background, our club provides a welcoming space for students to unite, socialize, and deepen their understanding of bhakti (devotion to God).

Through engaging discussions, group chanting (kirtan), and thought-provoking sessions on the Bhagavad Gita and Vedic philosophy, we aim to help students find inner peace, clarity, and purpose in their lives. Whether you’re curious about spiritual practices, seeking a deeper connection with yourself, or simply looking for a like-minded community, we invite you to join us in this journey of self-discovery and devotion.

We also organize weekly events that include lectures and provide free Indian food to attendees. Follow our instagram page for inquiries about events: https://www.instagram.com/iskcontallahassee
summary: The Bhakti Yoga Club is a group dedicated to mantra meditation, exploring ancient scriptures, and understanding the purpose of life through bhakti. Open to all, we provide a welcoming space for students to unite and explore Krishna Consciousness.

name: College Republicans of Florida State University
description:
Dating back to 1962, the College Republicans at Florida State University serves as the official auxiliary of the Republican Party on Florida State University’s campus. We strive to inform the students of our great campus about the many opportunities available and to them and to promote intelligent, dynamic, and cooperative group discussion. Providing FSU students and club members the means of obtaining practical political knowledge is a top priority.

 

The College Republicans at FSU serves as the largest conservative youth organization in Florida's capital city. We are here to provide a political forum for all Republicans at FSU and we encourage participation in the activities of the Republican Party as a whole. We promote relations with the Republican Party of Florida, other conservative organizations, candidates, and incumbent elected officials. In doing all the aforementioned, our most important goal is to accurately represent and transmit the views of the FSU community to the leadership of the Republican Party.
summary: The College Republicans at the Florida State University serves as the auxiliary of the Republican Party on FSU's campus.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies
description:
The Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies is a group of lawyers and law students, primarily conservatives and libertarians, interested in the current state of the legal order. It is founded on the principles that the state exists to preserve freedom, that the separation of governmental powers is central to our Constitution, and that it is emphatically the province and duty of the judiciary to say what the law is, not what it should be.

This group is the Florida State University chapter of the Federalist Society. Our primary purpose is to investigate and educate on topics related to the issues above. Our primary method to achieve this is through debates, panels, and other speaking events. 

We also attend regional and national conferences for our own education. We provide -- and take advantage of -- opportunities to network with other chapters and associated professionals. And, not to be diminished, we have fun together in and out of the classroom.
summary: A non-partisan conservative and libertarian organization dedicated to fostering balanced and open debate about the fundamental principles of freedom, federalism, and judicial restraint.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: The Flute Association at Florida State University
description: The Flute Association at Florida State University promotes the interest and study of the flute on the FSU campus and in the community, sponsoring recitals, masterclasses, and concerts given by renowned guest artists and members of the FSU Flute Studio. The organization also promotes flute education and instruction in the Tallahassee and the Leon County area.
summary: The Flute Association at Florida State University promotes the interest and study of the flute on the FSU campus and in the community, sponsoring recitals, masterclasses, and concerts given by renowned guest artists and members of the FSU Flute Studio.

name: Geriatrics Interest Group
description:
The Geriatrics Interest Group (GIG) of the Florida State University College of Medicine (FSU COM) is to educate and promote geriatric medicine at the Florida State University (FSU). The GIG promotes the involvement of students and professionals from various disciplines concerned with the care of the geriatric community, with the purpose of enhancing the multiple dimensions of geriatric care.

Members of the GIG, also, have the opportunity to join the American Geriatrics Society (AGS) and the Florida Geriatrics Society (FGS), which provide additional opportunities for informative meetings and conferences to attend. The primary focus of the GIG activities is to meet the needs of the geriatric community through education, community outreach, and student geriatric involvement.
summary: The Geriatrics Interest Group (GIG) of the Florida State University College of Medicine (FSU COM) is to educate and promote geriatric medicine at the Florida State University (FSU).
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: The Kudzu Review
description:
The Kudzu Review is FSU's undergraduate literary magazine established in 1988. Kudzu is a national publication that strives to provide undergrad students with opportunities publishing and literary experience in a full-scale editorial magazine. We accept submissions from within FSU and from undergrad students at universities across the country. 

 

     Our purpose is to serve English, Studio Art, and other students in FSU's community by providing an outlet for creative work of all kinds - poetry, essays, short stories, photography, and painting are just a few of the genres we publish by students from all majors and fields of interest. Additionally, Kudzu aims to provide FSU students interested in the publishing industry with practical experience in editing, writing, graphic design, and social media advertising.

 

     Students can participate in The Kudzu Review as a volunteer, class member, or intern (ENC 4942). The Kudzu course, ENG 3931, offers participants academic credit for an English elective. Staff applications open at the beginning of the fall and spring semesters.
summary: The Kudzu Review is FSU's undergraduate literary magazine established in 1988. Kudzu is a national publication that strives to provide undergraduate students with opportunities for publishing and literary experience in a full-scale editorial magazine.

name: The National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
description:
The principal objectives of the Association shall be:

To ensure the political, educational, social and economic equality of all citizens

To achieve equality of rights and eliminate race prejudice among the citizens of the United States

To remove all barriers of racial discrimination through democratic processes

To seek enactment and enforcement of federal, state and local laws securing civil rights

To inform the public of the adverse effects of racial discrimination and to seek its elimination

To educate persons as to their constitutional rights and to take all lawful action to secure the exercise thereof, and to take any other lawful action in furtherance of these objectives, consistent with the NAACPs Articles of Incorporation and this Constitution
summary: The mission of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People is to ensure the political, educational, social and economic equality of rights of all persons and to eliminate racial hatred and racial discrimination.

name: The National Council of Negro Women
description:
NCNW is a voluntary nonprofit membership organization with the mission to advance the opportunities and the quality of life for African American women, their families and communities. NCNW fulfills this mission through research, advocacy, national and community based services and programs in the United States and Africa. With its 38 national affiliate organizations and its more than 200 community based sections, NCNW has an outreach to nearly four million women, all contributing to the peaceful solutions to the problems of human welfare and rights.

The FSU Section of NCNW was founded on February 10th, 1999 and it continues to fill the gaps that exist within the campus community, particularly in meeting the concerns of women of color.
summary: NCNW is a voluntary, nonprofit membership organization with the mission to advance the opportunities and the quality of life for African American women, their families and their communities.
comment: Organization has not completed the re-recognition process for 2009-2010.

name: The Navigators
description:
The purpose of this organization is to meet students' spiritual needs in a deeper sense while providing fun interactions with other students.

The Navigators will meet in person on Wednesday nights at Dodd Hall. We host numerous bible studies in residence halls as well as off campus locations. A few events every semester are more activity and entertainment based.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to meet students' spiritual needs in a deeper sense while providing for fun interaction with other students. The Navigators will be meeting in Dodd auditorium. We meet spiritual needs by hosting Bible studies.

name: Undergraduate Art History Association
description: Florida State University's Undergraduate Art History Association is for anyone interested in art and/or the history of art—with the intention of increasing the undergraduate student body's interest in, involvement with, and accessibility to the visual arts community!
summary: To increase the undergraduate student body's interest in, involvement with, and accessibility to the visual arts community.The Undergraduate Art History Association is for anyone interested in art and/or the history of art.

name: Flying High Circus
description:
We are one of only two collegiate circuses in the United States. A unique tradition on the campus of The Florida State University since 1947, the Circus is a year-round program in which FSU students can participate. We boast an impressive student group that takes advantage of a marvelous learning opportunity from the circus as a part of their collegiate experience.

Once you see our show you will be amazed that it is put on by amateur college students. Even though they receive no class credit, they are able to put on a performance that rivals that of a professional show. Not only do our students perform in the show, but they also are involved in virtually every aspect of production from setup of our Big Top tent to the sewing of our costumes. The show is the culmination of the tremendous amount of hard work, dedication, and skill that our students bring to our organization. If you are enrolled at Florida State University, and would like to get involved, you can contact us at circus@admin.fsu.edu.
summary: We are one of only two collegiate circuses in the United States. A unique tradition on the campus of The Florida State University since 1947, the Circus is a year-round program in which FSU students can participate.

name: Animal Legal Defense Fund - Florida State University College of Law Chapter
description: SALDF-FSU Chapter is dedicated to providing a forum for education, advocacy, and scholarship. ALDF aims to protect the lives and advance the interests of animals through the legal system and strives to raise the profile of the field of animal law.
summary: ALDF - FSU Law Chapter is dedicated to providing a forum for education, advocacy, and scholarship, aims to protect the lives and advance the interests of animals through the legal system, and strives to raise the profile of the field of animal law.

name: Association for Computing Machinery
description:
The Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) is an international scientific, academic, and professional organization dedicated to advancing computing as a science and profession.  With a world-wide membership, ACM is a leading resource for computing professionals and students working in various fields of Computer Science, Computer Engineering, and Information Technology. 

The ACM's FSU student chapter serves all FSU students. We strive to enrich each member’s time at FSU, while preparing each individual for work in the industry, or further academic pursuits. Our membership is comprised of both undergraduate, and graduate students with a diverse range of interests, skills, and experience levels.
summary: The Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) is an international scientific, academic, and professional organization dedicated to advancing computing as a science and profession.
comment: Organization has not turned in the Recognition Application for 2010-2011.

name: Delta Chi Fraternity, Inc.
description: The Delta Chi Fraternity has dedicated itself to the goal of "Personal Growth Through Brotherhood." Your true friendships evolve by sharing common experiences and challenges with others, by being roommates, studying together, being teammates, or by just having a good time. Delta Chi will expose you to a multiplicity of lifestyles and backgrounds, providing an opportunity for you to learn and to work successfully with others.
summary: Delta Chi at Florida State is made up of a very diverse group of men who all believe in the values upon which our fraternity was founded: Education, Character, Justice, and Friendship. We all believe that through brotherhood we are enriching our lives!

name: Transfer Student Organization at FSU Law
description: Transfer Student Organization at Florida State College of Law is a program designed to facilitate a smooth transition for law students who transfer into Florida State University College of Law from a different law school. Our goal is to help acclimate all new transfer students to their new law school.
summary: Transfer Student Organization at Florida State College of Law is a program designed to facilitate a smooth transition for law students who transfer into FSU Law from a different law school.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Fraternity
description:
The purpose of this chapter shall be to provide an effective, cohesive forum, similar to that of a respectable law school student body; to provide for the professional advancements of its members; to promote the principles of liberty and equal justice under law for all citizens; to stimulate excellence in scholarship; to provide a forum for the interchange between members of this chapter and members of the practicing bar, the bench, and law professors; to develop lasting professional friendships among its members; and to promote the ideals, principles, and purposes of Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International as defined in Article II of the Constitution of the Fraternity and the Laws of the United States of America.

Phi Alpha Delta is a co-ed professional fraternity that strives to provide an opportunity for pre-law students to learn and improve the profession of law in the admission to law school and the bar. We also engage in philanthropic/community service activities and other networking events to give our members a step forward in their future legal career. Among them are special mixers with local attorneys and FSU students, chapter guest speakers, LSAT prep with exclusive discounts, community service events, campus involvement, and leadership opportunities.

For all up to date information, check out our instagram @phialphadelta_fsu
summary: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional law fraternity advancing integrity, compassion and courage through service to the student, the school, the profession and the community.

name: Pre-Optometry Club at FSU
description:
We meet every other Thursday at 5:15pm. Join our Canvas page for meeting information, reminders, and more!

Pre-Optometry Club is for anyone who is interested in learning more about the visual system and a career in optometry! There is so much to learn about the path to becoming an optometrist.

At our meetings, we have guest speakers such as representatives from various colleges and schools of optometry, local optometrists, ophthalmologists, and researchers to present to us.

Club members also have many opportunities for unique activities such as performing vision screenings with Special Olympics Opening Eyes, volunteering at the Kearney Center, shadowing local optometrists, and vision screenings on fellow student athletes! Active members also get discounts on OAT study materials.

 

Send us an email if you have any questions: fsupreoptometry@gmail.com

Join our Facebook page and GroupMe!
summary: Pre-Optometry Club is for anyone who is interested in learning more about the visual system and a career in optometry! We provide students opportunities to volunteer in the field, speak to recruiters, and learn from the best local optometrists.

name: The Big Event Tallahassee
description: The Big Event is one of the largest, one-day, student-run service projects in the nation where the students of colleges and universities will come together  to say ‘thank you’ to the residents of their college town. A tradition started at Texas A&M University 26 years ago, The Big Event will serve as a day of service to the Tallahassee community. Students, faculty, and staff will participate in this annual event to show their appreciation to the surrounding community by completing service projects such as yard work, window washing, and painting for community members. Although The Big Event has become one of the largest one-day, student-run service projects in the nation, our message and our mission will be to simply say “thank you.”
summary: The Big Event Tallahassee is one of the largest, one-day, student-run service projects in the nation where the students of colleges and universities will come together to say 'thank you' to the residents of their college town.

name: Slavic Student Association
description:
The Slavic Student Association at Florida State University is an organization dedicated to the promotion of Slavic culture and intercultural dialogue. While many of our members are of Slavic heritage or speak Slavic-languages, it is not a requirement to join! We boast members with different interests, majors, and more, making it a great place to expand your social circle. Join us as we indulge in a unique experience of cultural exchange and immersion! 

Find us on IG or join our GroupMe (QR code in gallery) for updates.
summary:
The Slavic Student Association at Florida State University is an organization dedicated to promoting cultural exchange by celebrating Slavic culture and languages.

Open to all majors

name: La società italiana
description:
La società hosts two main, reoccurring events throughout the Fall and Spring semesters:

-At the Tavola italiana you will have a chance to practice your Italian language skills in a casual setting through games and conversation with teachers and students of all levels! 

-At the film screenings you will improve your language skills while deepening your knowledge of Italian culture. 

-Additional events to include International Coffee Hour, International Bazaar, special interest film screenings, poetry readings, and socials.
summary:
La società italiana organizes weekly activities related to Italian culture.
In addition, we offer a few selected cultural events each semester to showcase Italian Literature and Culture.

name: Semper Fi Society
description: The Semper Fi Society is an organization devoted to seeking out and developing potential candidates that have the moral fortitude and character necessary to compete for the opportunity to earn a commission in The United States Marine Corps.
summary: The Semper Fi Society is an organization devoted to seeking out and developing potential candidates that have the moral fortitude and character necessary to compete for the opportunity to earn a commission in The United States Marine Corps.

name: Instructional Systems Student Association
description:
The Instructional Systems Student Association (ISSA) is a student-led organization representing the students, faculty, and alumni of the Instructional Systems and Learning Technologies (ISLT) program within the College of Education, Health, and Human Sciences at Florida State University.

ISSA is committed to fostering a sense of community, collaboration, and professional growth among ISLT students and alumni. Through events, workshops, and networking opportunities, we aim to strengthen connections across cohorts, encourage knowledge sharing, and celebrate the diverse experiences within our program.

Our mission is to enhance the academic and professional development of ISLT students while promoting the program’s local visibility. By organizing community gatherings, alumni talks, and discussions, ISSA provides a platform for students to engage, learn, and lead — preparing future instructional designers and learning technologists to make meaningful contributions in the field.
summary: The Instructional Systems Student Association (ISSA) is an organization comprised of students, faculty, and alumni of the Instructional Systems and Learning Technologies program at the College of Education, Health, and Human Sciences at FSU.

name: Tennis Club at FSU
description:
Club tennis at FSU provides a social and competitive environment for both recreational and tournament-level players. The organization partakes in statewide competitions throughout the fall and spring semesters. Official club practices take place at the Scott Speicher Tennis Center on Mondays and Thursdays from 6:30 pm to 8:30 pm. Neither tournaments nor practices are mandatory.

Please make sure to follow our Instagram @fsuclubtennis as this is our primary outlet for communication.
summary: Club Tennis at FSU offers a competitive environment for tennis players across a broad range of skillsets. Joining the club is an excellent way to expand your network and surround yourself with like-minded athletes.

name: Women's Ultimate Frisbee Club at FSU
description: We are a competitive women's ultimate frisbee club for Florida State University students. Our mission is to promote and grow competitive ultimate both on campus and in our community. Comprising players of all skill levels, we actively participate in the USA Ultimate Championship Series and numerous tournaments nationwide. Our goal is to foster a safe and competitive environment while emphasizing good sportsmanship and training habits. We hope to spark enjoyment of the sport for years to come!
summary: We are a competitive women's ultimate club team for Florida State University students. We organize, promote, and encourage the growth of competitive women's ultimate on campus and within our community.

name: Women's Club Lacrosse
description:
The purpose of this organization is to provide Florida State University with a competitive women's lacrosse team at the club level. We strive to excel in the sport of lacrosse in club competition while representing the University to our fullest ability.

Women's Lacrosse Club at FSU is a part of the SWLL (Southeast Women's Lacrosse League). We compete on a level much like Division I lacrosse, and compete for a spot in the National Championship Tournament at the end of the spring season. We play and are held to the same restrictions as programs of Division I caliber. Our goal, as a team, is to compete in the National Tournament.

In the Spring of 2022, our team received its first invitation to the WCLA National Tournament, making program history! In Spring of 2023, our program received another invitation, where we placed 9th in the National Tournament!
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide Florida State University with a competitive women's lacrosse team at the club level. We strive to excel in the sport of lacrosse in club competition while representing the University to our fullest

name: National Art Education Association Student Chapter at Florida State University, a Recognized Student Organization
description:
NAEA-FSU’s primary purpose is to promote the field of visual arts education through solidifying bridges of cooperative partnership within FSU’s College of Visual Arts, Theatre, & Dance, externally with other FSU academic departments, as well as with public and private entities within our local community. NAEA-FSU shall do this primarily by:

  • Acting as liaison and local representative of the National Art Education Association (NAEA);
  • Promoting the Mission and Vision of the NAEA as stated on its website at http://arteducators.org/about-us;
  • Educating non-art-education students and the greater community about the importance of the visual arts in life-long learning, human fulfillment, and global understanding;
  • Developing and maintaining resources for FSU Art Education students, including but not limited to: mentoring, networking, social activities, workshops, and other professional development opportunities.
summary: NAEA-FSU’s primary purpose is to promote the field of visual arts education by acting as liaison and local representative of the National Art Education Association.

name: Master of Science in Finance Association
description: The Master of Science in Finance Association prepares MSF students to enter the job market, serve their community and university, and advance the reputation of the Florida State University College of Business.
summary: The Master of Science in Finance Association prepares MSF students to enter the job market, serve their community and university, and advance the reputation of the Florida State University College of Business.

name: Cares Society at FSU
description:
Cares Society at FSU is a dynamic medical student organization devoted to helping the underserved and underprivileged populations both locally and internationally. Our mission is to provide health services, including medical care, education, and counseling to those people who would not otherwise have access to care while providing an interactive, clinical learning environment for FSU College of Medicine students.

 

We pride ourselves on local outreach because we realize that there are people who are in desperate need of medical help right in our own backyard! FSUCares has established a partnership with local organizations such as the Leon County Health Department, Neighborhood Health Services, the Bond Clinic, and the Tallahassee Shelter. Some local activities include setting up health information and screening at the Migrant Health Fair in Gadsden County, caring for patients at the Neighborhood Health Services Clinic, and establishing monthly health screenings at the local Homeless Shelter.

 

Our international and state medical outreach focuses on rural villages in Filipinas, Panama, and the underserved populations at the Texas-Mexico border and Immokalee, FL. The goals of our medical outreach programs are to focus on the unique public health factors that influence different regions of the world in order to expose and prepare future doctors for the ever-changing face of medicine and society.
summary: Cares Society at FSU is a dynamic medical student organization devoted to helping the underserved and underprivileged populations both locally and internationally.

name: Union Board
description: The Union Board was established to provide students, faculty, staff, and guests with programs and services offered through the FSU Union Complex. The Union Board consists of 12 elected student members and 5 appointed members (2 faculty, 2 staff, and 1 alumnus) that represent the university community and ensure that Union facilities and amenities meet their needs.
summary: The Union Board was established to provide students, faculty, staff, and guests with programs and services offered through the FSU Student Union. The Board represents the university community to ensure that facilities and amenities meet their needs.

name: We Take Education and Content Higher Club
description:
This innovative program allows you to expand your understanding of your science or mathematics major, explore the possibility of becoming a mathematics or science teacher, and develop a deep knowledge of teaching.

 

 

Members of WeTEACH do not have to be a part of the FSU-Teach program, though it is encouraged! This club provides a community of like-minded individuals with an outlet to learn and teach STEM subjects. This organization provides General Body Meetings for socialization, study nights to promote academic success and collaboration amongst our members, and service opportunities which promote exploration and discovery in STEM amongst the local Tallahassee community.
summary: WeTEACH is the official social, academic, and service club of the FSU-Teach program.
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: Sigma Delta Pi Spanish Honors Society
description:
Sigma Delta Pi is the National Collegiate Hispanic Honor Society (La Sociedad Nacional Honoraria Hispánica). It was founded to encourage students to continue studying Spanish, allowing them to meet people with whom they shared commom interests, and getting more involved by joining an organizaton that upheld the values and respect for the Hispanic world.

Sigma Delta Pi offers a growing scholarship program exclusively for its active members with annual undergradutae awards for summer study in Spain, Mexico, Ecuador, Peru and Bolivia (locations subject to change) and research grants for graduate students. Through its partnership with Experience, Inc., Sigma Delta Pi also offers its members free career services at its exclusive website:sigmadeltapi.experience.com.

Overall, Sigma Delta Pi is an organization composed of dedicated students, with a passion for Hispanic culture and Spanish language, that allows its members to create great networking relations as well as connections and support in their academic career.

In order to be eligible to become a member of Sigma Delta Pi, students must:

1. Have completed three years of study of college-level Spanish (eighteen semester credit hours) or the equivalent thereof, including at least three semester hours of a course in Hispanic literature or Hispanic culture and civilization at the junior (third-year) level. A student may be admitted to active membership while still enrolled in the second junior-level course if all other requirements have been met and if the instructor of the course certifies that the student's work is of "B" or "A" quality.

2. Have a minimal grade point average of 3.0 on a 4.0 point scale in all Spanish courses taken.

3. Must have completed at least three semesters or five quarters of college work.

Note: Graduate students who have been unconditionally admitted to a degree-granting program may be elected to membership upon completion of two graduate courses in Spanish with an average which, if continued, will make them eligible for a graduate degree.
summary: Sigma Delta Pi is the National Collegiate Hispanic Honor Society (La Sociedad Nacional Honoraria Hispnica). It was founded to encourage students to continue studying Spanish, allowing them to meet people with whom they shared commom interests,

name: Black Law Students Association, Graduate Chapter
description:
The Delores Poindexter Auzenne Chapter of the Black Law Students Association plays a pivotal role in helping acclimate law students to the legal field. BLSA’s main objective is to increase the number of professionally trained Black Lawyers, but our organization is open to
individuals of all backgrounds, races, sexual orientations, and political preferences. We sponsor and participate in a number of educational, political, social, and community service activities. We also offer a number of opportunities to network with local attorneys and our
Black Alumni network and are active in fighting for racial, gender, and social justice within the law school community and greater legal world. We encourage all BLSA members to take advantage of all opportunities that the College of Law and the National Black Law Students
Association have to offer.
summary: FSU BLSA helps integrate members into the College of Law community and the greater legal community through academic, mentoring, advocacy, networking, and community service opportunities in the Tallahassee area. We strive for excellence daily!

name: Men's Club Volleyball at Florida State University
description: We strive to compete with the best while aspiring to be the best. We are a traveling athletic team. We are looking for people to play competitively and have the drive to learn.
summary: We strive to compete with the best while aspiring to be the best. We are a traveling athletic team. We are looking for people to play competitively and have the drive to learn.

name: Student Nurses' Association at Florida State University
description: SNA at FSU strives to assist future nurses with nursing curriculum, community service, networking, and job search. We hope to create memories and friendships that continue well beyond our years at FSU by providing a nursing community that is helpful and inviting. The Student Nurses' Association at Florida State University is directly affiliated with the Florida Nursing Students Association (FNSA) and National Student Nurses Association (NSNA). We have the pleasure of attending NSNA and FSNA conventions, where we are able to learn and work among colleagues and peers with the same aspirations - to uplift the profession of nursing starting with the nursing student. Follow our Instagram @sna_fsu to see our latest event information.
summary: SNA at FSU strives to assist future nurses with nursing curriculum, community service, networking, and job search. We want this organization to facilitate current and future nursing in creating lasting community, support, and memories.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action at FSU
description:
Are you passionate about making a difference in your community while fighting for and educating the public about reproductive justice? If yes, then Planned Parenthood Generation Action at FSU is the place for you! Generation Action is the collegiate affiliate of Planned Parenthood and is comprised of students who support reproductive and fundamental justice for all. We host a variety of educational workshops throughout the semester, as well as condom crawls, panel interviews, and even the occasional protest. Abortion, women's issues, LGBTQ+ issues, sexuality, and sexual health have all been topics at previous meetings. During the spring semester, we attend legislative hearings at the Florida State Capitol to try and combat any legislation that is harmful to our mission. If any of this sounds appealing to you, then we would love to see you at our next event! 

 

Check us out on Instagram for future event information!
summary: Planned Parenthood Generation Action is an energetic, welcoming, and passionate student organization centralized around organizing youth to stand up for reproductive and fundamental justice for all!

name: Student Alumni Association
description: The Student Alumni Association (SAA) is a student organization that fosters spirit, tradition, and pride throughout the university. Our objective is to provide activities and programs to FSU students that can assist in their development as leaders, scholars, and future alumni. All current FSU undergraduate and graduate students are eligible to be a part of SAA and joining is one of the first steps to becoming a Seminole for life.
summary: The Student Alumni Association (SAA) is a student organization that fosters spirit, tradition, and pride throughout the university. Our objective is to provide activities and programs to FSU students that can assist in their development as future alumni.

name: Ophthalmology Interest Group
description: The Ophthalmology Interest Group seeks to provide a platform for medical students to explore ophthalmology, advance their knowledge and understanding of the field, and develop their skills in order to become successful future ophthalmologists. We strive to create a supportive, inspiring and inclusive community of peers to share experiences, discuss the latest advancements, and provide mentorship and guidance. We are committed to advocating for ophthalmology and inspiring others to pursue their dreams of becoming a part of this vibrant and growing field.
summary: The Ophthalmology Interest Group seeks to provide educational and clinical opportunity to medical students in the clinical practice of Ophthalmology and associated fields

name: Lebanese Social Organization
description: LSO is an organization connecting Lebanese students together at Florida State University. It aims to increase awareness of Lebanese culture, customs, traditions, history and language.

name: International Association of Emergency Managers - USA Student Chapter at FSU
description:
The International Association of Emergency Managers (IAEM) is a non-profit educational organization dedicated to promoting the goals of saving lives and protecting property during emergencies and disasters.  IAEM Student Chapters are an essential part of the organization as they help prepare today's students to be tomorrow's leaders in the field.

 

The FSU Student Chapter strives to promote community awareness through practical education and the dissemination of information on the field of Emergency Management.  Some of the many opportunities available to members include:

  1. Assistance with finding internships and volunteer experiences.
  2. Professional development and networking opportunities.
  3. Engaging fellow students in personal emergency preparedness.
  4. Assisting FSU Emergency Management and other stakeholders in our constant growth as a disaster resistant campus community.

IAEM's main site: https://www.iaem.org/

We welcome new members all year long!  Contact us today at iaematfsu@gmail.com for more information.
summary: The International Association of Emergency Managers (IAEM) is the preeminent professional development organization for those studying or working in the field of Emergency Management.

name: Chabad Students at FSU
description:
Here at Chabad at FSU, we pride ourselves on creating a "home away from home" for Jewish students at Florida State University and the surrounding colleges.  We serve thousands of Jewish college students, providing them with a wide array of unique and innovative programs helping to create a strong and vibrant Jewish community on campus.

 

We invite you to come and visit us and see first - hand the warmth and Jewish pride your child experiences at Chabad at FSU. We would be delighted for you to join us at one of our weekly Shabbat dinners. It's an experience that will stay with you... forever.

 

Through our diverse and dynamic programming, we offer everything from weekly Shabbat dinners to engaging Jewish educational opportunities. We are proud to say that we are able to reach many under-involved Jewish students and inspire a positive connection to Judaism and Israel.

 

In addition, Chabad’s campus family, Rabbi Schneur and Chanie Oirechman open their home to hundreds of students every week, welcoming them with the warmth of family. Their meaningful relationships with a broad spectrum of students are testimony to the Oirechmans personalities and commitment.

 

Chabad at FSU deepens Jewish students' connection to their heritage and to their future by developing their leadership skills, strengthening and revitalizing our entire Jewish community.

 

And, as always, we provide students with a wide array of award-winning programs that touch every aspect of their lives.
summary: Here at Chabad at FSU, we pride ourselves on being a "home away from home" for Jewish students at Florida State University and the surrounding colleges.
comment: Organization has not completed the recognition process for 2010-2011.

name: World Affairs Program and Model United Nations Team
description:
The World Affairs Program is a student-run academic program within the College of Social Sciences, which gives the students of FSU a social, academic, and competitive outlet in an educational setting. Membership in the organization is both free and open to students of all majors and levels. 

 

The primary mission of the World Affairs Program (WAP) is to promote an increased awareness of the global community, while at the same time encouraging personal, social, and political growth among those who participate in the program. Common ways of achieving that mission include, but are not limited to, the following:

  • Model United Nations Conference Simulations - the FSU Model United Nations (MUN) traveling team is ranked top ten in the country for overall performance, above universities such as Yale and Harvard. As a MUN delegate, you will have an opportunity to travel around the country at a subsidized cost to simulate United Nations and crisis body debates. If you are interested in making a travel team next year, please come to one of our MUN interest meetings on either August 27th, September 1st, or September 2nd of 2020.
  • Mentor Programs - through the WAP mentor-mentee program, members interested in affiliated career fields or organizations on campus can be paired with an upperclassmen with similar interests. Through a series of group activities and individual meetings, underclassmen can gain mentorship and guidance from their mentor.
  • Speakers - at our general body meetings, distinguished speakers such as professors and government officials may address the club and encourage group discussion.
  • Travel - if you are a member of one of our MUN traveling teams, you will get a chance to travel around the world for a subsidized price. This year, our program is planning to attend MUN conferences in Chicago, IL, San Francisco, CA, Washington D.C., Atlanta, GA, and much much more!

Up-to-date information on general body meetings, conferences, and trainings, as well as other general information on our program, can be found on our Facebook and Instagram @fsuworldaffairs.
summary: The World Affairs Program is a student-run academic program within the College of Social Sciences. WAP gives the students of FSU a social, academic, and competitive outlet in an educational setting; it promotes personal and professional development.

name: South Asian Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to create a safe space where individuals can learn about and celebrate South Asian cultures. We hope to provide “a home away from home” to individuals from South Asia where they can participate in their traditions, beliefs, cultures, practiced languages, and more. We are an inclusive organization, so we individuals who may not particularly be South Asian, and simply want to learn more about our cultures!

name: Club Downunder/Union Productions
description:
What is Club Downunder/Union Productions?

Union Productions is FSU’s student-run programming board! Over fifty students oversee every aspect of the organization: from planning, producing events, marketing, and promotions, to sound production and hospitality. This means that a dedicated group of FSU undergraduates orchestrates everything that goes into making Union Productions’ events fantastic — from booking performers, to setting up dressing rooms, to coordinating audio-visual requests, to advertising lineups.

Mission Statement

Union Productions provides captivating, inclusive and enriching entertainment for Florida State University. To foster student leadership and professional development, we empower students to cultivate their own events, ranging from concerts, comedy and lectures to special events, all of which are student-run from inception to completion.

What is Club Downunder?

Club Downunder is FSU's very own on-campus entertainment venue! Named one of Consequence of Sound's 100 Greatest American Music Venues in 2016, Club Downunder has been around for decades in this location and its stage has been host to many great names in entertainment history. At Club Downunder, you will find indie, punk, hip-hop, dance, drag, jazz, improv, standup, and everything in between. Club Downunder is always free for FSU students, so be sure to keep an eye out for upcoming events.

 

A Brief History
In the mid-’60s the first student-run programming board at Florida State University was created, the Union Program Council (UPC). Around 1970 the board changed its name to the Leisure Programs Office (LPO), and the name changed again in 1979 to the Union Program Office (UPO). Finally, in 1983, the organization changed its name to Student Campus Entertainment (SCE) and remained that way until 2005. Over the years the organization has been responsible been responsible for planning PowWow, the films series (in Moore Auditorium), major concerts in The Moon and Civic Center, the operation of the Club Downunder, late night programming initiatives, and many different special events.

As the organization has grew and as new facilities on campus were created, a few of those responsibilities changed (i.e. the Student Life Building programming the film series in its state-of-the-art theater in 2000, the Club Downunder becoming its own entity to plan club concerts in 2001, and PowWow becoming it’s own organization as well in 1999). In the fall of 2005, the Club Downunder programming board and SCE were joined together once again to form Union Productions, one programming board responsible for all special events and concerts planned by the Oglesby Union.

Weekly Programming Meetings
Everyone is welcome to come to discuss potential Special Events at our weekly Programming Meetings.

Meetings will resume in the Fall with meeting times and locations to be determined soon. For more information about programming meetings, email upprogramming@admin.fsu.edu

Annual Intern All Call

At the beginning of every Fall semester, we hold our annual Intern All Call! Interning with Union Productions is a great way to get involved with our unique organization. Positions in programming, hospitality, PR, sound production, and event staff will provide you with interactive exposure to a fascinating industry.

Dates and times for our Intern All Call will be announced at the beginning of the Fall semester. If you would like to find out more about interning with Union Productions, email upoperations@fsu.edu.

How to Utilize Club Downunder

Are you an RA, Campus Leader, or a member of an RSO, or SGA Agency? Would you like to know how to best utilize all Club Downunder has to offer? Fill out the Co-sponsorship form. We will do our best to work with your organization. Financial support is limited but use Club Downunder is FREE for co-sponsored events. To reserve the Club Downunder venue for an event that is not co-sposored with our organization, fill out this form with Campus Event Services. 

QUESTIONS: Email upcosponsor@admin.fsu.edu

Club Downunder is located in the Lower Level of the Student Union.
summary: Union Productions is FSU’s student-run programming board! We put on 130+ events a year including Concerts, Comedians, Special Events, Lectures, and much more at FSU's very own Club Downunder!

name: Crenshaw Lanes
description:
Bowling and Billiards have been a tradition at FSU for almost 60 years. Crenshaw Lanes, a twelve-lane bowling center located in the Oglesby Union opened in 1964 and operated until 2018.  Bowling returned to campus with the opening of the Union Bowling and Billiards Center in the new FSU Student Union starting the fall of 2022.

Programs include several bowling leagues, billiards tournaments, intramural bowling, parties, cosmic bowling, late night programming and open bowling. The Bowling and Billiards Center is a popular party spot for student and university groups.

They also serve as the home to the FSU Bowling Team. The bowling team represents FSU in intercollegiate bowling competition and has established itself as one of the elite club programs in the country.
summary: The Student Union Bowling and Billiard Center features 12 lanes of bowling and 8 full sized Brunswick Billiards Tables.

name: Student Union Art Center
description:
The Art Center is located in the ASLC lobby where the Grindhouse used to be. We are exclusively offering Paint-A-Pot at this time, both in person and to go.

Interested in having Paint-A-Pot at your next meeting or event? Click here for our group registration form!

Please see our website and social media (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter) for up-to-date hours of operation.

The FSU Student Union Art Center is located on Florida State University’s campus. We provide a number of creative outlets to FSU students and the public, including:

  • Art Classes are offered to everyone with a discounted rate for FSU students. No experience necessary, our instructors will teach you from the ground up. Courses range from wheel throwing to drawing to painting. For more details and course schedules, click here. Art classes will not be offered until the New Union building is completed, we apologize for any inconvenience.

  • Paint-A-Pot is a popular pastime that anyone can enjoy. Choose from a variety of pre-made ceramic pieces starting at just $2. Paint your piece and we’ll fire it so it’s ready for pick-up. No pre-registration needed, stop in and create something great today. Want to skip the firing process? Ask us about our acrylic paint options instead. Acrylic paints work great for Paint-A-Pot To Go!

  • Painting Express is a canvas painting class with a laid-back atmosphere. Live, step-by-step instruction will be given so participants can walk away with their very own work of art. All materials are provided. We are unable to offer Painting Express at this time, we apologize for any inconvenience.

  • The Frame Shop provides custom framing at an unbeatable price. Need to spruce up your dorm, office, or living space? Want to give a thoughtful gift? We’ve got you covered. Drop in to see our extensive selection and talk with a staff member who can help you find a frame that fits your needs. The Frame Shop will be closed until the New Union building is completed, we apologize for any inconvenience.

  • Private Events are always welcome. Choose from either Paint-a-Pot or Painting Express for your group. This is a great option for birthdays, sororities, or student organizations. See website for details or call today to schedule your next event with us! We are unable to offer Painting Express at this time, we apologize for any inconvenience.

(Updated 6/22/21)
summary: The Student Union Art Center is temporarily located in the ASLC Grindhouse and exclusively offering Paint-A-Pot at this time. See our website for information on private group reservations.

name: Sailing Club at FSU
description:
An association of students at Florida State passionate about sailing. We cover all aspects of sailing from teaching the basics to competitive racing. No experience is required to join our recreational club; we teach beginners how to sail. We compete as a part of the South Atlantic Conference (SAISA) of the Intercollegiate Sailing Association (ICSA), sailing against schools across the nation. We currently have a fleet of twelve 420s which we sail at Lake Bradford, more commonly known as the FSU Lakefront Park. Follow our Instagram @fsusailingclub to stay up to date with the club and team!

For all information about the club such as dues, meetings, and events please refer to our Instagram. If you are interested in joining, you can contact our officers through the Instagram or email fsusailing@gmail.com.
summary: An association of students at Florida State dedicated to recreational and competitive sailing. No experience is required to join our recreational club.

name: Leadership, Education, And Research Network
description:
We are an affiliation of students drawn from across Florida State University's Educational Leadership & Policy program, dedicated to sharing research ideas and supporting each others' pursuits in the field of education.

LEARN facilitates the exchange of ideas, knowledge, support, and professional development across the FSU campus. Recognizing that intercollegiate collaboration enables members to broaden their perspective on the educational issues that interest them, we seek to provide a forum for intellectual exchange and support, as well as identify events occurring across campus that may further broaden the scope of perspectives we bring to our own research.
summary: We are an affiliation of students drawn from across Florida State University's Educational Leadership & Policy program, dedicated to sharing research ideas and supporting each others' pursuits in the field of education.

name: PRIDE in Healthcare (Promoting Recognition of Identity, Dignity, & Equality)
description:
The mission of PRIDE in Healthcare Organization (Promoting Recognition of Identity, Dignity, & Equality)* at FSU College of Medicine is to provide support to LGBTQIA2S+ students and the community in the following areas:

  • Education – increase student and public knowledge of lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) healthcare concerns
  • Service – volunteer time to organizations committed to advancing the causes of the LGBT community
  • Advocacy – promote and campaign for equality in healthcare, employment, and family matters
  • Collaboration – work together with LGBT groups in other fields of study to further LGBT issues
  • Mentorship – offer support and mentoring to pre-medical students and LGBT youth
  • Support – provide supportive events with a welcoming and safe environment for all participants

*Previously known as GLAAM (Gays, Lesbians, and Allies Advancing Medicine)
summary: The mission of PRIDE in Healthcare Organization (Promoting Recognition of Identity, Dignity, & Equality) at FSU College of Medicine is to provide support to LGBTQ students and the community, particularly in regards to health and education.

name: Swing Dance Club at Florida State University
description: Group of dance enthusiasts that seek to promote Swing Dancing through social dancing and proper instruction.
summary:
Group of dance enthusiasts that seek to promote Swing Dancing through social dancing and proper instruction.

Please check out our Instagram group where we post most of our information! @swingdancefsu

name: American Marketing Association FSU
description:
The American Marketing Association (AMA) is the world's largest and most comprehensive professional marketing society. Our AMA chapter here at Florida State is currently ranked #4 out of 388 collegiate chapters internationally.

 

Membership within the American Marketing Association provides students the opportunity for professional development, practical marketing experience, and insights into the industry from current marketing professionals.  Along with industry-specific speakers at general body meetings, we also plan local agency tours, corporate travel tours, recruiter socials, and have unique opportunities such as Case Competition and Seminole Solutions. 

 

The Collegiate Case Competition is a semester-long project that our team comes together on to strategize solutions for a detailed marketing problem submitted by a sponsoring organization. AMA members work to develop a marketing strategy in this nationally recognized competition and compete for a chance to present at the International Collegiate Conference in Chicago. 

Seminole Solutions is a unique asset of the AMA as the only student-run marketing agency at Florida State. Our members work with real clients to gain hands-on marketing experience in an agency that most college students don’t have going into their first interviews. 

 

In addition to the professional side of the organization, the AMA also works to serve as a community for its members to support, encourage, and become resources for one another. We actively hold socials and encourage networking at AMA events. 

 

Interested in joining the American Marketing Association? DM us on Instagram @ama_fsu for more information or to find out the next steps you can take to join! You can also email us at floridastateama@gmail.com. \We are open to all majors.
summary: The American Marketing Association is the world's largest and most comprehensive professional marketing society. Membership within AMA provides opportunities for professional development, marketing experience, and insights from current professionals.

name: Undergraduate American Medical Women’s Association
description: AMWA at FSU is affiliated with AMWA nationals, our mission is to promote excellence in medical communication and to provide educational resources in support of that goal. AMWA strives to make a difference in Tallahassee through volunteer work with various organizations. We make it our goal every year to recruit new female members going into various pre-health fields (physician, physician assistant, nursing, physical therapy, dentistry, chiropractic, exercise physiology, dietetics, etc.) to join our organization. We believe it's important to educate our members on various pre-health career options, as well as promoting a healthy lifestyle that includes good study habits & general well-being in order to persevere through the stressful times of being a pre-health undergraduate. We pride ourselves on being a welcoming organization with friendly & intelligent members that will someday be our next health professionals.
summary: In this chapter of AMWA, we provide a support network for women going into health related fields. AMWA is a space for women to develop new skills, volunteer, participate in pre-professional activities, and network with like-minded people.

name: Network of enlightened Women
description:
NeW is the nation's premier club for conservative university women.  Started as a book club at the University of Virginia in 2004, NeW aims to cultivate a community of conservative women and expand intellectual diversity on university campuses through its focus on education.  NeW members meet regularly to discuss issues relating to politics, gender and conservative principles.  NeW has expanded to over 20 colleges nationwide.  We hope to connect women to opportunities to further their political career.

 

The ladies involved with Network of enlightened Women's (NeW) Florida State chapter meet regularly to discuss current women's issues such as abortion, domestic violence, feminism, and the hook-up culture. We strive to maintain a positive influence on our campus and community through volunteer activities. Come find out just what being a conservative woman is all about.
summary: NeW is the nation's premier club for conservative university women. Started as a book club at the University of Virginia in 2004, NeW aims to cultivate a community of conservative women and expand intellectual diversity.

name: Alpha Mu Alpha
description: The mission of Alpha Mu Alpha is to enrich Music Therapy students' academic experience with opportunities to professionally network with peers and professionals, provide scholarships for conference attendance, prepare students for academic requirements and to promote camaraderie within the major.  We also provide opportunities to perform community service and promote music therapy awareness in our community.
summary: To enrich music therapy students' academic experience with opportunities to professionally network with peers and professionals, provide scholarships for conference attendance, prepare students for academic requirements and to promote camaraderie.

name: Internal Medicine Interest Group
description: The purpose of the IMIG is to educate and promote Internal Medicine at Florida State University. This will be done primarily via meetings and events in which internists, residents, and students engage in discussion and provide information concerning current issues in this specialty.
summary: The purpose of the IMIG is to educate and promote Internal Medicine at Florida State University (FSU). This will be done primarily via meetings in which internists and residents participate in discussions and provide information concerning this field.

name: The Student Foundation at Florida State University
description: The mission of the Florida State University Student Foundation is focused on fostering a spirit of philanthropy and excellence as well as educating the student body on how the university operates and why it is important to give back. Furthermore, it will also be responsible for raising, investing, managing and distributing funds for the further advancement of The Florida State University.
summary: The mission of the Florida State University Student Foundation is focused on fostering a spirit of philanthropy and excellence as well as educating the student body on how the university operates and why it is important to give back.

name: Alpha Phi
description: Alpha Phi is a sorority that focuses on developing its members in the areas of sisterhood, scholarship, service, leadership, intellectual development, and character development.
summary: Alpha Phi is a sisterhood of outstanding women supporting one another in lifelong achievement.

name: Alpha Delta Phi
description:
Alpha Delta Phi  is a Greek-letter social college fraternity and the fourth-oldest continuous Greek-letter fraternity in the United States and Canada. Alpha Delta Phi was founded on October 29, 1832 by Samuel Eells and includes former U.S. Presidents, Chief Justices of the U.S. Supreme Court and U.S. Senators among its 50,000+ alumni. The Fraternity and the Society are both derived from Eells's vision for a "literary society," with each chapter upholding its literary tradition.

6.2.6
summary: Alpha Delta Phi is a Greek-letter social college fraternity and the fourth-oldest continuous Greek-letter fraternity in the United States and Canada.

name: Psychiatry Student Interest Group
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to enhance the education of the students and faculty at Florida State College of Medicine to the psychological and psychiatric issues which pertain to our future patients. To this end, we encourage an attempt at vigorous unbiased and friendly discussion on this topic including outside lecturers and debates as to be determined by the executive committee, and when appropriate, by general election. Additionally, we will host service opportunities involving psychiatric issues.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to enhance the education of the students and faculty at Florida State College of Medicine to the psychological and psychiatric issues which pertain to our future patients.

name: Turkish Student Association
description:
The purpose of TSA is:

  • To develop projects and activities to create awareness of Turkish culture, art, and history through talks, cultural events, and gatherings at FSU.
  • To promote social relations and cooperation among the members of the Turkish community in Tallahassee.
  • To help new students arriving from Turkey to better incorporate into the system of FSU.
summary: The purpose of TSA is to develop projects and activities to create awareness of Turkish culture, art, and history through talks, cultural events, and gatherings at FSU.

name: Biomedical Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: The FSU COM Biomedical Sciences Graduate Student Association is an organization of students, post-doctoral professionals, and faculty representatives that seeks to provide a forum for professional development in which members can gain invaluable knowledge to advance their scientific careers. We provide educational workshops, seminars by leading scientists, and an open environment for members to present ideas and receive feedback from fellow scholars. We believe that outreach and sharing ideas among members and colleagues will elicit positive collaborations and communication among scientists in their present and future endeavors. Creating positive, long-lasting working relationships and connections among fellow researchers allows us to broaden our impact and are vital to our future careers in science.
summary: We provide a forum for professional development where members gain knowledge to advance their scientific careers through workshops, seminars, and an open environment to present ideas and receive feedback from fellow scholars.

name: The Bass Fishing Club at Florida State University
description:
The Bass Fishing Club at FSU offers students the opportunity to compete in sanctioned collegiate bass fishing events alongside top programs nationwide.

 

Our organization is open to all FSU students and provides a structured environment for those passionate about fishing, conservation, and the outdoors. The club is designed for anglers who want to compete, and members are expected to have an interest in and understanding of tournament-fishing.

 

Interested students should contact our Instagram: @fsubassfishing
summary: The Bass Fishing Club at Florida State University brings together students who are passionate about bass fishing, competition, and conservation. The club offers members an opportunity to fish in national collegiate level tournaments

name: Light the Night at Florida State University
description:
We cannot approve membership through this medium. If you wish to get involved with our organization, please message us via DM on our Instagram @ltnfsu, check the linktree in our bio, and follow for updated information!

Light the Night at Florida State University is an organization dedicated to spreading awareness and advocating on behalf of those whose lives have been forever touched by blood cancers, such as leukemia, myeloma, and lymphoma. The funds raised by the Tallahassee community help fund lifesaving research, advocacy, and support for blood cancer patients and their families. As an organization, we plan the annual Light the Night walk every fall, which is one of the fundraisers that raises money towards these important causes through the Leukemia and Lymphoma Society.

At the event, friends, families, schools, corporate teams, and sponsors join together to celebrate, honor, and remember those touched by blood cancers. This is a volunteer-led event, organized by a large group of passionate volunteers who make up our executive board. As the only fully student-run chapter of LTN in the entire country, we are proud to bring our energy and dedication to support the mission of the Leukemia and Lymphoma Society.
summary: This organization is dedicated to spreading awareness and advocating on behalf of those whose lives have been forever touched by blood cancers, such as leukemia, myeloma, and lymphoma. Our organization plans the annual Tallahassee Light The Night Walk.

name: College of Communication & Information Student Leadership Council
description:
The College of Communication & Information Student Leadership Council will serve as a liaison group between the student body, the Dean of the College and the alumni of the College of Communication and Information and the Florida State University.  Additionally the Student Leadership Council will help cultivate alumni engagement from each and school and major within the college. CCI-SLC will facilitate various activities, meetings, workshops and other events to improve unity among the 3 colleges while working to provide students with professional development and networking opportunities.

All activities and functions of the College of Communication & Information Student Leadership Council shall be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.
summary: The College of Communication & Information Student Leadership Council will serve as a liaison group between the student body, the Dean of the College and the alumni of the College of Communication and Information and the Florida State University.

name: Action Shooting Club at Florida State University
description:
ABOUT THE CLUB

The Action Shooting Club at Florida State University promotes safe firearm handling techniques through shooting sports. Ranked as one of the top collegiate teams in the country, the Club competes in various shooting sports including SASP, USPSA, and IDPA. Club firearms and ammunition will be provided for use during practices and team guns will be provided during competitive matches. Club practices will focus on firearms fundamentals, safe weapons handling, competitive shooting techniques, and will utilize both paper and steel targets.

Admission to the Club is open to all experience levels. Members of the Action Shooting Club are also eligible to receive discounts with many of our amazing sponsors within the firearms industry.

Follow us on Instagram @fsufast to receive updates on our practice schedule and club info!

QUICK FACTS

  • Home Field & Practice Space: Talon Range located in Midway, FL
  • Practice Times: Sundays at 11am with additional practices to be added based on competition schedule and interest.
  • Regular Opponents: The University of Florida, Clemson University, Texas A&M University, West Point Military Academy, Hillsdale College, and the University of Arizona.
summary:
The Action Shooting Club at Florida State University is an organization that brings together students interested in both recreational and competitive shooting.

Check out our page at instagram.com/fsufast/

name: Skeet and Trap Club at Florida State University
description:
The Skeet and Trap Club is primarily a competitive shotgun shooting team that competes in American Skeet, American Trap, Sporting Clays, Super Sporting Clays, Doubles Skeet and Doubles Trap. However, recreational shooters are also welcome; this club is open to all FSU students and staff/faculty, both men and women, who enjoy clay target shooting. 

No prior experience with shotguns or clay target shooting is necessary to join. We provide fun and safe opportunities for members who have never fired a shotgun before, intermediate shooters, and the most advanced shooters. Our main goal is to exhibit and promote firearm safety while enjoying our favorite sport!

Each semester the club holds an open shoot where we encourage prospective members to come meet the team, try your hand at clay target shooting, and hopefully decide to join us! For information about the next upcoming open shoot, don't hesitate to use the contact button above or send us an e-mail at FSUSkeetAndTrap@gmail.com. We look forward to seeing you out there!
summary: The Skeet and Trap Team at FSU is a Sport Club open to all FSU students/faculty who would like to target shoot competitively or for fun. No experience necessary; we have opportunities for those who have never fired a shotgun to the most advanced shooters.

name: Venezuelan Student Association
description:
The purpose of the Venezuelan Student Association at the Florida State University is to promote unity among all Venezuelans, to share our culture with everyone in Tallahassee, to guide newly-arrived Venezuelan students in their process of cultural and social adaptation and to increase the general awareness of current events in Venezuela.

All activities and functions of the Venezuelan Student Association shall be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.
summary: The purpose of the Venezuelan Student Association at the Florida State University is to promote unity among all Venezuelans, to share our culture with everyone in Tallahasse, and to guide newly-arrived Venezuelan students in their process of cultural-etc

name: The Trumpet Guild of Florida State University
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be to:

  1. Unite trumpet players and enthusiasts for the continued development of musical and educational growth.
  2. Promote and encourage interest and study of trumpet on our campus and in the community
  3. Sponsor and promote musical productions: recitals, master classes, concerts. All in which the trumpet is featured. 
  4. Create and foster an environment of diversity and inclusion for members of marginalized groups. 
  5. Finally, to make music accessible to all students at Florida State University, and beyond.

All activities and functions shall be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.
summary: The mission of the Trumpet Guild at Florida State University is to foster community and musical excellence for trumpet players across the Florida State campus.

name: Military and Veteran Medicine Interest Group
description: Our mission is to initiate dialogue within the FSU College of Medicine which not only illuminate medical and research opportunities with the military, but also provide lessons on leadership and health spanning a wide array of professions, both within and outside of medicine.
summary: Our mission is to initiate dialogue within the FSU College of Medicine which not only illuminate opportunities in the military, but also provide lessons on leadership and health spanning a wide array of professions, both within and outside of medicine.

name: The Film Club at FSU
description:
The Film Club at FSU is dedicated to providing a fun, professional organization for students to participate in creating and discussing film and other digital media content. During our weekly meetings, Film Club teaches various filmmaking techniques and engages in discussing and analyzing films of all types. Whether you're a fervent film buff or a casual fan, Film Club is dedicated to providing a fun, welcoming community for all students.

Prospective members are highly encouraged to join our Discord server, where members chat and officers release information about future meetings and events: https://discord.gg/N5QKzXqSa8

For more information and discussion on the Film Club at FSU please follow our Instagram page: @filmclubfsu

If you have any questions, contact our 2025-2026 co-president Aidan Barry (akb22b@fsu.edu)
summary: The Film Club at FSU is dedicated to providing a fun, professional organization for students seeking to create and discuss films of all types!

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
description:
Sigma Phi Epsilon (SigEp) was founded on November 1st, 1901 on the campus of Richmond College (now called the University of Richmond) with the intention of developing balanced men through the ideals of virtue, diligence, and brotherly love. The men of Sigma Phi Epsilon consistently exemplify the highest level of academic, social, and physical prowess in all aspects of life. Together, we place opportunity before each member in order to further his own personal development – not only for his college career but for his life beyond it.
