Throughout the decades, brothers of Sigma Phi Epsilon have practiced their values and embraced the stated desire of the Founders, “This Fraternity will be different.”

• 313,000+ lifetime members rank SigEp among the top 3 largest fraternities in the nation.

• Over the past two decades, SigEp has recruited more men than any other collegiate fraternity.

• SigEp was the first national fraternity to install a chapter in all 50 United States and today boasts over 340 chapters in total.

• The leader in fraternity leadership cultivation, SigEp hosts numerous leadership academies each year including Ruck Leadership Institute, Carlson Leadership Academy, EDGE, and Tragos Quest to Greece.
summary: The Florida Epsilon Chapter of Sigma Phi Epsilon Fraternity at Florida State University.

name: Asian Pacific American Law Student Association
description: We aim to promote diversity and inclusion, advocate for AAPI leadership within the legal field, increase awareness on concerns of the Asian community and strengthen relationships within and outside of the law school.
summary: APALSA at FSU Law was created to support and advocate for Asian American Pacific Islanders in the legal community and as a whole.

name: Sport Management Student Association
description:
This is organization is based on the Sport Management department .

The purposes of this organization are to:

a.  Foster awareness of the sport management academic field

b.  Provide networking opportunities with professionals in the field as well as other students in the program

c. Develop student leadership skills

d. Participate in community service activities

e. Encourage interaction and fellowship between sport management students, faculty and other university and community groups.
summary: This organization is based on the Sport Management department; it's purpose is to create networking and professional social opportunities within the organization, department, and community.

name: Hugh O'Brian Leadership Student Association
description:
The Hugh O'Brian Leadership Student Association is a service-based organization whose goal is to unite HOBY alumni, friends, and faculty at Florida State University.  We want to inspire the FSU community to get involved in community service.  

 

Wanna know more about HOBY? Watch this video:

watch

Connecting with HOBY Alumni at FSU allows you to:

 

- Discover unique service opportunities at Florida State University.

- Have accountability to reach volunteer hour goals with both FSU ServScript and HOBY.

- Find exciting volunteer opportunities with all four Florida seminars.

 

www.floridahoby.org
summary: The Hugh O'Brian Leadership Student Association is a service-based organization whose goal is to unite HOBY alumni, friends, and faculty at Florida State University.

name: Association for Information Systems
description: AIS is a professional student organization dedicated to promoting better knowledge and understanding of information and technology in organizations. We provide the opportunity to listen, learn, and foster relationships between students and industry professionals. We are dedicated to helping students obtain internships, full-time positions and valuable networks. Our student org welcomes current or potential MIS majors, Business Analytics minors, or anyone interested in the IT industry!
summary: AIS is a professional organization dedicated to promoting better knowledge and understanding of information systems and technology. AIS aids students in career development via career building workshops, regular speaking engagements, and networking.

name: Pre-Veterinary Club at FSU
description:
Pre-Veterinary Club at FSU strives to provide a friendly and sociable environment where currently enrolled students will expand their interests in veterinary medicine and/or in animal related fields. The pre-veterinary club encourages applications towards vet school but is not limited to only pre-vet students. We strongly encourage the enrollment of anyone interested expanding their knowledge with animals or anyone who is looking for opportunities with animals.

The purpose of the Pre-Veterinary Club at Florida State University is to:

~provide an inspirational environment where students can expand their interests in veterinary medicine. 

~build a strong support group among students that are passionate about animals and/or considering a pre-veterinary career! 

~offer fun, influential, and educational opportunities to network and become familiar with animal-related pursuits.
summary: Pre-Veterinary Club at FSU strives to provide an environment where students can expand their interests in veterinary medicine and/or animal related fields.

name: Camp Kesem at FSU
description: Camp Kesem is a national nonprofit organization that provides a free week of camp to kids with a parent affected by cancer.  Students will be responsible for planning camp programs, recruiting campers from the community, fundraising, and reaching out to our Florida State campus! As stated, we are a chapter of a national organization, and there are over 100 chapters (different schools) involved! Camp Kesem FSU works to create a fun environment while also allowing the opportunity to show your leadership skills! This can be done by joining a committee, getting involved on our coordinator board, or showing leadership at camp, if chosen as a counselor! We have committee meetings every other week, and general body meetings every two weeks. We also have multiple fundraisers, socials,  and other events to attend throughout the semester! We hope you all become a part of our CK FSU Family!
summary: Love Kids? Hate Cancer? Camp Kesem is a national nonprofit organization that provides a free week of camp to kids with a parent affected by cancer! We are the Florida State University Chapter of Camp Kesem!

name: Kappa Kappa Psi Gamma Nu
description: The Gamma Nu Chapter of Kappa Kappa Psi - National Honorary Band Fraternity
summary: The Gamma Nu Chapter of Kappa Kappa Psi - National Honorary Band Fraternity

name: Florida Rural Health Student Association
description:
The purpose of this organization is to promote healthcare to rural citizens of the state of Florida in both policy and practice. It also gives students the opportunity to become affiliated with rural health organizations in Florida, provides knowledge of legislative issues pertaining to rural health, and serves rural communities in health related events.

     The FSU College of Medicine FRHA strives to educate medical students on the benefits and challenges of healthcare in the rural setting. The organization also helps introduce students from urban areas to rural culture. The addition of the Medical Student Chapter of the FRHA was a natural one, as the organization promotes the rural service mission of the FSU College of Medicine.
summary: FRHA promotes healthcare to rural citizens of Florida, affiliates students with Florida rural health organizations, provides knowledge of legislative issues pertaining to rural health, and serves rural communities in health related events.

name: Homecoming
description:
Find the full list of Homecoming events and how to get involved at homecoming.fsu.edu!

 

Florida State Homecoming’s purpose and mission are to unite and bring together Alumni, the student body, and Florida State fans. It is a week-long celebration held on campus yearly to remind our community of the love and spirit that the Florida state family has. Traditionally held in the fall during a home football game, a variety of events are put on to celebrate the school's success and progress. Homecoming is one of the oldest traditions at our University with the first one being held in 1948. It is hosted by the Homecoming Council and Alumni Association to bring together both past, present, and future students. Our newest event, Homecoming Live, is an annual pep rally that includes student performers, Chief and Princess reveal, and so much more! The week of Homecoming includes other events such as Spirit Night, Creative Event, Odds and Evens, Festival and many more! You can learn more about these events on our Instagram and Twitter @fsuhomecoming or on our website, homecoming.fsu.edu. Events such as the Parade and the Homecoming football game are open to all members of the community. These events are an important tradition at the school and are important moments in the FSU experience for many students, staff, and alumni. 

 

Overall Director - Nicole Karwowski 

Overall Assistant Director - Cali Berenis 

Homecoming Live Director - Sarai Francis 

Contact: fsuhcoveralldirectors@gmail.com or fsuhclive@gmail.com
summary:
Homecoming is a week of events designed to welcome back alumni and pump up school spirit!  homecoming.fsu.edu

Overall Director - Nicole Karwowski 
Overall Assistant Director - Cali Berenis 
Contact: fsuhcoveralldirectors@gmail.com

name: Lambda Pi Eta Communications and Information Honor Society at the Florida State University
description:
Lambda Pi Eta is an esteemed, honor society associated with the National Communication Association. The purposes of Lambda Pi Eta are: (1) to foster and reward outstanding scholastic achievement in communication; (2) to stimulate interest in the field of communication through community outreach and service; (3) to promote and encourage professional development among communication majors; (4) to provide an opportunity to discuss and exchange ideas in the discipline of communication; (5) to establish and maintain closer relationships and mutual understanding between communication faculty and students; and (6) to explore options for graduate education in communication.

 

Membership requirements include:

  • 3.0 overall GPA
  • 3.25 Major GPA
  • Must have completed at least 12 hours in the School of Communication 
  • Must have completed at least 60 hours overall
  • Currently enrolled as a student in good standing, as per FSU's policies
summary: Lambda Pi Eta National Communication and Information Honor Society at Florida State University is designed to honor those students who excel in their communication programs. We are determined to distinguish and better prepare them for the future.

name: Business Law Society
description: The Business Law Society (BLS) is dedicated to promoting academic and social interaction among students interested in the various aspects of business law. The BLS encourages interdisciplinary study of business, corporate, and financial law, as well as provides its members with unique opportunities to network in business and legal communities.
summary: The Business Law Society (BLS) is dedicated to promoting academic and social interaction among students interested in the various aspects of business law.

name: Public Administration Graduate Association at FSU
description:
Statement of Purpose - The Public Administration Graduate Association exists to enhance the educational experiences of MPA and Ph.D. students of the Askew School by fostering communication among professionals, alumni, practitioners, faculty and students while providing services that assist student development both educationally and personally.

 

Objectives:

  • Provide a forum through which students exchange information, network, and gain support from other students in an informal setting
  • Serve as a means for student socialization and recreation
  • Provide networking opportunities for students to connect with local alumni
  • Seek out and provide information on available internships, fellowships and assistantships
  • Assist with orientation services for incoming students
  • Encourage academic publishing activities by students
  • Provide a list of conferences open to students and assist students in obtaining the means to attend
  • Facilitate students' development of public administration job-related skills
  • Represent the student perspective on various faculty committees
summary: Public Administration Graduate Association at FSU exists to enhance the educational experiences of MPA and Phd students attending the Askew School of Public Administration and Policy.

name: The Dance Project at FSU
description: The Dance Project at FSU is a service organization where members teach free dance classes for children in local Tallahassee schools. Our goal is to provide children in Tallahassee with the opportunity to dance through choreography and freestyle. Our organization allows members to build leadership skills, interact with children, and meet other members through service and dance social events, all while earning service hours. We believe everyone deserves the chance to dance!
summary: The Dance Project allows members to teach free dance classes for children in Tallahassee schools. Our goal is to provide children with the opportunity to dance. Our organization allows members to build leadership skills, meet others, & earn service hours

name: Student Affiliates in School Psychology
description: The purpose of SASP shall be to present FSU school psychology graduate students as a unified body in order to discuss issues and concerns that affect students, both professionally and academically; to provide opportunities to develop and nurture leadership skills; to allow student mentorship and occasions for sharing relevant resources; to foster professionalism during training; to institute a means to affect changes within the department/program; to sponsor speakers and extracurricular training; to establish links with professional organizations and school psychology professionals in the community; and to afford networking opportunities for school psychology graduate students.
summary: SASP is a graduate student organization comprised of the students of the school psychology program at the Florida State University.

name: Environmental Service Program
description:
The Environmental Service Program fosters a strong sense of place, spreading knowledge and concern for local environmental problems in the North Florida area.

ESP aims to engage students and community members with the environment around them using education, service, and advocacy. We focus our activities on Leon, Jefferson, Wakulla, and Taylor counties through conducting educational service field trips and hosting speakers on pertinent environmental topics. We also encourage sustainable lifestyles that connect humans with the environment. ESP assists with petitions, letter-writing campaigns, and organizing social activism to raise awareness and achieve our goals. We take an active approach, empowering students to take action on important issues they feel strongly about as well as volunteering for causes.

All activities and functions of the Environmental Service Program shall be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.
summary: ESP aims to engage students and community members with the environment around them through education, service, and advocacy. We focus our activities on the unique ecosystems and needs of the region surrounding Tallahassee.

name: Student Life Cinema
description: Florida State University's Student Life Cinema (located in the Askew Student Life Center) is one of the nation's leading campus movie programs, featuring five to six nights a week of everything from the most recent blockbuster movies to documentaries, indies, and foreign films, to restored cinema classics. Our movies are selected by an all-student committee and are free to all currently enrolled FSU students with FSUCard unless otherwise noted, and $5 for general admission.
summary: Our movies are selected by an all-student committee and are free to all currently enrolled FSU students with FSUCard unless otherwise noted, and $5 for general admission.

name: ASLC Film Committee
description: The Film Committee is made up of students who select the diverse lineup of films playing at the ASLC. They organize special events, help maintain crowd control, and select films to both educate and entertain the FSU Student Body.
summary: The Film Committee is made up of students who select the diverse lineup of films playing Wednesday through Saturday at the SLC. They organize special events, help maintain crowd control, and select films to both educate and entertain the FSU Student Body

name: ASLC Game Committee
description:
Game Committee is a group that creates unique gaming events that take place at the Askew Student Life Center. They also select the games available for rental from the ASLC Cyber Café. The building has an array of gaming PC's with several titles available for use such as League of Legends, Overwatch, and Counter Strike, as well as several gaming consoles from different generations. Our main focus is planning several gaming socials each month to take place in the Cyber Café, main theater, and around the ASLC.

Game Committee has also helped the Child's Play Charity by hosting an annual Nole's Play for Child's Play Fundraiser.

If gaming is your hobby and you'd like to have some fun playing games here on campus, come to one of our meetings! Game Committee meets every Friday from 4-5PM in the Askew Student Life Center Programming Office to discuss gaming and plan events for the semester. New members are always welcome!

To learn more about upcoming events and updates, join our Discord: Game Committee Discord

https://discord.gg/BWqHr9a8Uv
summary: Game Committee creates and organizes all of the gaming events that take place at the Askew Student Life Center. They also select the games available for rental in the ASLC Cyber Café including console systems, board games, and PC library.

name: Diverse World Fashion Magazine
description: Since our first publication in 2012, one of the main focuses of DWF Magazine has continued to be highlighting the diversity of our student-body on campus by embracing how different cultures embody fashion trends and movements. By creating a space for students of all majors to gain hands-on experience working in various roles within a fashion publication, DWF presents the opportunity to thrive in a creative, fashion-forward, and team-focused environment.
summary: The focus of our organization is to highlight the diversity of our student-body on campus by embracing how different cultures embody current fashion trends and movements.

name: The Esports Club at FSU
description: At Esports at FSU, our purpose is to cultivate an inclusive and vibrant community for individuals from diverse backgrounds who share a profound passion for video games. Whether you aspire to embark on a professional career in gaming or simply seek to connect with like-minded enthusiasts, Esports at FSU is dedicated to serving as a gateway to the world of competitive esports. We warmly welcome all gamers aspiring to leave their mark in the competitive gaming landscape. By joining our community, you will gain access to a nurturing environment where your gaming talents can thrive. We are committed to fostering growth, learning, and the development of your skills to their utmost potential. Our mission is to empower you with the tools and support needed to excel in the esports arena, while fostering strong friendships and a sense of belonging among our members. For more information, check out our Discord or our (temporary) website.
summary: The Esports Club at FSU is home to our school's competitive gaming teams. We compete across many game titles across multiple intercollegiate leagues. We are also open to anyone interested in professional esports in general.

name: Business and Medicine
description: We believe it’s imperative for physicians to be knowledgeable about business and finance, in addition to the science of medicine. Business & Medicine is meant to help students acquire tools and resources to thrive in the health care system.
summary: The Business and Medicine Club will educate our students about the practical and financial aspects of working in the medical field in the 21st century.

name: MEDLIFE Health
description: MEDLIFE is a secular, volunteer-run global health organization whose mission is to help families achieve greater freedom from the constraints of poverty, empowering them to live healthier lives. Our patients did not choose to be poor, but they do choose to strive for a better life. MEDLIFE stands beside them in this pursuit. We seek to achieve this goal through partnering with motivated individuals from poor communities working to improve their access to MEDs: medicine, education, and community development. MEDLIFE believes access to quality healthcare, education, and personal development are basic human rights. To this end, we commit all our resources to bring medicine, education, and development to low-income families everywhere. MEDLIFE at FSU has three divisions: health, education, and community development. Our MEDLIFE Health Chapter focuses on the medical and communal aspects of MEDLIFE. MEDLIFE Health at FSU works with students who are interested in medicine, are interested in pre-health, or simply want to help others.
summary: MEDLIFE is a national organization that stands for Medicine, Education, and Development for Low Income Families Everywhere. MEDLIFE Health at FSU works with students who are interested in medicine, medical mission trips, or simply want to help others.

name: Spotlight Improv Club
description:
Spotlight Improv Club (formerly No Bears Allowed Improv Club)is a performative club focused on 100% improvised comedic storytelling. No scripts and all kindness. Our goal is to foster a community of comedians who uplift each other. We accept newcomers of all experience levels; everyone is always welcome to join.

We have meetings for all Levels (updated for Fall 25) 

     Level 1 - Jams: Tuesdays from 6:30pm - 8:30pm

     Level 2 - Workshops: Thursdays from 6:30pm - 8:30pm 

     - Members chosen through an audition process.

     Level 3 - Performance Team: Sundays from 6:30pm - 8:30pm
     - Members chosen through an audition process.

We have multiple shows per semester and they are super fun and cool you should come see them.

For more information and to stay updated on our practices, upcoming events, and upcoming performances:

Instagram: @spotlightimprov

Discord: https://discord.gg/cUbSCSC9JS

Facebook Group: No Bears Allowed
summary: Spotlight Improv Club is FSU's one and only 100% improvised comedy club.

name: Mexican American Student Association
description:
The name of this organization shall be Mexican American Student Association at the Florida State University. The organization may also refer to itself as “MASA.” MASA shall serve to promote and provide the welfare of the Mexican American student body at Florida State University. It shall function as an educational and cultural learning instrument for the FSU community, promoting Mexico’s positive attributes and rich cultural heritage. MASA shall foster unity by developing a network among Mexican and Mexican American students and encouraging student participation in the promotion of Latin America’s cultural expression.

All activities and functions of the Mexican American Student Association shall be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.
summary: MASA shall serve to promote and provide the welfare of the Mexican American student body at Florida State University.

name: Buddhist Student Association at FSU
description:
The Buddhist Student Association at FSU is a registered student organization at Florida State University.

The purpose of the Buddhist Student Association at FSU is to serve the Florida State University and the surrounding community by organizing Buddhism-related activities on or around the FSU campus.  These events include community service, meditation, camping retreats, community building, and education.  

The BSA will be guided by the following principles: 

  1. Support the study and practice of all authentic Buddhist traditions
  2. Support education about Buddhism from both academic and spiritual perspectives
  3. Promote community service that shows Buddhist ideals in action
  4. Provide the community with appropriate information about upcoming Buddhism-related events and activities         
  5. Encourage exchange between different traditions, cultures, and faiths with respect and acceptance         
  6. Help develop new generations of moral, wise, and compassionate Buddhist leaders of society

*Every Tuesday at 4:45 pm in the Globe we hold guided meditations.*

The first skill meditators learn is how to relax the body gradually while maintaining a correct posture. Then we learn to relax the mind and breath. Once we have a relaxed body, relaxed attitude, and natural breath we give our mind to a method. There are many methods that we explore in Chan/ Zen. There is mindfulness of the breath, silent illumination: awareness all the activities of the body as they arise and pass away, and also counting the breath. These are the methods of concentration used to sharpen and clarify our awareness. The method we use to develop a compassionate state of mind is loving-kindness meditation in which we genuinely wish for ourselves to be happy/ healthy/ safe/ and at peace, our family to be happy/ healthy/ safe/ and at peace, our friends to be happy/ healthy/ safe/ and at peace, neutral people we don't know to be happy/ healthy/ safe/ and at peace, people we dislike to be happy/ healthy/ safe/ and at peace, and lastly sending love to all beings with an open heart. The concentration practices are meant to increase our awareness of different bodily and mental states. Any mental affliction that comes up in the mind can also be understood and diminished through steady practice. The exercises of compassion bring us closer to a state of equal love for all beings and help us learn to send love to categories of people that we are not used to sending love to.

-peace and love

 

Keywords: Buddha, Buddhism, meditation, enlightenment, dharma, sangha, Mahayana, zen, chan, sutra, vipassana, community service, mindfulness, breathing,
summary: The Buddhist Student Association meets weekly to practice meditation and to discuss various topics related to Buddhism, meditation, and personal experiences. Meetings are from 4:45-6:00 pm on Tuesday in room 2500 in the Globe.

name: Pre-Medical American Medical Student Association
description:
If you want a well-rounded, close-knit community to help you through the journey to medical school, look no further! The FSU Pre­-Medical chapter of the American Medical Student Association is the organization for you!

The members of this chapter know that applying to medical school can be a long, comprehensive, and exhausting process. One of the things pre-medical undergraduates excel at is having the ambition and drive to get involved, diving headfirst into opportunities to make a difference, and experience something new. AMSA provides volunteering and shadowing opportunities, as well as valuable workshops with FSU College of Medicine students and faculty.

AMSA members are added to one of four Community Groups. These groups are lead by members of the executive board who plan and execute meetings and workshops to help them on their journey to medical school.

Should you have any questions or concerns, please contact the AMSA President, Connor Krassel via email at cjk21e@fsu.edu
summary: The pre-medical journey can be extremely arduous which is why AMSA aims to provide members which as much insight and preparation as possible. At AMSA you’ll make connections, meet mentors and unlock opportunities you wouldn’t elsewhere.

name: NSSLHA
description: The FSU chapter of NSSHLA, founded in 1972, is designed to benefit its members by providing preparation in becoming professionals in the fields of speech language pathology, speech and hearing science and audiology, as well as students interested in related majors and professional fields. Membership is open to all FSU students, undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral students included. (It is not a requirement to be in the Communication Disorders major.)
summary: The National Student Speech Language Hearning Association (NSSLHA) is a pre-professional organization for undergraduate and graduate students interested in learning about the study of communication sciences and disorders.

name: Students for the Effective Communication of Science
description: The purpose of this organization is to promote and facilitate interactions between the students enrolled in the Molecular Biophysics Graduate Program, as well as to promote wider understanding of the field of Biophysics.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote and facilitate interactions between the students enrolled in the Molecular Biophysics Graduate Program, as well as to promote wider understanding of the field of Biophysics.

name: Panamanian Student Association
description:
Mission:
PTY at FSU is a Panamanian student organization created to promote Panamanian culture and values, thus producing unity among Panamanians and non-Panamanians alike. Panama is a beautiful and unique country that consists of a variety of ethnic backgrounds. Its diversity is a dominant characteristic that is noted and promoted in PTY at FSU. Our goal is to establish a welcoming environment for present and future Panamanian students, as well as the general FSU community. We promote the Panamanian culture through fun and engaging events but we always make time to give back to the community. The Panamanian culture tends to be very family-oriented and as a result, we are exceptionally supportive of each other and always look out for one another. It is because of this that once one is integrated into PTY at FSU, from that point on, we stand together as one, creating ties that will last a lifetime.
summary: The goal of this organization is to promote Panamanian culture and values, create unity among all Panamanians, as well as to provide a welcoming environment for future Panamanian students and general FSU community, while giving back to society.

name: Art Students' League of Florida State University
description: The Art Student's League of Florida State University is a collective of students dedicated to the creation and proliferation of the visual arts in our community, and building members' professional abilities. ASL FSU offers the unique opportunity to participate in, and benefit from, educational resources, networking, and promotion within the art community of Tallahassee.
summary: A body of artists dedicated to building a community and learning professional art practices.

name: Women in Pre-Law Society
description:
The purpose of this organization is to serve as a stepping stone for women on their way to the legal field. This organization shall articulate the needs of the female pre-law student on the campus of the Florida State University and see to it that every possibility of fulfilling that need is examined. The organization shall:

(1) Act as a medium for pre-law women of the Florida State University to openly express their opinions;

(2) Conjugate effective relationships between the female pre-law student, law students, and established legal professionals;

(3) Indoctrinate professional development from the standpoint of attire to the necessities of networking;

(4) Host events and forums related to the advancement of members and the community that are legal under University, local, state, and federal laws;

(5) Expose female pre-law students to the challenges faced by female legal professionals in an attempt to prepare them for their future legal careers.
summary: This organization is a pre-law society geared towards women's advancement in the legal field.

name: Student Academy of Audiology
description: The Student Academy of Audiology is the national student organization of the American Academy of Audiology that serves as a collective voice for students and advances the rights, interests, and welfare of students pursuing careers in audiology. The SAA introduces students to lifelong involvement in activities that promote and advance the profession of audiology, and provides services, information, education, representation and advocacy for the profession and the public we serve.
summary: The Student Academy of Audiology is a national student organization that works to advance the rights, interests, and welfare of students pursuing careers in audiology.

name: Nepalese Student Association at Florida State University
description: The Nepalese Student Association at FSU (NSA-FSU) serves as a representative body of the Nepalese students at Florida State University. The mission of NSA-FSU is to represent Nepal in various international events to foster cultural and socio-economic awareness on Nepal among the global community. It aims to provide guidance to the prospective Nepalese students and also help the newly arrived in their process of adjustment to Tallahassee.
summary: The Nepalese Student Association at FSU (NSA-FSU) serves as a representative body of the Nepalese students at Florida State University.

name: RSO Executive Leadership Program
description: The RSO Executive Leadership Program is designed to equip emerging student leaders with the skills and resources needed to build student organizations that are successful, sustainable, and impactful at Florida State University. The Student Activities Center believes effective student organizations are ones that recognize leadership, are knowledgeable of campus resources and policies, and support the growth of their members.
summary: This program is designed to educate and support up-and-coming RSO student leaders.

name: The Association for Criminal Justice
description: The purpose of the Association for Criminal Justice is to expose current law students to practical topics in the criminal justice system. The organization shall introduce students to criminal law matters from judicial, prosecutorial, and defense perspectives.
summary: The purpose of the Association of Criminal Justice is to expose law students to practical topics in the criminal justice system. The organization shall introduce students to criminal law matters from judicial, prosecutorial, & defense perspectives.

name: Division of Student Affairs
description:
We envision a Florida State University where students, faculty and staff embrace an inclusive learning community with respect, responsibility and acceptance for all cultures. Graduates will make meaningful contributions to a global society through character, competence and integrity. They will make ethical decisions and be committed to a life of service and leadership.

The goals of the Division of Student Affairs are to facilitate student development, celebrate differences, and promote civic and global responsibility in both formal and informal educational experiences. The Division accomplishes these tasks through utilizing best practices as identified by international higher education professional organizations to create evidence-based programs and services. Find out more about us »

Florida State University’s Division of Student Affairs maintains, expands and protects the University’s focus on students. We achieve this by:

  • Advocating for students individually and systemically
  • Educating students through courses, programs and activities
  • Assisting students’ intellectual, emotional, physical, and spiritual development
  • Collaborating with campus and community partners to design policies and programs that are student-centered
  • Consulting with all University divisions to anticipate student needs and resolve issues

The Division accomplishes these tasks through utilizing best practices as identified by international higher education professional organizations to create evidence-based programs and services.
summary: The Division of Student Affairs collaborates with students, faculty, and staff to create welcoming, supportive and challenging environments that maximize opportunities for student learning and success.

name: New Student & Family Programs
description: New Student & Family Programs (NSFP) exists to aid in the transition of new students and their family members to the FSU community. We work with students from the moment they are admitted to Florida State University through the end of their first year. We continue to support and engage family members through Family Connection, publications, newsletters, online chats, and more. Additionally, our office accomplishes much of this work by selecting, training, and mentoring incredible student leaders who help us serve our community.
summary: New Student & Family Programs coordinates a series of programs for both students and families designed to support the successful transition to Florida State University.

name: Senior Class Council
description:
The Senior Class Council is an entity that serves the graduating classes at Florida State University. The mission of the Senior Class Council is to foster unity and pride among the graduating class through engaging programming that is deep rooted in tradition. 

 

Our goals are to create and uphold traditions that will enhance the experience of the students who identify themselves as Seniors.
summary: Working to create unity amongst the graduating classes and pride amongst students own graduating class.

name: Nutrition, Exercise, and Wellness (NEW) FSU College of Medicine
description:
Nutrition, Exercise, and Wellness (NEW) is a medical student–led organization dedicated to promoting a holistic approach to health by educating students, faculty, and the broader community on the essential roles of nutrition, physical activity, and overall wellness in the medical profession. Recognizing the unique physical and mental demands placed on those working in healthcare, NEW aims to empower future physicians to prioritize their own well-being while learning how to better support the health of their patients.

Throughout the academic year, NEW hosts a variety of events designed to foster both physical and mental wellness. These include invited guest speakers such as physicians, dietitians, fitness professionals, and wellness experts who provide evidence-based education on topics related to nutrition, exercise, stress management, and sustainable lifestyle habits. At least once each semester, NEW also brings in speakers to engage students in meaningful discussions that connect personal wellness with clinical practice.

In addition to educational lectures, NEW offers interactive nutrition and exercise workshops that emphasize hands-on learning. These workshops allow participants to apply practical skills such as reading nutrition labels, meal planning, and developing effective workout routines reinforcing the idea that wellness is both teachable and attainable.

To further encourage an active and balanced lifestyle, NEW hosts structured exercise classes each semester that are accessible to students and staff of all fitness levels. By creating a supportive and inclusive environment, NEW strives to help members build consistency, reduce stress, and develop lifelong habits that promote physical health and resilience.

Ultimately, NEW’s mission is to cultivate a culture of wellness within the medical community, one that values self-care, sustainability, and leading by example, so that future physicians can thrive personally while providing the highest quality care to their patients.
summary: NEW is a medical student organization that seeks to educate both students and the community about the nutrition, exercise, and wellness aspects of working in the medical field in the 21st century.

name: Student Council for Undergraduate Research and Creativity
description: The Student Council for Undergraduate Research and Creativity (SCURC) is committed to promoting awareness about undergraduate research and creative opportunities for students at Florida State University. Through generating awareness we hope to increase the number of students engaged in research and creative projects at FSU. SCURC believes that participating in undergraduate research and creative endeavors is an important experience for anyone interested in gaining more knowledge and a deeper appreciation of their academic field.
summary: The Student Council for Undergraduate Research and Creativity (SCURC) is committed to promoting awareness about undergraduate research for students at Florida State University.

name: Cuban American Bar Association, Florida State University College of Law Student Chapter
description: The mission and purpose of CABA FSU Law is to provide opportunities for student members to engage with legal professionals, further the networking of members with local professionals in all areas of legal practice, and educate members about perspective careers within the Cuban American Community. New networking and potential career opportunities will be created by hosting events through the Cuban American Bar Association, or “parent organization”, inviting guest speakers to the law school, and offering a nationwide mentorship program. CABA members obtain professional and job developmental skills by participating with CABA’s parent organization events throughout South Florida and the rest of the United States.
summary: The mission and purpose of CABA FSU Law is to provide opportunities for student members to engage with legal professionals in all areas of legal practice.

name: Student Government Association
description: The Student Government Association provides quality leadership for, and accountability to its constituency by recognizing that strength arises from diversity, engagement, and dialogue.
summary: The mission of the Student Government Association (SGA), is to provide FSU students with representation, services and advocacy within the university structure.

name: Student Government Association Accounting
summary: The SGA staff supports the student leaders in Student Government atFSU. We provide an educational atmosphere in which the student leaders can participate in the active and important role that SGA has in making effective changes in student policy.

name: Student Government Association Executive Branch
description:
Powers of the Executive Branch reside in the Student Body President and Vice President. Their fellow students elect them each spring semester to:

  • Administer and enforce Student Body laws;
  • Sign or veto Senate acts;
  • Call and preside over Student Body meetings;
  • Recommend legislation to the Senate; and
  • Create programs that address the needs of students or improve the campus.
summary: Working to enhance the quality of life for students, the executive officers voice concerns of students to the administration and community and help resolve those concerns.

name: Garnet Girls Club Cheer at FSU
description:
Welcome to Garnet Girls Club Cheer at FSU! 

Established in 2012, the Garnet Girls Club Cheer team is a student-run organization for FSU students interested in collegiate-level competitive cheerleading. The team, comprised primarily of girls with level 4 skills and above, practices two-three times a week throughout the fall and spring semesters in preparation for state-wide competitions in the spring, most notably NCA Collegiate Nationals in Daytona Beach, FL.

Our achievements at NCA College Nationals include placing Top 5 in the 2024 All-Girl Intermediate division, becoming NCA's 2023 Bronze Champions, and being recognized as a Top 10 overall-scoring team and the highest Day 1 score in 2023. 

Tryouts for Fall 2025 have already concluded. If interested still, DM the instagram @fsuclubcheer

Location: Leach Center Court 3

  •   Roster Size: 24
  •   Practice Site: Leach Center - Court 3
  •   Practice Times: see below for Fall 2025 practice times
  •   Governing Body: National Cheerleaders Association (NCA)
  •   Division: All-girl Intermediate
  •   Regular Opponents: UF, UNC, FAU, Texas A&M, Georgia Southern

Follow @fsuclubcheer on instagram for updates!

FOR ANY QUESTIONS, PLEASE CONTACT OUR PRESIDENT Anna Wallace: fsugarnetgirls@gmail.com

 

A HUGE SHOUT OUT TO OUR SPONSORS!

DiMaggio Law Offices and the Cross Family
summary: Established in 2012, the Garnet Girls Club Cheer team is a student-run organization for FSU students interested in collegiate-level competitive cheerleading. The team competes at NCA Daytona Nationals in April every year

name: Higher Education Student Association
description: The Higher Education Student Association (HESA) enhances and supplements the overall graduate student experience by supporting the academic study, professional development and socialization of its members. HESA serves as the collective voice of all students associated with the Higher Education program at Florida State University (FSU), functioning as the liaison between students, faculty, and university constituents.
summary: HESA serves as the collective voice and represents the needs of all students associated with the Higher Education program.

name: Center for Leadership and Service
description:
The Center for Leadership & Service transforms lives through leadership education, community engagement, and self-exploration, helping students develop skills and knowledge to become more effective leaders and engage responsibly with communities. To that end, we foster and sustain collaborative relationships among students, our campus, and the broader community.

We envision mutually beneficial relationships in which students develop into active, civic-minded leaders who serve in ways desired by the community, and community partners are supported in their work by student leadership capacity. We offer more than 20 programs that foster opportunities to create positive, sustainable change on campus, in our communities, and around the world. Opportunities range from civic engagement initiatives to intensive weeklong leadership training, from one-time local service trips with EngageTLH to global summers of service through the Moellership Program.
summary: Transforms lives through leadership education, community engagement, and self-exploration, helping students develop skills and knowledge to become more effective leaders and engage responsibly with communities.

name: North Florida Chapter of the American Meteorological Society/ National Weather Association
description: We are a local chapter of the American Meteorological Society and National Weather Association designed to serve the local Tallahassee community and the meteorological community in North Florida, South Georgia, and Southeast Alabama through outreach and social programs. We work with professionals to give students opportunities to network and learn about career opportunities within the meteorology and related fields. It is our goal to promote and improve the visibility of meteorology and the atmospheric and related sciences within current and future generations.
summary: We are a local chapter of the American Meteorological Society and National Weather Association designed to serve the local Tallahassee community and the meteorological community in North Florida through outreach and social programs.

name: Student Government Association Senate Branch
summary: Each of the 80 Senators represents one of FSU’s 17 divisions. First- and second-year students are listed under undergraduate studies. Third- and fourth-year students are classified by their majors.

name: Golden Torch Lecture Series
summary: Sponsored by The Student Government Association and Union Productions, the Golden Torch Lecture Series is a collaborative, student-focused effort that engages students in dialogues to enhance their academic, scholastic and humanitarian experience at FSU.

name: SG&A Student Publications
description:
We are a team of student creatives providing free color printing, graphic design, and photography services for Recognized Student Organizations on campus. We believe it is our mission to empower student voices on campus through our services. We're an affiliated project of Student Government Association, and we put our belief in diversity, inclusion, and freedom of expression into everything we create.

 

For more information on free printing, please check out our Printing Policies. You can also submit a design request on our website if your RSO needs a design!
summary: We are a team of student creatives providing free color printing, graphic design, and photography services for Recognized Student Organizations on campus. We're an affiliated project of Student Government Association. Come visit us in Thagard 425!

name: Student Government Association Supreme Court Branch
description:
The Student Body President appoints justices from among second- and third-year students at FSU’s College of Law. The Chief Justice appoints the Clerk of the Court, who can be any student, and the Florida State University Elections Commission, which is comprised of five students at FSU’s College of Law.

Any student may file a complaint with the Supreme Court by filling out a complaint form. The student must simply state the facts of the claim and the court’s jurisdiction, which covers:

1. Questions of the constitutionality of actions by student governing groups, organizations, and their representatives
2. Violations of the Student Body Constitution and Statutes
3. Conflicts between student groups
4. Violations of the student conduct code
5. Violations of the SGA ethics code
6. Appeals from the Florida State University Elections Commission
7. Advisory opinions, which may be requested by the Student Body President or any Senator
8. Issuing any writ necessary and proper to the complete exercise of the court’s jurisdiction
summary: The Supreme Court consists of the Chief Justice, four Associate Justices and a Clerk of the Court.

name: SAFE Connection
description:
Students who plan to be transported off campus and who are under the influence of alcohol, need to take advantage of the Night Nole program. S.A.F.E Connection promotes awareness of the dangers of driving under the influence of alcohol. A portion of the S.A.F.E. Connection program is sponsored by the Student Government Association and S.A.F.E. is an affiliated project with the FSU Police Department. The project promotes education, activities and programs concerning crime prevention and safety to the students, faculty, staff and visitors of Florida State University. Operating hours vary throughout the year as people are escorted about the service areas.

If you have questions about S.A.F.E’s services, employment or concerns please use the following email address: safeconnection@fsu.edu. For general information, please contact 850-644-1234.
summary: S.A.F.E. Connection, Student Alert Force Escort Connection, is a free transportation service provided to students.

name: WVFS Tallahassee 89.7FM (V89)
shortName: The Voice of Florida State - FSU College Radio
description: WVFS is FSU's student radio station, broadcasting 24/7/365 at 89.7FM and worldwide at wvfs.fsu.edu.  WVFS offers hands on experience in all facets of radio operations, is staffed by 150-200 volunteers, and recruits three times a year at the very beginning of the fall and spring semesters, and (for summer) at the very end of the spring semester.  WVFS welcomes all majors.  No experience is required.
summary: Mission: To provide diversity in programming to the Florida State campus and Tallahassee community while acting as a top-notch training facility for students and community members interested in gaining experience in radio station operations.

name: Student Government Association Senate Special Project Branch
summary: Special Projects classification of SGA Senate

name: Student Government Association Executive Projects
summary: Special Projects related to the SGA Executive Branch.

name: Student Government Association Elections Office
description: This branch of SGA manages and supervises all elections. There are 2 elections per year, one in the Fall semester and the second one in the Spring. The elections are for Student Body President, Vice-President, Treasurer, Student Senate, Campus Recreation Board, Union Board, and Black Student Union Leadership.
summary: This branch of SGA supervises all elections and oversees dispute resolution.

name: FSU Medical Response Unit
description:
The Florida State University Volunteer Medical Response Unit functions as a Basic Life Support Quick Response Unit at Florida State University.

The Medical Response Unit functions as a basic life support and quick response unit at Florida State University. Staffed by trained student volunteers, the Medical Response Unit is an affiliated project associated with University Health Services, FSU Police Department, Student Government Association, and FSU Division of Student Affairs. The Medical Response Unit offers support to existing emergency medical and safety units during campus events, such as intramural sports, 5K’s, the FSU Flying High Circus, Dance Marathon and many others.

The unit provides a safety net on the FSU campus in case of medical emergencies IN ADDITION to the normal response already provided by the FSU Police Department and the community 911 system. The unit is available to any campus organization requesting assistance for campus events, and to any students/faculty/guests of Florida State Main Campus in need of assistance.
summary: The Florida State University Medical Response Unit functions as a volunteer Basic Life Support Quick Response Unit at Florida State University.

name: Student Government Association Administration
summary: The administrative office for SGA.

name: Junior Class Council at Florida State University
description: The Junior Class Council serves the Junior Class at Florida State University. The Junior Class Council hosts programming dedicated to development for students here at Florida State University.
summary: A council to create unity and programming for the junior class.

name: Sophomore Class Council
description: The Sophomore serves the Sophomore Class at Florida State University and instills pride in the Sophomore Class. The Sophomore Class Council hosts events throughout the year dedicated to health and wellness and enrichment.
summary: A council to create unity and programming for the sophomore class.

name: Center for Global Engagement
description:
The Center is committed to enhancing FSU's internationalization initiatives through:

  • Intercultural Programs
  • Global Citizenship Certificate
  • Global Exchanges
  • Immigration Services
  • Collaborative efforts with academic and administrative departments
summary: The Mission of the Center for Global Engagement is to facilitate international diversity and foster global understanding and awareness within the FSU community.

name: National Alliance on Mental Illness at Florida State University
description:
NAMI on Campus Florida State University will strive to:

  • Serve as a community for those looking to advocate for mental health. 
  • Establish strong ties with the counseling center and other mental health resources on campus and participate in events put on by these entities.
  • Educate and increase students’, faculty, and administrators’ awareness of mental health issues.
  • Provide information and resources on mental health conditions to the entire college community.
  • Promote early detection and intervention for mental health conditions.
  • Combat the stigma that surrounds mental health conditions by increasing awareness of these conditions, their symptoms and available treatments.
  • Advocate administrators and legislatures to improve mental health care services on campus and promote education about mental illness among students.
summary: NAMI on Campus Florida State University is a student-led campus club that helps to address the mental health needs of students and raise mental health awareness among campus communities through peer support, education and advocacy.

name: Phi Eta Sigma National Honor Society
description:
Phi Eta Sigma was started on March 22, 1923 at the University of Illinois and is in its 58th year at Florida State University. There are over 400 chapters nationwide, 1,400,000 inducted members, and $400,000 in scholarships given out by nationals every year. 

Each spring, invitations are sent to full-time students who earned a cumulative grade point average of at least 3.5 during their first year at Florida State University. This does include both freshmen and transfer students. Lifetime membership is conferred upon induction. Participation in chapter programs and volunteer opportunities is required in order to be eligible to receive a cord upon graduation. Activities available exclusively to Phi Eta Sigma members include chapter leadership, events, banquets, trips, and numerous academically-based service projects. Selected members represent Florida State at the biannual national conference. Annually, members may apply for the Florida State chapter’s $1,000 endowed scholarship to support undergraduate research and national scholarships of $1,000-$10,000.
summary: Phi Eta Sigma is the oldest and largest national first-year honor society. Our goal is to encourage and reward academic excellence among first-year students in institutions of higher learning.

name: Council for Exceptional Children
description: As a student chapter of CEC we aim to promote the values upheld by the national CEC, including educational success, social inclusion, disability awareness and advocacy. We uphold these values through service and community involvement, informational programming, and professional development. We welcome all potential members with an interest in this mission, and those who endeavor to pursue careers serving individuals with exceptionalities.
summary: Our student chapter of CEC aims to advance the success of individuals with exceptionalities through education, advocacy, service, and professional development.

name: Ratio Christi Student Fellowship at Florida State University
description: Ratio Christi (a Student Apologetics Alliance) seeks to encourage and strengthen the faith of Christian students at secular and liberal institutions across the country, while also sharing Christ's message through the use of intellectual investigation.
summary: Ratio Christi (a Student Apologetics Alliance) seeks to encourage and strengthen the faith of Christian students at secular and liberal institutions across the country, while also sharing Christ's message through the use of intellectual investigation.

name: Real Property, Probate, and Trust Law Society
summary: The Real Property, Probate, and Trust Law Society at Florida State University College of Law is focused on providing networking opportunities and information about the practice of real property, probate, and trust law to its members.

name: COGS Administration
description: This is the administration branch for the Congress of Graduate Students. If you are looking for Conference Presentation or Attendance Grants, please visit cogs.fsu.edu.
summary: This is the administration branch for the Congress of Graduate Students.

name: Law School Council
description: LSC is a funding board for all College of Law RSOs that works in conjunction with COGS, SGA Administration, and COL Faculty, to ensure our unique campus receives the financial support it seeks. LSC and COGS function within the legislative branch of FSU’s Student Government Association.
summary: The Law School Council funds Law School organizations in conjunction with the Congress of Graduate Students.

name: Medical Student Council
description: The Medical Student Council (MSC) is composed of a board of Medical, PA, Masters, and Doctoral students who serve the student body at the Florida State University College of Medicine (FSU COM). MSC has the ability to act as the voice of the College of Medicine student body and has the authority to bring forth resolutions within Student Government Association (SGA) and the Congress of Graduate Students (COGS) in the best interest of medical students. MSC is the funding board for student activities and governs the Recognized Student Organizations (RSOs) at the FSU COM.
summary: This committee is part of the Congress of Graduate Students.

name: KINGS Dance Team
description: The mission of KINGS Dance Team is to share our talents with our fellow students of The Florida State University and the Tallahassee community while upholding our values of scholarship, service, and sisterhood. We are a team that strives for diversity, in which all styles of dances are welcomed into the group and presented to our peers. Our goal is to give our audiences a unique experience with each of our performances and events.
summary: KINGS Dance Team is an co-ed organization based on the campus of Florida State University.

name: Neuroscience Undergraduate Student Association
description: NUSA provides an avenue for academic, professional, and social activities to the undergraduate students at Florida State University who are interested in pursuing careers in neuroscience. Our program includes faculty and students across campus from multiple departments. The main goals of this organization are to facilitate the academic and professional development of its members and to promote collegiality and social interaction among the students and faculty of Florida State University. The organization also leads to provide outreach programs to the community to foster knowledge towards the field of neuroscience.
summary: NUSA is an undergraduate organization for students interested in neuroscience.  Its goals are to foster knowledge towards the field of neuroscience to its members and provide outreach programs to the community relating to the subject.

name: Transfer Leadership Institute
description: The Transfer Leadership Institute (TLI) is an executive project within the Student Government Association at Florida State University. The program encompasses weekly workshops, interest groups, a weekend retreat, and a peer mentor program for student participants.  During the workshops students are exposed to campus resources, opportunities, leadership development activities, and much more.  This unique opportunity allows transfer students to make connections and build relationships with other FSU students interested in leadership, personal, and professional development.  Not only do students have the opportunity to network, but they also gain valuable knowledge and skills to leave a legacy at our university.
summary: The Transfer Leadership Institute shall serve as the premier leadership organization for transfer students at Florida State University.

name: Freshman Leadership Institute
description:
The Freshman Leadership Institute’s mission aims to provide first year students with the tools to become active student leaders on campus while providing support as students adjust to the Florida State University campus and culture. With an emphasis on individual, group, and community values, students will develop a leadership identity with the support of their peers and Student Government Association staff. 

Please visit our website for more information, "Like" us on FaceBook, and follow us on Instagram @fsufli
summary: The Freshman Leadership Institute is an Executive Project through SGA which aims to provide first year students with the tools to become active student leaders on campus while providing support as students adjust to the FSU's campus and culture

name: Honors Medical Scholars Society at the Florida State University
description: Through this organization, students are eligible for early admission to Florida State University's College of Medicine and are provided access to additional resources to facilitate the pre-medical process. This organization offers support through the undergraduate’s career as a pre-medical student by allowing access to pre-health advising and volunteering opportunities. Recruitment takes place after incoming freshmen have been successfully accepted by the University’s Honors Program. They must submit a separate application for this organization and be invited by the selection committee to undergo interviews to assess their eligibility.
summary: Offers support through the undergraduate’s career as a pre-medical student by allowing access to pre-health advising and volunteering opportunities. Applications are submitted by freshmen once they are accepted by the University's Honors Program.

name: Undergraduate Mock Trial Program at FSU
description:
Mock Trial at FSU is an organization that competes in the American Mock Trial Association. Students portray attorneys and witnesses, learn about legal evidence and case law, develop high-end acting skills, and litigate a civil or criminal case across the country for 6-8 months. 

 

In 2013, FSU won the American Mock Trial Association National Championship, placing 1st out of 551 teams in the country, in 2014, FSU placed 5th and 8th place out of 551 teams in the country, and in 2016, FSU placed 4th out of 551 teams in the country. In 2021, FSU received an Honorable Mention at the National Championship Tournament, skyrocketing us to #18 in the nation out of over 550 teams in the country.

 

FSU's recent accomplishments are wide and consistent, including:

 

1st at UC Irvine's Anteater Tournament (2020), 1st at UF's Swamp Invitational (2020), 1st at Vanderbilt's Grand Ole Tournament (2019), 1st at FSU's Capital City Invitational (2019), 1st at the Ramblin' Wreck Invitational (2020), 1st at Dickinson's Red Devil Tournament (2020), 1st at Indiana's Hoosier Hoedown (2021), 2nd at Iowa's Mock Trial Invitational (2020), 2nd at the AMTA Regional Tournament (2021), and 1st at the Opening Round Championship Series (2021). In the 2020-2021 season alone, our teammates have received a total of 52 individual awards for being outstanding competitors.

 

Please reach out to us if you have any questions regarding membership or involvement!
summary: FSU Mock Trial is an organization that competes in the American Mock Trial Association. Students portray attorneys, witnesses, and litigate an extensive civil or criminal case across the country.

name: Business Case Club
description: The Business Case Club at FSU is an organization within the College of Business that trains students to read and analyze business cases, and present solutions to real-world business problems. Although the organization is in the College of Business, it is open to all students. We will be engaging in critical-thinking and public-speaking activities to enhance the presentation skills of students. Throughout the semester, students will collaborate with peers on multiple mock business cases to strengthen their team-building and leadership skills. Finally, at the end of each training cycle, students will be presented with opportunities to represent FSU at external competitions.
summary: The Business Case Club at FSU creates an all-inclusive, hands-on environment for students to enhance professional and interpersonal skills by developing innovative solutions to complex, real-world business problems.

name: Beta Theta Pi
description: Beta Theta Pi is one of the oldest social collegiate fraternities in North America. It was founded in 1839 at Miami University in Oxford, Ohio. The fraternity currently consists of 129 active chapters and colonies in the United States and Canada. Over 190,000 members have been initiated worldwide, with approximately 135,000 living initiated members. There are currently more than 8,000 undergraduate members. Working to cultivate gentlemen, leaders, and scholars from men on Florida State University's campus.
summary: Building men of principle for a principled life. Organization under the Florida State University Interfraternity Council.

name: Alpha Omicron Pi
description:
Alpha Omicron Pi is a Panhellenic sorority at Florida State University open to all women. Our sisterhood is built on our core values: Scholarship, Dignity, Character, and College Loyalty. All members of AOII are united through these values and bonded by our sisterhood. We all share a goal of inspiring ambition in young women and loving others boldly.

Our Alpha Pi chapter at FSU encourages us to live out these values daily. As one of our founders Stella George Stern Perry explained, "We cared so profoundly for one another that we unconsciously desired a society based upon original feeling... We felt that as love never faileth, a future on that foundation was assured." In an effort to exemplify our truly fraternal values, Alpha Omicron Pi supports the philanthropic efforts of the Arthritis Foundation. Through our annual philanthropy event, Strikeout Arthritis, AOII strives to raise awareness for arthritis and collect donations for this organization.
summary: Alpha Omicron Pi was founded in 1897 at Barnard College. While each chapter is unique, they are all founded on our principle values of Scholarship, Dignity, Character, and College Loyalty with a shared goal of inspiring ambition.

name: Psi Chi International Honor Society in Psychology
description:
1) Providing high-impact service opportunities for members that allow them to gain career-relevant experience while serving the community;
2) Connecting members to the professional knowledge and advice of Psychology faculty, graduate students, alumni and other members;
3) Providing opportunities for the campus community to be exposed to the knowledge of our science.
summary: Psi Chi is an international honor society whose purpose shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology.

name: Classical Guitar Society at Florida State University
description: The Classical Guitar Society at FSU seeks to further the knowledge, understanding, and appreciation of the guitar. Our mission consists of deepening the perspective on performing and interpreting the guitar's most rewarding repertoire.
summary: The Classical Guitar Society at FSU seeks to  further the knowledge, understanding, and appreciation of the guitar. Our mission consists of  deepening the perspective on performing and interpreting the guitar's most difficult and rewarding repertoire.

name: The Jim Moran Institute for Global Entrepreneurship
summary: The Jim Moran Institute for Global Entrepreneurship

name: College of Social Sciences and Public Policy Student Leadership Council
description:
Spring 2026 Applications Open January 5!

Check out our Linktree to follow our Instagram and join the Flare: https://linktr.ee/fsucossslc?utm_source=linktree_profile_share&ltsid=750b9763-a75c-488f-a945-3fc1d75eb2f5 

 

WHAT WE DO:

The Student Leadership Council is composed of discipline-specific, exemplary social science students who work directly with the college administration to provide a student perspective in deciding and implementing the goals and initiatives of the college. The council creates and oversees beneficial programming for students in an attempt to improve opportunities for personal and professional growth and to advance the College of Social Sciences and Public Policy and achieve the goals of the Dean's Office. Members may work on different committtees to help plan events, service and professional development opportunities, and conduct outreach for the council. 

 

MISSION STATEMENT: 

The COSSPP Student Leadership Council works to improve the lives of students in the college through providing professional development opportunities, facilitating a mentorship program, hosting service events, and creating a community within the college. Our values are leadership, service, and mentorship.
summary: The College of Social Sciences and Public Policy Student Leadership Council serves as a liaison group between the College's student body and the Dean of the College to provide programming, events, and opportunities to all students of the college and FSU.

name: The Cell and Molecular Biology Graduate Student Association
description: The Cell and Molecular Biology Graduate Student Association aims to foster solidarity primarily among students in the Cell and Molecular Biology graduate program within the Department of Biological Sciences at Florida State University, but also between other graduate students in the biology department and in other scientific disciplines, undergraduate students interested in research, faculty members, as well as the local community. We achieve this by providing students with opportunities to present their research, facilitating communication between graduate students, undergraduate students, and faculty members within the Department of Biological Science, other department, and encouraging interdisciplinary interactions between students from various fields of study.
summary: The Cell and Molecular Biology Graduate Association aims to foster solidarity between students in the Cell and Molecular Biology graduate program within the Department of Biological Sciences at Florida State University.

name: Bangladeshi Student Association
description: Our organization is dedicated to safeguarding, enhancing, and championing the interests of Bangladeshi descent students. We strive to encourage active engagement within the community, fostering mutual understanding and facilitating personal and professional growth. Through diverse cultural, educational, and recreational events, we aim to cultivate international goodwill among FSU students. Additionally, we actively promote and support a vibrant array of extra-curricular activities on campus, enriching the student experience and nurturing holistic
summary: This group is to protect, facilitate, improve and serve the interests of students of Bangladeshi descent.

name: Gamma Iota Sigma, Iota Chapter
description:
The purpose of this fraternity is to promote, encourage, and sustain student interest in insurance, risk management, and actuarial science as professions; to encourage the high moral and scholastic attainments of its members; and to facilitate interaction of educational institutions and industry through networking and by fostering research activities, scholarship, and improved public relations.

If you have questions on how to join, email fsugispresidents@gmail.com!

Our LinkTree is https://linktr.ee/fsu.gis.

Instagram is our primary social media platform and a great way to stay informed of our events: @FSU.GIS
summary: The purpose of this fraternity is to provide students who are interested in insurance and actuarial science the opportunity to interact with industry professionals and build connections with their fellow students.

name: Club Management Association of America at FSU
description: Club Managers Association of America (CMAA) is a professional organization compromised of public and private club managers from around the U.S. As a student chapter we are able to tap into their resources for education, training, and networking.
summary: CMAA, or Club Managers Association of America, is a professional organization compromised of public and private club managers from around the U.S. As a student chapter we are able to tap into their resources for education, training, and networking.

name: The Bengali Student Association of FSU
description:
The provinces of Bengal in India and Bangladesh, are home to the Bengalis, people widely known for their sense of warmth and hospitality, their indulgence in festivities, for being connoisseurs of great food, and a long history of intellectual aura with immense love for literature and music, not to forget a keen interest in soccer. The current Bengali culture is a unique marriage of cosmopolitanism with traditional sanctity, something that makes Bengal a unique state.

The Bengali student community of Tallahassee has a sizeable population and we have organized various cultural events in the past (at venues on and off-campus) that witnessed active participation from domestic and international students. The Bengali Student Association of FSU aims to foster solidarity between Bengali and non-Bengali students by showcasing the rich culture and tradition of Bengal to the whole student population at FSU, who come from varied nationalities and cultures.

The membership of this organization will be open to all interested students and not limited to any particular community or nationality. The primary goal of BONGONOLE would be to inform people about this wonderful culture and its elements and showcase it to the FSU community and encourage active participation from Bengali and non-Bengali students.

All activities and functions of The Bengali Student Association of FSU shall be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.
summary: The provinces of Bengal in India and Bangladesh are home to Bengalis, people known for their warmth, hospitality, festivities and taste in great food. We hope to showcase the culture of Bengal while fostering student interaction at FSU.

name: Her Campus at FSU
description: Want to get involved or have a story idea we should write about? Email our Campus Correspondents, Sophia Montoya and Isabelle Krukoski, at hc.fsu@hercampus.com!
summary: The official Her Campus chapter at Florida State University—your on-campus source for news, style, entertainment, career and more!

name: Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity
description:
Phi Delta Theta Tau is the local chapter of the national Professional Engineering Fraternity. Theta Tau has become the oldest and largest Fraternity for Engineers, with chapters across the country, and members around the world. Our mission is to develop ENGINEERING LEADERS for Service, Profession, and Brotherhood. The purpose of Theta Tau is to develop and maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship. Our goals are: Sustaining and enhancing Fraternity structure, Facilitating opportunities for achieving personal and professional aspirations, Providing alumni benefits and opportunities for engagement, and Expanding and driving chapter excellence, sustainability, and leadership

This Chapter of Theta Tau encompasses students in engineering and computer science Majors.
summary: Theta Tau is the oldest and largest Fraternity for Engineers, with chapters across the country, and members around the world. Our mission is to develop ENGINEERING LEADERS for Service, Profession, and Brotherhood.

name: Multicultural Marketing Student Association
description:
MMSA is dedicated to equipping its diverse members with meaningful experiences, robust networks, practical tools, and valuable resources essential for professional and personal success. MMSA unites FSU students, alumni, and professional development programs to foster impactful connections and support each member’s journey toward achieving their highest potential

 

Directly affiliated with the Center for Hispanic Marketing Communication, MMSA seeks to connect both undergraduates and graduates in the interest of multicultural marketing and upholds its motto: “Connecting Cultures, Building Futures”
summary: MMSA empowers its diverse members with valuable experiences, strong networks, essential tools, and key resources to support their professional and personal growth

name: Veterans Student Union
description: The Veterans Student Union is commited to serving all student veterans and the interests of those who support them. The primary goal of the VSU is to promote good academic standing and successful employment for all members. Within this mission we also promote and support the bonding of student veterans within the FSU community and help Florida State University create the "most veteran friendly public university in the nation."
