summary: The Veterans Student Union is committed to the collective interests of all student veterans of the armed forces and those who support them in alliance with the FSU Veterans Initiative.

name: Hispanic Graduate Student Association
description:
HiGSA is a diverse organization aimed at promoting the Hispanic culture at FSU and serving as a network support for new and current graduate students realizing the full potential of diversity and inclusion. The organization is constantly working towards promoting cultural exchange and mutual understanding and hopes to set an example of student-led organizations serving the diversity and inclusion goals across US universities.

Our goal is to promote Hispanic culture at FSU, to generate a support network that helps new graduate students settle and adapt to FSU and the local community, to mentor graduate students to succeed in academic life and goals and to generate awareness of social and political issues in Hispanic countries and their relationship with the USA. HiGSA is aligned with FSU’s vision committed to academic excellence, diversity, and free inquiry and constantly works to make its contribution to the university’s mission. These goals are accomplished through a series of social and academic activities at FSU and within Tallahassee’s community.

Check out our social media to stay tuned with our activities and meet our community @higsafsu

Check our original video here: HiGSA Origin Story
summary: HiGSA is a diverse organization aimed at promoting the Hispanic culture at FSU and serving as a network support for new and current graduate students realizing the full potential of diversity and inclusion. The organization is constantly working towards

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Inc., Kappa Epsilon Chapter
description: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is an organization of college-educated women committed to the constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Inc., is an organization of college educated women committed to the constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.

name: Nursing Student Leadership Council at Florida State University
description: The Nursing Student Leadership Council (NSLC) at Florida State University serves as the student executive board for the College of Nursing. Comprised of student leaders from each cohort, the council advocates for student needs, enhances communication between students and faculty, and promotes academic, professional, and community engagement. Through initiatives such as event planning, outreach efforts, and leadership development, NSLC strives to enrich the overall student experience and foster a strong, supportive nursing community.
summary: The student executive board for the College of Nursing comprised of student leaders from each cohort. The council advocates for student needs, enhances communication between students and faculty, and promotes academic and professional growth.

name: Connecting Experimental Lab and Life Sciences
description: Connecting Experimental Lab and Life Sciences, also known as CELLS, promotes research for undergraduates and provides a social and professional network for students engaged in biomedical, clinical, and social science research. CELLS at FSU will introduce and prepare undergraduate students for an exciting research experience by providing avenues for networking with research faculty. This organization offers workshops, mentoring, lab tours, service events, field trips, research symposiums, interactive discussions with research scientists, and advice on internships and biomedical research careers. Finally, CELLS at FSU also hosts several social events in order to unite the scientific community of students.
summary: CELLS promotes research for undergraduates and provides a  network for students engaged in biomedical, clinical and social science research. This organization offers workshops, mentoring, lab tours, service events, field trips, and research symposiums.

name: College Leadership Council for the College of Fine Arts
description:
The College Leadership Council at the FSU College of Fine Arts consists of undergraduate and graduate students from each of the College of Fine Art's six departments who use SGA affiliated funds to promote interaction between the college's students and highlight the impact our college is making.

Our primary means of promotion and interaction is through our annual publication of SIX, a magazine highlighting student work.
summary: The College Leadership Council seeks to promote connections amongst the six departments of the College of Fine Arts and promote the creative work of the college's student population.

name: University Counseling Center
description: The UCC is a welcoming and confidential place on campus where students can come to discuss issues ranging from homesickness and adjustment to college to relationships concerns, eating disorders, substance use, anxiety, and depression. We offer Individual, Couples, Group, and Crisis Intervention services by licensed and professionally trained staff to currently enrolled FSU students. Our services are paid for through student fees and are offered at no additional charge.
summary: University Counseling Center

name: Student Disability Resource Center
description:
As a primary advocate for students with disabilities, the OAS works with faculty and staff to provide accommodations for the unique needs of students both in and out of the classroom.

By providing support services at no cost to students with disabilities, the Office of Accessibility Services offers an opportunity for students to achieve their academic and personal goals.
summary: By providing support services at no cost to students with disabilities, Office of Accessibility Services offers an opportunity for students to achieve their academic and personal goals.

name: Center for Academic Retention and Enhancement
summary: CARE provides preparation, orientation, and academic support programming for students who are among the first in their family to attend college and may face unique challenges in college because of educational or economic circumstances.

name: Fundamentals of Observational Research and Career Experience
description: F.O.R.C.E. was created for the Florida State University pre-health student body to facilitate shadowing rotations and group visitations, as well as to enhance the professional knowledge of a pre-health student. As a team, F.O.R.C.E. is dedicated to providing pre-health students with shadowing opportunities and professional experience.
summary: Shadowing in Tallahassee can be competitive. FORCE is here to help dedicated, hardworking, diligent students find a health professional, and mentorship to well-round the individual before they start their future careers.

name: Association of IT Professionals: FSU Chapter
description:
Our Primary Goals:

1. To foster a spirit of professionalism and ambition in students by helping them meet other career-minded individuals through career development events such as employer visits, company tours, LinkedIn and resume workshops, guest speaker series, connecting students with mentors, and helping students find internship opportunities.

2. To provide avenues for social collaboration between students by organizing networking events, pizza nights, group hikes, and other social events.

3. To facilitate links between FSU students and the local technology community by partnering with local companies and organizations.

4. To provide opportunities for students to adopt leadership roles on campus by working with other clubs to fill executive board positions, encouraging student participation in other leadership program such as WISE and STARS, etc.
summary: The Association of IT Professionals (AITP) is a professional student organization dedicated to providing FSU students with the skills and resources necessary to build a career in STEM through events, workshops, networking, mentoring, and socialization.

name: Saving Smiles Club at FSU
description: Saving Smiles provides pre-dental students with the resources to further their dental knowledge and experience, prior to matriculating into dental school, via serving as volunteers in the dental community. Saving Smiles members are dedicated to improving the oral healthcare at all levels of community; at FSU, in Tallahassee, in the state of Florida, across the United States, and even abroad. In doing so, members are able to have hands-on experience and ensure that they are better prepared for dental school and feel comfortable with their career decision.
summary: Saving Smiles exists as a unique opportunity for pre-dental students with an interest in learning more about dentistry through serving within the dental community. We also provide a space to improve dental related skills such as manual dexterity.

name: Noles in Med at FSU
description:
Noles in Med is a student-led organization at Florida State University committed to empowering pre-health students as they pursue careers in medicine and healthcare. Our mission is to provide a supportive environment where students can grow academically, professionally, and personally.

We offer a range of opportunities, including:

  • Volunteering with local organizations to give back to the community.
  • Shadowing experiences to connect students with medical professionals.
  • Hands-on training such as CPR certification and clinical workshops.
  • Guest speakers ranging from medical students to healthcare professionals.
  • Networking events to build connections with peers and mentors.

Whether you're seeking to enhance your skills, gain valuable experience, or simply connect with others who share your passion for healthcare, NoleMed is here to help you succeed.

Join us to shape your future in medicine.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to offer resources, events, and volunteer opportunities for students interested in pursuing careers in healthcare. Our club organizes guest speakers and workshops to help students explore various healthcare fields.

name: The Consulting Group at Florida State University
description:
The Consulting Group at Florida State University is a premier, student-operated consulting firm serving businesses and organizations across the United States.
 
Our team, comprising top-tier students from the College of Business, collaborates with an advisory board bringing decades of operational expertise. By leveraging our diverse student backgrounds and business acumen, we provide clients with data-driven solutions and innovative strategies.

We work with for-profit and non-profit companies over the course of each semester (Fall and Spring). Each semester we take on 2-4 different client projects, and you meet with the client every other week. In the off weeks we meet as a full group where we give client updates, work on deliverables, and figure out what the next steps should be in the project. Project teams are made up of 1 project lead and 5-10 consultants. The Group lets students take on real clients, solve real-world business problems, and add real value to the companies they work with. 

If you are interested in joining this elite business organization, we have recruitment once a year in the Fall. Please check out our website, Instagram, and LinkedIn pages for more up to date information about recruitment. There are roughly 20-30 members in The Group at all times.
summary: The Consulting Group at Florida State University is a student-run firm leveraging top business students' insights to provide innovative, data-driven solutions to businesses nationwide. 2025 applications are live on our LinkTree: https://linktr.ee/cgatfsu

name: Yoga Club at Florida State University
description: The purpose of the Yoga Club is to share the practice and healing benefits of yoga through physical postures, breath, mindfulness, and meditation. We aim to provide FSU students with a variety of yoga classes, workshops, and social events every semester in order to cultivate a valuable and substantial yoga community on our campus. Yoga classes are held 3-5 times each week for Gold Members ($15 dues per semester), and a few times each month for the public with our certified instructors. Various styles of yoga will be explored per semester, including (but not limited to) Hatha, Ashtanga, Vinyasa, and Yin.
summary: The Yoga Club at Florida State University values the highest version of ourselves and aims to create a safe space for like-minded people to connect. Whether through practice or social events, we create a healthy community all are welcome.

name: International Studies Graduate Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to facilitate inter-program student communication, provide an environment of support for incoming and returning program students, and establish a medium for students in which to demonstrate their scholastic abilities and achievements. We aim to promote the importance of community involvement and cultural awareness within the city of Tallahassee.
summary: A graduate student organization for International Affairs, Russian and Eastern European Studies, and Asian Studies Programs.

name: The Philosophy Club at Florida State University
description: This organization is a collaborative enterprise of students with various political and philosophical beliefs to philosophize and discuss life in all its aspects. We meet every Friday at 5:00PM for a 1.5-2 hour discussion. Our meetings for the Fall semester will be typically held in Dodd hall room 155. Announcements for weekly club meetings will be made on our discord.
summary: This organization is a collaborative enterprise of students with various political and philosophical beliefs to discuss life in all its aspects. We typically meet every Friday at 5:00PM for a 1.5-2 hour discussion In Dodd hall room 155.

name: National Association for Music Education Collegiate of Florida State University
description:
It shall be the purpose of this organization to: make available to members opportunities for professional development; acquaint students with the privileges and responsibilities of the music education profession; provide all members with the opportunity to become acquainted with leaders in the music education profession through participation in programs, demonstrations, discussions, workshops, and performances planned by this chapter, the state music educators association; and NAfME: The National Association for Music Education; assist the school in various projects throughout the year; and provide the opportunity to have contact with collegiate members from other schools.

 

NAfME Website: http://www.nafme.org/

NAfME Collegiate Website: http://www.nafme.org/membership/collegiate/

Florida NAfME Collegiate Website: http://flnafmecollegiate.flmusiced.org/

Florida NAfME Collegiate Discord Server: https://discord.com/invite/Z5GkjV6eKZ

FMEA Website: http://fmea.flmusiced.org/

FSMA Website: http://fsma.flmusiced.org/
summary: NAfME Collegiate of FSU gives music education students a plethora of opportunities to gain knowledge about their future intended profession that they would otherwise not receive in the normal classroom setting.

name: Who We Play For Chapter at Florida State University
description:
Who We Play For has been a non-profit organization since 2011. Our fight is against Sudden Cardiac Arrest and to raise heart health awareness. Our movement is to inspire others to discover whom they play for, find their purpose, and make their impact. We fight Sudden Cardiac Arrest, the number 1 killer of student-athletes, through three main programs.

   1) Automatic External Defibrillator (AED) Placement Program
      a. We are committed to providing AED’s to schools and fields in need to protect against sudden cardiac arrest.
   2) Heart-Screening Program
      a. We provide low-charge heart screenings that can detect heart conditions associated with sudden cardiac arrest and save lives.
   3) The Heart Health Advocacy Program
      a. We are working diligently to increase awareness of sudden cardiac arrest and we are working with legislators to pass legislation similar to the Concussion Awareness Act.
summary: We fight sudden cardiac arrest and heart health through heart screenings in the local area. Our Movement is to inspire you with our story of overcoming adversity, while providing you the platform to make an impact for something greater than yourself!

name: Physics Graduate Student Association
description:
The main objective of this organization is to:

  1. Represent the needs, interests and internal concerns of Florida State physics graduate students to faculty and administration.
  2. Promote leadership and professional development among physics graduate students.
  3. Work in collaboration with the faculty and staff of Florida State physics department to address the needs, interests and internal concerns of physics graduate students.
  4. Enhance the socio-cultural and academic interest of physics graduate students.
  5. Promote academic and research excellence among physics graduate students.
  6. Act as the officially recognized liaison between the faculty, staff and administration of the department of physics and the graduate students of the aforementioned department.
summary: The main objective of this organization is to represent the needs, interests and internal concerns of Florida State physics graduate students to faculty and administration.

name: Society of Petroleum Engineers at FAMU-FSU
description:
The Society of Petroleum Engineers (SPE) is a professional student organization dedicated to enrichment and outreach for students at the FAMU-FSU College of Engineering. The organization is committed to providing members with all tools and resources necessary to succeed in not just the oil and gas industry, but all academic endeavors. Members of all disciplines are welcome! The chapter is a student chapter of SPE International (SPEI), which provides further opportunity with professional membership. SPE International is the largest individual-member organization serving managers, engineers, scientists, and other professionals worldwide in the upstream segment of the oil and gas industry. The mission of SPEI is to collect, disseminate, and exchange technical knowledge concerning the exploration, development, and production of oil and gas resources, and related technologies for the public benefit. Also, SPEI aims to provide opportunities for professionals to enhance their technical and professional competence.

 

As a student, one of the major benefits of joining SPE is networking and employment opportunities. As a matter of fact, SPE has already been the link for several students obtaining jobs in the oil and gas industry with members working at companies such as Shell, Chevron, Exxon, BP, and Halliburton. The chapter helps develop technical knowledge through guest speakers and presentations. In addition, members attend technical conferences, student symposiums, section meetings, and tour chemical plants. Other events the organization hosts include volunteering in the community, social events, intramural sports, and more.
summary: An organization dedicated to engineering students who wish to pursue and develop a career in the upstream and downstream sectors of the petroleum industry. As well as are interested in the exploration and production of natural crude oil and gas.

name: Surf Club at Florida State University
description: The purpose of Surf Club is to unite FSU students with a common interest in the ocean and surfing. We aim to provide a positive environment for our fellow surfers through our monthly meetings, events and trips to the coast. We enjoy being outdoors, together, and are always looking to surf.
summary: Our purpose and goal is to unite those ocean minded individuals in our student body who share a passion for surfing and the ocean. Whether you're a beginner or a expert, feel free to join our meetings and come to the beach with us.

name: Sustainable Campus
description:
What is sustainability?

The concept of sustainable development was first defined by the United Nations in the report, Our Common Future  (1987), "Meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs." (Page 41)

Everyone has a different understanding of sustainability, and can effect sustainable change in various ways. At Sustainable Campus, it is not about remembering a textbook definition, but rather embracing sustainability into your way of life and learning new ways to make a positive impact. This allows sustainability to be approachable for each individual to find their own passion and connection to sustainability.

We introduce the three pillars of sustainability to the FSU community: environmental, economic, and social justice-all of which are needed equally to be truly sustainable. The environmental pillar focuses on renewable energy, waste management, sustainable agriculture, and reducing deforestation. Economic focuses on long term growth, that is using resources in a healthy balance with the ecosystem and via sustainable methods. The social pillar focuses on equity and who has access to the resources, so that one group of people is not unfairly burdened at the benefit of another. By focusing on all three aspects,  we transcend the environmentalism movement into a holistic way of life that focuses on equity, justice, and inclusion. Sustainability is a living definition, meaning it is expanding and new ideas are incorporated into how we approach, talk about, and achieve sustainability.

 

What is a sustainable campus?

A sustainable campus is one that develops processes and systems that help create a vibrant campus economy and high quality of life while respecting the need to sustain natural resources and protect the environment.

Sustainable programs are those that result from an institution’s commitment to environmental, social, and economic health.

Who we are

Sustainable Campus seeks to have our University serve as a living model of sustainability, providing learning experiences that students, faculty and staff may develop, apply and practice at FSU and in their extended communities.

Sustainable Campus is rooted in the values of sustainability as a process, we need the participation, collaboration, and action by our campus community to be truely sustainable.

How to get involved

We offer a variety of ways to participate in outreach and volunteer opportunities. A complete listing can be found on the Get Involved page of this site.

We welcome all students, staff, and faculty to join us in making Florida State a more sustainable campus!
summary: Sustainable Campus Department

name: Securities Society
description:
Securities Society focuses on pre-professional development for all careers in finance. We strive to further our member's experience at Florida State across our two programs: Mentorship & Partners.

Securities Society Mentorship

Mentorship is designed to help students secure summer internship offers, maximize success in the Noles on Wall Street program, utilize technical learning resources, stay informed with market trends, and navigate the opportunities available in financial services. 

 

Our weekly program is designed to empower ambitious students starting from an introductory understanding towards professional placement by crafting compelling narratives, mastering high-finance recruitment, and discovering an ideal career path.

 

We are open to all majors and academic years, although an emphasis is placed on students eligible for investment banking and junior internship programs (Class of 2026 and 2027).


Securities Society Partners

Partners I is the first part of a two-semester sequence designed to provide an applied introduction to value investing, financial analysis, public company research, and projection modeling.

 

The first level of this program provides the foundational knowledge to succeed in the spring course Partners II focused on investment banking, corporate valuation, and M&A practical skills.


Partners is designed to provide an introductory hands-on experience and assumes no prior knowledge outside of strong written and verbal communication skills as demonstrated in traditional academic programs and compositions (research papers or equivalent).
summary: Securities Society focuses on pre-professional development for all careers in finance. We strive to further our member's experience at Florida State across our two programs: Mentorship & Partners.

name: Health Educated Asian Leaders Club at FSU
description:
HEAL embodies the belief that, in order to stand out in the field of health, one must foster the attributes: compassion, innovation, and leadership. In the growing world of medicine, public health, and science, those who truly shine are those who step up and take charge of their own lives as well as lead others to do the same.

HEAL strives to offer various opportunities to its members such as first-hand medical practicing, philanthropic work, physician shadowing, and also leadership building, all while maintaining a tight-knit member group. As a group, the members of HEAL will grow as established scholars and leaders.
summary: HEAL is an affiliate of AASU which strives to promote leadership and ambition in pre-health students by providing a variety of opportunities geared toward nurturing an interest in health care and advocating for diversity.

name: Cardiology Interest Group
description: The purpose of the Cardiology Interest Group is to promote an interest and understanding of cardiac medicine at Florida State University College of Medicine. This will be accomplished primarily through informative meetings, organized lectures, and clinics, in which professionals will provide fundamental information related to the field of cardiology.
summary: The purpose of the Cardiology Interest Group is to promote an interest and understanding of cardiac medicine at Florida State University College of Medicine.

name: Music Teachers National Association at FSU
description: The purpose of this chapter is to enhance students' studies by providing educational, musical, social, and professional experiences and opportunities in order to develop leadership skills and community involvement.
summary: Our chapter will provide support for Florida State University music students, local musicians, and private teachers, raise awareness among students about the importance of music and music education, and create a unique music culture at FSU.

name: Radiology Interest Group at Florida State University
description:
The Radiology Interest Group is a student-run organization that seeks to foster interest in the fields of Diagnostic and Interventional Radiology. RIG aims to provide opportunities for students to learn about the field and get involved in research, shadowing, and mentorship. Additionally, we aim to support and guide medical students in their pursuit of radiology as a future career. 

We intend to host presentations, panel discussions, and workshops to expose students to the subspecialties of radiology, various imaging modalities, the residency application process, and on-going research in the field. Through these opportunities, we hope to enable students to make informed decisions regarding a possible future career in diagnostic or interventional radiology.
summary: The Radiology Interest Group seeks to foster interest in the fields of Diagnostic and Interventional Radiology. RIG aims to provide opportunities for students to learn about the field and get involved in research, shadowing, and mentorship.

name: Florida Association of Environmental Professionals
description:
The Florida Association of Environmental Professionals (FAEP) at FSU works closely with the Tallahassee Chapter of FAEP to provide insight and networking into the different career possibilities in the environmental field. Speakers from all environmental fields will provide all kinds of possibilities for work after graduation. 

Through networking socials, luncheons, and meetings, FAEP FSU will provide a safe and easy place for dreams to be achieved!

Follow us on Facebook here and Instagram here for all updates, meeting information, and events!
summary: FAEP at FSU helps students interested in working in the environmental field by providing networking opportunities & career advice from professionals in the environmental field. This includes internship opportunities & potential jobs after graduation.

name: Audio Production Club
description:
In this club, you'll find producers, musicians, songwriters, and audio engineers in all stages of development with a passion for digital sound and music! We welcome all levels of expertise and all genres of interest.

Attend any of our meetings to meet the members! Most meetings for the Spring 2026 semester will be held at Bellamy 0001, Thursdays at 7:30pm unless announced otherwise. Follow our Instagram @apcfsu to keep the most up to date with our events and meetings! The best way to stay in the APC loop is our GroupMe chat: https://groupme.com/join_group/43172096/sQ4tJG
summary: The Audio Production Club brings artists, audio engineers, musicians, producers, and music enthusiasts together to build skills and connections within the FSU and Tallahassee music community.

name: Center for Health Advocacy and Wellness
summary: The Center for Health Advocacy & Wellness at University Health Services encourages students to make healthy lifestyle decisions that facilitate academic success and lead to life-long health and wellness.

name: Volunteer Tallahassee
description: Volunteer Tallahassee (VOLT) is an organization that is committed to enhancing the community  With an environmental focus but a large breadth of community contacts, VOLT focuses on engaging members in local efforts and programs, and establishing connections with local NGOs. Examples of past VOLT involvement include: Seacrest wolf Preserve, VOLT organized Plant seek and destroy ( Coral Ardisia, an invasive species), Partnership with Hang Tough Foundation, Project Annie, the Big Bend Homeless Coalition, MDA walks, maintaining VOLT's street ( a portion of Jefferson), partnership with local animal shelter for fundraisers/dog washes, the Big Event, etc. Our main focus for the 2021-2022 year is to serve the greater Tallahassee community, not just on FSU's campus. We plan to serve safely and perhaps virtually if necessary. If you are interested in joining, please visit our Facebook page (www.facebook.com/groups/fsuvolt) for updated information on events and meetings!
summary: Volunteer Tallahassee (VOLT) is an organization that is committed to enhancing the community. With an environmental focus but a large breadth of community contacts, VOLT focuses on engaging members in local efforts and programs.

name: CodEducation Club at FSU
description: Technology is all around us. It's in our hands as we send a text or capture a moment through a third-party app like Snapchat. It's at our finger tips while typing on a laptop or desktop. It's in the classrooms that students attend and the elevators that carry them down & out. No matter the major of enrollment, technology is becoming more integrated in our everyday lives and this is the IoT (Internet of Things). Join Codeducation, where we teach the fundamentals of technology to students from all majors and skills. Hone in on marketable skills and grasp the outgoing influence that technology has towards other fields.
summary: Codeducation is an organization that provides opportunities for individuals interested in programming at any skill level, regardless of major, and promotes immersion in technology.

name: Big Sister Little Sister at Florida State University
description: The Kinship of Big Sister Little Sister at Florida State University is an organization that was created to encourage fellowship among all female students regardless of age, race, religion, creed, economic status, personal doctrines, and/or beliefs. We aim to ensure that the underclassmen ladies (freshmen and sophomores) are confident in and make the best of their collegiate experience here at Florida State University. This mentoring program is a great source of networking; providing both social and professional resources, and thus also creating more opportunities for members to become well-rounded and developed individuals. Furthermore, our organization strives to give back to the community by also mentoring high-school females that are considered at-risk for losing interest in their academics. Overall, the Kinship of Big Sister Little Sister at Florida State University intends to make a positive impact on the lives of young women in the Tallahassee community.
summary: The purpose of BSLS is promote and advocate for the uplift of young women, especially all female incoming/current freshman of Florida State University.

name: Connecting Girls To STEM (official)
description:
Connecting Girls to Stem, formerly Campus Girl Scouts, was created to help teach young girls in the Tallahassee community about topics and the opportunities in STEM.  Membership is open to all students at Florida State University.

 

MEMBERSHIP

·         Attend or be employed by FSU

·         Believe in the principles of Girl Scouts (Promise/Law).

·         Complete Volunteer Application

WHAT WE DO

Ø  Events planning

Ø  Program Consulting

Ø  Project Management

Ø  Public Relations

Ø  Troop/Group Leadership

Ø  Campus Tutoring

Ø  Community Task Groups/Committees

Ø  Recruitment

Ø  Fund Development

Ø  Mentoring

Ø  Research and Analysis

Ø  Camping

BENEFITS

·         Leadership

·         Community Service

·         Experience

·         Networking

·         Skill Building

·         New Friends

·         Civic Engagement

·         Personal Growth/Development

·         SO MUCH FUN!!!!



A member of the Girl Scout council staff will be assigned by the council as liaison to work with the group.
summary: Connecting Girls to Stem, formerly Campus Girl Scouts, was created to encourage and promote the values and ideals of Girl Scouting while serving the college campus and surrounding community.

name: The School of Teacher Education Graduate Student Association
description: The School of Teacher Education has many graduate tracks from Math Education to Special Education. Many of us rarely have a chance to interact and learn from one another or only do so in the odd class every once in a while. This group is an effort to provide a relaxed, student-only, space where we can develop a supportive community in which we productively share our ideas, get feedback, and learn from each other.
summary: The School of Teacher ed has many graduate tracks from Early Childhood Education to Math Education to Special Education. This group seeks to develop a supportive community in which we productively share our ideas, feedback, and learn from each other.

name: Syncopation Tap Troupe
description:
The Syncopation Tap Troupe at Florida State University is a student-led club that strives to give students the opportunity to continue tap dancing while in college through weekly practices and various performances throughout the year.

 

Our team was founded in 2016 with ten students who met once a week to tap and perform around the Tallahassee area. Today, Seminole Tap Troupe is a team of students who still meet once a week to share their passion for tap dancing. We have performed at First Friday, FSU's So You Think You Can Line Dance, Tallahassee's Springtime Tallahassee, FSU's Homecoming Spirit Night, FSU's Dance Marathon, Light the Night for Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, FSU's Relay for Life, Step Up: FSU Edition, the ASLC, as well as our Annual Showcase. We love sharing our passion for dance and coming together as different years, majors, and levels to be one big tap family. 

 

If you are interested in learning more about our team follow us on Facebook and Instagram, or send us an email at syncopationtaptroupe@gmail.com

 

Go Noles!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7_SGYsEAtCg
summary: The Syncopation Tap Troupe at FSU is a student-led club that strives to give students the opportunity to continue tap dancing while in college through weekly practices and various performances throughout the year.

name: Christians on Campus FSU
description: Christians on Campus FSU welcomes all believers holding fast the common Chrisitan faith as mentioned in Titus 1:4. Our campus ministry encourages and equips college students to mature in the divine life, grow in the biblical truth, and function in their Christian service. Come grow in Christ with us! Together our faith changes the world and hastens the coming back of our Lord Jesus to gain the whole earth (2 Peter 3:12, Psa. 2:8, Rev. 12: 10-11)
summary: Christians on Campus FSU is a registered student organization. As a club at FSU, we connect students with local Christian homes and business professionals to provide mentoring  and a home away from home. Together we love the Lord Jesus and His word.

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society
description: Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society is a society for students, particularly for undergraduates. It seeks to encourage scholarly attainment in this field of learning by reserving its regular membership for those who achieve superior academic records and who indicates special aptitude for and major interest in the life sciences. It desires to cultivate intellectual interest in the natural sciences and to promote a better appreciation of the value of biological study and thus welcomes into associate membership all those students who are interested in biology. Beta Beta Beta also endeavors to extend the boundaries of man's knowledge of nature by encouraging new discoveries through scientific investigation and to this end encourages undergraduate students to begin research work. Beta Beta Beta also strives to give back to our local community through philanthropy and volunteering work. FSU’s Sigma Tau Chapter was founded in 2002 and has been actively involved in research and campus life since then.
summary: Beta Beta Beta is a nationally recognized honor society that focuses on promoting research and giving students the opportunity to participate in biologically relevant events. We also frequently host panels and community service events for our members.

name: Realizing Everyone’s Need for Emotional Wellness
summary: RENEW (Realizing Everyone's Need for Emotional Wellness) is an undergraduate mental health advocacy and peer-education program sponsored by FSU Counseling & Psychological Services.

name: Operation Smile Club at Florida State University
description: Operation Smile Club at FSU is a fairly new club on campus and is in need of passionate members! The club is dedicated to spreading awareness about children with cleft lip and palate around the world. This service club works hard to fundraise for the surgical care these children need. The money fundraised is sent to the international Operation Smile Organization, who sends teams of surgeons to countries around to world to provide children with the surgical care they need. We also team up with many local projects at FSU such as NSSLHA, The Big Event, and the Rez goes Green to provide local service projects to its members! Be sure to contact us for more information about upcoming events!
summary: Operation Smile is a group of students passionate about spreading awareness about children affected by cleft lip and palate and raising money for surgical care to provide smiles to these children.

name: Human Development and Family Science Club
description:
The Purpose of the Family and Child Sciences Club is:

  • To provide the student body of FSU the opportunity to enhance their knowledge of lifespan development.
  • To provide service and opportunities that support children, adults and families.
  • To provide members with a network of information relevant to the intended current issues of family and child sciences and professional development opportunities.

This Organization is a great way to meet new people who are interested in this amazing major! Many of our members are either minoring or majoring in Family and Child Sciences, while some members just want to learn more and partake in volunteering opportunities! Come check us out, meet new people, and grow in knowledge of what Human Development and Family Sciences is all about!
summary: The Purpose of the Human Development and Family Sciences Club is to provide the student body of FSU the opportunity to enhance their knowledge of lifespan development, and to provide service and opportunities that support children, adults, and families.

name: Criminology and Criminal Justice Student Leadership Council
description:
The Student Leadership Council (SLC) is a Recognized Student Organization (RSO) by Florida State University’s Student Government Association. The SLC serves as the liaison between the student body, the college administration, and the FSU student government.

 

The council is composed of undergraduate majors and graduate students in criminology and criminal justice who work directly with the Dean, a faculty adviser, and the college administration to provide a student perspective. They create and oversee beneficial programming for students in an attempt to improve opportunities for personal and professional growth within the discipline.
summary: The CCJ Student Leadership Council serves as a liaison between the College and the students within the college. Together, we work to enhance the Criminology and Criminal Justice program here at FSU, and bring more opportunities to FSU.

name: American Sign Language Club at Florida State University
description:
The purpose of the ASL Club at Florida State University is for students to facilitate awareness of ASL and the Deaf community while developing their own skills and knowledge. The main focus is to encourage students to practice sign language in an active group setting. Members of the ASL Club will participate in ASL-based events and gatherings. As a club, we will gradually learn more about Deaf culture through interpersonal interactions while acquiring ASL skills.

Contact Jacqueline Tinoco for more information: jt23d@fsu.edu
summary: The purpose of the ASL Club at Florida State University is for students to facilitate awareness of ASL while developing their own skills and knowledge. The main focus is to encourage students to practice sign language in an active group setting.

name: Center for the Advancement of Human Rights
description:
The mission of CAHR is to facilitate interdisciplinary human rights courses, research, and events as well as engage in direct community and global human rights advocacy.  

The issuance of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights in 1948 began what some have called “The Human Rights Revolution.” Since that time, the human rights movement has become a global phenomenon, challenging people and governments everywhere to guarantee the basic rights of all the world’s citizens. As part of that Human Rights Revolution, Florida State University in 2000 established the Center for the Advancement of Human Rights.

The Center has the mandate of:

  • Facilitating the development of interdisciplinary human rights courses throughout the university and of fostering human rights scholarship;
  • Establishing human rights field placements for FSU students and engaging in direct human rights advocacy;
  • Supporting non-governmental organizations (NGOs) throughout the world that engage in human rights work.

Join us in our work!
summary: The mission of CAHR is to facilitate interdisciplinary human rights courses, research, and events as well as engage in direct community and global human rights advocacy.

name: Research Interest Group
description: The Research Interest Group (RIG) seeks to inform medical students at all stages of training about the rewards and opportunities of participating in research during their medical education and as future physicians.  RIG seeks to (1) engage medical students to participate in different types of research opportunities during their medical education; (2) equip medical students with resources and guidance to find success in future research endeavors; and (3) serve as a platform of communication between students, faculty, and alumni to share resources, find mentors, and encourage greater participation in research.
summary: The Research Interest Group (RIG) seeks to inform medical students at all stages of training about the rewards and opportunities of participating in research during their medical education and as future physicians.

name: University Housing
summary: To be added.

name: Residential Conduct Board
description:
The Residential Conduct Board is a group of on-campus students trained to hold informal hearings involving violations of the Florida State University's Student Conduct Code and Guide to Residence Living. The members of the Residential Conduct Board actively upholds University Housing policies to maintain a safe, responsible, and inclusive  campus community. The Residential Conduct Board provides a unique opportunity for residential students to serve the community by promoting care and accountability within the residence halls. Conduct Board hearings take place on Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday evenings, depending on case availability. Every Conduct Board Hearing consists of 3-5 Board members that review case documentation and conduct a hearing to hear testimony from the charged residential student(s).

 

As a member of the Residential Conduct Board, you will gain professional skills in conflict management, critical thinking and public speaking. Additionally, students selected for the Residential Conduct Board will participate in various university housing programs that promote ethical development and safety and security within the residence halls. This is a great leadership opportunity for those students interested in pursuing a career in student affairs, law, or government.

 

Students that are interested in joining the Residential Conduct Board must be a current resident of University Housing with no semester live-on requirement. Students must be in good conduct standing and good academic standing (minimum 2.5 GPA). In addition, members of the Residential Conduct Board are not able to serve as a Night Staff or Resident Assistant employee for University Housing. Residential Conduct Board typically seeks new members in the beginning of the fall semesters. 

 

If you are interested in becoming a member of the Residential Conduct Board, click HERE to apply.
summary: The Residential Conduct Board is a group of on-campus students dedicated and trained to help maintain our community standards, the FSU Code of Conduct, and the University Housing Guide to Residence Living.

name: Residential Scholars Program
summary: To be added.

name: InterProfessional Allies
description:
The purpose of the IPA is to educate and promote interprofessional collaboration at the Florida State University (FSU).  This will be done in the charter year primarily via coordinating meetings and events with other student organizations at FSU in which learners and professionals from multiple FSU colleges and professions respectively will participate in activities and discussions that promote collaboration and provide information concerning current issues in Medicine.

 

The members of IPA will have the opportunity to join regional and national professional organizations that promote interprofessional collaboration based on student interest and membership requirements.  These organizations will provide additional opportunities for other meetings and events to attend.

              

The IPA will promote involvement of students and professionals from various disciplines to foster the FSU mission of excellence in teaching, research, creative endeavors, and service with an innovative perspective of interprofessional collaboration.

 

The IPA will focus on activities that will transform the lives of our students and shape the future of our state and society in a climate of inquiry, engagement, collegiality, diversity, and achievement, in keeping with the FSU vision.

 

IPA will include membership from all Florida State University colleges.

 

A primary focus of IPA activities is to meet the needs of the local community through education and community outreach/ service learning with an interprofessional collaborative approach.
summary: The IPA will promote involvement of students and professionals from various disciplines to foster the FSU mission of excellence in teaching, research, creative endeavors, and service with an innovative perspective of interprofessional collaboration.

name: Cuban-American Student Association
description: The Cuban-American Student Association, also known as C.A.S.A., is committed towards enhancing the Cuban-American culture through social and service activities; to serve the needs of Cuban-American students; to unite and educate the student body and the Tallahassee community; to promote cultural exchange through the sharing of our Cuban heritage; to contribute to the well-being of the Hispanic community as a whole.
summary: The Cuban-American Student Association shall be committed towards enhancing the Cuban-American culture throughout the Florida State student body via social, service, education, and political activities.

name: ChemiNoles Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide a support network for FSU Chemistry graduate students. It will function to provide an opportunity for community among these students, to allow them to share research and teaching experiences, work together for scientific outreach, and serve as an organized voice for Chemistry graduate students at FSU. The organization will also seek to aide in professional development for students in the sciences both graduate and undergraduate, and promote the love of science in the greater community.
summary: This organization is for Chemistry Graduate Students looking to develop professionally, network in and out of the department, and take part in outreach within the city of Tallahassee as well as globally.

name: Social Spear
description:
If you are interested in Marketing, Advertising, professional networking, professional development, creative expression, and/or simply being a part of a group of dedicated students seeking to grow and learn then you are in the right place! 

Social Spear is an organization for all majors that are interested in the marketing industry.

With over 100 members, Social Spear strives to maintain an active presence at FSU. As a member of Social Spear, you will be exposed to exciting guest speakers with real-world marketing experience, graphic design workshops, as well as hands-on marketing games, challenges and more! 

Great for networking, out-of-the-classroom learning, and resume building!

There is no membership fee. 

Please email jtc22@fsu.edu if you have questions / are interested in joining!

Find more information through https://linktr.ee/socialspear_fsu?utm_source=linktree_profile_share&ltsid=35f81ddd-7b6a-4777-a51b-5260992fc24b
summary: Social Spear is an organization for all majors that are interested in the marketing industry. We aim to teach members how to market themselves as an individual through guest speakers, workshops, and marketing games.

name: White Mouse Productions at FSU
description: White Mouse Productions is committed to driving impactful social change through theatre. We achieve this by spearheading workshops, crafting innovative theatrical productions, and delivering compelling performances that resonate with audiences in Tallahassee and beyond. Our mission is to inspire, educate, and provoke action towards a more equitable and inclusive society.
summary: White Mouse Productions creates transformative theater for social change. Through workshops, innovative productions, and powerful performances, we inspire action and foster community engagement in Tallahassee and beyond.

name: Collegians For Christ Campus Ministry
description: The purpose of the Collegians for Christ Campus Ministry is to provide an extracurricular activity to those students and/or faculty interested in a more in depth study of the Bible in a Christian environment that welcomes discussion and questions. Collegians for Christ does not focus on a worship service but rather a relaxed Bible study setting where students can engage deeply in studying the Bible. Collegians for Christ provides opportunities for students to participate in Christian growth in the areas of study, prayer, fellowship, witness, and missions.
summary: Collegians for Christ Campus Ministry (CFC) is to provide an extracurricular activity to those students and/or faculty interested in a more in depth study of the Bible in a Christian environment that welcomes discussion and questions.

name: Graduate Literature Organization
summary: The Graduate Literature Organization seeks to 1) facilitate cohesion among graduate English literature students, 2) advocate on behalf of literature interests, 3) organize an annual symposium/conference, and 4) access further professionalization training

name: Sigma Alpha Iota
description: Sigma Alpha Iota is an organization which promotes interaction among those who share a commitment to music. Members of SAI are active in all areas of campus music and campus life, working closely with faculty, administration, campus and community groups, music professionals and patrons. In addition to personal encouragement and support, members may receive scholarships, loans and awards in many areas and at all levels of music-related study. Sigma Alpha Iota has long been recognized as a leader in the field of music and provides a lifetime of fraternity contact.
summary: Sigma Alpha Iota is an organization which promotes interaction among those who share a commitment to music. Women of SAI are active in all areas of campus music and campus life, working closely with faculty, administration, campus and community groups

name: Seminole Striders Running Club
description:
Striders of the State is a running club at FSU open to anyone willing to run, no matter their previous experience! We cater our workouts to meet every running level and our club incorporates both cross country and track style practices. For the 2025 Fall and Spring semester, we meet at Langford Green at 5:30 PM. We host a variety of runners, from those training to run in local 5k, 10k, half- and full- marathon races, to those just running for fun!  No attendance or running distance requirements! We are simply a community of runners (and friends!) who want to encourage one another to excel at our own paces, and would love to run with you! First official day of practice will be on 01/12/2025.

Join our Flare for weekly updates! ---> https://flare-event.app.link/GIigHp8GsUb

 

DSE Form (Sport Clubs > Run/FSU Run Club > Register) ---> https://fsu.dserec.com/online/dashboard
summary: Striders of the State is a running club at FSU! We are a no-experience needed club open to all, come join us on on our runs!

name: Golden Anchor Society
description:
This organization is tied to the Florida Agricultural and Mechanical Naval Reserve Officer Training Corps (NROTC) Unit, which represents midshipmen at TCC, FAMU, and FSU. A large portion of our unit is FSU students, a fact that is not often recognized. We aim to bring the Naval and Marine Corps ROTC culture to the FSU campus.

6.2.5
summary: This organization is tied to the Florida Agricultural and Mechanical Naval Reserve Officer Training Corps (NROTC) Unit and to the Veterans Student Union. We aim to bring the Naval and Marine Corps ROTC culture to the Florida State Campus.

name: Association for Women in Mathematics Student Chapter at Florida State University
description:
The AWM is a student organization, open to all undergraduate and graduate students, that hopes to encourage women and other minorities to study and to have active careers in the mathematical sciences, and to promote equal opportunity and the equal treatment of all people in mathematics.

We have monthly meetings where we focus on the professional development of our members, for example through resume workshops and research talks. We also have social events like potlucks, movies, and game nights where we include math-related activities.

We participate in events such as Math Fun Day and colloquiums. We also have a mentoring network where we facilitate communication and advice sharing between undergraduate and graduate students.
summary: The AWM is a student organization that hopes to encourage women and other minorities to study and to have active careers in the mathematical sciences, and to promote equal opportunity and the equal treatment of all people in mathematics.

name: Women's Club Basketball
description: The Woman's Basketball Club will be an all-girls club basketball team where we will have practice, games, and team bonding. Practice will happen multiple times a week, and games/tournaments roughly biweekly, depending on the schedule made in the fall with bordering universities. Team bonding will take place in the form of volunteering, fundraising for our team, and fun activities to bond us as a group. We are hoping to start in the spring and have tryouts in roughly September, there is a possibility for 2 teams. The garnet and gold teams will consist of girls who want the opportunity to play against other schools and even travel. The garnet team is the B team which will be brought up if gold members cannot go to a given tournament and will go to tournaments where other schools have a B team as well. The garnet team will only be established if a minimum of 10 committed players (not including the gold team) express interest in participating.
summary: The Women's Basketball Club will host practices, team bonding, and play in games against other universities. The executive board will be in charge of information and tryouts will be held in the fall and spring. Roughly 20 girls will make the travel teams

name: Literatures and Linguistics Interdisciplinary Conference at Florida State University
description:
The (L)iteratures & Linguistics (In)terdisciplinary (C)onference (LINC) is a graduate-led organization hosted by the Department of Modern Languages and Linguistics at Florida State University. Its mission is to provide graduate students with hands-on leadership experience in planning and hosting an interdisciplinary academic conference while fostering collaboration among graduate students from multiple departments across the university.

LINC supports scholarly exchange in linguistics, literature, and related fields by creating an inclusive academic space for graduate students to present research, engage in interdisciplinary dialogue, and build professional skills. Through conference participation and organization, members develop leadership, communication, and academic presentation skills that prepare them for future national and international conferences and for professional careers in academia and related fields.
summary: Annual interdisciplinary graduate student conference within the Department of Modern Languages and Linguistics at Florida State.

name: 30in60 Sketch Comedy Club
shortName: 30in60 Sketch Comedy; 30in60 Players; 30in60; Thirty in Sixty
description:
30in60 is a sketch comedy troupe original to Florida State's campus that relies on sketch-writing, workshopping, and in-show improvisation. In a random order that the audience decides, we perform 30 original sketches in 60 hysterical minutes, making the audience chuckle, giggle, laugh, hoot, holler, and repeatedly slap their hands together in delight. The troupe races against the clock to bring the people 60 minutes of hilarity with sketches about wacky characters or silly stories. But if the clock ticks past the hour, the audience gets a prize! We gotta go fast, so be on your toes for when a member calls, "Scene! Pick a number!"

Check us out on all the social sites:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/30in60/
YouTube: http://www.youtube.com/user/30in60

Instagram: @30in60fsu
Twitter: @30in60
summary: 30in60 is a sketch comedy troupe original to Florida State's campus. Twice a semester the troupe presents 30 original sketches in 60 minutes, in an order the audience decides! What happens if we don't beat the clock? Come find out!

name: Society for the Advancement of Material and Process Engineering Student Chapter at the FAMU-FSU College of Engineering
description:
Interested in Advanced Materials and Processing?

The Society for the Advancement of Material and Process Engineering (SAMPE) is a global professional member society, that provides information on new materials and processing technology either via conferences, exhibitions, technical forums, publications, or books in which professionals in this field can exchange ideas. ​As the only technical society encompassing all fields of endeavor in materials and processes, SAMPE provides a unique and valuable forum for scientists, engineers, and academicians.

Promoting market diversification, SAMPE disseminates information about the commercial and industrial applications of material and processing engineering. These include land transportation, construction, marine, biomedical and medical, sports and recreation and industrial applications. It is SAMPE's opinion that the well being and career development of its members as well as the industry by which they are employed requires that technology transfer be successful.

Membership Benefits
As a SAMPE member, we welcome you to take advantage of offers opportunities and services available only to SAMPE members:

Meetings, Seminars, and Literature

  • Meetings, seminars, publications, and technical papers are forums available for members to discover and access the most advanced materials and processes being developed by the scientific community. Local chapters provide organized gatherings, allowing you to meet others in your community.

Networking Opportunities

  • For many of its dedicated members, the most important aspect of SAMPE is the ability to help make new contacts, forge enduring associations, and promote lasting friendships, as well as significant career advancements.

Employment Assistance

  • SAMPE offers strong employment assistance for members through its international network of contacts.

Leadership Growth

  • Active members are encouraged to become an officer and serve in SAMPE's international arena. SAMPE is the only technical society disseminating military and NASA sponsored advanced material information.

SAMPE Journal Subscription

  • Your complimentary subscription to this informative and acclaimed bi-monthly publication will provide you with a steady stream of technical articles, industry and international technical news, product and new literature announcements, book reviews, technical events calendars, and local SAMPE information.
summary: SAMPE is a global professional member society, that provides information on new materials and processing technology.   We focus in the areas of composites, polymers, and nanomaterials. We are based out of the College of Engineering, but welcome everyone.

name: Turning Point USA at Florida State University
summary:
The official grassroots conservative student movement at Florida State University.

- Educate
- Empower
- Mobilize

name: Men's Club Basketball
description:
Men’s Basketball Club at Florida State is a competitive Sport Club. We practice for 2 hours, 2 times a week. We are a part of NCBBA, but also compete in other local tournaments.

Quick Facts:

Roster Size: Approximately 26 making up 2 teams (About 13 each)

Home Field & Practice Space: Leach Gymnasium &/or Tully Gymnasium

Fall Practice Times: 2 days a week, 2 hours a day

Spring Practice Times: 2 days a week, 2 hours a day

Governing Body: National Club Basketball Association (NCBBA)

Conference: South Atlantic – South

Regular Opponents: Florida Gulf Coast, Nova Southeastern, University of Florida, University of Central Florida, Ole Miss,  University of Miami, and other local schools
summary: Florida State Men’s Basketball Club is a competitive Sport Club. We practice for 2 hours about 2 times a week. We are a part of NCBBA, but also compete in other local tournaments.

name: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine
description: The National Society of Black Women of Medicine is an organization that strives to provide underrepresented black women with the skill set, knowledge and experience  to successfully emerge in health care fields. We strive to uplift the lives of women in the black community who aspire to obtain an occupation in medicine volunteer opportunities, unity, mentorship and research. We seek to empower the minds of our future female health professionals through the strategies that we implement. We ensure that black underrepresented women know they are trailblazers in the health field
summary: Black Women of Medicine is an organization that strives to provide underrepresented black women with the skills, knowledge and experiences to professional participation in health care fields. We strive to uplift the lives of women in the black community.

name: Seminole Dining
description:
About

Seminole Dining brings the best of Florida to campus, offering dining experiences that are Unique, Local, and Authentic. We are a diverse family of talented chefs and nutrition experts dedicated to food-focused menus and student-centered service. We promise our guests real, fresh, and delicious food.

FSUnique™

Better From Scratch means local ingredients, prepared by hand, in our kitchens on campus, by our innovative culinary team. Our Seminole Test Kitchen incubates culinary innovations and creates FSUnique menus that are chef-tested and community-approved.
summary: Seminole Dining brings the best of Florida to campus, offering dining experiences that are Unique, Local, and Authentic. We are a diverse family of talented chefs and nutrition experts dedicated to food-focused menus and student-centered service.

name: Power of We
description: Power of WE aspires to be a pioneer in a national discourse across university campuses. Through interactive programming that convenes diverse members of the campus community, Power of WE aims to build student capacity to engage across different perspectives, exchange contrasting ideas, and embrace shared experiences, in an effort to prepare citizen leaders poised to tackle the challenges of a complex, polarized world.
summary: Power of WE is a student-led, University initiative that seeks to inspire a campus culture of innovation and collaboration by challenging echo chambers, building shared spaces, and bridging civic divides.

name: Humanities and Medicine Society
description: The purpose of HAMS is to educate and promote the Humanities at Florida State University (FSU). This will be done primarily via meetings in which students, professionals, and physicians participate in discussions and provide information concerning current issues related to the Humanities, ethics, and medicine.
summary: HAMS is an organization at the FSU COM to promote conversations among medical students concerning relevant interdisciplinary topics, thereby bridging medicine and the Humanities.

name: Saudi Students Association
description:
The objectives of the organization shall be:

  1. To assist and provide support to Saudi Students in the University.
  2. To hold events that aim to promote the rich Saudi culture and to help them get involved in on and off campus.
  1. To collaborate with other clubs and participate in the University’s events representing Saudi Arabia.
  1. To provide a forum for dialog to all students.
summary: Help and support Saudi students and their families in FSU, also to show Saudi's culture "customs & traditions" to American students, International students, and the community of Tallahassee

name: Nu Rho Psi - Beta of Florida Chapter
description:
The Florida State Beta Chapter of Nu Rho Psi is the National Honor Society in Neuroscience, founded in 2006 by the Faculty for Undergraduate Neuroscience, and was initially represented by the Neuroscience Undergraduate Student Association (NUSA). Membership is by application and is open to undergraduates, graduates, and alumni of neuroscience.

The purpose of Nu Rho Psi shall be to:

(1) encourage professional interest and excellence in scholarship, particularly in neuroscience;

(2) award recognition to students who have achieved such excellence in scholarship;

(3) advance the discipline of neuroscience;

(4) encourage intellectual and social interaction between students, faculty, and professionals in neuroscience and related fields;

(5) promote career development in neuroscience and related fields;

(6) increase public awareness of neuroscience and its benefits for the individual and society; and

(7) encourage service to the community.

You can also attend NUSA meetings and participate in their outreach events to gain additional points towards local membership.

Please follow us on Instagram (@fsu_nurhopsi) for announcements and updates!
summary: Nu Rho Psi is the National Honor Society in Neuroscience, founded in 2006 by the Faculty for Undergraduate Neuroscience. Membership is by application and is open to undergraduates, graduates, and alumni of neuroscience.

name: Student Veterans Law Symposium
description: An organization of students dedicated to the legislative advancement of  legal issues affecting veterans, military and veteran families, and students interested in military service.
summary: An organization of students dedicated to the legislative advancement of veterans' issues and the support of veterans within the legal community.

name: Hearts for the Homeless Tallahassee
description: Hearts for the Homeless (H4H) Tallahassee serves to provide FSU students with a unique volunteering experience. Its mission aims to enlighten and impassion students that volunteer their help and skills for a greater cause. Furthermore, it works to help the homeless community in Tallahassee by providing free blood pressure screenings and creating caring interactions between volunteers, homeless individuals, and all of those in the community. Hearts for the Homeless Tallahassee plans to work with other non-profit organizations, veterans’ organizations, health care clinics and medical professionals to enhance the effectiveness of this registered student organization. This organization aspires to help shape and mold the minds of our members into knowledgable, compassionate leaders.
summary: Hearts for the Homeless Tallahassee seeks to contribute to the health and well-being of one of the most vulnerable populations in our nation through free blood pressure screenings, education, and outreach opportunities for students who wish to contribute

name: CHAARG
description:
CHAARG aims to ignite a passion in college-aged girls for health + fitness ++ shows them that fitness can [+ SHOULD] be fun! Since every girl has a different preference of what fun is, we create opportunities for our CHAARG girls to “find their fit.” We do this by partnering with local studios once a week to lead a workout, whether it be CrossFit, Pure Barre, or salsa dancing — and everything in between.

Once a girl joins CHAARG, she is welcomed into a supportive + empowering community of 10,000+ girls! When you workout, you feel good — but, when you surround yourself with people to support you, you feel even better. CHAARG combines good friends + a good workout to keep every girl feeling her happiest + healthiest self.

If you are interested in joining the movement, you can grab your membership HERE! Please contact Alex at fsuchaarg@gmail.com if you have any questions.
summary: CHAARG stands for Changing Health, Attitudes + Actions to Recreate Girls. It is an organization at over 100 universities that aims to ignite a passion for health + fitness in college aged girls, while showing them that fitness can [+ SHOULD] be fun!

name: Boxing Club
description:
Our club is open to all skill levels, from beginners learning the fundamentals to experienced boxers sharpening their technique.

We offer instructional training sessions focused on proper form, footwork, conditioning, and ring strategy, all taught in a supportive, safety-first environment. Members can expect a mix of technical drills, cardio workouts, and sparring opportunities, designed to build confidence, fitness, and self-defense skills.

  •   Roster Size: 25-40
  •   Governing Body: USIBA, USA Boxing

  • If you have questions regarding attendance, membership, and others, please email an22o@fsu.edu or lld23@fsu.edu so we can provide you with thorough answers and get you started on the team. We welcome every skill level, new or seasoned fighters. 

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM @FSUBOXING
summary: Established in 2011, the FSU Boxing Club is a student-run organization for students interested in collegiate-level boxing and competition.

name: Arab Student Union
description: This organization connects Arab students at FSU with each other to form a community where they meet other people with similar experiences and backgrounds in order to elevate their sense of belonging here. We host events during the semester as well as guest speakers and General Body Meeting in order to reach out and interact with others on campus who might not know or understand the cultures they are from.
summary: We aim to unite Arab Students at FSU and create a community based on similar experiences and backgrounds that is also open to inform and educate others about our different and diverse cultures.

name: FL Zeta Phi Delta Epsilon
description:
PhiDE provides educational opportunities, supports philanthropic projects, and serves as a foundation for the development of relationships among medical professionals throughout a medical career.

Through a host of planned activities during the academic year, students develop their leadership and professional skills, which complement a basic understanding of medical science. When the time comes to apply for medical school or match for residency, active membership in PhiDE is evidence of that a student is well rounded and motivated. In addition to our numerous resources for the MCAT and USMLE, there are many chapter activities to help relieve the stress of studying.

Since 1904, when PhiDE's professional coeducational organization was founded, PhiDE is continuously striving to meet the following objectives:

  • To promote fellowship, equality, and unity.

  • To promote the highest scientific and educational standards in the field of

    medicine.

  • To promote, uphold, and maintain the highest standards of ethics in the practice

    of medicine, medical teaching, and research.

  • To promote non-profit service to the community.

  • To promote discussions at all Fraternity level of current social and economic

    issues as related to medicine, the practice of medicine, and other health care related matters.phide.org

 

If interested, please contact Daniel Lang, the Vice President of Recruitment, at fsuphide@gmail.com
summary: Phi Delta Epsilon is an International professional medical fraternity supporting the need for camaraderie and mentoring among medical students.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America at Florida State University
description: ALPFA (Association of Latino Professionals For America) is the longest standing Latino organization with 95,000+ members assembled in 44 professional and more than 160 student chapters across America. Our vision is to be the premier business organization for expanding opportunities for Latino leadership in the global market.
summary: ALPFA is a national, not-for-profit professional association that creates opportunities, adds value, and builds relationships for its members, the community and its business partners while expanding Latino Leadership in the global workforce.

name: Graduate Researchers of Geography
description: The Graduate Researchers of Geography (GRG) is a creative and supportive community for graduate students in the Department of Geography. The purpose of the organization is to unite graduate students, while providing a forum for discussions on geographical, educational, pedagogical, or professional topics.
summary: This organization serves as a creative and supportive community for graduate students in the Department of Geography.

name: Institute of Transportation Engineers FAMU-FSU Student Chapter
description: Welcome to the Institute of Transportation Engineers (ITE) Student Chapter at Florida State University and Florida A&M University. This is a group of students from all majors with an expressed interest in transportation engineering and urban planning. The organization is a branch of the Student Government Association (SGA) and its activities are in accordance with the parent organizations: International ITE, Florida Puerto Rico District ITE. The main goal of our organization is to foster closer association with industry leaders, researchers, and educators through general body meetings, seminars, and conferences with a professional spirit, and encourage common transportation interests among the student members.
summary: ITE FAMU-FSU Student Chapter introduces its members to the transportation profession and promotes close association with industry leaders, researchers, and educators through general body meetings, seminars, and conferences.

name: The Student Academy of The American Academy of Physician Assistants at FSU
description: 1. The Student Academy of The American Academy of Physician Assistants at Florida State University (SAAAPA-FSU) aims to serve as the official organization for the students of the FSU Masters of Physician Assistant Practice. SAAAPA-FSU is dedicated to fostering intellectual curiosity and excellence of the PA profession as well as promoting activism for the health of our community. SAAAPA-FSU is also committed to promoting inter-professional collaboration within the FSU College of Medicine and beyond.
summary: The Student Academy of The American Academy of Physician Assistants at FSU will represent the PA Program of the College of Medicine to the community at large. SAAAPA-FSU will participate and advocate for students at both the state and federal level.

name: Flag Football Club at Florida State University
description: Club Flag Football at Florida State is a flag football team for FSU students. This team will have the opportunity to travel and compete with other collegiate flag football teams across the nation as well as local teams. We will focus on teamwork and learning the different components of the game while striving to reach our full potential. This team-based environment will improve the skills of the players not only on the field, but off the field as well. Our goal is to create an enjoyable, safe and competitive outlet for all students participating in our club team.
summary: Florida State's Club Flag Football is a flag football team for FSU students to travel and participate in games and tournaments with other collegiate teams. Currently, we only compete in women’s competitions.

name: Medical Brigades at FSU
description: Medical/Dental Brigades at Florida State University is a chapter of Global Brigades, the largest student-run international movement in the world.
