We work in remote, rural, and under-resourced communities in Honduras, Panama, Guatemala, Nicaragua, Greece, Ghana, and more, who would otherwise have limited to no access to health care. These communities receive a brigade every three to four months where hundreds of patients are provided access to healthcare and volunteers deliver public health workshops. Our chapter will contribute by taking an annual trip abroad for 7-9 days at a stationed clinic in one of the four countries offered by Global Brigades. Electronic patient records are collected for future visitations and to monitor overall community health trends. In conjunction with our Medical Program, Global Brigades also supports communities with economic development, sanitation and clean water projects, and uniquely implements these programs in a holistic model to meet a community’s health and economic goals. Our model systematically builds community ownership and collaboratively executes programs with the end goal of sustainably evolving to a relationship of impact monitoring.
summary: Medical Brigades is a movement of students and medical professionals working alongside local communities, in both Tallahassee and abroad, to implement sustainable health systems.

name: Seminole Student Boosters
description: Mission: To instill the tradition of supporting Florida State athletics in current students and to generate financial contributions to enrich the overall educational and athletic experience for over 500 student-athletes participating in 20 sports programs.
summary: Join the only Student Organization on campus dedicated to Florida State Athletics!

name: The Sports Analytics Club at FSU
description:
Fall 2025 Meetings will be at RBA 104 from 6:00pm-7:00pm

Meeting Dates: 9/8, 9/22, 10/6, 10/20, 11/3, 11/17 and 12/1
summary: The Sports Analytics Club was founded in order to bring the University together through sports and data analysis. We focus on the development of our members through projects, learning from leaders in the industry, and supporting our members' endeavors.

name: Minorities on a Mission Founding Chapter
description: This organization was established to create more opportunities for Pre-med, nursing, pharmacy, dental, and all other pre-health professions, to gain clinical experience while traveling abroad on a medical mission trip. We aspire to serve underserved populations on both a global and local spectrum as minorities of the future medical field.
summary: Creating the opportunity for Pre-med, nursing, pharmacy, dental, and all other pre-health professions, to take an annual mission trip to a underserved population.

name: FSU Athletics
description:
Support the Seminoles all year long and join us at all the home games and events. Download the Spear It Rewards app to earn points for attending events to redeem for exclusive prizes and to improve your chance for student ticket lotteries. For more information or a full schedule of events and upcoming games, visit seminoles.com

 

If you are interested in interning with the marketing department, visit seminoles.com/marketing
summary: Seminole Sports Marketing

name: Garnet and Gold Investment Club
description: Garnet and Gold Investment Club is a welcoming community where everyone can learn the essentials of investing. Whether you're majoring in business or another field, our resources are designed to help you invest in both yourself and your finances. With around 80% of Americans making poor financial decisions, our mission is to change that trend. We offer financial literacy workshops in a fun and friendly environment that is inclusive for all. Additionally, we are dedicated to career development, helping students explore and identify potential career paths they wish to pursue.
summary: A financial literacy organization who's goal is to inform students on how to manage their money, and the different ways that they can accumulate wealth.

name: The Diversity and Inclusion Institute
description: The Diversity and Inclusion Institute is an executive project within the Student Government Association at Florida State University. The mission of this program is to provide a space for Florida State University students to fully understand the range of diversity and multiculturalism, including but not limited to ability, socio-economic, age, rural/urban, veterans, sexual orientation, ethnicity, race, gender identity & expression, religion, and faith structures through workshops that are focused on awareness, discussion and collaborative learning activities. Through the 8-week program held in the Spring Semester, participants are challenged to gain knowledge about how to support fellow students with various identities to create a more united campus. Weekly 2-hour workshops ensure that this vision can be met.
summary: The Diversity and Inclusion Institute is an executive project within the Student Government Association at Florida State University aimed at increasing awareness about social identity and leadership development through weekly workshops and activities

name: The Renegade Leadership Honor Society
description: The Renegade Leadership Honor Society strives to uplift and recognize Black leadership at the Florida State University. By establishing a network of success, we will pave a path for the advancement of future generations. With unconquered spirit, we aim to support Black and minority students in our communities.
summary: This organization is the premier youth society of black leaders that strive to create a network dedicated towards the success and advancement for people of African descent.

name: Gift of Life at FSU
description: Gift of Life at FSU raises awareness for the bone marrow donation process. In collaboration with the Gift of Life Marrow Registry, one of the nation's public bone marrow and blood stem cell registries, Gift of Life at FSU organizes events, campaigns, trainings, and seminars to register new potential donors, raise monetary funds, and educate the community about the bone marrow donation process. Gift of Life at FSU is dedicated to expanding the bone marrow registry and supporting those who have been affected by the cancer experience.
summary: Gift of Life at FSU organizes events to register new potential donors in the bone marrow donor registry and raise awareness for the bone marrow donation process.

name: Society of Undergraduate Mathematics Students
description: The mission of SUMS is to promote a greater sense of mathematical community within Florida State University and provide resources to aid in student success, especially as it relates to mathematics. We host guest speakers, coordinate volunteer opportunities along with a host of exciting meetings throughout the semester. Follow us on Instagram for updates or join our GroupMe! @fsu.sums
summary: The mission of SUMS is to promote a greater sense of mathematical community within Florida State University and provide resources to aid in student success, especially as it relates to mathematics. Follow us on instagram for updates! @fsu.sums

name: Jewish Medical Student Association
description: The mission of the Jewish Medical Student Association (JMSA) is to serve both the spiritual and educational needs of FSU's medical students. We will strive to connect medicine to the principles of Judaism and explore how they can be further related to patient care. Additionally, as a medical community within a larger University community, we plan to maintain strong relationships with other Jewish communities of FSU and partner in a variety of Jewish events campus-wide. JMSA sanctioned events will include community outreach, guest speakers specializing in both Judaism and medicine, along with traditional social events such as Jewish holiday celebrations. It is with great pleasure to offer our community this service and to continue to spread the understanding of medicine in Judaism and vice versa.
summary: The mission of the Jewish Medical Student Association is to serve both the spiritual and educational needs of FSU's medical students. We will strive to connect medicine to the principles of Judaism.

name: Afro-Latinx Student Association
description: As an Afro-Latinx organization, we spread information about our rich Afro-Latinx culture that runs deep in our roots. We strive to teach those to learn to embrace their culture and spread awareness for our community in order to give Afro-Latinx a sense of identity.
summary: As an Afro-Latinx organization, we spread information about our rich Afro-Latinx culture that runs deep in our roots. We strive to teach those to learn to embrace their culture and spread awareness to our community.

name: Women Wednesday's Association at FSU
description: Women Wednesday's Association at FSU is a social, community-building organization that aims to help female students with life during college and the transition after graduation. We partner with on and off-campus organizations, provide education about access to campus resources, and offer low-stress technical workshops. Women Wednesdays holds bi-weekly meetings to help you get connected, stay healthy, and educated while in college. We strive to create personal, social, and professional growth by promoting campus resources, volunteer opportunities, and external partnerships on social media.
summary: Women Wednesday's Association at FSU is a social, mentoring organization that helps women with life during college and the transition after graduation by promoting campus resources.

name: Florida Restaurant and Lodging Association at Florida State
description:
The FSU chapter of Florida Restaurant and Lodging Association is a professional hospitality organization that aims to help Dedman College of Hospitality students broaden their knowledge about the various sectors of the hospitality industry through guest speakers, social events, local site visits, and annual trips around the U.S.  

In order to become a member of FRLA at FSU, interested students must fill out the Google Form Membership Survey, join the FRLA at FSU GroupMe chat, follow FRLA at FSU on social media, and lastly pay club dues. If you have any questions about this membership process, or if you are looking to get more involved in the organization, please reach out directly to the current FRLA at FSU President, Nydia Clement, who will be more than happy to answer any questions and send over the links to the above information. Her email address is nac21c@fsu.edu.
summary: The FSU chapter of the Florida Restaurant and Lodging Association aims to help hospitality students enter into the various sectors of the industry through guest speakers, site visits, and networking opportunities.

name: Beach Volleyball Club at FSU
description: The Beach Volleyball Club at FSU hopes to create a more established group for the community of beach volleyball players at Florida State. Our goal is to be a successful Club Sport at Florida State, so that we may compete under a national governing body. We are a coed community who competes in tournaments, participates in service, and supports the other club sports and organizations at Florida State.
summary: The Beach Volleyball Club FSU hopes to create a more established group for the community of beach volleyball players at Florida State. Our goal is to be a successful Club Sport at Florida State, so that we may compete under a national governing body.

name: Car Club at Florida State University
description: Welcome to Car Club at FSU! This is a club for all automotive enthusiasts. We organize group activities in and around Tallahassee, including weekly meets, autocross, club dinners, photoshoots, and more! We welcome all students regardless of what type of car you have, or no car at all!
summary: The club is simply for car enthusiasts to meet and make friends with people that have similar interests. Please fill out new member application!

name: Synthesis Dance Company
description: Synthesis Dance Company is an enthusiastic community of dance lovers. We come together to learn and fuse all styles of dance. During our public lessons on campus, we focus on the Latin dances such as bachata. As an FSU club, we strive to bring students a fun environment to communicate through various forms of dance.
summary: Synthesis Dance Company is a dance club that offers Latin Dance classes and social dancing on campus and around Tallahassee. We focus primarily on the Sensual Bachata Style of dancing, but we also often teach certain Bachatango and Bachata Fusion moves.

name: The League of Graduate Artists
description:
Working Method Contemporary is the dedicated gallery space for Masters of Fine Art Students at Florida State University. The program is composed of approximately thirty graduate students pursuing a terminal degree in Studio Art. The gallery provides students a space for exhibition, experimentation, and documentation of works created while attending Florida State.

We operate as an alternative, non-traditional space dedicated to showing recent works, as well as collaborations, by students currently enrolled in the program. On select Thursdays the gallery opens a new exhibition for the public to explore. These exhibitions include painting, sculpture, photography, ceramics, video works, installations, and performance art. Working Method Contemporary Gallery also offers a platform for the students to participate in the art world at large. In the past, Working Method showed work from all gallery artists at Fountain Art Fair, which runs concurrently with Art Basel Miami. These two forums for contemporary art are held yearly in greater Miami. Each December, these events draw artists, art galleries, museums, and collectors from all over the globe providing international exposure to all participants.

While the premises is provided to the graduates by the university, Working Method is run by student volunteers, who are responsible for maintenance, publicity, and all other day to day gallery functions. The primary source of support for the gallery comes from donations and fundraising activities initiated by the students themselves.

If you are interested supporting Working Method Contemporary gallery by donations, have questions, comments, or would like to touch base with one of our artists, please contact our gallery: workingmethodcontemporary@gmail.com.
summary: LGA provides MFA students a gallery space for exhibitions, documentation, and curation experience of work while attending Florida State University.

name: Sales Club at FSU
description: The FSU Sales Institute Sales Club exists to connect highly motivated students to professionals in the workforce. Members of our club can connect with speakers for potential jobs, internships, or simply to learn how to be successful in the workforce.
summary: Professional student organization dedicated to guide Business students toward a Sales career in different industries by bringing guest speakers who are leaders in their industries.

name: The Animal Society at FSU
summary: We are a student-led organization focused on volunteering & having fun with animals in the Tally area by hosting events, bringing educational guest animal speakers, and much more! We hope to see you there🩵

name: Student Florida Education Association
description: We are a committed group of aspiring educators who believe in preparing ourselves to be the best possible version we can become. SFEA supports aspiring educators to help grow their love of teaching, the joy of reading, and serving our community through outreach programs. The liability is just an added bonus to your membership. We offer professional development, resources, state and national conferences – a network of over 50,000 aspiring educators around the country.
summary: We are a committed group of aspiring educators who believe preparing ourselves to be the best possible version we can become. The liability is just an added bonus to your membership. We offer professional development, resources, state and national confer

name: Friday Night Live
description: Friday Night Live is FSU's only comedy variety show, featuring live sketches, digital shorts, news, and music. We follow the format of NBC's Saturday Night Live, including Weekend Update, guest hosts and musical acts.
summary: Friday Night Live is FSU's only comedy variety show, featuring live sketches, digital shorts, news, and music. We follow the format of NBC's Saturday Night Live, including Weekend Update, guest hosts and musical acts.

name: Anthropology Graduate Student Association
description: The Anthropology Graduate Student Association (AGSA) provides a community to graduate students who are studying or are simply interested in anthropology at Florida State University. AGSA offers engaging professional and social opportunities by hosting prominent guest lectures, organizing skill-building and volunteer opportunities, and continuing annual events such as the Hale G. Smith Pig Roast and the Robert C. Dailey Fish Fry.
summary: The Anthropology Graduate Student Association (AGSA) provides a community to graduate students who are studying or are simply interested in anthropology at Florida State University.

name: Spanish Graduate Student Organization
description: This organization was created to serve graduate students in the Spanish graduate program of the Department of Modern Languages and Linguistics, by offering opportunities for professional and academic development and interdisciplinary networking opportunities. Through this organization, graduate students interested in Spanish language instruction and research can take part in academic and cultural events, including guest speakers, workshops, and community outreach events.
summary: This organization was created to aid graduate students interested in Spanish language instruction and research through academic and cultural events, including guest speakers and workshops.

name: Department of Urban and Regional Planning Student Association
description:
(a) Promote professional and intellectual stimulation among the students by service as a vehicle for exploration of ideas outside the formal classroom contents;

​

(b) Facilitate the exchange of information between the faculty, staff, and students of the Department of Urban and Regional Planning at Florida State University;

​

(c) Promote good fellowship through the sponsorship of social activities;

​

(d) Assist new planning students and visiting educators through such activities as determined by the executive committee or as the membership may direct.
summary: This organization has been formed for the purpose of professional development, community outreach, networking, academic productivity, and mentorship of students pursuing careers in Urban and Regional Planning.

name: Chess Club at FSU
description: The Chess Club at FSU was established with the primary goal of enhancing the chess experience for both novice and seasoned chess enthusiasts at FSU. The club is dedicated exclusively to the art of chess, encompassing various activities such as gameplay, exploring openings, mastering endgames, and tackling puzzles. Members will engage in friendly matches with individuals of all skill levels, participate in local tournaments, and benefit from informative lectures.
summary: The Chess Club at FSU exists to allow experienced and new chess players alike to compete against each other in chess. The club is focused on developing everyone as a chess player in all stages of the game, as well as simply enjoying the game.

name: Terminal Medical Ethics
description: Many medical caregivers desire to maintain a personal and professional posture that is consistent with the four principles of medical ethics: Autonomy, Beneficence, Non-Maleficence, and Justice. The sheer scope of medicine, variety of ethical dilemmas encountered, and speed at which the medical profession advances can make it difficult to develop a logically coherent and personally satisfying ethical “code” in this regard. Terminal Medical Ethics endeavors to add clarity by promoting civil discussion about these medical ethical issues in the context of invited presentations, small group meetings and collaboration, and recommended readings. Ethical issues of particular interest to the Terminal Medical Ethics organization include: end-of-life care; physician-assisted suicide; elective abortion; human embryonic stem cell research, human cloning, and the status of human embryos in reproductive medicine; defining human death, and the ethical procurement of human organs for transplantation; direct or indirect use of fetal human cells and/or products therefrom as medical therapeutics.
summary: TME seeks to add clarity by promoting civil discussion about Life/End-of-Life Issues in the context of invited presentations, small group meetings, and recommended readings.

name: Minority Student Nurses Association
description:
The Minority Student Nursing Association, in short MSNA, garners much of its values and standards by those of the National Coalition of Ethnic Minority Nurse Associations. MSNA strives to broaden the scope of knowledge on diversity in the healthcare field; advocate for positive advancement and innovation in healthcare; demonstrate a standard of excellence in our practice; grow as leaders within our fields; and endorse ethnic minority nurse leadership in areas of health policy, practice, education and research. These goals are met by involvement with and servicing the Tallahassee community, FSU campus, and other student organizations, intending to create a positive impact, while also growing and teaching the members of the association, from all backgrounds, what diversity truly is and how best to advocate for this vision. As an all inclusive club, individual of ALL backgrounds are encouraged to participate and contribute to the organization's current level of comprehension, as well as obtain and implement insight from other members. As MSNA grows the goal is for its influence to spread and be an instrument for facilitating the progression and amelioration of the healthcare field and communities served by it.

The Minority Student Nursing Association plans to execute its ambitious goals by engaging in community service in areas that inspire the members; hosting speakers knowledgeable about diversity in the healthcare field; nominating members to present on topics that explore and achieve the goals of the organization; encouraging engagement from the public through fun and educational events; and developing leaders and thinkers for the present and future.  

Diversity starts with one individual, the Minority Student Nursing Association aims at unifying this diversity one member at a time.
summary: MSNA advocates for equity and justice, exploration and encouragement of diversity in the healthcare field. We encourage Pre-Nursing and Nursing students from all backgrounds to join our community and add their own perspective to build our insight.

name: Thrift Club at FSU
description:
Our goals:

- Create a community of students at FSU who share a love for secondhand shopping and all things vintage.
- Promote ethical ways to buy and donate clothing to help the environment and our local community.
- Giving donated clothes a second life through local organizations in Tallahassee.
- Teach students how to earn extra income through reselling thrifted finds online.

 

Our objectives:

- Meet at least bi-weekly to educate students and achieve the goals of Thrift Club.
- Organize thrift outings where we go to thrift shops and estate sales as a group.
- Hold workshops to help students get started reselling their clothes.
- Collaborate with other organizations to further advance Thrift Club's values.
summary:
Thrift Club is a community of students at FSU who share a love for secondhand shopping and all things vintage.
At our core we support sustainability, entrepreneurship, and community involvement!

name: Alliance for Black Women
description: Alliance for Black women is an organization that strives to empower Black women through service and sisterhood. We meet to discuss current events and provide support to sisters. We also host a variety of internal bonding events for sisters that include retreats and more. There are opportunities for community service, professional development, and growth.
summary: Alliance for Black Women is an organization that strives to promote the holistic development of Black women through empowerment, sisterhood, and service.

name: Campus Event Services
summary: Coming soon!

name: If/When/How: Lawyering for Reproductive Justice at FSU Law
description:
If/When/How is a national non-profit network of law students, professors, and legal professionals committed to fostering the next wave of legal experts for the reproductive justice movement. Mobilizing and mentoring new lawyers and scholars is a long-term strategy that will build capacity, vision, and leadership for a more successful reproductive justice movement. Law functions as both a catalyst to prompt the expansion of rights and a tool with which to hold governments and communities accountable for the delivery of liberty, equality, dignity, and fairness promised by those rights.

Reproductive justice will exist when all people can exercise the rights and access the resources they need to thrive and to decide if, when, and how to create and sustain a family with dignity, free from discrimination, coercion, or violence. We dedicate ourselves to ensuring access to medically accurate, age-appropriate, culturally competent sex and sexuality education, as well as comprehensive, quality reproductive healthcare, including prenatal care, postnatal care, contraception, abortion services, and alternative reproductive technologies for all people, free from coercion, discrimination, and violence.
summary: If/When/How educates, organizes, and supports law students and legal professionals to ensure that a new generation of advocates will be prepared to protect and expand reproductive rights as basic civil and human rights.

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: MEGSA is an organization that aims to bring together graduate students from different focuses in mechanical engineering and foster interdisciplinary interest and discussion within the academic community, and represent FSU in the ME community at large.

name: Jamaican-American Student Association
description:
The Jamaican American Student Association (JAMSA), established in 2019 and re-established in 2022, exists to bring together students of Jamaican descent and the wider Florida State University community. Guided by our motto “One Love, One Family”, we intend to preserve and celebrate the richness of our Jamaican heritage through interactive events, workshops, and general community engagement. 

 

Our mission is to build unity, promote pride and leadership among students of Jamaican and wider Caribbean backgrounds, and foster awareness of our country's vibrant history, music, food, and traditions. Members are offered opportunities for cultural immersion, recognition, and exclusive access to events, merch, and trips- all while finding a home away from home on campus!

 

JAMSA welcomes all FSU students who want to celebrate, share, and be part of the vibrant community that embodies Jamaican culture!
summary: Our mission is to expose the entire student body to the rich culture of Jamaica through interactive, social, and community-based activities; as well as to be the home-away-from-home for all Jamaican students at Florida State University.

name: Badminton Club at FSU
summary: We provide a platform for all students at FSU who enjoy competitive badminton. We also welcome students who would like to experience the sport recreationally. We host practices and open plays throughout the semester and welcome everyone.

name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers at FAMU/FSU
description:
Website: https://web1.eng.famu.fsu.edu/ieee/

The Institute of Electrical and Electronic Engineers (IEEE) is a global association and organization of professionals working toward the development, implementation and maintenance of technology-centered products and services. IEEE is a non-profit organization that promotes the development and application of electrotechnology and allied sciences for the benefit of humanity, the advancement of the profession, and the well being of our members. Its intitials stand for the "Instistute of Electrical and Electronic Engineers". Its purpose is to promote the practice of electrical engineering, computer engineering, and the related arts and sciences.

We are the student chapter at FAMU-FSU College of Engineering in the city of Tallahassee, FL. We provide EE and CE students a way to get experience in the field, engage with a community that shares similar interests, and participate in opportunities to network with several companies.

IEEE is pronounced “Eye-Triple E”.
summary: The Institute of Electrical and Electronic Engineers (IEEE) is a global association and organization of professionals working toward the development, implementation and maintenance of technology-centered products and services.

name: Transfer Student Union
description:
The Transfer Student Union is dedicated to supporting transfer students by offering guidance, fostering community, providing mentorship, and facilitating access to essential resources that promote success and integration into Florida State University. 

The organization seeks to offer meaningful opportunities that enable members to: 

  • Establish supportive peer relationships. 
  • Cultivate personal and professional skills. 
  • Access tools and resources for academic, career, and personal development. 
  • Develop leadership capabilities. 
  • Engage in community service initiatives. 
  • Participate actively in campus life. 
  • Network with fellow transfer students.

name: Special Olympics Club at FSU
description:
Special Olympics at FSU was founded in the Fall of 2019. The mission of Special Olympics at FSU is to promote inclusion at Florida State University and in the community through sports training, competitions, and campaigns for people with and without intellectual disabilities. We play over 17 different sports including basketball, flag football, volleyball, soccer, tennis, cheerleading, and more. Additionally, we host events like FSUnified Day and run campaigns like the Spread the Word Campaign to promote inclusion for all people. 

 

Click HERE for our LinkTree. You can access our Facebook & Instagram pages as well as links to current and upcoming events and campaigns.
summary: Special Olympics at FSU’s purpose is to unify college students and Special Olympic athletes (individuals with an intellectual disability) together on the same team. Through sports, we aim to include everyone.

name: The Peruvian Student Association
description: The purpose of The Peruvian Student Association shall be to provide insight and promote the Peruvian culture to its members and non-members alike. Also, the organization will provide an opportunity for students in all undergraduate/graduate fields of study who already obtain knowledge about the Peruvian Culture to further enhance it through leadership positions and events geared towards Peru. With the commitment of voluntary service to help encourage and maintain academic growth among the students.
summary: The purpose of the Peruvian Student Association shall be to provide insight and promote the Peruvian culture to its members and non-members alike.

name: Experience College
description: To get plugged in, please DM the Experience College Instagram page, or follow @experienceourchurch for college event updates! You can email INFO@EXPERIENCEOURCHURCH.COM requesting more information, or fill out a Connect Card on our website to be contacted by someone your age who can help you learn more about the ministry!
summary: Experience College is the college ministry branch of Experience Church. We will hold services on campus that include worship and a life giving message.

name: Surfrider Foundation FSU
description: Surfrider aims to engage students and community members with the environment around them through education, social events, and political advocacy. We conduct educational and service group trips and hosting speakers on pertinent environmental topics, encouraging sustainable lifestyles that connect people with the environment, and keeping our members up to date on pertinent legislation. We may also collect petitions, host letter-writing campaigns, train members on how to lobby, or organize social activism to raise awareness and achieve our goals. We take an active approach, empowering students to take action on important environmental issues.
summary: The Surfrider Foundation at Florida State University is dedicated to engaging students and community members with the environment around them through education, social events, and political advocacy.

name: Saxophone Association
summary: The Saxophone Association at Florida State University aims to promote music and provide educational opportunities through guest artists, fundraising events, and social networking for FSU students and all members in the Tallahassee community.

name: Students for Life at Florida State University
description:
Students for Life at FSU is the pro-life club at Florida State. We exist to support pregnant and parenting students on campus and to advocate for the preborn.

For some students, being pregnant on campus can be a lonely experience. Our organization will empower pregnant students on campus to be successful in their academic careers. We can help connect them to local resources and inform them of their Title IX rights. We aspire to create a community where young parents feel included and supported.

Our organization advocates for the unborn on both the state and federal levels. Some of our events include attending the March for Life in Washington, D.C., tabling on campus, and hosting apologetics trainings. Our goal is to defend the rights of the preborn while aiding mothers in crisis.
summary: Students for Life exists to support students in unplanned pregnancies and to stand for the humanity of all persons, preborn and born.

name: Orthodox Christian Fellowship
description: We seek to cultivate a rich community in Christ, to inspire a lifelong love of God and neighbor. We base our meetings and activities on the four main elements of OCF: fellowship, education, prayer, and service.
summary: OCF transforms the lives of college students by guiding them along the path to Jesus Christ through His Church, cultivating a campus community of worship, witness, service, fellowship, and education.

name: International Genetically Engineered Machine at FSU
description: The International Genetically Engineered Machine (iGEM) Competition is the premier student competition in synthetic biology. Every year during the fall semester, iGEM teams from all over the world meet in Paris, France to compete for medals in a four-day competition. Since 2004, participants of the competition have experienced education, teamwork, sharing, and more in a unique competitive setting.  iGEM teams spend the summer addressing a need and/or solving a problem using synthetic biology. This experience provides lifelong memories and nurtures leadership skills that are useful for professional and personal challenges throughout life.
summary: International Genetically Engineered Machine (iGEM) at FSU is a multidisciplinary team involving students and the public in the development of innovative prototype solutions to worldly problems through an annual international synthetic biology expo.

name: The Theology Club
description:
We are active throughout the year in three primary ways. Firstly, we meet every week to discuss a topic in theology. Typically, a student presents a historical position they researched. Secondly, four times a semester, in union with the Thomistic Institute, we host guest speakers to present on topics in their field of expertise. Lastly, we meet weekly for a book club where we pursue greater philosophical and theological understanding by reading through great works. Everyone is welcome to any or all events.

General Club Chat and Announcements: https://groupme.com/join_group/88533942/3XQ2loLd

Book Club Announcements: https://groupme.com/join_group/91992532/O8uN1Vsa
summary: The Theology Club meets regularly to study and discuss a variety of topics in Christian theology.  We are interdenominational and welcome those from any faith background who want to dive intellectually into the divine mystery.

name: Strategies for Ecology Education Diversity and Sustainability FSU
description: SEEDS hopes to collaborate with undergraduate students as they pursue studies in ecology or related fields to help them find success during their time in college and after graduation. This will include networking with faculty, exploring research opportunities, and establishing relationships with like-minded peers who value both ecology and diversity. Another part of this organization consists of sharing the field of ecology with younger students in underrepresented populations to show them that they can have a future in this field.
summary: SEEDS aims to diversify the field of ecology to include underrepresented populations at all education levels.

name: University of Choice Initiative at FSU
description:
Our mission is to provide all students with disabilities the opportunities and resources they need not only to be successful at FSU but to become the best version of themselves and to be the change they want to see in the world. 

Main Objectives:

  • empower students to express themselves and to see disabilities as abilities
  • promote activities that support the disability community
  • foster community and inclusion by connecting students with and without disabilities
summary: The University of Choice Initiative endeavors to make Florida State University a nationally-recognized university of disability access and inclusion.

name: Army ROTC Organizations
description:
Seminole Guard practice expertise in the form of Drill and Ceremony according to Army regulations and fulfill the requests of various and diverse groups, around the community and throughout the state, to perform color guards and presentations of the flag of the United States of America. The goal of Seminole Guard is to help build good relations with the community and to help build the leadership qualities of future officers in our program.

Ranger Challenge trains hard every week. Including long-distance runs and rucks, swims, calisthenic work, and circuit training. The team usually consists of 11 individuals. Ranger Challenge competes against all the Army ROTC schools in our brigade to see who is the best. Ranger Challenges purpose is to build mental fortitude and a close team bond with strong people who do not quit when faced with tough challenges. They train hard so they can take the trophy at the West Point Sandhurst Military Skills Competition.
summary: Seminole Guard (Army ROTC drill team) and FSU Ranger Challenge. These two organizations within the Army ROTC program offer students the opportunity to get involved outside the classroom.

name: Vegan and Vegetarian Club at FSU
description:
We are dedicated to community-building and activism on campus and across Tallahassee.

Our members learn about the ways veganism and vegetarianism is beyond a diet, and hold conversations on ways to align lifestyle and personal morals. We support vegan and vegetarian students by providing meal ideas and pointing to local resources to further assist if needed. In our organization, we educate the student body through events/campaigns/outreach and build community through potlucks, activism, and volunteering. We represent the needs of plant-based individuals at FSU specifically and the Tallahassee area at large. We are inclusive to anyone who wishes to learn more about animal rights/environmentalism and how plant-based lifestyles impact the community.
summary: Vegan and Vegetarian Club at FSU is not exclusive to vegan and vegetarian students. We welcome all interested in the lifestyle. We encourage members to recognize the impacts we can make as individuals and to advocate for the welfare of all species.

name: Black Men in Medicine Inc.
description: Black Men in Medicine is an organization that serves to uplift black males who have an interest in medicine and to provide them with knowledge and experiences in professional healthcare settings. We strive to drastically increase black male participation in the health-care field in order to fulfill the deficit of black male physicians.
summary: Black Men in Medicine is an organization that serves to uplift black males who have an interest in medicine and to provide them with knowledge and experiences in professional healthcare settings.

name: Dermatology Interest Group
summary: The purpose of DIG is to promote interest in the specialty of dermatology among the medical student population and increase the awareness and appreciation for the importance of dermatological knowledge in a primary care setting in Florida.

name: The Society of Black Female Future Attorneys
description: The Society of Black Female Future Attorneys is a pre-law society designed to support minority female students at Florida State University pursuing careers in the legal field. The purpose of this organization is to empower, encourage, and assist Black and minority students across Florida State's campus to pursue their law careers in the face of adversity. Throughout the semester, we host various events, including bonding, professional development, community service, and panel discussions. We provide many opportunities for members to network with other students, professionals, or employers in their chosen fields. Additionally, we host meetings every month that allow members to stay up to date with important information or upcoming events.
summary: The Society of Black Female Future Attorneys is a pre-law society designed to support Black and minority female students as they pursue their career paths toward the law field at FSU.

name: Women in Government at FSU
description: Join our Flare here! Our club is open and accepts any interested members throughout the year. Women In Government (WIG) at FSU was founded on the idea of supporting women through their journey with government. From running for office to working for state government, WIG is here to fill the gap that exists for so many women when searching for a career in government. The organization is nonpartisan and will provide a safe space for beneficial conversations on the state of our nation's government system. WIG will also serve as a pipeline of government internships and opportunities for any woman who may be interested, as well as hosting formidable panelists and speakers. WIG is a holistic organization which hopes to see the success of all women interested in a government job!
summary: Empower, embrace, and create long lasting relationships amongst like-minded women who are interested in government. WIG will help develop young women into outstanding leaders through internship opportunities, networking, and group discussions

name: 2099 Comic Book Club
description: The Comic Book Club gathers to appreciate both mainstream comic publishers like DC and Marvel, but also discover and gain a deeper understanding of Indie Comics, as well as independent creators. This is not limited to screenings, debates, discussions, or simply sharing interesting reads. We also hope to take a deep dive into the process of making comics, and all the people involved, along with its history. Above all else, the goal is to create a safe environment for fans of all kinds of comics.
summary: The FSU Comic Book Club is a collection of fans of all kinds of Comics, not just limited to mainstream publishers or superheroes. Our goal is to gain a deeper understanding of the medium, as well as share some interesting reading recommendations.

name: Blacks in Computing
description:
Our mission is to:

  • Provide professional development skills to black students in related fields

  • Provide networking and career opportunities for black students in this field

  • Mentor young Black students off-campus and bring awareness to the opportunities available for them in this field 

  • Helping to provide resources to underclassmen and help the retention rates of black students in related majors

 

Our aims are to:

  • Increase the recruitment, retention, and production of Black students in CS-related fields

  • Provide opportunities to advance knowledge & understanding of the current state of technology

  • Equip students with the skillset to exceed in achieving their desired positions in tech fields
summary: Blacks in Computing is an organization dedicated to providing a space for Black & minority students studying computer science-related fields to learn through professional development, mentorship, and networking.

name: The Maji Project
description: The Maji Project strives for a greater acknowledgement of the importance of clean water as a source of life and a human right in our global community. In order to do so The Maji Project is employing a combination of advocacy, service, and fundraising initiatives across Florida State University’s campus.
summary: A student-lead fundraising and education initiative geared towards addressing the global water crisis. By doing so, we hope to create a greater acknowledgement of the importance of clean water, specifically as a human right, across the FSU campus.

name: Humans vs. Zombies
description:
Important: To become a member, you need to show up to a skirmish or event first. Information about when our skirmishes/events are in the Discord linked below.

Humans vs. Zombies is a version of Nerf Tag played on college campuses. There are two teams: Humans, who carry Nerf Blasters and Socks, and Zombies, who do not carry Blasters or Socks but Tag instead. The Zombies' goal is to Tag all the Humans to turn them into Zombies. The goal of the Humans is to survive the Zombie Horde and complete missions through strategy and teamwork.

Discord (For Communication and Event Details): https://discord.gg/VQDvCAe
summary: A Nerf Blaster Tag Club that meets weekly for skirmishes and has multi-day events throughout the year.

name: Federal Bar Association at FSU
description: The Federal Bar Association at FSU (FBA at FSU) serves as the law student chapter of the national Federal Bar Association here at Florida State University College of Law. The purposes of FBA are to further the objectives of the Federal Bar Association, to represent the Association to law students at Florida State University, to stimulate the interest of law students in the activities of the Association, and to conduct programs of interest and value to law students centering around federal practice and the federal judiciary.
summary: The Federal Bar Association at FSU will serve as a law student chapter of the larger Federal Bar Association national organization.

name: Poetry Club at FSU
description:
Poetry club is a place for all students who enjoy poetry to read, write, and share work. We will meet once a week to read a poem, and write according to a prompt given by the president - we also have fun activities to do as a group relating to writing. Any skill level is welcome. We encourage all poets to share their work but it is not required. 

Stay up to date with announcements and local events (including those across Tallahassee) by joining our Discord https://discord.gg/qyuSQsGEYS and following us on instagram @poetryclubfsu
summary: A place to read, write, and discuss contemporary poetry with other students. Any skill level is welcome; this is an all-inclusive environment.

name: The Trombone Studio at FSU
description:
The trombone studio at Florida State University is led by Dr. John Drew, Professor of Trombone and Coordinator of Winds and Percussion, and Dr. Kevin Jones, Assistant Professor of Jazz Trombone.  The studio consists of FSU undergraduate and graduate students enrolled in trombone lessons.  

​If you have any questions about the studio please don't hesitate to contact us at FloridaStateTromboneStudio@gmail.com. 

 

Additional information about the Brass and Wind Area at Florida State University can be found here: 

 

https://music.fsu.edu/brass-and-woodwinds
summary: This organization is made up of members of the FSU Trombone Studio at the FSU College of music, as well as non majors with an interest in trombone.

name: Student Art Studio for Healing
description:
Studio H aims to bring fun and accessible art events to FSU's student body, highlighting community and decompression. This organization offers a supportive and accessible outlet for students to relive stress and connect with peers.

 

This club does not require a skill set and is not exclusive to any type of student.

 

Beginners are welcome, even if they have never picked up a paintbrush before.

 

Check out our Instagram for more info on events, workshops, and how to get involved!

@studiohfsu
summary: Our purpose is to bring art to the student body of FSU as a way to have fun and decompress. This club does not require a skill set and is not exclusive to any type of student. Beginners are welcome, even if they've never picked up a paintbrush before.

name: The Jewish Student Union
description: The Jewish Student Union serves as a welcoming and accepting environment for Jewish students, Jewish allies, and all interested students. We are the centralized home for the Jewish community at Florida State University. We educate and inform the student body of the culture of Judaism as well as issues pertinent to the Jewish community, such as the Holocaust and Antisemitism.
summary: JSU is an SGA Agency meant to discover and celebrate the unique culture of Judaism as well as to educate the student body.

name: Leading Women of Tomorrow at FSU
description: The purpose of this club is to motivate the next generation of women to seek leadership opportunities and representation in government. We will do this through networking events and workshops that will help remove gender equality barriers and inspire members to seek these positions.
summary: Leading Women of Tomorrow at FSU is a bi-partisan organization seeking to bridge the gender gap, empower women, and encourage womxn to seek leadership opportunities.

name: Minorities in Public Health Association
description: This organization was created to inform and raise awareness to minorities of public health issues. We seek to empower the minds of future public health workers through the strategies and community service we implement. We aspire to serve underserved populations on both a global, and local spectrum as minorities of the public health field.
summary: This organization was created to improve and promote the important of quality health for minorities, eliminating racial disparities.

name: The History Club at FSU
description: The History Club at FSU is an organization open to any student that seeks to spread awareness of historical topics, FSU’s historical resources, and to build connections among those interested in history on campus.
summary: The History Club at FSU is an organization open to any student that seeks to spread awareness of historical topics, FSU’s historical resources, and to build connections among those interested in history on campus.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers at FSU
description:
SASE is dedicated to the advancement of Asian heritage scientists and engineers in education and employment so that they can achieve their full career potential. In addition to professional development, SASE also encourages members to contribute to the enhancement of the communities in which they live.

SASE’s mission is to

  • Prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world.
  • Celebrate diversity on campuses and in the workplace.
  • Provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local communities.

SASE membership is open to students of all majors and backgrounds.
summary: The mission of SASE is to prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world, celebrate diversity on campus, and provide opportunities for members to make  contributions to their local communities.

name: Themed Entertainment Association at Florida State
description:
The Themed Entertainment Association (TEA) is an international non-profit organization representing the world's leading creators, developers, designers, and producers of compelling places and experiences. The members of TEA (students, professionals, ambassadors, and enthusiasts) bring the experience of engaging storytelling and entertainment to hotels, casinos, restaurants, museums, zoos, theme parks (Disney, Universal, Six Flags, Cedar Fair, Hershey Park, etc.), and more destinations. The association provides opportunities to network, educate, and connect professionals to the amusement industry. On the academic level, TEA's NextGen initiative focuses on university clubs that operate to connect students to the TEA network. Being part of the Themed Entertainment Association involves getting a behind the scenes look at theme parks, getting connected with industry professionals, and forming new relationships along the way.

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/tea.fsu/
GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/70679585/ylR7X8ps
summary: The NextGen Initiative of the Themed Entertainment Association (TEA) helps students and recent graduates find their way into the themed entertainment/visitor attractions industry. Our initiative at Florida State was founded in Fall 2020.

name: Undergraduate Law Review at FSU
description:
Founded in 1996 and re-established in 2020, the Undergraduate Law Review at Florida State University strives to connect minds across campus and delve deep into current legal events. Our organization is entirely student-run, edited, and published. We aim to bring like-minded students across FSU’s campus together to engage in legal discourse. Over the course of a semester, each of our members gains insight into the field of law and legal writing through the writing and editing process. We strive for excellence in writing and hope to offer various unique perspectives on notable legal issues.

We digitally publish a volume of articles once per semester on our digitized print journal, and we routinely publish short-form blogs in the fall and spring semesters. We accept new writers and editors at the beginning of every semester. Membership to our organization is conducted via an application process, and application decisions are based on a combination of academic merit and demonstrated interest in our organization. We hold informational sessions at the beginning of fall and spring semesters for prospective members to learn more about us.

 

Our members consist of the executive board, editorial board, article editors, and both long- and short-form writers. Through involvement with our organization, members learn how to successfully craft arguments, peer-review other papers, and cite sources according to the Bluebook citation style. Our social events and general body meetings also provide an opportunity for our staff to get to know one another and network with like-minded pre-law students here at Florida State. Our organization is a chance for students to connect with their peers and get published in an academic journal.

 

You can contact us via email at ulr.fsu@gmail.com and follow us on Instagram @ulr.fsu. You can also find us on LinkedIn.
summary: The Undergraduate Law Review at Florida State University is comprised of a team of dedicated undergraduate writers and editors. We are a pre-law organization that is entirely student-run, and we publish long- and short-form legal writing on our website.

name: Arabic Honor Society
shortName: AHS, Arabic Honor Society, Arabic HS
description: The Arabic Honor Society is a student organization that aims to recognize students who have maintained excellent academic performance studying the Arabic language, its culture, and people at Florida State University. It promotes excellent scholarship and creates a safe space where students can exchange ideas, thoughts, and achievements regarding the Arabic language in particular and the Middle East in general. In addition, the Arabic Honor Society will offer tutoring services to students of Arabic, host seminars by luminaries in the field, and provide resources that will enhance the learning experience of its members.
summary: The Arabic Honor Society is a student organization that aims to recognize students who have maintained excellent academic performance studying the Arabic language, its culture, and people at Florida State University.

name: Lambda Sigma Mu- Chapter of Chi Sigma Iota at Florida State University
description: We promote a strong professional identity through members (professional counselors, counselor educators, and students) who contribute to the realization of a healthy society by fostering wellness and human dignity.
summary: Lambda Sigma Mu- Chapter of Chi Sigma Iota at Florida State University is an international honor society that values academic and professional excellence in counseling.

name: Willow Partners
description:
Willow Partners is the leading undergraduate investment banking organization at Florida State University, founded in 2015 with the intent of promoting the general success of FSU's College of Business.

 

Over the past ten years, we’ve consistently placed our members in top financial centers—including New York, Chicago, Charlotte, and Houston—where they’ve gone on to excel in both buy-side and sell-side roles.

 

Through our flagship program, Noles on Wall Street, we offer a year-long, mentorship-driven workshop that encompasses technical training, case studies, and behavioral interview prep. Our mission is to prepare students for their respective recruiting cycles by equipping them with the skills, confidence, and network needed to thrive in the most competitive sectors of finance.

 

If you are an interested first or second year student, please feel free to reach us at willowpartnersfsu@gmail.com

 

For more information regarding being selected into our next Noles on Wall Street cohort, make sure to visit our website.
summary: We are FSU's preeminent investment banking organization in the College of Business. Since 2015, we've successfully helped members secure investment banking, private equity, and other high finance jobs through rigorous training and student-led workshops.

name: Epilepsy Foundation of Florida at FSU
summary: Our mission is to raise awareness about the challenges of living with epilepsy and to promote research on therapies to stop seizures, find cures, and save lives.

name: Skateboarding Club
description: Skateboarding Club at FSU is an organization dedicated to connecting the student community through skateboarding and social events. We would like to build the Tallahassee skate community by fostering skate meets and introducing new people to skating. Additionally, we aim to connect with local and other collegiate skate organizations.
summary: FSU Skateboarding Club is an organization dedicated to connecting the student community through skateboarding and social events. We would like to build the Tallahassee skate community by fostering skate meets and introducing new people to skating.

name: The Museum Society at Florida State
description: The Museum Society at Florida State serves as a resource for museum lovers and aspiring museum professionals to enter into a community of like-minded individuals. We provide networking opportunities and engagement opportunities with museum professionals from a variety of organizations, and we aim to help members aspiring for a museum-based career to develop their professional experience. Alongside these professional development opportunities; we share museum tours, exhibitions, and events with a global and inclusive focus for our members to participate in as a community. Whether you want to work in a museum one day or simply enjoy visiting museums, this is the organization for you!
summary: We aim to promote dialogue surrounding museum experiences and knowledge across all disciplines. The organization will act as a resource to students to gather a community of like-minded individuals who both enjoy visiting and working in museums.

name: Make Medicine Human Society
description:
MMH focuses on the experience of the individual who lives with a medical condition in their life. Through their stories, we want to encourage future professionals from our organization to connect this with their medical studies and make medicine as a result, more human. Our goal is to remind pre-health students of the true focus of healthcare and its impact on patients as they continue their undergraduate studies. 

If you are interested in joining, please request through NoleCentral or email a member on the E-board in order to be added to the Canvas site.
summary: MMH is an organization that gives a platform for individuals who live with a disability or disease to come speak to future health care professionals about their personal experiences.

name: Florida PIRG Students
description: Florida PIRG Students is a student-led, non-profit, non-partisan organization that is focused on giving students the tools that they need to make their voices heard on issues that they care about. With campaigns such as ending hunger and homelessness, college affordability, and breaking free from plastics Florida PIRG Students is committed to bringing positive change to FSU's campus.
summary: Florida PIRG Students is a student-led, non-profit, non-partisan organization that is focused on giving students the tools that they need to make their voices heard on issues that they care about.

name: ASLC Special Events Committee
description: The Special Events Committee gives students the opportunity to plan fun, interactive, and unique monthly events at the ASLC. They organize events that tie into the movies or gaming programmed for the month or non-film-related events meant to entertain, educate, and cultivate community among the study body.
summary: The SLC Special Events Committee...

name: Jewels Incorporated at Florida State University
description: Jewels Inc provide venues where young urban girls can acquire an understanding of technology as well as social skills necessary to assume leadership roles through actual practice. Hosting collaborative events between Jewels Inc. and our Junior Jewels will instill responsibility for producing community-based events: entertainment, education, and promotion.
summary: Jewels Inc at Florida State University is a non-profit organization that mentors young, urban girls through Education, Training & Social Activism 💎💜

name: Collegiate Entrepreneurs Organization
summary: We are the FSU chapter of a larger national organization dedicated to facilitating college students in the pursuit of their entrepreneurial endeavors through networking events and hands-on trainings with various successful entrepreneurs.

name: Living Intentionally Finding Togetherness, a Collegiate Recovery Program
description:
The mission of LIFT is to support students interested in recovery from addiction and substance misuse thrive during their college experience. LIFT views the process of recovery as "an individualized, intentional, dynamic, and relational process involving sustained efforts to improve wellness” (Recovery Science Research Collective, 2020). Students learn beneficial coping skills in an accountable recovery community where they can develop friendships with like-minded peers.

Pillars serve as the guiding ethical norm of our organization. LIFT is organized around the four pillars of Community, Scholarship, Resiliency, and Personal Accountability.

  1. Community is achieved through group meetings, activities, and service informed by the principals of recovery.
  2. Scholarship is achieved through professional and academic internships, research in addiction and recovery sciences, and academic advising.
  3. Resiliency is gained by members through overcoming challenges and setbacks often associated with addiction and substance use.
  4. Personal accountability is taught through cultivating a culture of honesty and autonomy.

Join our GroupMe
summary: The mission of LIFT is to support students interested in recovery from addiction and substance misuse thrive during their college experience.

name: JHealth Pre-Health Society
description: JHealth is a national society designed for Jewish undergraduates to build their resumes, learn from experienced Jewish personnel in various health fields, and synthesize their professional aspirations with Jewish values.
summary: JHealth is a national society designed for Jewish undergraduates to build their resumes, learn from experienced Jewish personnel in various health fields, and synthesize their professional aspirations with Jewish values.

name: Hispanic Law Students Association
description: HLSA represents diversity and inclusion. We seek the advancement of Hispanic-Latinos at Florida State University’s College of Law, as well as the legal community at large. By providing social and academic support, along with networking opportunities, we are certain we can increase the presence of Hispanics in the legal field.
summary: The purpose of HLSA is to provide opportunities for social, academic and professional development for students interested in law at Florida State University. Our goal is to empower all students within the university as well as the community at large.

name: The Studio Orchestra Society at FSU
description:
The Studio Orchestra Society at FSU offers student arrangers, composers, and conductors the opportunity perform music from film, video games, and other entertainment media for the student body and Tallahassee community.

Membership is open to current students, faculty, and staff of Florida State University.
summary: The Studio Orchestra Society at FSU offers student arrangers, composers, and conductors the opportunity perform music from film, video games, and other entertainment media for the student body and Tallahassee community.

name: American Hellenic Educational Progressive Association Scholars at Florida State University
description:
• To stimulate love and admiration of Hellenic culture, traditions and ideas; to promote throughout the world, and especially the United States and Canada, a better and more comprehensive understanding of the Hellenic people; to encourage the study of Hellenic letters, arts and sciences, and the preservation and teaching of the Hellenic language.
  • To serve members through professional and social networking within the Hellenic community
  • To be a positive influence on the community through service, volunteerism, and promotion of education
summary: To promote the ancient Hellenic ideals of education, philanthropy, civic responsibility, integrity, and family and individual excellence through celebration of heritage, service, and brotherhood.

name: Law Students Practicing Wellness Club
description: Law Students Practicing Wellness Club focuses on the mental wellbeing of law students and provides them learning opportunities and social events that center on bettering mental health in the legal field.
summary: An organization for law students focused on mental wellbeing, relaxation, and tools and resources to practice wellness in the legal field.

name: Jiu Jitsu Club
description:
Jiu Jitsu is a martial art that focuses on self defense based off of the principles of leverage and timing. Our club focuses on empowering people with the ability to defend themselves against a stronger aggressor.

 

Many people believe that jiu jitsu is solely limited to ground and grappling techniques, but this could not be further from the truth. Jiu Jitsu encompasses all aspects of a fight including striking, throwing, self-defense from surprise attacks, and grappling.  

 

If you're interested in joining, please reach out on our instagram @fsuclubbjj ! We guarantee a safe, positive training environment for all.
summary: Jiu Jitsu is a martial art that focuses on self-defense and grappling. Our goal is to raise our student's confidence while having fun. We guarantee a safe and positive training environment. For roster spots reach out on instagram. See you on the mat!

name: Masters in Applied American Politics and Policy Association
summary: The MAAPP Association is designed for those who seek active careers in a number of political fields: Campaigns, Fundraising, Political Communications, Lobbying, Legislative Affairs, etc. All members of the MAAPP program and those interested are welcome!

name: JBIZ
description: Jbiz at Florida State University is one of over 20 chapters in the national Jbiz community and we aim to help undergraduate students access unique career-building opportunities through networking events, professional mentoring, and career workshops. We also hope to bring business students closer together with social events to foster a sense of community for Jews at FSU!
summary: Olami’s Jbiz society is a business network for aspiring Jewish business students who want to get a head start and build their professional network.

name: Forward at FSU
description: The mission of ForwardFSU is to foster a positive, diverse, professional and enriching culture among dedicated student leaders and prioritizing tradition, advancement, and intentionality. We aim to achieve this through building relationships and a sense of community as we take the next step together to advocate for the best interests of all students. FSU, let's move #ForwardTogether.
summary: Building a positive & enriching culture among student leaders who seek to serve our FSU community!

name: Young Americans for Liberty at Florida State
description: Young Americans for Liberty (YAL) is a non-profit organization and the most active and effective pro-liberty youth organization advancing liberty on campus and in American electoral politics. Our four-step mission is to identify, educate, train, and mobilize youth activists to make liberty win. We value personal liberties, free speech, and how we can effect change on our government.
summary: Young Americans for Liberty is a pro-liberty youth organization focused on advancing liberty on campus and in American electoral politics.

name: Mondays@TBD
description: It’s time for the church to move beyond 4 walls. Mondays@TBD is powered by Engage Church and is designed to be mobile and active outside of the 4 walls in which services are typically held. Mondays@TBD is geared towards college students and young adults with two words in mind - consecration and creativity. We believe this is what God is after in this season - to consecrate the next generation to Himself, and to draw them through creativity. It is for this reason that we will preach the explicit Gospel and seek to have creativity as a part of our design and engagement with the people around us. We are on a mission to think outside the box in order to add real value to students, the campuses, young adults, and this city. We’re committed to being on the move, engaging God, People, and Culture.
summary: Mondays@TBD is powered by Engage Church and is designed to be mobile and active outside of the 4 walls in which services are held. We are on a mission to think outside the box in order to add real value to students, campuses, young adults, and the city.

name: Black Acting Methods Society
description: Black Acting Methods Society is a chapter of the Black Acting Method Studio founded by Sharrell D. Luckett, PhD.  The society’s purpose is to educate, re-educate, recuperate and disseminate the vast contributions of Africans and African Americans to the field of performing arts.  We aim to be a vast network of future theatre leaders invested in the field, practice, and cultural legacy of black acting methods.  The BAM Society is a knowledge-based and knowledge-driven organization, meaning that Society members should be dedicated to attaining knowledge primarily through reading, group discussion, master classes, and observations.
summary: The Black Acting Methods Society aims to be a vast network of future theatre leaders invested in the field, practice, and cultural legacy of black acting methods.

name: Club Golf at FSU
summary: Provide an opportunity for students of Florida State University to play golf competitively, to promote leadership and competitive skills within our community.

name: Counseling and School Psychology Students Helping to Advocate, Organize, and Serve
description: This RSO is for the students in the Combined Doctoral Program in Counseling Psychology and School Psychology in the College of Education, Health, and Human Sciences. We seek to advocate, organize, and serve on behalf of our field. All others interested in our mission are welcome.
summary: This RSO is for the students in the Combined Doctoral Program in Counseling Psychology and School Psychology in the College of Education, Health, and Human Sciences. All others interested in our mission are welcome.

name: Dental Brigades at FSU
description: Dental Brigades at FSU is a chapter of Global Brigades, the largest student-run international movement in the world. We work in remote, rural, and under-resourced communities in Honduras, Panama, Guatemala, Nicaragua, Greece, Ghana, and more, who would otherwise have limited to no access to health care. These communities receive a brigade every three to four months where hundreds of patients are provided access to healthcare and volunteers deliver public health workshops. Our chapter will contribute by taking an annual trip abroad for 7-9 days at a stationed clinic in one of the four countries offered by Global Brigades. Electronic patient records are collected for future visitations and to monitor overall community health trends. In conjunction with our Dental Program, Global Brigades also supports communities with economic development, sanitation and clean water projects, and uniquely implements these programs in a holistic model to meet a community’s health and economic goals. Our model systematically builds community ownership and collaboratively executes programs with the end goal of sustainably evolving to a relationship of impact monitoring.
summary: Dental Brigades at FSU is a chapter of Global Brigades. Our chapter contributes by taking an annual trip abroad for 7-9 days at a stationed clinic in one of the four countries offered by Global Brigades who otherwise have limited access to healthcare.

name: Phi Mu Alpha Sinfonia Fraternity Inc.
description:
Phi Mu Alpha Sinfonia is the world’s oldest and largest secret national fraternal society in music. Sinfonia was born on October 6, 1898, at the New England Conservatory in Boston, when a group of thirteen young men under the guidance of Ossian Everett Mills met “to consider the social life of the young men students of that institution [and] to devise ways and means by which it might be improved.” Sinfonia became a national fraternity on October 6, 1900, with the admission of a group of men at the Broad Street Conservatory in Philadelphia. For over a century, Sinfonians in nearly every field of study and professional endeavor have transformed music in America. The opportunity of becoming a Sinfonian is offered to as many men as possible who, through a love for music, can assist in the fulfillment of the Fraternity’s Object and ideals either by adopting music as a profession or by working to advance the cause of music in America.

Sinfonia’s chief purpose is to develop Fraternity in music.
Sinfonia upholds the highest standards in music.
Sinfonia inspires musicians to be better men.
Sinfonia is a movement for the betterment of mankind.
Sinfonia champions the power of music.

FSU’s chapter of Phi Mu Alpha Sinfonia, Epsilon Iota, celebrated its 72nd anniversary recently, calling back to its rich and diverse history. The chapter has hosted many high achieving alumni such as famed Opera composer, Carlisle Floyd and Grammy Award winning trumpeter, Christopher Moore. Recently, the Epsilon Iota brothers have dedicated time towards music-making and fufillment across Tallahassee at several Assisted Senior Living Facilities. Any student attending FSU who is passionate about Music is welcome to attend rush week and meet the brothers. If you are interested in rushing or would like to know more about FSU’s chapter of Phi Mu Alpha, please contact us at pma_ei@outlook.com!
summary: The object of this fraternity shall be for the development of the best and truest fraternal spirit; the mutual welfare and brotherhood of musical students; the advancement of music in America and a loyalty to the Alma Mater.

name: The Women’s Network FSU
description: TWN is a collegiate women’s networking organization that focuses on empowering and connecting high-achieving women to each other and to leaders across many industries, as well as celebrating ambition. We hold a variety of events: speaker meetings with high-profile leaders, LinkedIn, resume, and internship workshops, alumni receptions, community-based discussion panels, and virtual networking opportunities with other members on campus and around the nation. It’s a great way to expand networking, gain exposure to different industries, build confidence, find career opportunities, and meet ambitious people.
summary: TWN is a collegiate women’s networking organization that focuses on empowering and connecting high-achieving women to each other and to leaders across many industries, as well as celebrating ambition.

name: POLY/PMSE Student Chapter at FAMU-FSU
description: A student-run organization affiliated with the ACS Division of Polymer Chemistry (POLY) and the Division of Polymeric Materials Science and Engineering (PMSE) that provides networking, career development and opportunities to students and post-doctoral associates in the polymer field
summary: A professional student run organization affiliated with American Chemical Society for students in a polymer chemistry field. POLY/PMSE is a joint organization between FAMU and FSU.

name: Cryptocurrency Organization for Young Professionals
description:
We welcome anyone interested in learning about cryptocurrencies. Our club exists to grow connections between people interested in cryptocurrencies and also to share knowledge between club members. We want our club to be interactive and for students to bring ideas and share with us. Every member is treated equally, and there will be countless opportunities to help grow this club in the future.

You can connect with us and our community here. 

Here is something that you can expect when participating in one of our general meetings:


Topics include U.S. & International Regulations, current cryptocurrency markets and forecasting of cryptocurrency markets, discussion related to exciting/up-and-coming currency projects, understanding blockchain technologies, and discussion of trading strategies. We also plan on discussing various topics, including the different fundamentals of crypto projects, including Layer 1, Layer 2, Gaming, Metaverse, and meme projects. We will discuss why some projects do better than others and which fundamentals are safer, and why that is. We will also discuss Web 3, DeFi, dApps, Stablecoins, POW/POS, tokenomics, bullish and bearish charts, security, crypto fraud, and more.
