This club is for you if you want to familiarize yourself with the future of finance at an early age. While everyone is skeptical and unsure of the future, it is time to educate yourself on these topics.


Sometime in the future, we plan to invite guest speakers and representatives of financial tech firms at the forefront of cryptocurrency development.

Please consider joining us; we would love to have you!


It is important to note that none of us is giving financial advice. Upon admission to the club, you must sign a waiver stating that our organization only gives opinions and is intended for informational purposes only. Our club does not provide investment advice. You should take independent financial advice from a professional in connection with, or independently research and verify, any information you would find in our organization and wish to rely upon, whether to make an investment decision or otherwise.
summary: Our organization is dedicated to discussing cryptocurrency topics, networking with those familiar/new to the cryptocurrency industry, and learning about exciting cryptocurrency projects. Regardless of experience, everyone is welcome.

name: Students Demand Action at Florida State University
description:
• The purpose of the Students Demand Action organization is to raise awareness about gun safety. It is a student lead grassroots organization that pushes sensible gun legislation and shifting gun culture in America.

    By registering people to vote, hosting community rallies, speaking to legislators and informing and educating our peers we can help to shift that culture and end gun violence in America.

    We use direct and indirect communication with our representatives at the federal, state, and local levels to push for reform.
summary: Students Demand Action organization aims to raise awareness about gun safety. It is a student-led grassroots organization that pushes sensible gun legislation and shifting gun culture in America.

name: Stitch Fashion and Design Club
summary: The singular Fashion & Design club at Florida State University, Stitch teaches any curious individuals in Tallahassee about the various significances of fashion and its unique position as an art form in today's world.

name: School of Dance Student Advisory Council
description: At Florida State University’s School of Dance, our Student Advisory Council is dedicated to fostering an inclusive and supportive environment where every member of our community can thrive. Elected representatives, with the support of the College of Fine Arts Leadership Council and FSU’s Student Government, champion the values of intersectionality, equity, and inclusion, recognizing that diverse perspectives and experiences enrich our artistic and academic endeavors. Our mission is to advocate for the needs and voices of all students, bridge the gap between undergraduate students and faculty, and promote a culture where every individual feels valued and empowered. By fostering engagement through intentional programming, providing both internal and external professional experiences, and promoting transparency between administration and the wider department, the Student Advisory Council invests in the student experience because it cultivates a vibrant and dynamic environment where creativity flourishes and academic excellence thrives. Through collaborative efforts and open dialogue, we strive to create a dynamic and equitable dance community that celebrates and supports the unique contributions of each member and aims to further support them in their artistic and professional experience.
summary: School of Dance Student Advisory Council is a council to help build community within the School of Dance by being a resource for both students and staff. We strive to provide transparency in the community and help students' voices become heard.

name: School of Teacher Education Graduate Student Association
description: The School of Teacher Education has many graduate tracks. This group aims to be a supportive community in which we share our ideas, get feedback, and learn from each other. In addition, we aim to support our development as researchers via workshops, guest speakers, social events, and other activities.
summary: The School of Teacher Education Graduate Student Association (STEGSA) aims to create a supportive community in which we share our ideas, get feedback, and learn from each other.

name: Script2Screen
description:
Script2Screen is a screenwriting club dedicated to providing a creative space to storytellers at FSU. We get together every week at 3 PM in Williams Common Room 013 to talk about various topics surrounding storytelling. Be sure to keep up to date with S2S via Instagram and Discord!

Instagram: https://instagram.com/s2s_fsu

Discord: https://discord.gg/dtyQyAn5d4
summary: Script2Screen is solely dedicated to making screenwriting accessible and fun for students. If you're interested in movies, television, podcasts, and other forms of media, this is the club for you!

name: Quiz Bowl Association at Florida State
description: The purpose of the Quiz Bowl Association at Florida State is to provide the opportunity for students who excel in many different fields of knowledge to test their knowledge in buzzer-based academic competitions at home and around the United States. We welcome students from all walks of life at FSU to join us in forming a team of diverse individuals to use their knowledge to compete at all levels.
summary: The purpose of the Quiz Bowl Association at Florida State is to provide the opportunity for students who excel in many different fields of knowledge to test their knowledge in buzzer-based academic competitions at home and around the United States.

name: Women In Sports Association at FSU
shortName: Women In Sports Association At Florida State University
description:
Women In Sports Association At Florida State University serves to:

Bring awareness to the influential role women play within the landscape of sports.

Develop a community where students, student-athletes, faculty, staff, and sports professionals can discuss and share their experiences in sport to educate all involved.

Bring awareness to the numerous opportunities women can seek within a career in sport.

Equip members with the knowledge and professionalism needed to excel within the industry of sports.

Develop leadership skills, personal growth, and self-confidence through membership of this association.

Empower women to reach their goals not only in sport, but their goals in life as well.
summary: Women In Sports Association (WISA) At Florida State University educates, empowers, and assists with the advancement of women in sport.

name: Sigma Chi Fraternity
summary: Sigma Chi (ΣΧ) International Fraternity is one of the largest social fraternities in North America. Founded in 1855, the fraternity has 244 active chapters across the United States and Canada and has initiated more than 350,000 members.

name: Irresistible GarNettes
description:
The Irresistible GarNettes, previously known as Nolettes, a fresh addition to Florida State's campus, are dedicated to embodying femininity, sophistication, and grace with every step, both on and off the dance floor.

         Our mission is to promote confidence in women through the art of a majorette-based style of dance. Through our sisterhood, we strongly encourage supporting one another while having fun through our bonding events and out of town trips. We aim to actively take part in both campus events and community initiatives, striving to contribute positively to both spheres.
summary: This organization was created to allow students to learn, practice, and perform majorette style dancing while engaging in a sisterhood. Dancers also learn and execute different styles/genres of dance such as jazz, contemporary, hip hop, technical, etc.

name: Surge FSU
summary: Surge FSU is the progressive political party that puts candidates into Student Government, advocates for progressive tangible change, and creates positive initiatives.

name: National Organization for the Professional Advancement of Black Chemists and Chemical Engineers
description: The mission of the National Organization for the Professional Advancement of Black Chemists and Chemical Engineers (NOBCChE) is to build an eminent cadre of successful diverse global leaders in STEM and advance their professional endeavors by adding value to their academic, development, leadership, and philanthropic endeavors throughout the life-cycle of their careers. To that end we have established educational partnerships with school districts, municipalities, businesses, universities, and other organizations in the public and private sectors to provide and support local, regional, national, and global programs that assist people of color in fully realizing their potential in academic, professional, and entrepreneurial pursuits in chemistry, chemical engineering, and allied fields.
summary: The mission of the NOBCChE is to build an eminent cadre of successful diverse global leaders in STEM and advance their professional endeavors by adding value to their academic, development, leadership, and philanthropic endeavors.

name: Cubing Club at FSU
description:
Our club is geared towards bringing Rubik's Cube Solvers(Cubers) together, teaching people how to solve and/or speedsolve Rubik's Cubes, holding cubing related events for FSU and non-FSU students to join, and fostering a community.

There are no member requirements, if you want to be a member you can be! 

We have held 9 WCA Competitions at FSU previously and plan to have another one this semester. Feel free to check out our linktree below containing our discord, groupme, and Instagram. We also have a 3x3 tutorial linked at the bottom of this page.
summary: Cubing Club at FSU is an RSO here on campus that aims to teach cubing, form a fun community of cubers, and bring cubing competitions at FSU. People of all skill levels are welcome, whether you know how to solve a cube or have been doing it for many year

name: Political Discourse Club
description: Political Discourse is a college student run organization in which a vast variety of political issues are discussed and debated. In these times of extreme polarization and partisanship, our goal is to express opinions about politics in a respectful and driven manner and to encourage mindful and accurate discussion. https://linktr.ee/Political_Discourse
summary: Political Discourse is a college student run organization in which a vast variety of political issues are discussed and debated. In these times of extreme polarization and partisanship, our goal is to respectfully express opinions about politics.

name: Silver Wings
summary: Silver Wings (SW) is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education about national defense.

name: Rho Epsilon Chapter of Sigma Tau Delta International English Honor Society
description:
“Sigma Tau Delta, International English Honor Society, was founded in 1924 at Dakota Wesleyan University, to confer distinction for high achievement in English language, literature, and writing; and to foster literacy and all aspects of the discipline of English. With over 850 chapters in large and small, public and private, colleges and universities in the U.S. and abroad, Sigma Tau Delta is the most active honors organization in our field and one of the largest members of the Association of College Honor Societies.

In addition to the prestige of membership in an international honor society, there are many other
benefits of affiliation: thousands of dollars in scholarship and award opportunities, regional and
national conferences, and internship and publication possibilities. One especially noteworthy
benefit of membership is that students are invited to submit papers for presentation at Sigma Tau
Delta’s annual spring convention, held each year in one of the Society’s six regions of the
country. Students selected to present their work in this national venue also have the opportunity
to win convention writing awards and to attend presentations and workshops by nationally
recognized authors such as Neil Gaiman, Ursula Le Guin, and U.S. Poet Laureates Natasha
Trethewey and Kay Ryan. In summary, Sigma Tau Delta is about recognition and opportunity,
and is dedicated to making a real difference in the life of each and every Sigma Tau Deltan.

Our local chapter, the Rho Epsilon Chapter of Sigma Tau Delta was founded in 1950. We participate in service projects that fosters literacy, love of language, literature, and writing alongside of fellowship. The purpose of the Society is to confer distinction for high achievement in all areas of English studies at the undergraduate and graduate levels; to promote interest in literature and the English language in surrounding communities; to foster all aspects of the discipline for English, including literature, language, and writing; and to serve society by fostering literacy.
summary: Rho Epsilon of Sigma Tau Delta English Honor Society recognizes and encourages excellence, among undergraduate and graduate students, in all aspects of English language, literature, and the many closely-related fields, including literacy initiatives.

name: Office of the Mental Health Council
description: The mission of the Mental Health Council is to empower students with mental health resources through de-stigmatization, community involvement, and advocacy. We do this by increasing dialogue about mental health issues, advocating for student body access to mental health resources, and working with the Counseling Center to provide programming to encourage outlets of support
summary: The mission of the Mental Health Council is to empower students with mental health resources through de-stigmatization, community involvement, and advocacy.

name: Pi Sigma Epsilon Fraternity
description:
Pi Sigma Epsilon is a co-ed professional business fraternity focused on marketing, sales, and management. However, it is open to all majors. We give members the chance to gain hands-on experience through experiential learning opportunities to promote knowledge and skill. 

PSE offers national & regional conferences, networking events, marketing & sales projects, professional competitions, and more to foster personal and professional growth. Our goal is to stimulate improved methods and techniques in the fields of marketing, professional selling, and sales management. 

We host recruitment every fall and spring for anyone interested in joining! Follow our Instagram @psefsu to keep up to date with recruitment information.
summary: Pi Sigma Epsilon is a co-ed professional business fraternity in the fields of marketing, sales, and management. We give members the chance to gain hands-on experience through experiential learning opportunities, national/regional conferences, and more.

name: The African Student Association
summary: The organization serves to promote African culture and strengthen the African network, at the Florida State University. Through the emphasis on familial bonds and shared cultural experiences we aim to provide a comforting and free environment

name: Attack Dance “Crew”
description: Attack Dance "Crew" is a competitive hip-hop student based organization that gratifies brotherhood, excellence, and perfection.
summary: Attack Dance "Crew" is a competitive hip-hop student based organization that gratifies brotherhood, excellence, and perfection.

name: Chi Phi Fraternity
summary: Our mission is to build better men through lifelong friendships, leadership opportunities, and character development.

name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space at FAMU and FSU
description:
The purpose of SEDS is to serve as a vehicle to promote collaboration amongst the different disciplines and backgrounds within intersectional academia; applying the principles of space exploration as a catalyst for uniting our human experience. 

To magnify our purpose, we aim to unite people from varied communities to increase representation from diverse cultures, backgrounds, disciplines, and perspectives. Utilizing space exploration as an example, we facilitate literacy and awareness for the students between FAMU, FSU, and those across communities to ultimately encourage leadership and professional development. To empower the next generation of professionals we aim to build a collaborative community among different institutions and academic disciplines. 

Ultimately, we work to challenge the isolation of academic disciplines and a historical lack of teamwork in research by discussing interdisciplinary projects to propel academic opportunities. Thus, from the SEDS experience, students will identify shared commonalities and differences to expand upon our human space exploration efforts and integrate lessons learned in a direction of their choice.
summary: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space at FAMU-FSU is an organization that seeks to unite people from all different disciplines and backgrounds on their shared interest in space through interdisciplinary projects and educational outreach.

name: Track & Field Club @ FSU
description:
The Track and Field Club at FSU welcomes athletes across all levels of experience. We aim to provide a space for athletes to continue their passion for track and field, as well as introduce new athletes to the sport. Throughout the year we will have opportunities to compete (i.e., cross country, and indoor and outdoor track meets). Competing is not mandatory, and athletes are encouraged to attend practice! 

Please reach out to our instagram @fsuclubtrack for access to our Flare group chat!

Practices

Pole Vault: TUES & THURS, 5:30pm-7pm @ Maclay School- 3737 N Meridian Rd, Tallahassee, FL 32312


Distance and Sprints (Weekday):

TUESDAY, 6pm @ Railroad Square FAMU Skate Park- 810 FAMU Way, Tallahassee, FL 32310

Weekend Practices:

Sprints Session

Address: 3737 N Meridian Rd, Tallahassee, FL 32312
summary: The Track and Field Club at FSU welcomes athletes across all levels of experience. We aim to provide a space for athletes to continue their passion for track and field, as well as introduce new athletes to the sport.

name: Sport Clubs at FSU
description: A sport club is a registered student organization that has been formed for the purpose of competing and/or participating in a particular sport. Sport clubs can compete at a high level but do not officially represent, recruit, or compete for Florida State Athletics.  Each club’s level of competition or activity is unique and is dependent on club leadership. Sport clubs are student-initiated, student-led and student-managed, providing an opportunity for the development of leadership, other life skills and contribute to the overall college experience.
summary: All sport clubs officers should be added to this page to stay up to date on information.

name: Campus Emergency Response Volunteers
description:
Campus Emergency Response Volunteers (CERV) is similar to FEMA's Community Emergency Response Team (CERT) program, except our volunteers specialize in on-campus emergency relief.

 

 

CERV (Campus Emergency Response Volunteers) is a volunteer program that was first established in 2019. CERV is open to all students, faculty, and staff and offers training throughout the year that can support campus public safety initiatives and response efforts.

 

Any student, faculty, or staff member is eligible to join, and may assist with some emergency-related activities on campus, such as shelter operations, distributing goods throughout campus, or providing basic first aid.

 

CERV has monthly training opportunities that are open to everyone, even non-CERV members! Join a training to see if you're interested in helping your campus community during times of need.

 

This group is managed by FSU Emergency Management, a part of FSU Public Safety. To fully join the organization, please enroll in our Canvas page.
summary: Campus Emergency Response Volunteers (CERV) assists with some emergency-related activities on campus, such as shelter operations, distributing goods throughout campus, or providing basic first aid. Any student, faculty, or staff member may join.

name: SLC Video Production Committee
description: The Video Production Committee is a sector of the Student Life Cinema that focuses on everything Pre-production and Post-production. We are the ones that are behind the camera, acting, writing the scripts, and editing the videos. If that sounds link something you are interested in, email us at slcmedia@admin.fsu.edu using your student account then join us on Discord! https://discord.gg/Gae653gjXj
summary: We are the committee in charge of Filming, Pre /Post Production and  Editing for all things ASLC. If you are interested in learning to edit, acting in videos or filming projects please join us!

name: Noles for Heart Health
summary: Noles for Heart Health is an undergraduate student organization dedicated to heart disease advocacy and awareness. N4HH aims to provide an inclusive and diverse community with health education and public health being central to its cause.

name: The Music Industry Connection
description:
This organization was established to allow student talent to network on campus with aspiring music executives in publishing, supervision, law, licensing, and management. Through this space, we hope club members will forge career-long and lifelong relationships with fellow students and peers. But, it's also for music fans alike! We host open mics and concerts for people to hear and meet local and student talent.

TMIC also hosts industry experts via guest lectures, panels, contract workshops, and conference passes to equip members with the necessary skills, knowledge, and mentorships to fulfill their desired role in the music world.
summary: This organization promotes connection among aspiring music professionals on campus and equips them with the skills, information, opportunities, and mentors they need to successfully navigate the music industry.

name: Society of Minorities in Sports
description: MIS strives to unite students with a passion for sports, inform them on industry career options, and provide other resources beneficial to their success. We aim to provide students with access to a network that is dedicated to breaking down barriers for young minority professionals pursuing a career in the sports industry. As an event-based organization, MIS leverages community-building, real-world experiences, and intentional mentorship to increase representation in the business of sports, ensuring the industry reflects the diversity of those who engage with and influence it every day.
summary: MIS aims to connect, empower, and provide an opportunity for minorities working in all facets the sports industry.

name: Technology & Entrepreneurship Club
summary: TEC brings education, networking opportunities, and speakers from the worlds of technology and entrepreneurship to FSU law students.

name: Muslim Student Union
description:
The Muslim Student Union’s mission is to create a strong community for Muslims on campus. We believe that religion is not only a belief but a way of living. Being able to meet people who hold the same core values as you is a gift, and InshAllah the bonds you form in this club will extend beyond this campus and much further into the future.

Whether you’re just looking to meet other Muslims, learn more about Islam, or strengthen your spirituality, we have events for everyone. We work to host many different types of events - social, religious, volunteering, and more! 

To join the MSU fill out our membership form to be added to our WhatsApp group chat!
summary: MSU is a club that strives to unite Muslims at Florida State and create a strong, welcoming community. By hosting an array of social, religious, and volunteering events, we hope to offer a diverse and safe space for Muslims of all spiritual levels at FSU

name: Florida-Eta Chapter of Tau Beta Pi
description:
The Tau Beta Pi Association is the second oldest honor society in the country, founded in 1885 to mark in a fitting manner those who have conferred honor upon their Alma Mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering, or by their attainments as alumni in the field of engineering, and to foster a spirit of liberal culture in engineering colleges. Our membership requirements are more stringent than virtually any other engineering honor society, admitting only juniors in the top eighth of their class and seniors in the top fifth. Candidates for membership are selected by invitation only. And unlike other honor societies, which only consider academic performance, membership in Tau Beta Pi is only granted to those who have also demonstrated exemplary character in addition to excellent grades. We honor engineering students as great people, not merely as great scholars.

All majors offered at the College of Engineering are potentially eligible for membership. In fact, Tau Beta Pi is the only honor society that represents the entire engineering profession.

Once initiated, new members take advantage of the host of opportunities and benefits we offer:

Professional development. Although Tau Beta Pi recognizes its members as distinguished scholars and people, membership comes with the expectation of continuing in personal and professional development. Our best-known professional development program is the award-winning Engineering Futures program, which provides engineering students with the soft skills needed for a successful career that are rarely taught in school.

Networking and community. Through Tau Beta Pi, top engineering students have the opportunity to get to know each other and come together to serve and mentor to our community, both at the College of Engineering and locally. We have an obligation to help our fellow students who are struggling, and our chapter provides a means to that end. In addition to service, we also offer social activities that are just for fun!

Fellowships and scholarships. The national Association awards up to 30 fellowships of $10,000 to engineering students for graduate study and up to 250 scholarships of $2,000 to undergraduate engineering students in their senior year.

And membership is lifetime--you pay your dues only once.

The bent is the official badge of the Association, a watch key in the form of the bent of a trestle. Our College has a wooden monument of the bent in Building A, right in front of the main door.

The member initiation process is exclusive in nature, only revealed to those who have first been evaluated as candidates, tested in their character, and elected into the organization as full members by the current membership. Its memory is one that our alumni continue to cherish long after graduation as one of the cornerstones of their college experience. We offer initiation once in the Fall semester and once in the Spring. Initiates are honored with a commemorative certificate, membership card, a 4-year subscription to The Bent magazine, and a name-engraved bent pin or bent charm.

If there were ever a time to dare, to make a difference, to embark on something worth doing,

it is now.

... You owe it to yourself to make your days here count.

... Anything is possible. There is only one you. And you will pass this way only once.

Do it right.

-Author Unknown.

Tau Beta Pi. Integrity and excellence in engineering.
summary: Tau Beta Pi was founded to mark in a fitting manner those who have conferred honor upon their Alma Mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as engineering students. Membership is by invitation only to those near the top of their class.

name: Orthopedics and Athletic Medicine Club
description: OAM started three years ago at the University of South Florida and now we are branching to Florida State University. The prime meaning of the club is to engage in healthcare while having fun and staying healthy. OAM is currently partnered with the Florida Special Olympics and has donated so much to them as we participate in many of their activities in Florida. We have biweekly general body meetings with various guest speakers who are in any health professional career and have socials which are sports days where we go play any type of sport.
summary: OAM is a pre-Health organization geared toward students passionate about medicine and healthcare in general. We are partnered with the Florida Special Olympics and which is an athletic-driven club focused on staying healthy.

name: DevLUp at FSU
description:
DevLUp is a non-profit organization dedicated to programming and the arts as it applies to real-time graphics and Video Game development. We aim to educate and provide resources to interested FSU students, from hobbyists to professionals. We encourage respect for others as well as the values of ethical game development and design.

Note: We don't use Nole Central to conduct events or communicate directly with members, instead we use Discord. You can join here. You can also visit our website to learn more.
summary: A Game Development Club at FSU.

name: African Graduate Students Association
description: The African Graduate Students Association (AGSA) at Florida State University seeks to promote African cultures on campus; facilitate mutual relationships among students of African descent; address and advocate for their needs on campus; and provide opportunities for personal and career growth, service, and relationships-building between its members and the broader FSU and Tallahassee community.
summary: The African Graduate Students Association (AGSA) at Florida State University promotes African cultures and traditions on campus, facilitates mutual relationships among students of African descent, and advocates for their needs on campus.

name: STEMental Health Association
description: The STEMental Health Association strives to create a safe space where students can build new friendships, learn to manage school along with everything else, and have fun!  Oftentimes times students in STEM and other majors forget to balance academics with allowing time to decompress and enjoy the college experience in its entirety.  The competitive and overworking mentality makes it very easy for students to get overwhelmed, which is why we are here for you, whether you need a break or want to learn more about mental health and awareness.
summary: The STEMental Health Association is a student-led organization on campus focusing on destigmatizing mental health conversations within the STEM field. Our aim is to create a supportive, educational, and safe space.

name: Blitzball Club
description:
The Blitzball Club at FSU is a recognized Sport Club under FSU Campus Recreation that brings together students who love baseball and friendly competition—with a twist. Blitzball is a fast-paced, plastic version of baseball played with a lightweight bat and a uniquely designed plastic ball that can curve, rise, and dip in incredible ways. It combines skill, creativity, and athleticism in a casual, accessible format.

Our club is open to all students, regardless of skill level or experience. Whether you’re a seasoned pitcher looking to master new pitches or someone who just wants to get outside and have fun, there’s a place for you here. We host regular practices, and we’re working toward competing against other universities and outside teams.

The Blitzball Club offers a great way to stay active, make friends, and be part of a growing community of players who share a passion for the game. Join us for a relaxed yet competitive atmosphere where fun and sportsmanship come first!

Find out more about Blitzball on their website: https://www.blitzball.com/
summary: We are a Sport Club looking to grow the sport of Blitzball on campus and beyond!

name: Step-by-Step Club at Florida State University
description: We, the Step-by-Step Club at Florida State University, are here to create a better environment and improve the lives of those less fortunate, including children in need within Tallahassee, by collecting and donating needed items while also participating in local volunteer and charity events. Join us for volunteer and service hours! Find more info at Stepbystep.com.
summary: The Step-by-Step Club at Florida State University is here to create a better environment and improve the lives of those less fortunate, including children in need and the homeless population within Tallahassee, by collecting and donating needed items.

name: The Nineteen Ten Honorary
description: The Nineteen Ten Honorary serves to recognize women and people who support women who have shown dedication to ambition, compassion, and innovation. We strive to create an empowering community to inspire the next generation of women leaders.
summary: The Nineteen Ten Honorary serves to recognize women and people who support women who have shown dedication to ambition, compassion, and innovation. We strive to create an empowering community to inspire the next generation of women leaders.

name: The Rosenstrasse Foundation at FSU
description: The Rosenstrasse Foundation was established to educate the public about the Rosenstrasse Protest, as well as its impact and significance, honoring the memory of the protesters involved, and creating a platform for the descendants of those involved in the protest to reunite through the research we conduct. We also aim to create a student community aimed at furthering the ideals of civil courage demonstrated by the women who led the Rosenstrasse Protests and all other intermarried couples who survived the Holocaust.
summary: The Rosenstrasse Foundation was established to educate the public about the Rosenstrasse Protest, as well as its impact and significance, honoring the memory of the protesters involved.

name: Undergraduate Moot Court Program at Florida State
description:
The Undergraduate Moot Court Program at Florida State University focuses on appellate arbitration proceedings in which members will draft and create oral arguments to use in various competitions entailing a simulated court environment. Under the American Moot Court Association (AMCA), FSU Moot Court competes against other schools in teams of two. 

 

This program has three main goals: (1) to foster a positive and uplifting environment for aspiring lawyers and those with a strong interest in a similar field; (2) to excel in and strive for success in every aspect of competition; (3) to refine skills that are necessary in future related professions.
summary: The Undergraduate Moot Court Program at FSU will provide an opportunity for students to engage in appellate advocacy and formal legal writing, and represent Florida State University in American Moot Court Association competitions.

name: Private Equity & Venture Capital Club at FSU
summary: The Private Equity & Venture Capital Club at FSU is committed to providing its members with career and educational opportunities in private equity and venture capital. We provide a platform for students interested in private equity and venture capital.

name: Psychology Club at FSU
shortName: FSU Psych Club, Florida State University Psych Club
summary: Psychology Club at FSU is a registered student organization focused on social, professional, and academic support of the undergraduate students at Florida State University interested in Psychology and related fields of study.

name: GIVE Volunteers at Florida State University
description:
Act locally. Think globally

GIVE at FSU...

Represents Global Citizenship
- We will discuss global topics, but also promote a globally-minded lifestyle. This means taking actions environmentally, socially, and economically with the mindset that all of our actions affect others outside of our home country

Practices Sustainable Volunteering and Living
- While there are many community service opportunities out there, there aren’t many that focus on the long-term solutions. We will be offering volunteer initiatives that are sustainably-focused with a lasting impact, rather than a simple one-time service day.

Seeks Adventure and Spreads Good Vibes
- Not only are we a group of globally-minded individuals, we are also people who care to explore and stretch our comfort zones!! We are a group of friends who want to empower each other and live life to its fullest!
summary: Our purpose is to inspire growth, empower the marginalized, and encourage sustainable change worldwide through promoting sustainable volunteer efforts, globally-minded initiatives, environmentally-conscious lifestyles, and adventurous activities.

name: Perfect Pair Chapter at FSU
description:
Perfect Pair started because there was a lack of programming that helped to establish meaningful, personalized, and one-on-one connections between residents in assisted living buildings and college students. 

Perfect Pair is unique in that it is largely individualized: meetings and activities will not look the same for every pair participating in our program. This really allows for both the senior and the student to organize their meetings in a way that is best tailored to their needs and interests. Through a more personalized connection, members are able to establish a stronger and more fulfilling relationship with a person whom they may have never had the opportunity to meet without the help of our program. 

Through this RSO, we are helping to extend the Perfect Pair Organization to Northwest Florida.
summary: This program creates 1-on-1 connections between senior citizens & college students. Pairs are based on similar hobbies & interests. Through laughs, conversation, & activities, our members have the opportunity to foster meaningful, lasting relationships.

name: Society of Physics Students at Florida State University
description: The Society of Physics Students, or SPS, is a professional association for students interested in physics. Our goals are to encourage and assist students interested in physics, help facilitate professional development among students, and promote public interest in physics.
summary: SPS is a professional association for students interested in physics. Our goals are to encourage and assist students interested in physics, help facilitate professional development among students, and promote public interest in physics.

name: Magic: The Gathering Club at FSU
description: Our goal is to create a community of players who can come together to play Magic: The Gathering as well as welcome new people who wish to learn how to play the game. By fostering an environment where players of all levels are able to hone their skills we are able to ensure that the spirit of the game is upheld.
summary: Our goal is to create a community of players who can come together to play Magic: The Gathering as well as welcome new people who wish to learn how to play the game.

name: Bold Campus Ministry at FSU
description:
Matthew 28:18-20 NIV
Then Jesus came to them and said, "All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me. [19] Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, [20] and teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age."

 

FSU Bold Campus Ministry is "Sold Out" in uniting everyone in the love of the Kingdom of God. We have various events, meetings, and activities weekly.

Whether you consider yourself “religious” or not, our events are open to all people from all spiritual backgrounds.

 

We love to study the Bible and build convictions on scripture to call us higher in our relationships with God. One thing we’re always looking for is open minded individuals to learn and live out the teachings of Jesus with preaching evangelism of all nations, we strive for unity and diversity among all of God's people.

At the end of the day, we just want to do life with Jesus—with you! So what’s holding you back? Come hang with us, grow bold in your walk with God, and let’s love big, together!
summary: FSU Bold Campus Ministry is devoted to furthering anyone in their relationship with God! We are disciples of Jesus that want to learn with others what it means to follow Jesus according to God’s Word. Family, Love, and Truth is what we do!

name: Spoonbill Society: Birding Club at FSU
description: The mission of this club is to encourage enthusiasm about birding and to foster curiosity about the natural world. The Spoonbill Society seeks to do so by creating a nurturing environment of birders from all levels of experience to better enjoy nature together while strictly adhering to the ABA Code of Birding Ethics.
summary: The mission of this club is to encourage enthusiasm about birding and to foster curiosity about the natural world.

name: Delta Sigma Phi
description: Our mission is to empower and encourage our members to become Better Men – men of culture, men of harmony, and men of friendship. Our vision is to become America’s leading fraternity, with an unparalleled leadership experience; recognized as among the most respected and influential organizations on campuses; and a culture that compels all living members to actively participate.
summary: Our mission is to empower and encourage our members to become Better Men – men of culture, men of harmony, and men of friendship. Our vision is to become America’s leading fraternity, with an unparalleled leadership experience.

name: Crochet Society at FSU
description:
Crochet society offers every-other week meetings where members can get help/to learn/socialize/or show off, we like to treat our crochet circles as fun opportunities to learn and to see what our members have crocheted. We love seeing new creations. You can find many details online on our website! All events are listed on our website for Fall 2025!

https://www.fsucrochetsociety.com/home
summary:
Crochet Society offers a learning experience for beginners to experts in crochet and more.
https://www.fsucrochetsociety.com/home

name: Future Women of Dentistry at Florida State University
description: The goal of Future Women of Dentistry is to further the advancement of women in dentistry by learning more about the dental field and the contributions of women in the field. This will be done as the organization will host professional workshops with visiting dental schools as well as community service and fundraising events.
summary: The goal of Future Women of Dentistry is to further the advancement of women in dentistry by learning more about the dental field and the contributions of women in the field.

name: Girl Gains at FSU
description: Our mission is to promote female weightlifting and empower women to feel strong and beautiful in a judgment-free community. Whether you’re a dedicated gym goer or a curious beginner, Girl Gains will support you in every stage of your fitness journey.
summary: Girl Gains FSU is a female weight-lifting organization that provides resources and community for women of all backgrounds and fitness levels.

name: The Political Review at Florida State University
description: The Political Review at Florida State University is an entirely student-run publication hosting a nonpartisan political forum for Florida State University students on issues and policy matters that affect local, state, or national governments.
summary: The Political Review at Florida State University is an entirely student-run publication hosting a nonpartisan political forum for Florida State University students on issues and policy matters that affect local, state, or national governments.

name: Health Equality Reassurance and Education For Y♀U: Women's Health and Wellness Association at Florida State University
description: H.E.R.E For Y♀U, in affiliation with Florida State University's Gynecology Clinic, aspires to provide adequate women’s health care relief and education on women’s wellness to the FSU and the surrounding community. In our club, H.E.R.E. For You (Health, Equality, Reassurance, and Education), we are dedicated to advancing the health and wellness of women and children in our community. As a student-led organization at Florida State University, our mission is to bridge the gap in healthcare access by providing essential resources, education, and support to underserved women and families. Through impactful community service initiatives, outreach programs, and educational resources, we strive to create a supportive environment that empowers women to take charge of their health and well-being. Together, we work to foster a community where every woman has the opportunity to thrive, regardless of her background or circumstances. We are H.E.R.E. For You—because everyone deserves access to a healthier future.
summary: H.E.R.E For Y♀U, in affiliation with Florida State University's Gynecology Clinic, aspires to provide adequate women’s health care relief and education on women’s wellness to the FSU and the surrounding community.

name: Arnold Air Society at FSU
description: Arnold Air Society was created to aid in the development of Air Force Officer candidates in particular, within the reserve officer training corps. We host meetings to teach other cadets in ROTC about history, important facts and training that we call 'warrior knowledge' as well as host motivational runs and workouts. There is a chapter of AAS at most schools that have an Air Force ROTC unit.
summary: Arnold Air Society is a professional honorary service organization advocating the support of aerospace power. You are required to be an Air Force ROTC cadet to be in AAS, but we operate separately from ROTC's standard operating procedures.

name: Bike Club at FSU
description:
The Bike Club at FSU is a student organization that provides a space for people with a love for biking to get together and do what they love most- bike! We are a hobby cycling organization that welcomes all skill levels on our Tallahassee rides! We promote the sustainability of FSU students by encouraging more biking and by providing members that need bikes organizations that can assist them. 

Our weekly rides are on Friday and range from 5-8 miles, we meet by the Landis Green fountain at 4:40 pm, and have bi-weekly bike rides that have longer mileage (10-45). Some of our routes include: Cascades park, Tallahassee Farmers Market, St. Marks Trail, Lafayette Heritage Trail Park, Lake Ella, FSU Rez and Wakulla Springs. 

Instagram: @fsubikingclub

Groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/62711512/q4yBbvMJ
summary: The Bike Club at FSU is a student organization that provides a space for people with a love for biking to get together and do what they love most- bike! We are a hobby cycling organization that welcomes all skill levels on our Tallahassee rides!

name: Flavor Society
description: Flavor Society is a food science organization providing students with hands-on experiences for developing skills in preparing foods from a variety of cuisines and food products. We explain the science behind all the food products we eat and see every day. We plan to host lectures on a variety of topics in the development of building recipes, meal prepping, and fun science experiments in product development.
summary: Flavor Society is a food science organization providing students with hands-on experiences for developing skills in preparing foods from a variety of cuisines and food products. We explain the science behind all the food products we eat and see every day

name: Lutheran Student Fellowship
description:
"A Jesus-Centered Community of Scripture, Faith, and Grace." - We keep Jesus and His teachings at the center of what we do and believe. We believe that the Bible is ultimately the thing that we trust and check ourselves against when we are talking about God and how to live a good life. We believe that we come to God with only trust (faith) on our part, nothing that would entitle us to a relationship with Him. We believe that He engages with us simply out of generosity (grace) on His part, a generosity that includes us into His Kingdom. To find out more, connect with us. 

 

925 West Jefferson Street (across from the Ragans/Wildwood housing complexes)
Tallahassee, FL 32304-8019
(parking off Pensacola Street across from the “Garnet and Gold” Stadium Location)
850-224-6059 (office)
850-284-9507 (cell)
Pastor Jay Winters - jwinters@fsu.edu

www.universitylutheranchurch.org

  • Sunday Worship - 11am (In Person and Online) 
  • Wednesday Student Night - 6:30pm

Call or visit our website, universitylutheranchurch.org for more information.
summary: Lutheran Student Fellowship is an organization of university students who meet in a Jesus-centered community of scripture, faith, and grace.

name: Argentinian Student Association
description:
The Argentinian Student Association (ARSA) at Florida State University is a vibrant community that fosters cultural exchange, academic support, and social connectivity among Argentinian students and those interested in Argentine culture. Embracing diversity and inclusivity, ARSA provides a welcoming space where members can celebrate their heritage, share experiences, and engage in activities that promote a deeper understanding of Argentina's rich history, traditions, and contemporary dynamics.

Our mission at the Argentinian Student Association (ARSA) at Florida State University is to cultivate a strong sense of belonging and pride among Argentinian students, while also serving as a bridge for cultural exchange within the broader university community. Through educational events, social gatherings, and community outreach initiatives, we aim to promote cross-cultural understanding, academic excellence, and personal growth. ARSA is committed to nurturing a supportive environment where members can thrive academically, socially, and personally, while forging lasting connections and contributing positively to the vibrant tapestry of Florida State University.
summary: ARSA at Florida State University fosters cultural exchange, academic support, and social connectivity among Argentinian students and enthusiasts. We promote understanding, excellence, and community for personal growth. Vamos Argentina and Go Noles!

name: Association of Herpetologists and Entomologists at FSU
description: We welcome everyone who wants to learn about herpetology and entomology! We go on weekly field trips to find frogs, salamanders, snakes, lizards, turtles, crocodylians, spiders, scorpions, insects, and other creatures. We have guest speakers give talks on herpetological and entomological research, and we work to connect club members with research opportunities. We contribute to citizen science databases, and educate others on conservation issues relevant to herpetology and entomology. For members interested in pursuing careers in herpetology and entomology, we also work on developing career relevant skills.
summary: The Association of Herpetologists and Entomologists at FSU exists for students who have an interest in reptiles, amphibians, insects, arachnids, and myriapods to learn more about these fascinating organisms and go herping.

name: Anesthesiology Interest Group
description: Our mission is to address critical aspects of anesthesiology, with a focus on increasing the presence of anesthesiologists in underserved rural areas both nationally and globally. We strive to deepen understanding of the specialty’s role in combating the United States opioid epidemic and educate the medical community on the diverse roles of general and subspecialty anesthesiologists. Through outreach and education, we aim to dispel misconceptions about anesthesiology procedures among healthcare professionals and the public. Additionally, we are committed to fostering diversity and inclusion within the field, ensuring equitable opportunities for all aspiring anesthesiologists.
summary: The mission of the Anesthesiology Interest Group (AIG) at Florida State University College of Medicine is to foster education, collaboration, advocacy, research, and networking for students and physicians pursuing careers in anesthesiology.

name: The Linguistics Organization
description:
The Linguistics Organization (LINGO) strives to create a collaborative, student-centered club that creates a community for aspiring linguists. Lingo provides numerous resources, such as internship opportunities, faculty connections, guest speakers, social events, and more. We aspire to help students build connections with other linguists as well as enrich their education in their linguistics field and build their resumes for their future careers.

 

Please click on this link (linktr.ee/lingofsu) for information and access to our membership form.
summary: The Linguistics Organization (LINGO) strives to create a collaborative, student-centered club that creates a community for aspiring linguists.

name: Active Minds at FSU
shortName: Active Minds, Active Minds at Florida State, Active Minds at Florida State University
description: Active Minds is a student-led chapter at Florida State University dedicated to spreading awareness and ending the stigma around mental health. We are part of more than 600 Active Minds chapters on college campuses across the U.S. focused on impacting students each year through campus awareness campaigns, events, advocacy, outreach, and more. We meet every week and our meetings are open to all! Check our Instagram (@FSU.ActiveMinds) and join our GroupMe to find out when the next meeting will take place!
summary: Active Minds is a student-led chapter dedicated to spreading awareness and ending the stigma around mental health.

name: After They Arrive
description: After They Arrive supports immigrants and refugees in the Tallahassee area. The organization works with three nonprofit refugee resettlement agencies: the International Rescue Committee (IRC), Center for the Advancement of Human Rights (CAHR), and Neighborly. Through engaging in community service activities, organizing fundraisers for local nonprofits, tutoring, hosting donation drives, and becoming immersed in other volunteer opportunities, the members of this organization offer hands-on support to families in need.
summary: After They Arrive at FSU is a student-led organization that dedicates time, resources, and community service to help immigrants and refugees in the Tallahassee community.

name: Women in Sports Law Society
description: The Women in Sports Law Society is dedicated to the promotion of women within the professional scope of sports law. The goal is to create a robust community of support and professional development for women with aspirations in the sports law profession.
summary: The Women in Sports Law Society is dedicated to promotion of women within the professional scope of sports law.

name: Child Psychology Club at Florida State University
description: The primary goal of the Child Psychology club aims to provide interested students with more experience in the field of Child Psychology and development. We aim to promote education and awareness of psychological tools and advice for future members of child psychology and other contributing fields of study! Our goals also include providing research opportunities and other services to support the development of children and adolescents.
summary: The Child Psychology club aims to provide interested students with more experience in Child Psychology and contributing fields of study by promoting education and advice supporting the development of children and adolescents!

name: Student Innovators at FSU
summary:
Student Innovators at FSU provides students from all majors with a pathway to grow their professional and technical skills through their work on unique projects. Housed in the Florida State Innovation Hub.

Open to everyone. No experience required.

name: Central American Latinx Organization
description:
The Central American Latinx Organization (CALOR) is an identity-based organization with the purpose of representing Central American students at Florida State and educating others about our diverse cultures through scholarship, service, and leadership.

"Siete países, una familia"

HOW TO JOIN: CALOR is open to any and all currently enrolled Florida State students interested in learning more about Central American culture. There is no sign up nor membership required to attend our events. Although there is no formal membership, by clicking "Request Membership" you can be added to our NoleCentral roster to receive event updates through email.

Stay up to date with everything CALOR by following our Instagram! @calorfsu
Join our GroupMe by using the link in the linktr.ee! www.linktr.ee/calorfsu
summary: The Central American Latinx Organization is an identity-based organization with the purpose of representing Central American students at Florida State and educating others about our diverse cultures through scholarship, service, and leadership.

name: GenCLEO at FSU
description: GenCLEO is the youth-led branch of the CLEO Institute, a nonprofit, nonpartisan organization in Florida exclusively dedicated to climate education, advocacy, and engagement. The purpose and goals of this student organization are to teach college-aged students valuable skills such as lobbying elected officials, interpreting complex climate policy, organizing their communities for climate justice, and public speaking. With Florida on the frontlines of the climate crisis because of sea-level rise, extreme heat, intensifying natural disasters, failing infrastructure, and more, pressing climate action is integral to our survival. The GenCLEO Chapter at Florida State University aims to educate and empower the youth to demand climate action and ensure a safe, just, and healthy environment for all.
summary: GenCLEO is the youth-led branch of the CLEO Institute, a nonprofit, nonpartisan organization in Florida exclusively dedicated to climate education, advocacy, and engagement. GenCLEO at FSU aims to educate and empower the youth to demand climate action.

name: The Laboratory For Emotions in Groups and Organizations at FSU
description:
The LEGO aims to enhance the educational experience of FSU undergraduates and graduate students interested in sport psychology. We meet bi-weekly to discuss and conduct research on emotions in groups and organizations, particularly in the context of sport, and to engage in knowledge translation efforts to inform the broader FSU and sport communities about high-quality sport psychology research findings.

Our CEHHS website details our current studies, involvement opportunities, and the unique interests and experiences that each member contributes.

The LEGO: Laboratory for Emotions in Groups and Organizations | College of Education (fsu.edu)
summary: The Laboratory for Emotions in Groups and Organizations operates as a body of both researchers and applied individuals who aim to make findings digestible and useful for coaches, athletes, performers, leaders, and students.

name: Association of Women Surgeons
summary: The AWS, is a not-for-profit educational and professional organization. With more than 3,000 members of all genders, AWS is one of the largest organizations dedicated to enhancing the interaction and exchange of information between women surgeons.

name: American Heritage Club at FSU
summary: Mission Statement: Cultivating an inclusive culture by celebrating diversity, encouraging unity, and inspiring originality.

name: The Sunshine Society at FSU
description:
The FSU Sunshine Society is a student research group dedicated to shining a light on Florida politics.

We are dedicated to providing Florida State University students with nonpartisan research on legislative policies that impact them, allowing students to better educate themselves and make informed political decisions.
summary: We are a nonpartisan student research group dedicated to shining a light on Florida politics. We provide students with nonpartisan research on legislative policies, allowing them to better educate themselves and make informed political decisions.

FSU Art Society is a newly formed club aiming to include any student interested in the arts. Art history, sculpture, figure drawing, painting, printing, graphic design and more! This club is meant to foster creativity for everyone. We will have meetings, art activities, workshops, events, and opportunities to get involved with the greater Tallahassee art community.

 
Our club sponsor is Elizabeth Di Donna

name: Civil and Environmental Engineering Graduate Students Association

We aspire to be the ultimate hub for networking, engaging workshops, robust research support, and unparalleled professional development opportunities. Our mission is to empower engineers, providing a conducive environment for academic excellence and professional growth. Whether guiding students towards fulfilling industry careers or supporting those choosing to stay in academia, we are committed to facilitating a journey of thriving and achievement for all our members. Join us in shaping a future where engineers not only succeed but excel both academically and professionally.
summary: This platform aims to be a hub for networking, workshops, research support, and professional development opportunities, empowering engineers to thrive both academically and professionally, helping students find their future goal for industry or academia.

name: Delight Ministries at FSU
summary: Delight is a college women's ministry that welcomes both Christian and soon to be Christian women in a space of comfort, learning, and growth. There are weekly Bible study meetings and events for FSU women to come together to bond over Christ.

name: Detachment 145 Cadet Honor Guard
summary: The Cadet Honor Guard is AFROTC Detachment 145's Honor Guard and Drill Teams, responsible for conducting color guard presentations around FSU and Tallahassee.

name: Next Level Noles Dance Team
description: Founded September 10th, 2023. Next Level Dance Troupe aims to foster artistic expression, promote physical fitness, and build community among our members. Through dynamic performances, creative choreography, and collaborative learning experiences, we aim to inspire confidence, discipline, and a lifelong love for dance. Our organization strives to provide a supportive space for individuals to develop their skills, celebrate diversity, and contribute positively to the cultural landscape through the powerful mediums of majorette and hip-hop dance.
summary: Next Level Dance Troupe is a majorette and hip-hop organization that brings in all students from any level of dance to have the opportunity to express themselves through movement.

name: Italian Heritage Club at FSU
summary: The Italian Heritage Club seeks to foster a community of Italians, Italian Americans, and people interested in Italian culture through social events, traditions, and various Italian dishes. Our general meetings are posted on our GroupMe and Instagram.

name: My Soul Sister
summary: Soul Sister is a local nonprofit organization that strives to create constant impact through community service, mentorship, professional development, and higher education by redefining and rebuilding the outlook on African American women and youth.

name: PERIOD @ FSU
description:
With chapters in over 45 US states and over 35 countries, PERIOD. activists have made strides for menstrual equity in their communities around the world.

Chapters work to advance PERIOD.’s mission of eradicating period poverty and stigma by creating action plans that employ our three pillars of service, education, and advocacy in their own community!
summary: With chapters in over 45 US states and over 35 countries, PERIOD. activists have made strides for menstrual equity in their communities around the world.

name: Music and Memory Society
description: As a volunteer-based club, Music and Memory is an organization that unifies music and medicine in a unique way to improve the quality of life for memory care patients. We volunteer at local long-term care facilities and play music for the residents in hopes to create a nostalgic and uplifting atmosphere, especially for those who face cognitive challenges.
summary: Music and Memory is an organization that unifies music and medicine to improve the quality of life for memory care patients. We volunteer at local long-term care facilities and raise awareness of music therapy.

name: Treasure Campus Ministry
summary: A campus ministry seeking God and making disciples.

name: MMA Club at FSU
description: Whether you aspire to become a competitor or simply want to stay fit while learning valuable self-defense skills, MMA at FSU welcomes you to join our club. Experience the adrenaline that comes with learning, striking, and grappling in a safe environment
summary: Whether you aspire to become a competitor or simply want to stay fit while learning valuable self-defense skills, MMA at FSU welcomes you to join our club. Experience the adrenaline that comes with learning, striking, and grappling in a safe environment.

name: Southeast Review
description:
The Southeast Review, established in 1979 as Sundog, is a national literary magazine housed in the English department at Florida State University, edited and managed by graduate students. Our mission is to present emerging writers on the same stage as well-established ones. We publish literary fiction, creative nonfiction, poetry, book reviews, interviews, and art across our biannual print issues and online. With nearly sixty members on our editorial staff who come from throughout the country and around the world, we publish work that is representative of our diverse interests and aesthetics, and we celebrate the eclectic mix this produces.

 

Work from SER is anthologized or noted in Best American Essays, Best of the Net, Best New Poets, Best Small Fictions, and the Pushcart Prize Anthology. Previous contributors include Ai Ogawa, Carl Phillips, Danez Smith, D.A. Powell, Denise Duhamel, Brandon Taylor, Erica Berry, Hai-Dang Phan, Justin Phillip Reed, Megan Giddings, Natalie Shapero, Tiana Clark, and Zeina Hashem Beck
summary: The Southeast Review, established in 1979 as Sundog, is a national literary magazine housed in the English department at Florida State University, edited and managed by graduate students.

name: Wakeboarding Club at FSU
description:
The purpose of the Florida State Wakeboard Club is to provide ample opportunities for any Florida State student who would like to either learn how to wakeboard or refine their current skills.

We especially want to bring wakeboarding to those who may have previously been deterred by cost, distance, or physical ability. 

Our vision is to share our passion for water sports and create a team of talented and motivated riders at any level of experience.
summary: The purpose of the Florida State Wakeboard Club is to provide ample opportunities for any Florida State student who would like to either learn how to wakeboard or refine their current skills.

name: Snow & Ski Club at FSU
shortName: Florida State University Snow and Ski Club
description: The purpose of the club is to bring people together who enjoy skiing or snowboarding.  We aim to provide convenient ways to bring passionate people on trips on which they can partake in skiing or snowboarding, improve skiing and snowboarding skills, improve comradery among fellow FSU students, and compete against other college clubs in the southeast region.  We aim to build a positive group of skiers and snowboarders who feel as if they are a strong and united team.  The club will bring people together in a way that benefits peoples’ interests in social activity, as well as interests in snow sports.  The club will provide FSU students with leadership opportunities and a sense of community centered around the enjoyment of skiing and snowboarding.
summary: The purpose of the club is to bring people together who enjoy skiing or snowboarding.  We aim to provide convenient ways to bring passionate people on trips on which they can partake in skiing or snowboarding, and improve skiing and snowboarding skills.

name: Helping Adults With Autism Perform and Excel Club at FSU
description: The Florida State University HAAPE chapter promotes a community of employment support and opportunities for adults with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). We work to foster an environment suitable for the challenges unique to adults with autism.
summary: The Florida State University HAAPE chapter promotes a community of employment support and opportunities for adults with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). We work to foster an environment suitable for the challenges unique to adults with autism.

name: Women in Finance & Economics
description: Women in Finance & Economics at FSU is an organization for all students attending FSU who are interested in the sectors of Finance and/or Economics. This organization is a place to provide opportunities for all and a chance to empower women in the world of Finance and Economics. Women in Finance & Economics at FSU is determined in providing workshops and events in these respective careers that range from Investment Banking to Corporate Law.
summary: Women in Finance & Economics at FSU is a place to provide opportunities and empower women in the world of Finance and Economics.

name: Ecuadorian Student Association at Florida State University
description:
The purpose of our student association is to provide Ecuadorian students at Florida State University with a place like home where they can immerse themselves in our culture, and history, and share these with anyone interested in learning more about Ecuador.

 

Our mission is to promote the exchange of cultures as well as inclusivity between Ecuadorians and other students from around the world at Florida State University. It is meant to be a community where we can come together to share our common customs and traditions. To leave an everlasting legacy for future students to come. Here we give Ecuadorian students a cultural acceptance and voice! We embrace diversity and Ecuadorian excellence, making this organization not only a cultural stop but a place to grow professionally.
summary: The purpose of our student association is to provide Ecuadorian students at FSU with a place like home where they can immerse themselves in our culture, and history, and share these with anyone interested in learning more about Ecuador.

name: Doctoral Business Student Association
description: The Doctoral Business Student Association brings business doctoral students together to coordinate both academic and social planning to make this interaction effective. It provides a forum for providing input to the Ph.D. Policy Committee on program design, academic policies and procedures and general student issue.  Additionally, the DBSA schedules social events throughout the academic year involving doctoral students, faculty and their families.
summary: The Doctoral Business Student Association (DBSA) brings doctoral students in business together for academic and social purposes.

name: SIAM at FSU
description: The SIAM(Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics) Student Chapter of FSU is an organization that aims to promote the application of mathematics in industry and create a community for those interested in applied mathematics.  SIAM has a variety of social and professional development events that introduce members to the applied mathematics community, develop industrial skills, and learn about the latest advances in applied mathematics research.  Members have valuable networking opportunities and free membership to the international SIAM organization.  Follow our Instagram page  (@siamatfsu) for updates!
summary: The SIAM Student Chapter of FSU is an organization that aims to promote the application of mathematics in industry and create a community for those interested in applied mathematics.

name: Students Supporting Israel at Florida State University
description:
Students Supporting Israel is a rapidly growing Zionist international campus movement that supports the State of Israel. Our mission is to be a clear and confident pro-Israel voice on FSU's campus, and to support students in grassroots pro-Israel advocacy. 

We are committed to promoting a better understanding of Israel throughout North and South America as a member of the family of nations, with a fundamental right to exist as a Jewish, democratic state, within secure borders. 

SSI at FSU wants to provide students on on campus with the opportunity to support the position of Israel in the Middle East, and to reassure students who oppose the demonization of the State of Israel on campus that they are not alone. We are changing the anti-Israel climate many students encounter on campus. By being part of a united, strong and thriving international movement, students are empowered to express their views in support of Israel.
summary: Students Supporting Israel is a rapidly growing Zionist international campus movement that supports the State of Israel. Our mission is to be a clear and confident pro-Israel voice on FSU's campus and support students in grassroots pro-Israel advocacy.

name: Student Aviation Club
description:
The Student Aviation Club at FSU is a dynamic and inclusive organization dedicated to fostering a passion for aviation among students at Florida State University. Our club serves as a hub for aviation enthusiasts to connect, collaborate, and cultivate their interest in various aspects of the aviation industry.

With a diverse membership ranging from aspiring airline pilots to future military aviators, our club provides a supportive and engaging environment for students to explore their interests in aviation. Whether you're dreaming of taking to the skies as a pilot, navigating the complexities of air traffic control, or delving into the fascinating world of aerospace engineering, there's a place for you in our club.

Through a variety of activities and initiatives, including guest speakers, field trips to aviation facilities, flight simulations, and networking events with industry professionals, we aim to provide valuable opportunities for learning, skill development, and career exploration.
summary: The Student Aviation Club at FSU is a vibrant community of aviation enthusiasts at Florida State University. Through diverse activities and events like guest speakers and field trips, members explore their passion for aviation and gain valuable skills.

name: Songwriting Club
description: Songwriting Club is a club centered around teaching students how to write songs, expanding their knowledge of songwriting, building community, and hosting various other music events. Some of our events include open mics, songwriting workshops, guest speakers, listening gatherings, and songwriting picnics.
summary: Songwriting Club is a club centered around teaching students how to write songs, expanding their knowledge of songwriting, building community, and hosting various other music events.

name: Operation S.W.I.F.T.
description:
Welcome to Operation S.W.I.F.T.! Our club's main goal is to help students at FSU feel better in all areas of their lives - physically, mentally, and socially. We want to make sure everyone has the tools and support they need to reach their health goals, whatever they may be. Our aim is to help our members achieve the health goals they set for themselves each semester. Here are some of the things that we do!

1. Getting Fit: We're here to help you get moving! Whether you're into group workouts or prefer doing your own thing, we've got tips and tricks for all levels of fitness. Plus, we'll clue you in on all the great places on campus where you can break a sweat.

2. Tackling Mental Struggles: Taking care of your mental health is just as important as taking care of your body. We'll have chats and activities focused on feeling good about yourself and dealing with any stress or struggles you might be facing.

3. Making Friends: College can be a lonely place sometimes, so we're all about bringing people together. We'll be hosting fun events where you can meet new people and make some lasting connections all while working on your health!

4. Eating Right: Let's talk about food! We'll have discussions on how to make healthier choices when it comes to what you eat. Whether you're wondering about meal prep or just need some snack ideas, we've got you covered. Discussions from both trained dietitians, as well as your peers, will be sure to give you all you need to make great choices!

5. Setting Goals: The biggest part to becoming more healthy is to set goals. Whether it's increasing your strength, running a marathon, or increasing your flexibility, we'll work with you to set goals that make sense and then help you stick to them.

So if you're looking to feel better, get moving, and make some friends along the way, come check us out!

If you want to stay up to date with about Operation S.W.I.F.T. join our GroupMe:

https://groupme.com/join_group/99447154/g4OHE4aB
summary: We serve to promote all different types of health on campus. We strive to help each other reach our own personalized fitness goals all while having fun and building a strong sense of community!

name: NeuroMinds Neuroscience Society
summary: We aim to provide a welcoming community for individuals passionate about the brain and its mysteries, creating an environment where members can come together to meet like-minded peers, engage in study sessions, and participate in hands-on experiments.

name: International City/ County Management Association FSU Student Chapter
description:
The Florida State University International City/County Management Association (ICMA) Student Chapter was created to promote professional development and leadership in local government. Our chapter provides students with opportunities to engage in public service, network with local government professionals and develop skills for successful careers in public service.

Each year student members have the opportunity to attend the ICMA Annual conference where they can connect with local government professionals, learn about emerging trends in local government and strengthen their professional networks.

Connect with our Chapter on Instagram or email us at FSUICMAStudentChapter@outlook.com
summary: ICMA Student Chapters introduce and integrate students into the local government management profession and familiarize them with members, resources, and the ICMA Code of Ethics.

name: Freshman Class Council

The Freshman Class Council (FCC) is an entity that serves the Freshman class at Florida State University. The mission of the Freshman Class Council is to foster unity and pride among the class through engaging programming that is deeply rooted in tradition.

Our goals are to host both fun and impactful events that will enhance the experience of the students who identify themselves as Freshmen.
summary: The Freshman Class Council serves the Freshman Class at Florida State University. The Freshman Class Council hosts programming dedicated to development for students here at Florida State University.
