name: YouthfulVoice
description:
YouthfulVoice is a vibrant Christian organization that fervently embraces the teachings of Jesus Christ. With an unwavering passion for faith, we are a dynamic club committed to igniting the flame of devotion in the hearts of all members. As well as embodying a spirit of inclusivity and encouragement. As an organization, YouthfulVoice is dedicated to guiding individuals on a journey of righteousness and holiness. We provide a platform for spiritual growth, fostering an environment where members can deepen their understanding of the Word, connect with like-minded individuals, and engage in meaningful discussions about faith and life.
Our activities encompass a wide range of opportunities, including regular prayer meetings, worship sessions, Bible studies, and community outreach programs. Through these initiatives, we aim to not only nurture the spiritual well-being of our members but also extend a helping hand to those in need, embodying the teachings of compassion and service that Christ exemplified. YouthfulVoice stands as a beacon of hope, radiating the message of Christ's love, acceptance, and transformation. With open hearts and minds, we strive to create an atmosphere where everyone feels empowered to embrace their unique journey of faith and contribute positively to the world around them.
summary: YouthfulVoice is a dynamic Christian organization that centers its beliefs on the profound message of Jesus Christ's sacrifice on the cross for the redemption of humanity's sins. Our core mission is to serve as representatives of Christ.

name: Chi Psi
description: Chi Psi fraternity, also known as "ΧΨ," is a national organization dedicated to cultivating a lifelong brotherhood based on the principles of truth, honor, and personal integrity. Founded in 1841 at Union College, Chi Psi emphasizes leadership, scholarship, and community service in its mission to develop men of integrity and moral character. The fraternity values academic excellence, personal growth, and social responsibility, aiming to create a supportive and inclusive environment where members can thrive both personally and professionally. Through its commitment to brotherhood and service, Chi Psi strives to inspire its members to lead with integrity and make a positive impact in their communities.
summary: Chi Psi fraternity, also known as "ΧΨ," is a national organization dedicated to cultivating a lifelong brotherhood based on the principles of truth, honor, and personal integrity.

name: Lifestyle Medicine Interest Group at FSU
description: The LMIG at FSU is a student interest group affiliated with the American College of Lifestyle Medicine. The mission of the organization is to support optimal health and wellness through lifestyle practices based on the 6 Pillars of Lifestyle Medicine. At least three events are planned every semester in hybrid formats with guest speakers, group activities, and even food prep.
summary: The LMIG at FSU is a student interest group affiliated with the American College of Lifestyle Medicine. The mission of the organization is to support optimal health and wellness through lifestyle practices based on the 6 Pillars of Lifestyle Medicine.

name: Marriage and Family Therapy Graduate Student Organization
description: The Marriage and Family Therapy (MFT) Graduate Student Organization (GSO) is a student self-governing organization that serves as a liaison among graduate students and between graduate students, faculty, and administration in the Department of Human Development and Family Science at Florida State University. It is a source of aid and services to graduate students and a voice for them to express their needs, concerns, and ideas. It organizes social events, research- and career-related affairs, and creative endeavors in helping to foster a professional, warm, helping, and collaborative culture in the MFT program.
summary: The Marriage and Family Therapy (MFT) Graduate Student Organization (GSO) is a student self-governing organization that serves as a liaison among graduate students and between graduate students, faculty, and administration in the department of HDFS.

name: Bonsai Club at FSU
summary: The bonsai club is a place where people talk about trees.

name: Investment Trading Association
description:
Many students are familiar with basic investments like purchasing stocks, but some seek to explore more advanced strategies such as day trading, options, or futures trading. Our aim is to educate as many students as possible about the diverse market transactions available for engagement. Here at ITA, we focus primarily on active short-term trading styles and dive into nearly every aspect of investing, including cryptocurrency.

 

The Investment Trading Association will convene at least once a week to host "learning sessions," where we will educate students about various aspects of trading. For instance, one day we might dive into technical indicators like RSI, MACD, and EMA, while another session could focus on evaluating market trends with quantitative analysis.

 

Moreover, we strive to provide a more "hands-on" learning environment for our community by engaging in what is known as "paper trading." This approach allows our members to participate in the market with zero risk, using simulated funds to purchase stocks, stock options, futures contracts, and more. By applying the knowledge gained from our weekly meetings, students can practice trading and gain valuable experience without risking real money.

Flare Link
summary: Our goal at ITA is to educate students about investments, covering options, futures, and cryptocurrencies. Through hands-on paper trading, we will create a practical learning environment that allows students to confidently engage in the market!

name: LEONES
shortName: Leaders Empowering Others in Need of Education and Service
description:
Leaders Empowering Others in Need of Education & Service.
 
LEONES is a service-based organization at Florida State University dedicated to mentoring and supporting underrepresented youth in the Tallahassee community. Our mission is to empower students, particularly those in Title I schools, through consistent mentorship, academic support, and educational enrichment. LEONES also provides meaningful service opportunities for FSU students who are passionate about making a difference in education and youth development.
 
~ An Initiative of the Alpha Mu Chapter of Phi Iota Alpha Fraternity, Inc. ~
summary: LEONES, which stands for Leaders Empowering Others in Need of Education & Service, is an outreach program designed to provide mentoring and tutoring services to underprivileged youth in the Tallahassee community.

name: The Explorer's Guild
description: The Explorer's Guild is designed to facilitate undergraduates in their efforts to decide on a major and career path. The organization offers undergraduate students the opportunity to build a community with other students that entered FSU unsure of their future, whether in the short term (college major) or long term (career path). With alumni from the Exploration & Discovery Living Learning Community, students will have the opportunity to experience mentorship and guidance from individuals who were initially unsure of their plans and have since explored different majors/career paths and discovered their goals.
summary: Designed to facilitate undergraduates in their efforts to decide on a major and career path while also providing them the opportunity to build a community with like-minded individuals who may also be questioning the direction of their future.

name: Lake Munson Student Advocacy Group
description:
The Lake Munson Student Advocacy Group aims to raise student awareness of the social, environmental,  and community health issues at Lake Munson. The Group is raising money for local advocacy groups like Friends of Lake Munson.

Visit our Custom Ink fundraiser page below to help support Lake Munson!
summary: Lake Munson has been a polluted body of water in Tallahassee for over 100 years and has a long history of community health issues. LMSAG aims to raise student awareness of the issues at Lake Munson and raise money for local advocacy groups.

name: Notion at Florida State University
summary: Notion at FSU is an extension of Notion's student Campus Leaders program, run by select university Campus Leaders that share and teach Notion effectively and build strong campus communities!

name: Women in Politics at FSU
description: Women in Politics at FSU was created to empower women, promote representation, and collaborate with local organizations and initiatives to address gender-based political disparities and create positive change in our community.  Together, let's make our voices heard and shape the future of politics! Whether you're a seasoned political enthusiast or just beginning your journey into the world of politics, all are welcome!
summary: Empowering, inspiring, and advocating for women's voices in the political arena, our club is a vibrant community where students passionate about politics come together to foster dialogue, support, and action.

name: William Dodd Society
description:
Established in 2024, the William Dodd Society is a student-run debate society at Florida State University. Members engage in productive conversations on landmark social, cultural and contemporary issues. "The half of knowledge is to know where to find knowledge."

 

Please message our secretary Oscar D'Amato for inquiries: ohd22a@fsu.edu
summary: Established in 2024, the William Dodd Society is a student-run debate society at Florida State University. Members engage in productive conversations on landmark social, cultural and contemporary issues.

name: First Gen Community
description:
The purpose of this organization is to alleviate the challenges faced by first-generation students who may not have the same support network as their peers. Recognizing that these students cannot simply rely on parental guidance, our aim is to create a community where they can lean on each other for support. Drawing from our shared experiences, we believe that by coming together, we can mentor and guide one another to navigate the academic journey more smoothly.

      Our mission is centered on fostering a vibrant and supportive community on campus, with core objectives that include networking among minority students, engaging with the broader community, and providing avenues for job and scholarship opportunities. To achieve this, we have developed a mentoring program where upperclassmen will offer guidance to younger students in the same major or with similar interests. We emphasize the importance of building strong student relationships through various means such as social events, tutoring sessions, peer mentoring, volunteer opportunities, and providing a platform for members to voice their ideas and concerns. Additionally, we encourage students to take leadership roles in organizing events that align with the organization's goals, with our club offering support in bringing these ideas to fruition.

      Furthermore, we aim to collaborate with other organizations on campus to host community gatherings that cater to the diverse needs of our members. Whether it's connecting with Latin organizations, Cultural Student Associations, or Black Student Unions, our goal is to provide our members with access to resources and create a sense of belonging within the broader campus community.

      In further detail, the organization will host events that foster community building, allowing students to assist each other based on common interests in their majors. This may involve referring to different organizations, scholarships, or internships, and sharing experiences of how they obtained these opportunities. Upperclassmen will have the chance to connect with others who share similar interests, forming groups where they can offer tutoring assistance or provide guidance on relevant contacts and resources, such as professors who can offer support. The organization will also seek to develop valuable skills in its members, including public speaking, time management, interviewing techniques, and community service, among others.
summary: Our mission will be dedicated to creating a vibrant and supportive community on campus, driven by the following core objectives: Networking between minority students, community engagement, and providing each other jobs, mentoring and scholarships.

name: Poker Society at Florida State
description:
The Poker Society at Florida State University provides students with a professional and educational environment to explore the game of poker. Fully committed to state and federal laws, the club does not involve real money gambling. Instead, it focuses on learning, strategy, and responsible engagement in a safe and structured setting open to all skill levels.

General body meetings include workshops on poker strategy, psychology, history, and culture, along with occasional guest speakers from the casino and gaming industry. Members can participate in coaching circles, free live practice games, and discussions centered on the mathematical and analytical side of poker. Social events such as poker-themed movie nights further build community and shared interest.

Overall, the FSU Poker Club is more than a hobby. It is a space for critical thinking, professional development, and connection with others who share a genuine interest in the game.
summary:
Visit our Instagram @FSUPoker and join the GroupMe for updates, announcements, and events.
The Poker Society at Florida State offers a professional, educational space for students who share a passion for poker.

name: Global Legal Empowerment Brigades at FSU
description: This is a chapter of Global Legal Empowerment Brigades, an international movement of students and attorneys providing legal education and financial resources for vulnerable families to resolve legal challenges. We focus our work with women in remote, rural, and under resourced communities who would otherwise have limited to no access to legal resources. Volunteers facilitate legal clinics with lawyers to provide pro-bono consulting and the financial resources for community members to help resolve cases. nnIn conjunction with our Legal Empowerment Program, Global Brigades also supports communities with healthcare, economic development, clean water and sanitation projects, and uniquely implements these programs in a holistic model to meet a community’s health and economic goals. Our model systematically builds community ownership and collaboratively executes programs with the end goal of sustainably evolving to a relationship of impact monitoring.
summary: This chapter is a part of an international movement of students and Panamanian & Honduran attorneys providing legal education and financial resources for vulnerable families to resolve legal challenges.

name: Undergraduate Investment Group at FSU
description: The Undergraduate Investment Group at FSU exists to help members better hone their investment skills. We learn stock pitching, research stocks, and present stock pitches to the student investment fund.
summary: A Club to Improve Investment Skill

name: Rotaract Club at FSU
description:
Mission: We provide service to others, promote integrity, and advance world understanding, goodwill, and peace through our fellowship of business, professional, and community leaders.

Vision: Together, we see a world where people unite and take action to create lasting change — across the globe, in our communities, and in ourselves.

Rotaract will bring together people ages 18 and older to exchange ideas with leaders in the community, develop leadership and professional skills, and have fun through service. In communities worldwide, Rotary and Rotaract members work side by side to take action through service. From big cities to rural villages, Rotaract is changing communities like yours. Solving real problems takes real commitment and vision. For more than 110 years, Rotary's people of action have used their passion, energy, and intelligence to take action on sustainable projects. From literacy and peace to water and health, we are always working to better our world and stay committed to the end.

Common goals for which Rotary works with the Rotaract organization to accomplish include (but are not limited to): Promoting Peace, Fighting Disease, Helping Mothers and Children, Supporting Education, Growing Local Economies, Protecting the Environment, and Providing Clean Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene to communities worldwide.
summary: Rotaract is a worldwide organization found in universities and communities. Rotaract gives back to the community it is established in, through service and uniting young people. We work with Rotary to promote peace, support education, and much more!

name: Disabled Student Union
description: The Disabled Student Union (DSU) is an organization for Florida State University students to come together and represent, advocate for, and support the disabled community while educating the student body on issues, information, and history relating to disabilities. The DSU shall create a welcoming, safe, and affirming environment for all students with disabilities and their allies.
summary: The DSU at Florida State University supports students with disabilities, educates the student body and faculty on disability related topics, and fosters an inclusive campus environment.

name: Bodybuilding and Fitness Club at FSU
description: Welcome to the Bodybuilding and Fitness Club at FSU! Whether you want to step on stage and compete, or simply improve your overall strength and physique, our club offers a supportive and motivating environment for like-minded individuals of all fitness levels to connect and achieve their bodybuilding goals. From nutrition tips to lifting techniques, we like to share knowledge and discuss all aspects of the sport of bodybuilding with the goal of helping each other reach our fitness ambitions. Our club hosts regular workouts, meetings, and guest speaker events to help members learn proper techniques and nutrition for bodybuilding. We want to foster a supportive gym community that Florida State University students can enjoy.
summary: Our mission is to promote a healthy, active lifestyle through bodybuilding and fitness while fostering a supportive gym community at FSU. We believe that through proper training, nutrition, and recovery, individuals can achieve their fitness goals.

name: Kindness Matters at FSU
description:
Kindness Matters 365 is an organization that delivers innovative programs to support the well-being of kids, teens, and adults. Grounded in the principles of Social Emotional Learning (SEL) and providing skill-building through experiential learning activities, KM365’s unique approach teaches individuals to discover how to care for themselves, each other, and our world through exploring self-awareness, self-navigation, empathy, a growth mindset, and a dedication to service-learning.

One of the primary focuses of KM365 is providing handmade gifts to those in need. These gifts are not just material possessions but tangible expressions of care and support. Whether it's crafting blankets for homeless shelters, care packages for the elderly, or toys for children in hospitals, the club pours its creativity and energy into making a difference.

Beyond gift-giving, the club also organizes events and initiatives centered on spreading kindness. This could include random acts of kindness days, where members perform small gestures like paying for someone's coffee or leaving uplifting notes around campus. Additionally, they may collaborate with other organizations to host awareness campaigns, workshops, or fundraisers aimed at fostering a culture of kindness.

KM365 serves as a beacon of positivity in the community, reminding everyone that even the smallest acts of kindness can have a ripple effect, spreading joy and compassion far and wide. Through their dedication and unwavering commitment to making the world a better place, they inspire others to join in their mission of kindness, creating a more caring and connected community for all.
summary: Kindness Matters at FSU is a heartwarming club dedicated to spreading acts of kindness throughout the FSU campus and the Tallahassee community. Their mission revolves around promoting compassion, empathy, and generosity in everyday life.

name: Global Markets Group at Florida State
shortName: Sales & Trading Club, GMG at FSU
description: Global Markets Group at Florida State University is the leading on campus community for students passionate about financial markets. We provide education, mentorship, and networking opportunities to empower our members to excel in sales & trading, quantitative finance, and related careers. Our flagship program is Markets Mentorship, a highly selective and competitive workshop for sophomores looking to break into sales & trading, hedge fund, quantitative analysis, and investment management roles. Markets Mentorship provides students with mentorship and mock interview experience that helps them excel at networking and succeed during interviews, as well as the tools and resources needed to gain technical knowledge to stand out from other candidates.
summary: Global Markets Group at Florida State is the premier community for students passionate about financial markets. We provide education and mentorship opportunities to empower our members to excel in sales & trading, equity research, and related careers

name: Lifting Our Voices Incorporated at FSU
description: https://youtu.be/_oFbvKMoKIg?si=Z0fAGRgI3qBszi2_
summary: Lifting Our Voices, Inc. (LOV) is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization. We focus on empowering the voices of our most vulnerable populations in our communities through need-based service projects.

name: We Raise the Bar
description:
We Raise the Bar is a student-led nonprofit organization dedicated to promoting diversity and inclusivity within the legal field. RTB empowers aspiring legal professionals from underrepresented backgrounds through mentorship, professional development, and community engagement.

Our mission is to break down barriers to entry into the legal profession by fostering a supportive network and providing resources that inspire, educate, and prepare students for success. We host workshops, networking events, and outreach initiatives that connect members to legal professionals, build leadership skills, and create opportunities for meaningful impact in the community.

RTB aims to uplift the next generation of legal trailblazers while addressing systemic challenges in the field of law through a commitment to advocacy, education, and collaboration. Together, we raise the bar for diversity, excellence, and equity in the legal profession.
summary: We Raise the Bar is a student-led nonprofit organization dedicated to providing opportunities in the legal field to Black students. We achieve this by prioritizing community service and educating students in the Tallahassee community.

name: WorldWide Community at FSU

  • A warm and receptive atmosphere to bring International, Exchange, and U.S. students together into a strong, unified, and fun community. 
  • Members and friends coordinate opportunities and events to get to meet, travel, and share new cultures and languages.
  • An environment to help participants keep in touch with a growing circle of like-minded and adventurous friends.
summary: WorldWide Community at FSU unites International, Exchange, and U.S. students, fostering a welcoming environment for cultural exchange and friendship.

name: Raw Health FSU
description: Raw Health FSU is a student-led organization focused on holistic health and wellness. We empower students to live healthier, more balanced lives through education, events, and peer support, emphasizing clean eating, fitness, and mental well-being. Our mission is to create a supportive community that fosters sustainable, natural health practices for both the mind and body.
summary: Raw Health FSU promotes holistic wellness through clean eating, fitness, and mental health support, empowering students to live healthier, balanced lives.

Led by peers who have walked similar paths, our group aims to foster connection, understanding, and mutual support. We believe in the power of community and shared experiences to help each other navigate the challenges of recovery. Join us to share your journey, gain valuable insights, and build lasting connections with others who truly understand. To register for our support group, click the link here to sign up!
summary: Our organization is dedicated to providing a safe, supportive, and accepting environment for students struggling with eating disorders on campus. Led by peers who have walked similar paths, our group aims to foster connection, understanding, and support.

name: Clinical Psychology Graduate Students Association
description:
The Clinical Psychology Graduate Student Association (CPGSA) is composed of graduate students and faculty across research labs and cohorts within the Clinical Psychology doctoral program. The Association serves as a forum for collaboration, professional development, and exchange of ideas to enhance the quality of research, clinical training, and education within our program.

The CPGSA promotes scholarship and practice that reflect the diverse interests and professional goals of our students and faculty. Our initiatives aim to strengthen training experiences, support student-led research, and foster connections within the department and the broader professional community.

The Association also supports efforts to ensure that our program provides an inclusive and supportive environment for students, faculty, and clients. We seek to cultivate professional growth and cultural humility that members can carry into their future academic, clinical, and research settings.

The CPGSA includes several branches, such as outreach and research, which organize programming, events, and initiatives throughout the year. Our activities are supported by the department, university, and external organizations.
summary: CPGSA is composed of graduate students and faculty in the clinical psychology doctoral program and serves as a forum for collaboration, professional development, and exchange of ideas to enhance the quality of research, clinical training, and education.

name: Group for Artificial Intelligence Navigation
summary: GAIN at FSU

name: Business Students from Abroad at FSU
description: The mission of Business Students from Abroad is to create a welcoming community for international students pursuing business degrees at Florida State University. Our purpose is to provide international business students with opportunities to network, collaborate, and learn from one another. We strive to enhance the academic and social experience of our members through various activities, workshops, and events focused on business education.
summary: Business Students from Abroad is a welcoming community for international students pursuing business degrees at FSU. Members engage in the academic and social experience of our club through various activities, workshops, and events.

name: Student Intelligence & Emergency Response Association
description:
The Student Intelligence and Emergency Response Association (SIERA) aims to foster a collaborative environment at Florida State University where individuals can explore and discuss topics related to emergency management, intelligence, emerging technologies, national and international security. SIERA connects like-minded students, faculty, and professionals from various fields through its meetings and events, offering a forum to educate and engage individuals on these critical issues.

SIERA provides opportunities for members to engage in meaningful dialogue about current challenges and the future of homeland security in an unbiased and nonpartisan setting. Additionally, SIERA is dedicated to promoting FSU’s Emergency Management and Homeland Security Program, as well as its intersection with student opportunities and academic knowledge.

By facilitating these connections and discussions, SIERA strives to contribute to the knowledge and preparedness of the FSU community.
summary: The Student Intelligence and Emergency Response Association (SIERA) is an organization where individuals can explore and discuss topics related to emergency management, intelligence, emerging technologies, national, and international security.

name: bLu crew
description:
We are a collaborative, student-led initiative championing the vision of the Lucy Fernandez Foundation by cultivating leadership, advocacy, responsibility and philanthropy. We aim to create a stronger and more loving community by teaching and inspiring others to become their best selves in all aspects of life.

 

What is the Lucy Fernandez Foundation?

The Lucy Fernandez Foundation was established in Miami, Florida, in honor and loving memory of Luciana Cristina Fernandez after her passing in a fatal boating accident in September of 2022. Through this foundation, two initiatives were established: the ‘Love Like Lucy’ mission and the ‘Fly High, Bird’ Boater and Watercraft Safety Initiative. By championing these two initiatives, the foundation is able to carry Lucy’s light and legacy to lead a community of responsible, dedicated and compassionate members.
summary: Student-led initiative championing the lucyfernandezfoundation vision by strengthening community and promoting watercraft safety.

name: Women, Gender, and Sexuality Student Organization
summary: A space to discuss and engage with feminist, gender, and sexuality theory. Helping to connect such academic theory to our day to day and current events.

name: Business Brigades at FSU
description: We work in remote, rural, and under-resourced communities in Honduras, Panama, Guatemala, Nicaragua, Greece, Ghana, and more, where students from Florida State University have the opportunity to share financial, marketing, accounting, etc. knowledge with small local business owners, families, and communities as a whole. Our chapter will contribute by taking an annual trip abroad for 7-9 days with local banks during the oversight of promoting economic development within the different communities located in the country of work. In conjunction with our Business Program, Global Brigades also supports communities with community health and clean water projects, and uniquely implements these programs in a holistic model to meet a community's health and economic goals.
summary: Business Brigades at FSU is a chapter of Global Brigades, volunteers facilitate financial literacy workshops and provide consulting to micro-enterprises on topics ranging from marketing to accounting.

name: Holistic Health Club
description: Our organization is dedicated to educating students about the benefits of a holistic lifestyle. Our vision is to cultivate a campus culture where college students prioritize their physical, mental, and emotional well-being. We empower students to eat cleaner, move their bodies with intention, and care for their mental health through natural and sustainable practices. By fostering a supportive community rooted in education, self-awareness, and balance, we strive to make holistic wellness a lifestyle—accessible, enjoyable, and transformative.
summary: Our mission is to educate, learn about, and discover the power of the holistic lifestyle.

Members of Philosophy, Politics, and Economics at Florida State University will have the opportunity to gain a multidisciplinary perspective of social, political, and economic challenges of the world.
summary: We aim to promote the study and teachings across the fields of philosophy, politics, and economics to encourage perspective on social and political problems that cannot be gained from any discipline alone.

name: Second Language Acquisition Network Group
description:
(Second) Language Acquisition Network Group (S.L.A.N.G)

Addressing the Gap in Language Learning Opportunities

Issue: Many students studying a foreign language struggle to find effective ways to enhance their language acquisition. Key challenges include:

  • Limited real-world use of a second language across diverse topics.
  • Difficulty in fostering friendships and connections with individuals from different cultures and language backgrounds.
  • A significant gap between learners of different languages (e.g., learners of English and Spanish).
  • Uncomfortability in conversing with strangers in a foreign language.
  • Strong desire to actively produce and practice a second language.

Mission: To establish a robust network within Tallahassee that enhances the acquisition of second languages and to create an organization that provides solutions to the issues indicated above.

Promotion of Cultural Diversity and Inclusion:

  • Language exchanges in which we will bring international and internationally-minded/rooted students together to converse in various languages.
  • Sharing cultural aspects of language through presentations on topics like language diffusion, language extinction, etymology, scientific language use, etc
  • Partnering with other cultural clubs for mutual benefits and greater diffusion.
  • Organizing music exchanges and performances by culturally diverse bands.
  • Cultural Showcases: Hosting events where members can present cultural dances, traditional attire, and other cultural artifacts.
  • Film Screenings: Regular screenings of foreign films followed by discussions to explore cultural contexts and language nuances.
  • Cuisine Nights: Collaborating with local restaurants or cooking clubs to offer authentic culinary experiences from various cultures.
  • Storytelling Sessions: Inviting native speakers to share traditional stories and folklore, enhancing understanding of cultural narratives.
  • Art Exhibitions: Featuring works by artists from different cultural backgrounds to highlight the intersection of language and visual art.
  • Cultural Workshops: Conducting workshops on calligraphy, traditional crafts, and other cultural practices.
  • Heritage Celebrations: Recognizing and celebrating international festivals and holidays to foster a deeper appreciation of global cultures.
  • Language Buddy Program: Pairing members with native speakers for one-on-one language practice and cultural exchange.
summary: SLANG is dedicated to helping individuals wanting to acquire and speak new languages through language exchanges, cultural events, movie screenings, and so much more! The primary activity SLANG hosts is language exchanges.

name: Page Turners at FSU
description:
Hello fellow readers!

What is Page Turners Club at FSU’s mission? To create a safe and fun environment for students to share a love of reading! Reading is a special hobby that can transport you from one place to another, and the only thing better than that is sharing it with others! In addition to book discussions, the club organizes author talks, writing workshops, and literary seminars to enhance members' understanding and appreciation of literature. The club also emphasizes community outreach by partnering with local libraries and schools to promote literacy through book drives and reading programs. By providing a structured yet flexible environment, Page Turners at FSU ensures that members can immerse themselves in the world of books, share their insights, and grow together as a community of passionate readers.
summary: Page Turners at FSU is a dynamic organization dedicated to fostering a love for reading and literary exploration among its members. The club operates by selecting diverse books each month, which members read and then discuss in lively, engaging meetings.

name: Akoma Ntoso Foundation at FSU
description: Akoma Ntoso is a non profit organization, that I am already a part of, in where both American and Ghanian volunteers come together to help disadvantaged people in villages in Ghana. This club gives pre med, pre nursing, or anyone who just wants to volunteer the opportunity to gain once in a lifetime volunteering experience.  As a nonprofit partnership between dedicated American and Ghanaian volunteers, we are deeply committed to uplifting disadvantaged communities in rural Ghana. Our mission is clear: to enhance the medical well-being, promote healthy living, and provide educational opportunities that empower residents to break the cycle of poverty. It is also lots of fun too. The foundation goes twice a year. Once during Winter break and once during summer. We just got back from our last trip with volunteers from FAU and each person said they absolutely loved it and they will be returning. My plan is to spread the word, create a club at FSU, and bring FSU volunteers to help transform lives.
summary: Akoma Ntoso Foundation is a nonprofit partnership between American and Ghanaian volunteering groups whose vision is to contribute to the medical well-being of residents in disadvantaged communities in rural Ghana (Medical mission).

How to join: All currently enrolled Florida State students are welcome to apply! Applications for membership are rolling—apply now at https://forms.gle/XKLpo9WBBiT26tZG9. Tour guide applications are only open in the fall to general body members.

✰ Advocacy
✰ Impact
✰ Girlhood
✰ Community

Our two forms of membership fosters women’s leadership and professional development while making change on campus and in the community! name: Prep & Conquer club at Fsu
description: The Prep & Conquer Club at FSU is a comprehensive pre-law test preparation and law school application support organization designed to guide students through the competitive process of applying to law school. The club's mission is to empower members by providing them with the tools, resources, and knowledge they need to excel in their legal pursuits. Through a variety of services—including LSAT prep workshops, one-on-one tutoring, essay review sessions, mock interviews, and professional development events—the club ensures that students are fully equipped to meet the demands of the law school admissions process. Additionally, the Prep & Conquer Club serves as a support network, fostering a collaborative environment where students can connect with peers who share similar goals. The organization also partners with legal professionals and law school alumni to offer insights, mentorship, and networking opportunities, further preparing members for success in law school and beyond. By combining rigorous academic preparation with practical guidance on applications, personal statements, and interviews, the Prep & Conquer Club aims to be the leading resource at FSU for aspiring law students, helping them navigate the complexities of the admissions process and conquer their goals with confidence.
summary: The Prep & Conquer Club at FSU is a student-led organization dedicated to helping members successfully prepare for law school admissions. The club offers a comprehensive approach to test preparation, including LSAT and other law-related exams.

name: Music Minds Tallahassee Club
description: Music Minds Tallahassee introduces music into people's lives so that they can enjoy everything that it has to offer while also strengthening their cognitive health. Our aim is to make music more accessible and do our best to ensure that everyone has the chance to prevent cognitive impairment. We focus on working with young students, providing them with music education and offering free music lessons for under-resourced schools. Simultaneously, we also work with the older generations in providing them with engaging and brain-stimulating musical activities and performances. Aiming to bridge the generational gap, we have a culminating annual event where the students we teach are able to perform for the elderly, fostering connection through music. Finally, we also focus on leading donation drives for instruments and fundraising for Alzheimer's research.
summary: We promote cognitive health through music by offering music education to younger generations and engaging older ones with brain-stimulating musical activities such as recital performances and playlist creation.

name: Overbooked
description: Overbooked meets twice a month and reads a new book every four weeks. We operate on a system of voting to choose our books. We aim to provide a safe, inviting environment where our members will be free to voice their opinions and share creative ideas. Snacks, chit-chat, and casual discussion are the main elements of our club. We look forward to seeing you at our next meeting & follow @overbookedfsu on instagram for more details!
summary: Overbooked is a women's book club. We read books written by women, centered around women, or both! We aim to empower fellow FSU students who have a love for reading, a passion for creativity, and a desire to be involved in the literary community.

summary: FSU's one and only punk & metal club.

name: Digital Media Production Student Organization
description: We are Digital Media Production majors wanting to create a community among our major with a big/little program to build bonds between classes, media production for reels/portfolios not associated with DMP classes, and community events for hosting talks about certain softwares and how to get an internship or job.
summary: We are Digital Media Production majors wanting to create a community among our major with a big/little program, media production for reels/portfolios not associated with DMP classes, and community events.

name: The Capsule Journal at FSU
description:
The Capsule is a student-led Journal started at USF. This health humanities journal aims to promote an authentic and impactful platform for students to illuminate the human experience (patients, caregivers, professionals) in public health, healthcare, medical contexts through various mediums of storytelling. We follow the HIPAA confidentiality guidelines, and we have submission guidelines. Every student is paired with a Peer Reviewer.
Mediums: Poetry, Scholarly Articles, empirical studies, literary, cultural or media analysis. Fiction & Nonfiction Multimedia (photography, studio art) Other kinds of art (i.e. dance.)

We currently count with the support of faculty members in the Biology, Public Health & Sociology, English, and Digital Media departments to engage in outreach efforts.
summary: The Capsule is a student-led health humanities journal offering students a platform to explore the human experience in health via poetry, scholarly articles, fiction, nonfiction, multimedia, and art, following HIPAA and submission guidelines.

name: Women's Progress FSU
summary: We were founded on ensuring career and leadership success for women in all fields, regardless of their backgrounds and beliefs. Women's Progress is committed to being an advocate for women all across the Florida State campus and beyond.

name: The Bioethics Association at Florida State University
summary: The Bioethics Association hopes to increase discourse on pressing bioethical matters and places an emphasis on discussing these matters in an intellectually safe environment.

name: Students for Sexual Assault Awareness
description: Our mission is to raise awareness about sexual assault on FSU's campus. We aim to create a safe and supportive environment for survivors and advocates for prevention through education, outreach, resources and activism. We are committed to fostering an inclusive space where everyone is welcome! Please join us in our first step towards making difference!
summary: The goal of the student-led group Students for Sexual Assault Awareness (SSAA) is to stop sexual assault by educating, advocating for, and supporting victims. We raise knowledge of consent, support survivors, and build a respectful, safe environment

name: Spanish For Professional Success at Florida State University
description: The goal of the organization is to develop the members Spanish to a professional level of working proficiency. In order to do so we will hold workshops every other week, and these will be treated almost like language exchanges. In order to do so we will divide ourselves into three to four groups based on proficiency, all led by the amazing executive board full of Spanish speakers of all levels. On the off-weeks we will hold workshops designed to help our members improve in areas that they are struggling, this could be writing, reading, conjugation or speaking just to name a few. Finally, we will hold social events, like going out to eat and practicing our Spanish in a social environment.
summary: Spanish For Professional Success is a registered student organization that is designed to help students develop their Spanish linguistic skills to a working level of proficiency while also creating a community of like-minded individuals.

name: Sri Lankan Student's Association at FSU
description:
The Sri Lankan Student's Association at Florida State University is a vibrant community dedicated to promoting and celebrating Sri Lankan culture on campus. We provide a platform for Sri Lankan students to connect with each other, share experiences, and build lasting friendships. We are open for all students at FSU.

Our organization is committed to fostering a sense of belonging and identity for Sri Lankan students at FSU. We organize a variety of cultural events throughout the year, including traditional performances, food festivals, and celebrations of significant Sri Lankan holidays. These events not only showcase the rich heritage of Sri Lanka but also offer opportunities for students to learn about and appreciate other cultures.

In addition to cultural activities, the Sri Lankan Student's Association actively engages in community service initiatives. We participate in various volunteer projects that benefit local and global communities. These activities promote social responsibility and contribute to a positive impact on society.

By joining the Sri Lankan Student's Association at FSU, you become part of a supportive network of like-minded individuals who share your passion for Sri Lankan culture. We welcome students from all backgrounds to participate in our events and activities. Together, we strive to create a vibrant and inclusive community that celebrates the diversity of Sri Lankan culture at Florida State University.
summary: The Sri Lankan Student's Association at Florida State University is a vibrant community dedicated to promoting and celebrating Sri Lankan culture on campus.

name: Global Aid
description:
Join the GroupMe and follow the instagram to stay updated on meeting times and org details: 

LinkTree
summary: This student-run organization learns about and discusses ways to alleviate humanitarian crises around the world. We plan fundraisers for specific issues abroad, learn from guest speakers, and create a supportive community on campus!

name: Beta Upsilon Chi
summary: We are a Christian Social Fraternity founded under the idea that brotherhood should be done in a way that honors Jesus Christ. We are a part of the university's newest Greek council-- the UGC.

name: The International Lampoon Review at Florida State University
description: The International Lampoon is a satire organization, comprising of writers, researchers, and film crews to create a scripted, multimedia live show. The group will have one big story a semester in their big show with several smaller ones in between. Our mission is to make news understandable, even palatable at times, but also to make everyone laugh.
summary: The Lampoon is a performance comedy club, focusing on everyday news and social issues. Satire is the name, and Satire is the game.

name: The Huddle, Graduate  Club for Sport Management at FSU
summary: The Huddle is a community for Sport Management graduate students. We offer networking, professional growth, social events, and mentorship opportunities, ensuring members thrive academically and personally throughout their graduate journey.

name: CatholicMed
description: CatholicMed is a pre-medical organization readying students for careers within Medicine through the lens of the Catholic faith. This is a space where students can find answers to bioethical questions, medical school application questions, and network with Catholic physicians who practice in various fields. Students of all backgrounds are encouraged to join this community!
summary: CatholicMed is a pre-medical organization readying students for careers within Medicine through the lens of the Catholic faith. This is a space where students can prepare for medical school and network with Catholic Physicians.

name: Astronomy Club at FSU
description:
The Astronomy Club at FSU is dedicated to fostering a community of students passionate about exploring the universe and sharing their curiosity about space and astronomy. Our mission is to promote the study and appreciation of the cosmos through engaging activities, educational events, and hands-on experiences.

Club activities include:

  • Stargazing Nights: Enjoy evenings under the stars with guided telescope sessions, where we observe celestial objects like planets, star clusters, and distant galaxies.
  • Astrophotography: Dive into capturing the beauty of the night sky through photography, with tips and techniques for beginners and enthusiasts alike.
  • Trips to Dark Sky Locations
  • Social Events and Outreach: Build friendships with fellow astronomy lovers and inspire the next generation by participating in outreach programs with local schools and communities.

Whether you're pursuing a degree in the sciences or just have a fascination with the stars, the FSU Astronomy Club is the perfect place to connect with like-minded individuals and explore the universe together. Join us to expand your horizons
summary: Explore the wonders of the universe with the FSU Astronomy Club! Open to students of all majors, our club provides a platform for those interested in astronomy and space science to connect, learn, and engage in celestial exploration.

name: The Philanthropist in You
description: Our mission is to empower students to create meaningful change by supporting underrepresented communities both locally and globally through philanthropy, education, and advocacy. Like the roots of a tree, we are deeply connected to our heritage and communities, drawing strength and purpose from these ties to fuel our desire to make an impact around the world. We serve to inspire and encourage a foundation for growth by providing resources and opportunities so that these communities can thrive. We hope to encourage a culture of giving back on campus through philanthropic efforts. Together, we plant seeds of hope, cultivate resilience, and build a future where every branch of our local and global community can reach its fullest potential.
summary: Our mission is to empower students to create meaningful change by supporting underrepresented communities both locally and globally through philanthropy, education, and advocacy.

name: Movement Association
summary: Movement is a weekly sports club that offers a variety of activities, changing every session. From yoga, the next a run, and another week volleyball. The goal is to keep workouts fresh and exciting, helping students stay active!

name: Phi Delta Theta FSU
description:
Phi Delta Theta at Florida State University is a chapter of one of the nation's most prestigious and well-respected fraternities. Refounded in the fall of 2024, the chapter is dedicated to creating a strong, values-driven brotherhood grounded in the core principles of friendship, sound learning, and rectitude. The re-establishment of the chapter marks a new chapter in its history at FSU, with a focus on academic excellence, personal development, leadership, and service to both the university and the greater community.

The chapter has quickly reasserted itself as a vibrant and influential presence on campus, actively participating in philanthropy events and leadership initiatives. Phi Delta Theta’s philanthropy efforts are centered on supporting the ALS Association, and the chapter hosts a variety of events each year to raise awareness and funds for ALS research.

Phi Delta Theta at FSU strives to develop young men who uphold the highest standards of integrity, responsibility, and achievement. With a renewed focus on academic success and personal growth, the chapter provides a supportive environment where members are encouraged to challenge themselves and build lifelong friendships.

As the chapter moves forward, it is committed to fostering a dynamic brotherhood that emphasizes leadership, community involvement, and character development. Phi Delta Theta at FSU is poised to become an even more integral part of the campus community, shaping future leaders both on and off the university’s grounds.
summary: Phi Delta Theta at FSU, refounded in fall 2024, is dedicated to building a brotherhood based on friendship, sound learning, and rectitude. The chapter focuses on academic excellence, leadership, community service, and supporting the ALS Association.

name: Official Young Americans for Freedom at FSU
summary: Young Americans for Freedom (YAF) is a conservative youth activism organization that was founded in 1960 as a coalition between traditional conservatives and libertarians on American college campuses.

name: American Chemical Society (ACS) Student Chapter
description: The FSU ACS Student Chapter is a student-led organization affiliated with the American Chemical Society, the world’s largest scientific society for chemistry. The chapter aims to create a collaborative and inclusive environment for students interested in chemistry and related fields. It hosts a range of activities, such as research presentations, career workshops, and outreach programs, to inspire and engage members while promoting scientific literacy. The chapter also facilitates connections with industry professionals, faculty, and peers, fostering a sense of community and preparing students for future careers in chemistry and the sciences.
summary: The ACS Student Chapter fosters interest in chemistry through professional development, networking, and outreach. It hosts seminars, workshops, connecting students with peers, faculty, and industry to promote chemistry education and career preparations.

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi Fraternity
summary: Alpha Epsilon Pi (AEPi) is the world’s Jewish college fraternity. The fraternity’s mission is to develop the future leaders of Jewish communities, demonstrated every day through acts of brotherhood, Tzedakah, and social awareness.

name: Dedman College of Hospitality Cecil B Day Student Leadership Council
description: The Dedman SLC is comprised of approximately 10 exemplary hospitality students representing each of the three majors within the Dedman College of Hospitality. The SLC will be assisting with recruitment networking events, inspire college students to seek opportunity through the Dedman College of Hospitality, and focus on retention of the students involved in our current programs. Members meet as needed to discuss council business, student engagement, collaboration and connection both within the college and among the community and participate in professional development activities.
summary: The SLC will be assisting with recruitment networking events, inspire college students to seek opportunity through the Dedman College of Hospitality, and focus on retention of the students involved in our current programs.

name: American Library Association (ALA) Student Chapter at FSU
description:
The ALA chapter at Florida State University is devoted to supporting all students as they explore the worlds of Information, and Information Technology. We seek to partner with other student organizations to offer a dynamic University experience. Students are encouraged to pursue excellence in scholarship, citizenship, and in their personal and professional growth. Our hope is to foster engagement in the mission of responsible information management.

Join our Canvas site here: https://canvas.fsu.edu/enroll/973ADE
summary: The ALA chapter at Florida State University is devoted to supporting all students as they explore the worlds of Information, and Information Technology. Our chapter actively promotes diversity, equity, and inclusion.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law & Government Fraternity
description:
Are you passionate about pursuing a career in law or government? Kappa Alpha Pi (KAPi) at Florida State University is a community of driven individuals committed to empowering future legal professionals. Our mission is to provide pre-law and government-focused students with the tools, resources, and experiences needed to excel on their journey to law school and beyond.

Our Core Pillars
KAPi is founded on professionalism, service, and brotherhood, values that guide everything we do. From developing leadership skills to creating meaningful connections, we aim to cultivate excellence and build a network of supportive, ambitious individuals who share a passion for justice and integrity.

What We Offer

  • Professional Development: Gain access to LSAT preparation resources, law school panels, resume and LinkedIn workshops, mock trial events, and more to help you stand out.
  • Networking Opportunities: Connect with legal professionals, law school admissions teams, alumni, and like-minded peers to expand your horizons.
  • Leadership Growth: Hone your leadership skills through chapter initiatives, preparing you to excel academically, personally, and professionally.
  • Social Events: Build lifelong friendships through mixers, formals, big-little pairings, and other social activities that foster camaraderie within our fraternity.
  • Service and Philanthropy: Participate in initiatives that give back to the community, making a meaningful impact while embracing the spirit of service.
  • Supportive Brotherhood/Sisterhood: Join a tight-knit community dedicated to guiding and supporting you through your legal aspirations.

A Growing Legacy
With 30+ chapters nationwide, KAPi is part of a growing network of leaders devoted to justice, integrity, and success. Whether you're preparing for law school, exploring government roles, or seeking personal growth, KAPi provides the resources and community to help you thrive.

Take the first step toward achieving your legal aspirations by joining Kappa Alpha Pi at FSU. Together, we’ll pave the way for your success!
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi is a co-ed pre-law fraternity, empowering future legal professionals through professionalism, service, and brotherhood. Members gain support, skills, and connections through LSAT prep, networking, leadership opportunities, and events.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America at FSU
description: YDSA at FSU brings together students in Tallahassee to fight for a more equitable, just, and liberated world. Follow our Instagram at @fsu_ydsa! Our GroupMe and other info is in our Linktree in our bio.
summary: YDSA at FSU's mission is to educate and organize students to play a helpful and principled role in the movement for social and economic justice.

name: Futsal Club at FSU
description:
The Futsal Club provides a much-needed space for players of all skill levels to enjoy indoor soccer, filling the gap left by limited organized soccer opportunities on campus. Our fast-paced games and inclusive environment let you improve skills, stay active, and connect with others who share a love for the sport.

With regular pick-up games, practice sessions, and tournaments, we focus on teamwork, ball control, and quick decision-making in a fun and supportive atmosphere. Whether you're an experienced player or new to the game, the Futsal Club is the perfect place to grow, compete, and build community.
summary: The Futsal Club is open to players of all skill levels, offering a fun, fast-paced indoor soccer experience. Join us for pick-up games and tournaments in a welcoming environment where you can improve your skills, stay active, and connect with players!

name: Trinidadians for Science and Art Club
description: The point of the Trindadian Student Association is to inform people about Trinidad and Tobago. Furthermore, it is somewhat of a mini-renissance. Guest speakers will be invited to desplay their passion for science and art.
summary: There are barely any people from Trinidad and Tobago on campus. So I thought about making a club. The point of TT is not just to inform people about Trinidad and Tobago, but also to allow people to express their interests in science and art.

name: Women in IT/ICT Sharing Experiences
description:
Women in IT/ICT Sharing Experience (WISE) strives to help create an inclusive and empowering environment for women interested in male-dominated careers. Our mission is to bridge the gender gap in STEM industries, where women remain underrepresented.

WISE is committed to fostering leadership, innovation, and collaboration by providing a platform for women to connect, mentor, and inspire one another. Through professional development workshops, networking opportunities, and educational programs, we hope to encourage women to advance in their careers with confidence.
summary: The organization is focused on students in the Information Technology (IT) and the Information, Communication, and Technology (ICT) programs, however, we welcome students from all majors.

name: Psychology Association of Asian Americans
description:
The Psychology Association of Asian Americans (PAAA) at FSU is dedicated to promoting diversity, inclusion, and representation in psychology. We focus on the unique mental health experiences of Asian American communities while welcoming all students interested in advocating for mental health equity. Through discussions, career development, and community outreach, we aim to foster an inclusive approach to psychology.

We are Asian Interest, not Asian Exclusive.
summary: Focused on strengthening diversity and supporting the Asian American community in the field of psychology at FSU.

name: Christian Engineering Society
description: The Christian Engineering Society at the FAMU-FSU College of Engineering is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring the intersection of engineering and Christian faith. Rooted in the Apostle's Creed and guided by a commitment to follow Jesus Christ, the society encourages FSU engineering students to integrate Biblical principles such as justice, stewardship, and service into their academic and professional journeys. Members seek to use their skills to responsibly advance technology, address societal challenges, and contribute to God’s Kingdom by serving the needs of the disadvantaged and fostering ethical engineering practices. The organization provides a supportive community for students to grow spiritually and professionally, aligning their purpose and priorities with their faith.
summary: CES at the FAMU-FSU College of Engineering is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring the intersection of engineering and Christian faith. Rooted in the Apostle's Creed and guided by a commitment to follow Jesus Christ.

name: The Society of Black Women in Education
description:
The Society of Black Women in Education (SBWE) is dedicated to empowering, supporting, and advocating for Black women pursuing careers in education. Recognizing the unique challenges Black women face in academic and professional spaces, SBWE fosters a community of mentorship, professional development, and advocacy to promote success and meaningful change in the field of education.

Our mission is to uplift and support Black women in education by providing networking opportunities, leadership development, advocacy initiatives, and community engagement programs. We strive to create a safe and inclusive space where members can share experiences, gain valuable career insights, and build connections with peers and professionals who understand their journey.

SBWE hosts panels, workshops, mentorship programs, and social gatherings while collaborating with other student organizations to create opportunities for growth. We actively advocate for equity, representation, and systemic change in education to ensure Black women have the support and resources needed to thrive.

At SBWE, we believe that representation and community matter. Whether you’re an aspiring educator, administrator, or passionate about educational equity and inclusion, SBWE is a space where you can learn, lead, and grow.

Join us in building a more inclusive and empowered future for Black women in education!
summary: The Society of Black Women in Education (SBWE) empowers Black women pursuing careers in education through mentorship, advocacy, and professional development. We foster community, leadership, and equity in education. Join us and make an impact!

name: Seminole Blockchain Society
description: https://youtu.be/Fpc6-Kb6sxk
summary: The Seminole Blockchain Society (SBS) empowers FSU students to explore Web 3.0 technologies, including blockchain, DeFi, and NFTs. Through workshops, networking, and potential partnerships with organizations like Ripple, SBS fosters innovation& education

name: Akofena Kinship of Big Brother Little Brother Mentoring Program, Inc
summary: Big Brother Little Brother Mentoring Program, Inc. is dedicated to helping high school seniors have a smooth transition into college life by matching these students with upperclassmen who have the knowledge and experience to help!

name: Penny's Flight Foundation Chapter at FSU
summary: Through Penny’s story, Penny's Flight Foundation aims to expand knowledge around NF(Neurofibromatosis), while inspiring others to find beauty in imperfection, positivity in the face of challenge, and faith over fear.

name: If You're Reading This at FSU
summary: A collection of letters written by FSU students to connect each other through experiences linked to mental health, academics, and personal growth.

name: AAVENUE
description: AAVENUE Magazine is a non-profit, student-led publication that uplifts Black voices at Florida State University through photography, fashion, and videography. By challenging misconceptions and celebrating Black culture, we empower students to embrace their identities, honor their legacy, and explore the connections between culture, history, and the future. While centered on Black experiences, we welcome all students to engage with and appreciate these diverse narratives. We aim to educate, connect, and cultivate a community that showcases Black excellence while breaking stereotypes.
summary: AAVENUE Magazine is a non-profit, student-led publication that uplifts Black voices at Florida State University through photography, fashion, and videography. we empower students to embrace their identities and honor their legacy.

name: Empower, Navigate, Destigmatize, and Overcome (E.N.D.O.) Community at Florida State University
description:
The purpose of Empower, Navigate, Destigmatize, and Overcome (E.N.D.O.) Community at Florida State University is to spread awareness of endometriosis and cultivate a supportive community for individuals affected by the condition. This organization seeks to educate the broader student body on campus, advocate for those living with endometriosis, and provide resources to help students navigate life with a chronic illness. This organization will also strive to bring outreach to the community, whether that be schools or after school programs.
E.N.D.O. Community at FSU attracts students who are passionate about women's health, chronic illness advocacy, and medical education. Our initiatives include:
- Collaborating with other campus organizations to present educational slideshows on endometriosis.
- Hosting workshops to educate friends and partners of individuals with endometriosis on how to provide support.
- Organizing menstrual product drives in partnership with campus organizations and community partners to promote menstrual equity.
- Inviting guest speakers to discuss alternative treatments and overall well-being for those with endometriosis.
- Facilitating social events where members can share experiences and find support within the community.
- Providing mentorship to incoming students with endometriosis to help them navigate college life with a chronic condition.
summary: The purpose E.N.D.O. Community at Florida State University is to spread awareness of endometriosis and cultivate a supportive community for individuals affected by the condition.

name: Judge Advocate Student Association
summary: The Judge Advocate Student Association brings together FSU College of Law students who are interested in serving in the United States Military in any branch as a Judge Advocate.

name: CompNeuroSociety at FSU
description: In recognition of the growing intersection between neuroscience and computational methods, we established CompNeuroSociety at Florida State University. Our mission is to foster a community that supplements the education of those curious about computational neuroscience through interactive workshops, engaging journal clubs, and collaborative projects. By providing these opportunities, we aim to increase the confidence of our members in the field of computational neuroscience, preparing them for future endeavors in this innovative field, while also giving space to discuss their interests.
summary: We established CompNeuroSociety at FSU in Spring '25. Our mission is to create a peer-led learning community, to increase access for FSU students who are interested in Computational Neuroscience, through a peer-led learning community.

name: Taiwanese American Student Association
description:
• Our organization is dedicated to promoting awareness and appreciation of Taiwanese culture through its rich culinary traditions, traditional clothing, and unique customs. From the bustling night markets serving bubble tea and scallion pancakes to the cultural significance of traditional attire like qipao and hanfu, we offer opportunities to learn and experience Taiwan’s heritage firsthand. Whether you have a personal connection to Taiwan or are simply interested in exploring a new culture, we welcome everyone to join us in celebrating and sharing the beauty of Taiwanese traditions.

  •
summary: Taiwanese student organization to spread awareness and understanding of Taiwanese culture and bring a sense of community to not just Taiwanese people but anyone that wants to learn and join.

name: Digital Media Club at FSU
description: We are Digital Media Club at FSU, we are primarily focused on photography and design, and open to any major. We will be hosting workshops for all things photography, from cameras to software. We also host photography excursions, competitions, how-tos for navigating social media, socials, and much more!
summary: We're open to any major and anyone who has a passion for photography and design! We will host photography excursions, workshops for design software (Adobe Products, Canva, etc.), fun competitions, socials, guest speakers, and more!

name: Wii Sports Club
description: The Wii Sports Club is a place for fans of the iconic Wii Sports series to come together and appreciate the fun, competition, and nostalgia it brings. Whether you’re a casual bowler, a golf competitor, or just enjoying a good game of baseball, this club is all about celebrating the beloved game in a social and engaging way. We host friendly tournaments for players of all skills.
summary: The Wii Sports Club is a place for fans of the iconic Wii Sports series to come together and appreciate the fun, competition, and nostalgia it brings. Casual fun and group bonding through Wii.

name: Sociology Collective
summary: The Sociology Collective is a mentorship network and social club for our undergraduate sociology majors and minors. The programming will focus on how to get where you want to go with sociology and making connections within the major.

name: United Greek Council
summary: The United Greek Council (UGC) at Florida State University is a governing body that represents a group of unique values-based social fraternities and sororities that meet a multitude of niches not fully represented within the broader FSL Community.

name: Campus Cats Club at FSU
description: The Campus Cat Club at FSU aims to promote responsible management of the feral cat populations on Florida State University’s campus through the collaboration of students, staff, local rescues, and FSU Facilities. We aim to provide consistent care, reduce litter, and prevent population growth while providing volunteer opportunities for students.
summary: The goal of this organization is to connect students with FSU Facilities and local Trap Neuter Vaccinate Return groups to care for stray cat populations on FSU's campus.

name: First Gen Abroad
description: First-Gen Abroad is a community-driven organization dedicated to helping first-generation students access the resources they need to study abroad. Through our three pillars—Access, Mentorship, and Community—we provide guidance on scholarships, financial aid, and securing essential documents like passports. We connect students with mentors, host workshops on budgeting and cultural adjustment, and foster a supportive network to make global education more accessible. Our mission is to break down barriers and ensure first-gen students have the tools, knowledge, and confidence to explore the world.
summary: First-Gen Abroad—a community-driven org that helps first-gen students access the resources they need to study abroad. Through Access, Mentorship, and Community, we guide students on scholarships, financial aid, passports, and more!

name: National Lawyers Guild at FSU
summary: The NLG is dedicated to the need for basic change in the structure of our political and economic system. Our aim is to bring together all those who recognize the importance of safeguarding and extending rights.

name: Business and STEM Association at FSU
description: The Business and STEM Association (BSA) at FSU aims to connect students with varying backgrounds to build a culture of innovation and service for our community and the greater population. The BSA will also focus on taking what we learn in the classroom and applying it to real-world experiences through service learning, pitch competitions, networking, innovation projects, and startup workshops.
summary: The Business & STEM Association (BSA) at FSU unites students with diverse backgrounds to foster innovation, service, and real-world impact through learning, networking, competitions, and startup projects.

name: AI in Business at Florida State University
description: The purpose of AIIB is to educate, empower, and support students in understanding the evolving role of artificial intelligence in the business sector. AIIB aims to bridge the gap between technological advancements in AI and their practical applications in business environments. Through fostering an inclusive community comprising students, faculty, and industry professionals, AIIB provides avenues for intellectual growth, networking, and professional development.
summary: The purpose of AIIB is to educate, empower, and support students in understanding the evolving role of artificial intelligence in the business sector. AIIB aims to bridge the gap between technological advancements in AI and their practical applications.

name: Fiction and Film Book Club
description: The Fiction and Film book club meets once a week for an hour. During these meetings we discuss everything that happened during the assigned chapters and how we feel/interpreted the material. We do this by having discussions and completing group activities. At the end of the month when the book has been completed, we get together and watch the movie. When the movie is over we compare, contrast, and analyze the written and visual literature.
summary: The Fiction and Film book club meets once a week to review assigned chapters, by completing discussions and activities. When the book is complete we then watch the movie to compare, contrast, and analyze the written and visual literature.

name: Competitive Sports League
description: Campus Sports League is a student-created and student-led organization at Florida State University dedicated to giving students more consistent and competitive opportunities to play the sports they love. From basketball and soccer to pickleball and flag football, CSL provides a structured but fun environment for students to compete in pickup games, tournaments, and seasonal leagues. Whether you’re looking to stay active, meet new people, or fuel your competitive side, CSL is here to build a community around sports for anyone who wants in — no tryouts or previous team experience needed.
summary: Campus Sports League is a student-run club at FSU that organizes year-round, competitive pickup games and tournaments in sports like basketball, soccer, pickleball, and more, helping students stay active and build community.

name: People Power for Florida
description: People Power for Florida is a voter registration and political engagement organization dedicated to empowering and training students to become engaged in the voter registration and political organizing process.
summary: People Power for Florida is a voter registration and political engagement organization that is dedicated to giving power back to the people through efforts on campus and in the community.

name: Flame: Law in Fashion, Art, Media, and Entertainment
description: FLAME, which stands for Law in Fashion, Art, Media, and Entertainment, offers a unique perspective on the legal field. We understand that the traditional perception of law is often limited to areas such as criminal justice and personal injury, but there is a whole other side of law that is dedicated to the creative industry. Unfortunately, this side of law is often overlooked and not explored as much. At FLAME, we aim to provide our members with a fresh perspective on law that aligns with their creative interests. Our organization explores how the law applies to fashion, art, media, entertainment, and other related fields, making it a great opportunity for anyone with an interest in these areas to learn more.
summary: FLAME is a student organization that explores how the law applies to fashion, art, media, entertainment, and other related fields, making it a great opportunity for anyone with an interest in these areas to learn more.

name: The Eventing Team at FSU
summary: The Eventing Team at FSU aims to foster a supportive community of 3-day eventers. We compete under the USEA Intercollegiate Eventing Program and attend various challenges around Area lll.

name: The ColorStack Chapter at FSU
description:
The ColorStack chapter at FSU supports Black and Latinx students in computer science by fostering community, academic success, and professional growth. Through workshops, speaker events, coding projects, mentorship, and social gatherings, we create an empowering space where students can connect, grow, and succeed in tech.

Whether you're new to CS or already deep into your degree, there's a place for you here. Join our Discord!

🔗 Learn more about ColorStack

🔗 View Our Website
summary: ColorStack @ FSU is dedicated to building community, boosting representation, and helping Black and Latinx computer science students thrive, both on campus and in their future careers.

name: Rare Ribbon Association
