description: In this undergraduate chapter of Rare Ribbon Association, we are dedicated to empowering cancer survivors through compassionate support and education, while engaging communities and individuals passionate about advancing cancer research and improving survivorship outcomes with a focus on rare forms of cancer. We will conduct general body meetings, host speakers, and have activities focused on different forms of cancer each month to bring awareness and education towards the subject.
summary: We empower cancer survivors with support and education, bringing people together through events and speakers to enhance research and survivorship.

name: Asian Pacific American Medical Student Association
summary: APAMSA is dedicated to celebrating Asian American cultures and promoting awareness of the unique healthcare challenges faced by Asian American communities.

name: Bradfordville Young Adults Campus Ministry
description:
We are a Christ-centered campus ministry dedicated to being a welcoming community where students and young adults can grow in faith, friendship, and purpose. Our mission is to put Jesus first in all we do, building a culture of fervent prayer, grounded in the Word, and fueled by a deep dependence on Him. We are more committed to following Jesus than to any one tradition or program, and we aim to cultivate a diverse, unified community that loves like family and reflects the Kingdom of Heaven.

On a campus where culture often promotes division, we will lead with unity and live out the “one anothers” of Scripture, encouraging, serving, and honoring one another in love. Through consistent gatherings and public outreach events, we seek to create space for community, discipleship, and spiritual growth. Our campus ministry is a safe place to ask questions, grow in faith, and walk alongside others in a Christ-centered environment.

This is a place where everyone, from the curious to the committed, can find a spiritual home. We believe that serving isn’t about obligation, but about obedience to God’s calling. Our community will be built not on the talents of a few, but on the faithfulness and sacrifice of many.

We are called to serve, and that means supporting our campus and broader community in any way we can. Our prayer is to remain rooted in why we are here: to glorify Jesus, love people deeply, and be a light that brings hope to our campus and beyond.
summary: We are a family on mission together for Jesus, in Tallahassee and to the world. We aim to be a welcoming Christian community of students and young adults who share the common goal of glorifying God and sharing His Word.

name: Fulbright Students Association
description: Oriented toward Fulbright students with diverse academic and cultural interests, this organization will facilitate the exchange of ideas, knowledge, support, and professional development across campus programs and organizations. Aligned with the vision of the Fulbright Student Program to foster mutual understanding and peaceful relations among students from around the world through international educational and cultural exchange, we aim to create a more peaceful and cooperative world. By promoting interdisciplinary collaboration, we strive to provide a platform for intellectual exchange and identify events across campus (and host our own) to enrich the perspectives we bring to our research, practice, and cultural engagement.
summary: Fulbright Students Association (FSA) is an organization promoting interdisciplinary collaboration, cultural exchange, and professional development to foster mutual understanding and peaceful relations among Fulbright students at FSU.

name: Plant Futures Chapter at Florida State University
description: The mission of this organization shall be to educate and engage with students passionate about plant-centric food systems and provide a network through which they can apply this passion to better both their own lives and engage in meaningful professional development and career exploration.
summary: "We are equipping students to become the next generation of ethical leaders, systems thinkers and effective advocates for a plant-centric future for our planet, people, and animals."

name: The Eggplant at FSU
description: We are proudly the go-to source of satirical news content for FSU’s students— from written articles to man-on-the-street interviews. Check us out on our very own website as well as on all social media platforms, especially Instagram (wink wink). If you'd like to join our writers' room, please complete and email us this application here!
summary: The Eggplant is the leading satirical news publication on campus with prominent platforms across all social media, including its own website. Our team of writers meets weekly to produce articles and "newsworthy" stories from FSU, Tallahassee, and beyond.

name: Human Trafficking and Policy Law Society
description: The Human Trafficking and Policy Law Society will bring together students interested in brainstorming and discussing policies and laws related to human trafficking. We will explore legal issues in Florida, United States, and Internationally.
summary: The Human Trafficking and Policy Law Society will bring together students interested in brainstorming and  discussing policies and laws related to human trafficking. We will explore legal issues in Florida, United States, and Internationally.

name: Bachelor of Science in Public Health Student Association
description: The purpose of the Bachelor of Science in Public Health Student Association (BSPHSA) is to promote public health education, awareness, and professional development for the field of public health among undergraduate students. The organization aims to connect students with opportunities for professional and personal networking and community service. BSPHSA advocates for and engages students in activities that support three pillars of public health: prevention, promotion, and protection. To these ends, the BSPHSA fosters partnerships that enhance both student experiences and community health outcomes.
summary: BSPHSA promotes public health awareness, education, and professional growth, connecting students with service, networking, and leadership focused on prevention, promotion, and protection.

name: Bricks and Bridges Club
description: Bricks and Bridges is more than a club, for me it’s a movement to help build connection and understanding in the community we call home. Through creative LEGO building moments, where we bring FSU students and neurodivergent children together in a space that celebrates individuality, sparks joy, and fosters genuine friendships. Many of these kids face challenges in finding social connections, and something as simple as building with LEGO bricks becomes a powerful shared language. Each brick we place represents not just creativity, but the bonds we form and the bridges we build. Bridges between people, experiences, and perspectives. By partnering with local autism and neurodivergent programs in Tallahassee, we aim to inspire students to volunteer, advocate, and be part of lasting change that strengthens our community from the ground up.
summary: Bricks and Bridges Club has the goal to bring FSU students and neurodivergent children in Tallahassee together to build LEGO creations, share laughter, and celebrate each child’s unique strengths while creating a space where everyone feels connected.

name: Hospitality Sales and Marketing Association International
description: FSU’s chapter of the Hospitality Sales and Marketing Association International (HSMAI) connects students with top professionals in hotel sales, marketing, and revenue management. As the leading global organization in the field, HSMAI offers members access to industry speakers, hands-on workshops, networking events, and exclusive opportunities like conferences and professional development trips. Whether you're looking to explore career paths in hospitality, sharpen your skills, or grow your network, HSMAI at FSU is the place to start.
summary: This org connects students to top hospitality pros through guest speakers, workshops, networking events, and industry trips. Join us to grow your skills and explore careers in hotels, travel, events, and more.

name: Beautify Florida State University
description: Beautify Florida State University is the first collegiate affiliate of Keep Florida Beautiful. We focus on principles of beautification, education, and litter prevention, and use art, knowledge, and service to enrich our campus as well as the surrounding area. Join us for monthly events and community service opportunities!
summary: Beautify FSU operates as an affiliate of Keep Florida Beautiful, and focuses on principles of beautification, education, and litter prevention. Join us for monthly art, learning, and cleanup events!

name: Health Informatics Student Association
summary: The FSU Health Informatics Student Association (HISA) is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring the intersection of healthcare, technology, and innovation. Our mission is to empower students to shape the future of health informatics through co

name: The Finance Society
description:
Our mission is to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world application by providing members with the platform to pursue their careers.

We focus on four distinct career verticals: Wealth & Asset Management, Corporate & Commercial Banking, Investment Banking & Alternatives, and Corporate Finance & Treasury. 

Through career-specific programming, we offer tailored workshops, corporate tours, guest speaker events, and peer-led mentorship to help students discover and pursue their path within financial services.
summary: The Finance Society is a pre-professional student organization designed to expose and prepare students for various careers in finance through weekly workshops, corporate tours, and guest speakers.

name: Kappa Gamma Delta at FSU
description: Kappa Gamma Delta (KGD) at FSU is a sisterhood for anyone who identifies as a woman and is passionate about the health professions. We’re committed to academic excellence, leadership and service; hosting study groups, guest panels with local healthcare professionals and mentorship circles to help you grow. Our hands‑on community service, builds your event‑planning, fundraising and public‑speaking skills. Beyond academics, KGD offers retreats, sister socials and alumni networking so you’ll forge friendships and professional connections that last.
summary: Kappa Gamma Delta (KGD) at Florida State University is a pre‑health sorority dedicated to empowering future healthcare leaders through leadership development, academic support and community service.

name: The Litigating Ladies Society at FSU
description:
The Litigating Ladies Society at Florida State University is a dynamic and empowering student organization dedicated to fostering confidence, competence, and community among students interested in the legal field. Our mission is to create an inclusive and supportive space where aspiring litigators can refine their public speaking, advocacy, and courtroom skills in preparation for law school and beyond.

Each week, members participate in interactive workshops covering essential topics such as the Federal Rules of Evidence, courtroom etiquette, legal reasoning, and persuasive speaking. We host guest speakers including judges, practicing attorneys, and legal scholars to provide professional insights and mentorship. Additionally, we offer hands-on experiences through mock trials, moot court exercises, structured debates, and competitive events, where students have the opportunity to showcase their skills and earn recognition for excellence.

Whether you're exploring a future in law or looking to develop powerful communication and critical thinking abilities, The Litigating Ladies Society offers a space to grow, lead, and thrive.
summary: The Litigating Ladies Society at FSU empowers students through weekly workshops on public speaking, courtroom skills, and legal education. Members participate in mock trials, debates, and competitions, with opportunities to learn from legal professionals

name: Geographical Society at FSU
description:
Welcome to the Geographical Society at FSU. Our organization seeks to support the academic, professional, and social growth of students in the Department of Geography, as well as any other student who is interested in the study of geography.

1. As students of a social science, we aspire to create a space for passionate undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral students of geography to socialize. This space is also open to any non-majors who have an interest in the discipline of geography.

2. We wish to create academic opportunities to aid in our studies and our research. This means helping students get into undergraduate research opportunities, inviting speakers, and furthering our studies independently.

3. Finally, we wish for our space to facilitate professional connections between FSU students and faculty, as well as other geography students and scholars in Florida, the US Southeast, and the nation at large. This includes workshops on applying to graduate school and travel opportunities to conferences and research symposiums.

We hope to see you around!
summary: Supporting the academic, professional, and social growth of geography students at Florida State University.

name: The Rural Med Collective
description: The Rural Med Collective is a student-led organization committed to enhancing healthcare delivery in underserved rural communities. Each academic year, we select a rural hospital to support through targeted fundraising and innovation initiatives. The Fundraising Sector leads donation drives, organizes community fundraisers, and plans a major spring event to maximize contributions for the selected hospital. Concurrently, the Innovation Sector identifies a critical healthcare challenge faced by the hospital, develops a cost-effective solution during the fall semester, and advances its creation and implementation in the spring. Members may participate in one or both sectors, gaining leadership experience in project management, event coordination, and solution design. When feasible, the organization arranges a site visit to the hospital to better understand its needs and foster direct community engagement. Meetings are every other Tuesday from 6:30 pm - 7:30 pm in the Innovation Pitch Room
summary: A student-led organization committed to advancing healthcare in rural communities. It consists of two divisions: one overseeing fundraising and events, and another dedicated to designing cost-effective medical solutions for rural healthcare facilities.

name: Future Forward at FSU
description:
Future Forward is a student led organization that is dedicated to promoting basic health and wellness education for children in our community. Our mission is to empower kids with essential knowledge about personal hygiene, nutrition, and safety. If permitted by local schools, this would be done both in person and through digital resources, such as tutorials and fact sheets.

Through lesson-planned visits to local elementary schools, we teach important topics such as proper handwashing, dental care, nutrition basics, sun protection, and how to respond to minor injuries. With enough fundraising, we also aim to provide schools with wellness supply bins containing essentials like deodorant, toothbrushes, toothpaste, and thermometers for children who need them. Future Forward is dedicated to making a tangible difference in children's lives while giving our members experience in leadership and community service.
summary: Educating children on basic health and wellness procedures, indicators, and enhancements to increase wellness in everyday life through lesson-planned visits to local elementary schools around Tallahassee and creation of digital resources.

name: Innovation Club at FSU
summary: The Innovation Club seeks to help students look at everyday problems and find solutions through collaboration, experimentation, and innovation. Our goal is to bring innovation to FSU through workshops, guest speakers, events, and competitions.

name: American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative
summary: The American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative (ALCSI) at FSU raises awareness about lung cancer and promotes early detection through education, outreach, and advocacy for life-saving screenings.

name: United Party at FSU
summary: We are a voice for the students at FSU. We aim to foster change and innvonation on our pillars of intergrity and representaion. We build the leaders of tommorow, today.

name: Future Business Leaders Organization
description: The Future Business Leaders Organization is a student-led club dedicated to empowering the next generation of business-minded individuals from all academic backgrounds. Our mission is to provide students with real-world insights into the inner workings of businesses through guest speakers, hands-on experiences, and collaborative learning. Whether you're an aspiring entrepreneur, creative thinker, or future executive, FBLO is a space to explore the many paths within the business world and build valuable skills for your future.
summary: Future Business Leaders Organization (FBLO) is a student-led club uniting aspiring business leaders and professionals. We host speaker events, networking opportunities, and workshops to develop real-world business skills across all majors.

name: American Society of Exercise Physiology
description: The American Society of Exercise Physiologists at Florida State University will be a student-led organization dedicated to advancing academic, professional, and personal growth in the field of exercise physiology. Through educational events, inspiring guest speakers, and hands-on learning experiences, we aim to strengthen academic knowledge, build professional networks, and expand the understanding of exercise physiology’s role in enhancing performance, promoting wellness, and supporting recovery.
summary: The American Society of Exercise Physiologists at Florida State University aims to advance the teaching, research, and professional practice of exercise physiology by promoting its role in health promotion, disease prevention, and recovery.

name: Cyber Org at FSU
summary: Our mission is to foster a community of awareness, education, and collaboration in cybersecurity at FSU and the surrounding communities. Through workshops, seminars, and hands-on experiences, we aim to raise awareness on cyber threats.

name: Incubate Debate at FSU
summary: Incubate Debate challenges Seminoles to debate. We do this by providing free, open access debates and workshops to discuss timely and timeless issues relating to everyday politics and public discourse. We present these debates in a civil manner for all.

name: Justice-Impacted Student Organization
summary: The Justice-Impacted Student Organization (JISO) empowers students with lived-experience in the criminal legal system to transform resilience into leadership, and education into opportunity. Students who wish to join as "Advocates" are encouraged.

name: Cabales Serrada Escrima Club
description:
Cabales Serrada Escrima is a Filipino weapons-based martial art that can be practiced with any objects or performed empty-handed. The goal is to teach people to use objects safely and effectively for self-defense.

Join the GroupMe, Discord or follow our Instagram for the schedule and content from class.
summary: This club will teach you how to use everyday objects to help defend yourself. We will practice with rattan sticks to learn techniques, blend this style with other martial arts, and engage in sparring.

name: The National Society of Leadership and Success
summary: We help students identify career goals, hone their leadership skills, and turn ambitions into action. Our program provides a life-changing experience giving you access to essential tools to excel in your future and make a change in the world.

name: Mathematics Graduate Student Council at FSU
description: We serve as the liaison between graduate students and FSU Math Department administration. GSC is a resource for faculty to learn student issues and helps current, incoming, and potential students transition smoothly into our programs.
summary: We serve as the liaison between graduate students and the Math Department. The GSC is a resource for faculty to learn student issues and helps current, incoming, and potential students transition smoothly into our programs.

name: Balkan Student Association at FSU
description: The Balkan Student Association (BKSA) aims to create a welcoming and inclusive space for students of Balkan heritage, as well as anyone interested in learning more about the diverse cultures of the Balkans. Our mission is to celebrate our cultural identities, foster a strong sense of community, and offer support through shared experiences. We hope to cultivate pride in our heritage while educating the broader campus community about the history, traditions, and contemporary life of the Balkan region. Through engaging events, meaningful dialogue, and collaborative initiatives, the BKSA will become a platform where culture meets community, identity meets education, and students form lasting bonds. BKSA is open to all students, whether they identify as Balkan or are simply curious to learn more about the region.
summary: The Balkan Student Association (BKSA) aims to create a welcoming and inclusive space for students of Balkan heritage, as well as anyone interested in learning more about the diverse cultures of the Balkans.

name: Clean Up Culture
description: Clean Up Culture is a student-led club focused on sustainability and environmental awareness at our university and in our college town. We host regular campus and community clean-ups while promoting education and action for a cleaner, greener future.
summary: Clean Up Culture is a student-led club focused on sustainability and environmental awareness at our university and in our college town. We host regular campus and community clean-ups while promoting education and action for a cleaner, greener future.

name: PULSE Incorporated at FSU
description: PULSE Incorporated at FSU is dedicated in its mission of sharing independent news with the student body. We are excited to share our passion for journalism and storytelling with the FSU community.
summary: PULSE is a student-led broadcast news network dedicated to sharing campus news, stories, and information with the FSU community.

name: Moles for Miracles
description: Through educational campaigns, community outreach, and fundraising initiatives, we aim to empower individuals with the knowledge to protect their skin and recognize the warning signs of melanoma and other skin cancers. By promoting healthy habits, partnering with local organizations, and supporting research and patient care, Moles for Miracles strives to create lasting change and save lives—one miracle at a time.
summary: Moles for Miracles is a student-led organization dedicated to raising awareness about skin cancer prevention and early detection.

name: Mas 'n' Steel Band
summary: Mas 'n' Steel is FSU's very own Trinidadian-style steel band. The ensemble performs a variety of repertoire ranging from traditional works for steel band, Panorama tunes, and arrangements encompassing classical, jazz, and popular music styles.

name: Patient Advocacy Association
description: Patient Advocacy Association (PAA) is a student-led organization committed to supporting patients and families in our local community by promoting health literacy, navigating healthcare systems, and addressing barriers to care. We host educational events, volunteer with local organizations, and create space for dialogue on the challenges patients face—especially those from underserved backgrounds. Through direct service, outreach, and collaboration, we aim to empower both students and community members to become informed, compassionate advocates for better healthcare access and equity.
summary: The Patient Advocacy Association empowers students to support patients and families in the local community through education, outreach, and service that promote healthcare access, health literacy, and compassion-driven advocacy.

name: Secret to Success at FSU
description: Secret to Success (S2S) is a student-led organization committed to empowering high school students as they prepare for life after graduation. Our mission is to increase the number of confident, college- and career-ready students through mentorship, academic support, and resource-driven programming. By connecting students with local high schoolers, we aim to bridge the gap between secondary and higher education, promote academic success, and inspire future leaders to reach their full potential.
summary: Secret to Success (S2S) is a student-led organization committed to empowering high school students as they prepare for life after graduation.

name: Garnet & Gold Toastmasters Club
description:
Our club offers a warm, welcoming environment for members to safely practice developing their
public speaking skills. Members receive constructive feedback to help improve and perfect their
communication skills and leadership style.
summary: At Garnet & Gold Toastmasters we help develop our members speaking skills, leadership skills, and confidence.

name: Letters of Love at FSU
description: Letters of Love is a globally recognized non-profit organization that provides emotional support to children battling serious illnesses in Children's Hospitals through the creation of handcrafted cards.
summary: Letters of Love is a globally recognized non-profit organization that provides emotional support to children battling serious illnesses in Children's Hospitals through the creation of handcrafted cards.

name: Albums in Review at FSU
description: Albums in Review at FSU is a student organization that brings music lovers together to experience and discuss albums in a similar way to a book club, focusing on novels. Each meeting, members listen to and analyze a selected album, exploring its themes, artistry, cultural impact, and personal resonance. The club fosters open discussion, critical thinking, and community through a shared passion for music, creating a space where students can deepen their appreciation of albums across all genres and eras.
summary: Albums in Review at FSU is a student organization that brings music lovers together to experience and discuss albums in a similar way to a book club, focusing on novels. Members listen to and analyze a selected album, exploring themes and artistry.

name: Lex et Lux Society
description:
At Lex et Lux, our mission is to illuminate the often-overlooked dimensions of law by fostering a deeper understanding of its academic, practical, and societal applications. While traditional conversations about legal careers often focus on attorneys and judges, we seek to highlight the diverse and impactful paths within the legal field—including law professors, clerks, court reporters, legal scholars, policy analysts, and more.

Through engaging discussions, workshops, and collaborative learning, Lex et Lux strives to demystify legal academia and reveal how law intersects with daily life. We believe that law is not merely a career—it is a lens through which we understand truth, advocate for justice, and shape the world around us. In the spirit of our name, meaning “Law and Light” or “Truth through Law,” we are committed to equipping students with the insight, inspiration, and tools to explore law beyond the courtroom and into every corner of society.
summary: Lex et Lux shows all parts of the law; academia, professional opportunities in- and out-of-country, research, law school experience, and more. We dive into 1L experiences, legal and legal adjacent careers, and things many pre law students don't consider.

name: Women in Pre-Med Inc.
description: This organization is geared toward supporting people who identify as women on their path to medical school. This organization will provide resources, networking opportunities, and more knowledge on the medical school application process
summary: This organization is geared toward supporting people who identify as women on their path to medical school. This organization will provide resources, networking opportunities, and more knowledge on the medical school application process

name: Spikeball Club at Florida State University
description: At the Spikeball club, both nets and balls will be provided for people that want to participate in the Spikeball club. This club is meant for both beginners and experts alike that want to meet new people/competitors to play or just to simply learn the sport!
summary: This club is to bring together people that share a similar interest in Spikeball.

name: Students for Progressive victory at FSU
summary:
Progressive Victory at FSU
is a student-led
organization dedicated to
advancing progressive
policy through collective
action. We empower young
voices on campus to hold
leaders accountable and
work toward a
progressive future.

name: Engineering Brigades Group at FSU
description: Welcome to Empowered Engineering Consulting
summary: Student-led group partnering with Global Brigades and underserved communities to design sustainable water, sanitation, and infrastructure projects. Hands-on engineering, fundraising, travel, and leadership development.

name: Take One Film and Podcast Club
description: Take One Film and Podcast Club is dedicated to creating a fun, collaborative space for film enthusiasts and thoughtful discussions. Our goal is to foster an open-minded community where students can learn both cinematic techniques and interpersonal skills. We meet twice a week: one day for workshops, the other for a general meeting. Workshops offer hands-on experience in film techniques, writing, and editing. General meetings focus on learning new techniques, sharing ideas, and reviewing the month’s agenda. A podcast will be hosted at least once a month, with the time and date to be decided at the first general meeting of that month.  Take One hopes to provide opportunities for students who need guidance on video projects (whether that be cast members, editors, or a helping hand), as well as a welcoming community for anyone passionate about film and podcasts.
summary: Take One Film and Podcast Club is committed to providing a fun learning environment for film lovers and eager conversationalists. We hope to create an open-minded space that will teach students both cinematic techniques and interpersonal skills.

name: Consumer Advocates Society
description: The Consumer Advocates Society aims to promote, educate, and advocate for consumer rights at the state and federal levels. This organization strives to help the FSU community understand consumer rights in Florida and to promote more robust protections. The CAS also aims to highlight the various career opportunities in the field of consumer law to law students and introduce opportunities to get hands-on experience with attorneys through internships, externships, and fellowships.
summary: The Consumer Advocates Society aims to promote, educate, and advocate for consumer rights at the state and federal levels. This organization aims to educate the FSU community about consumer rights in Florida and advocate for stronger protections.

name: Esther Funds Foundation
description: Esther Funds Foundation (EFF) is a Christ-centered nonprofit ministry dedicated to preventing college dropouts by providing emergency support, educational resources, and faith-based encouragement to minority, first-generation, and low-income students. Founded on January 23, 2024, by Shayna Vincent, EFF began as a vision in 2023 to break financial and systemic barriers that hinder students from completing their education. We believe that higher education should not be a privilege but an accessible reality for all students, especially those facing financial hardship and a lack of support. Through advocacy, mentorship, service, and donations, we equip students with the resources they need to persist in their education and graduate. Our initiatives include emergency support, financial assistance, access to books, groceries, tuition relief, electronic repairs, rent and gas assistance, and mental health resources. Additionally, we are actively establishing collegiate chapters to provide localized support on campuses, ensuring that students receive the help they need to stay in school. While our primary focus started in Tallahassee, we are expanding throughout the entire United States, working toward a future where higher education completion is the norm, financial barriers are eliminated, and every student has the support, knowledge, and faith to finish what they started.
summary: Esther Funds Foundation (EFF) is a Christ-centered nonprofit ministry dedicated to preventing college dropouts by providing emergency support, educational resources, and faith-based encouragement to minority, first-generation, and low-income students.

name: ACLU at FSU
description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) is our nation’s guardian of civil liberties. We work in courts, legislatures, and communities to defend and protect our rights and liberties guaranteed by the U.S. Constitution, The Florida Constitution, and State laws. The ACLU of Florida’s headquarters is in Miami. The mission of the ACLU of Florida is to protect, defend, strengthen, and promote the constitutional rights and liberties of all people in Florida. We envision a fair and just Florida, where all people are free, equal under the law, and live with dignity.
summary: The primary goal of the ACLU of Florida Campus Club of Florida State University is to raise awareness of public policy issues affecting civil liberties, and to safeguard those civil liberties both on and off campus.

name: Human Development and Family Science Graduate Student Organization
summary: Foster community among graduate students in HDFS. Support academic and professional development. Provide opportunities for peer mentorship, social connection, and wellness. Promote service, outreach, and visibility within the department, and university.

name: Morgan's Message Club at Florida State
description: Morgan’s Message is a national organization dedicated to raising awareness about student-athlete mental health, reducing stigma, and promoting open conversations. I founded the FSU chapter to create a supportive community on campus by hosting events, sharing resources, and encouraging dialogue among peers. Our goal is to empower student-athletes to prioritize mental well-being and know they are never alone.
summary: Morgan’s Message is a national organization that raises awareness for student-athlete mental health, fights stigma, and encourages open conversations. I founded a chapter to host events, share resources, and support peers on campus.

name: A Women's Pregnancy Center
summary: A Women’s Pregnancy Center educates, supports and empowers women facing unplanned pregnancies with compassionate care. If you think you may be pregnant or you are considering abortion, we are here to help!

name: Rem on Campus at FSU Club
description:
Rem on Campus is a professional development and consulting program for college students created by Rem and Company. Its goal is to help students gain real-world business experience, build leadership and problem-solving skills, and make an impact in their local communities. The program connects students with small businesses that need help improving strategy, marketing, or operations, giving participants hands-on consulting experience.

Students typically move through three stages during their time in the program. They start by learning the fundamentals of consulting and business strategy, then apply those skills by working directly with clients, and eventually take on leadership roles where they guide teams and manage client relationships. Throughout this process, students develop communication, analytical, and teamwork skills while producing tangible results for real companies.

By the end of Rem on Campus, participants leave with a strong foundation in consulting and project management, valuable professional connections, and meaningful work experience that helps them stand out in their careers.

https://www.remandcompany.com/

https://www.remandcompany.com/remoncampus
summary: Rem on Campus is a student consulting program by Rem and Company that helps college students gain real-world experience by solving business challenges for small companies while developing leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.

name: Hearts for Healthcare Association
description: The Hearts for Healthcare Association supports local healthcare initiatives.
summary: The Hearts for Healthcare Association supports local healthcare initiatives.

name: The Green Bandana Project at FSU
description: The Green Bandana Project at FSU is a student led mental health initiative that works to make campus a more supportive and informed place. Students who join the program complete a short training on how to recognize signs of distress, listen without judgment, and connect peers to the right resources. After training, members carry a green bandana on their backpack to show that they are a safe person to approach. The project focuses on reducing the stigma around asking for help and encourages open conversations about mental health. It also partners with organizations, plans outreach events, and shares information about campus services so students know where to turn when things get overwhelming. The goal is to build a community where no one feels isolated and where support is easy to find, especially during stressful points in the semester.
summary: The Green Bandana Project at FSU promotes mental health awareness by training students to support peers, sharing resources, and creating a campus community where no one feels alone.

name: Tabletop Gaming Club at FSU
description: We are an organization that aims to foster a community built around tabletop games and related hobbies. We hope to attract open-minded students who are willing to learn new gaming systems and connect with others.
summary: We are an organization that aims to foster a community built around tabletop games and related hobbies. We hope to attract open-minded students who are willing to learn new gaming systems and connect with others.

name: Undergraduate Society of Women in Research at FSU
description: The Undergraduate Society of Women in Research empowers women pursuing research across all disciplines by fostering a supportive, inclusive community. Our organization provides mentorship, professional development, and opportunities to gain hands-on research experience. Through workshops, networking events, and peer collaboration, we help members build confidence, strengthen academic skills, and explore career paths in research. We aim to uplift women scholars, promote representation, and create a space where curiosity and innovation thrive.
summary: This organization is a society dedicated to uplifting undergraduate women in research through skill development, mentorship, and hands-on opportunities.

name: Bridge for Civic Leadership Council
summary: Bridge for Civic Leadership Council is an organization dedicated to bridging the gap between academic learning and professional application. This will be done through our semesterly Civic Project, mentorship, workshops, and community engagement.

name: Legacy Party at Florida State University
summary: SGA Political Party aiming to connect students with leaders and future leaders of FSU, bettering the community by understanding what areas of campus could use improvement and working with SGA entities to advance campus life through action.

name: Public Policy Journal at FSU
description:
The Public Policy Journal at FSU functions as a way for aspiring researchers to submit existing projects as well as work with journal editors to produce new research pieces to be published on our website. Some of the different aspects of the journal include:

 

  • Research Help: At the Public Policy Journal, our editors will work with you every step of the research process to ensure that any applicants are producing high quality research. From proposal to submission, we’re here to help you edit, format, research, and officially post your work on our site! 
  • Paper Shoutouts: Have a paper you feel proud of? Here at the Public Policy Journal, we take existing papers and edit them to fit our guidelines so that they can be uploaded in a polished manner!
  • Professional Development: We aim to submit outstanding papers to other well-recognized journals throughout the US. Anybody who presents their research with us will have a chance to be selected for this opportunity. 
  • Community: Meet and engage with other individuals who are also passionate about public policy subject areas and learn something new!
summary: The Public Policy Journal at FSU is intended to foster research excellence among those in the university with interests in international, national, and state issues. It will provide an undergraduate alternative to students seeking to publish in journals.

name: Students for Dental Dignity at Florida State University
description: Students for Dental Dignity is a student-led service organization at Florida State University committed to restoring dignity, reducing preventable oral pain, and educating communities about the importance of oral hygiene. Our organization serves individuals experiencing homelessness and underserved populations through hands-on outreach, the distribution of personalized dental-hygiene kits, and compassionate, community-centered support. In addition to providing dental care kits to local shelters, we also visit underserved elementary schools to teach younger children how to care for their oral health. During these visits, members demonstrate proper brushing using dental models, lead activities like dental bingo to make learning engaging, and distribute individualized hygiene kits to reinforce healthy habits at home. Students for Dental Dignity combines service, education, and advocacy to expand access to oral care while fostering meaningful connections throughout the Tallahassee community.
summary: Students for Dental Dignity is a student-led organization dedicated to restoring dignity and improving oral health through service, education, and outreach for individuals experiencing homelessness and underserved communities in Tallahassee.

name: Clarinet Society at Florida State University
summary:
1.	Promote the appreciation and performance of clarinet music across campus and the community.
2.	Provide educational opportunities and resources to clarinetists of all skill levels.
3.	Foster a supportive communit

name: Students For Bead the Change
description: Beads and Noles is a creative, service-driven student organization dedicated to spreading love, hope, and encouragement through handmade beaded bracelets. Our members come together once a month to design and craft bracelets that are donated to women’s shelters, children’s hospitals, and other organizations supporting individuals in need. Each bracelet serves as a small but powerful reminder that someone cares. Through compassion, creativity, and community, we aim to brighten lives, uplift spirits, and make a positive impact one bead at a time. Members can also earn volunteer hours for their participation, as every bracelet made and every meeting attended contributes directly to service within our community.
summary: The purpose of this club is to create handmade beaded bracelets that spread joy and encouragement by donating them to women’s shelters, children’s hospitals and other organizations in need.

name: The Oaks Collaborative at FSU
description: We exist to help incoming freshmen and transfer students find their people, place, and purpose by connecting them with other new and current students as well as various Christian churches and campus ministries offered at FSU and the Tallahassee area. Our biggest event we put on every year is called The Heritage Retreat, which is a 3-day, 2 night conference event in early August before the school year starts. The retreat is an opportunity for incoming students to form friendships with other new and current students and provide an opportunity for newcomers to experience staying in dorms on campus and to find their community in college before the fall semester starts. We also are active throughout the year holding other events such as our spring festival fundraiser and benefit nights.
summary: We exist to help incoming freshmen and transfer students find their people, place, and purpose by connecting them with other new and current students as well as various Christian churches and campus ministries offered at FSU and the Tallahassee area.

name: Hispanic/Latine Pre-Law Society
description: A Hispanic/Latine led organization aimed at Hispanic/Latine (but not limited to) undergraduate students to increase representation of our community in the legal profession by helping them with resources, tips, and a smooth transition in environments like law schools, courts, and more. We hope to be able to help these students pursue this interest in law as well as guide them into a community of those with similar stories.
summary: This is a Hispanic/Latine led organization focusing on increasing representation in the legal profession. We hope to help all undergraduate Latine students pursue this profession in the form of meetings, group speakers, workshops, and more.

name: Association of Claws and Clauses
description: Claws & Clauses is an organization constructed to bridge the conservational gap between science, lawmakers, and the public. This organization teaches students of all majors how to read scientific publications and create realistic solutions to conservational crises based off the newest wildlife and conservational research. Part of this organization's goal is to help the general public easily access new scientific work in ways everyone can appreciate, through engaging social media posts, hands-on field trips, and even coherent legislature that is presentable to local government. Weekly meetings consist of reading new Floridian wildlife publications, watching influential media on promoting wildlife conservation, and learning how to spread awareness and promote the solutions to local conservational issues within our own community.
summary: Claws & Clauses bridges the gap between science, conservational policy, and the general public. By learning how to read scientific literature and promote engagement, students from all majors can help in protecting Floridian wildlife with science.

name: Mindful Corner Podcast
description: Mindful Corner strives to use the media to start conversations about mental health. We believe the media is one of the most powerful ways to raise awareness, which is why we started this podcast. We are looking for people interested in both mental health advocacy and media production. We hope to foster collaboration within the student body through wellness activities, mental health seminars, and of course, creation of the podcast itself. Our hope is that this initiative will inspire compassion and a call to action for those who have difficulties with mental health.
summary: This club brings together students passionate about both mental health advocacy and media production to create a podcast centered around mental wellness with the perspectives of college students.

name: Student Innovator's Beyond Borders
description:
The Sustainability In Technology Club will be dedicated to fostering a diverse community where creative problem-solving and ethical adaptation are encouraged to achieve a meaningful, positive impact through bringing helpful technology to international communities. 

 

- Innovation: Adaptability and original thinking are crucial in an evolving world. SIT will highlight the constant pursuit of creative and ethical solutions, recognizing that diverse perspectives are our greatest advantage.

- Inclusivity and Collaboration: We foster an open and welcoming environment where every student, regardless of their background or major, is encouraged to contribute their unique ideas. We will operate on the principle that significant change requires positive collaboration and a willingness to say "yes" to new approaches.

- World Travel and Cultural Learning: We emphasize cultural learning, world travel (where feasible), and cross-border understanding as essential foundations for responsible innovation and effective international action. The world is vast, and we want our impact to be as well.

- Sustainability: SIT will promote and apply technologies, policies, and integrated practices to advocate for the long-term health, preservation, and equitable use of Earth's resources and ecosystems.
summary:
The Sustainability In Technology Club (SIT) empowers students to develop technology-driven solutions to worldwide environmental issues. 

Note: Originally known as Student Innovators Beyond Borders

name: Grass Volleyball Club at FSU
summary: The Grass Volleyball Club at FSU is focused on introducing people to a different style of volleyball and create a community where both experienced and new players can come together. We are a COED organization who plan to compete in tournaments.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha - The National Political Science Honors Society
description: Pi Sigma Alpha is an honors society specifically for political science students or related social science majors. The organization is intended to foster community, provide career development opportunities, highlight student achievements, and explore political science topics. Qualifications for joining the honors society are listed on the Pi Sigma Alpha website.
summary: Pi Sigma Alpha is an honors society specifically for political science students or related social science majors. The organization is intended to foster community, provide career development opportunities, and highlight student achievements.

name: Future Scholars Society
description:
I have been tutoring at Appilachee Elementary School for the last year and a half. I work with Mrs. Jones, a fifth-grade science teacher. The school is underfunded, and the teachers often require additional help, which they rarely find. Mrs Jones plans her weeks around the days I come, basing lesson plans and activities on my availability. In the class I tutored, the average for state testing was a 48%, a 22% jump from the school average of 26%. 

I aim to connect more students who wish to tutor, whether for volunteer work or experience, with teachers in the school. Students can list their availability, and teachers can "match" with them based on their hours. 

I aim to help more students learn and to give them a chance to be inspired by hardworking older kids.
summary: Working with Leon County School Districts, we will connect students who wish to tutor with teachers to earn community service hours. This way, teachers can plan lessons that need additional help. In the classroom, I go tofor a 20% bump in state test scor

name: Speech & Oratory Development Association
description: The Speech and Oratory Development Association is a non-competitive organization dedicated to fostering a supportive community for students with an interest in speech and public expression. The association emphasizes professional development through workshops, guest speakers, and collaborative discussions, while helping members refine their speaking and writing skills for academic, professional, and civic settings.
summary: The Speech and Oratory Development Association is a non-competitive organization dedicated to fostering a supportive community for students with an interest in speech and public expression.

name: Debate & Oratory Collegiate Honor Society at FSU
description: DOCHS at FSU provides students with opportunities to develop critical thinking, research, and communication skills through competitive debate, speech events, and community engagement. Guided by DOCHS' motto, “Interrogare Nobile Est, Responde Ars,” which translates to "To Question is Noble, to Answer is Art", the honor society promotes ethical discourse, intellectual growth, and leadership development. By joining, members are exposed to various forms of debate styles and a community that values education, civic engagement, and the transformative power of speech to shape communities and society.
summary: FSU Debate & Oratory Collegiate Honor Society (DOCHS) is an honorary society dedicated to the art of speech and debate, fostering excellence in public speaking, performance, and advocacy.

name: Silver Strings Club at FSU
description: Silver Strings is a community service–based organization dedicated to improving the quality of life of elderly individuals through music therapy. The club brings together student musicians who volunteer their time and talents to perform at nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and senior centers. Through live music, Silver Strings aims to promote emotional well-being, cognitive engagement, and social connection among older adults. In addition to musical performances, the organization participates in other service initiatives that support the elderly community. By combining compassion, service, and the therapeutic power of music, Silver Strings strives to create meaningful intergenerational connections and positively impact the lives of seniors.
summary: Silver Strings is a community service–based student organization uses music therapy to support the elderly. FSU student musicians perform at nursing homes to promote emotional well-being and social connection while fostering meaningful connections.

name: Peace protection project
description:
The PeaceProtectionProject was founded in 2021 by Joshua Thompson during his sophomore year of high school. As we approach four years of service, our mission remains rooted in uplifting marginalized communities and at-risk youth through direct action and compassionate leadership.

Joshua was inspired to launch the initiative after conducting research in his AP Seminar class on the underrepresentation of students from marginalized backgrounds in advanced academic programs. He discovered that many of the barriers to their success stemmed from systemic challenges outside the classroom—ranging from poverty to lack of access to basic necessities. Realizing that not all students from these communities face the same hardships, Joshua asked himself how those with fewer obstacles could step up and make a difference. From that reflection, the PeaceProtectionProject was born.

 

Since our founding, we’ve organized:

 

  • Back-to-school drives and backpack giveaways for low-income families
  • Clothing drives and distributions for individuals experiencing homelessness
  • Toy drives for homeless and hospitalized children
  • Feminine hygiene product collections for survivors of domestic abuse
  • Community park cleanups to promote environmental stewardship
  • Mentorship programs for youth from all walks of life
summary: We are a community driven nonprofit organization providing the next generation of teenagers and adults with opportunities to serve marginalized communities, at risk youth, and promote environmental conservation.

name: Students To End Neurodegenerative Diseases Club at FSU
description:
In creating STEND, the goals for this organization are to share information about different neurological diseases, specifically those that are neurodegenerative, to the public. We will be able to:

  • Lower the barrier of entry to accessing this information
  • Provide people with the tools to identify these diseases early on and how they can prevent them
  • To teach others the wonders about the workings and malfunctions the brain
  • Inspire others to join this field
  • Teach others the volunteer and research gaps/research opportunities within this field and around their area
  • Fundraise for patients of these diseases
  • Do our part to help be able to cure these diseases
  • Give others the tools they need to be successful neuroscience researchers
  • Help progress future research
  •  

We aim to do this through presenting diseases through a series of Presentations to: the public, organizations at FSU and around the immediate area, interested classes, interested research groups, and anyone else that is interested! We also wish to collaborate with any organization on campus that has a similar goal.

 

Another goal is to foster neurodegenerative community enjoyment and gathering. These events would serve as a way to spread more knowledge through community, fundraise money for patients, and have fun while spreading the information about neurodegenerative diseases overall. 

 

As a part of this organization, you are responsible for championing for those that can’t, and helping prevent further neurodegenerative diseases through education and inspiring the next generation! We trust all of you do, with this be either through:

  • Research
  • Asking questions
  • Teaching others

We trust you will make a change in this field. Thank you for joining STEND, and we hope you too, as a Student, pledge to End Neurodegenerative Diseases!
summary: At STEND, we aim to change the field of neurodegenerative diseases by learning and teaching others about related pathology, risk factors, new studies, findings, and developing therapies, whilst supporting at volunteer opportunities and fundraisers.

name: Pioneer Party at Florida State University
summary: A student political party dedicated to the issues of Safety, Enrichment, and Prosperity on campus.

name: Robotics Club at FSU
description: This club will present the opportunity for students of every major (but comprised mostly of computer science and engineering students) to get involved in team robotics and mechatronics projects. We aim to create engineering projects as a team so members can gain experience working in an engineering and collaborative environment. Another goal of ours is to have members create teams in which they will submit formal proposals for robotics projects and we will support and oversee the creation of it. At the end of the semester we want these teams to present their projects. We, as the board, will then help students create portfolios of their projects that they completed in and out of the club for any future employer that they want to submit it to.
summary: A club for passionate  students to get involved in robotics and have hands-on experience working with electronics, practical applications of robots, programming, and engineering in a team.

name: Quantitative Finance Club at Florida State University
description: The Quantitative Finance Club at Florida State University is a student led organization dedicated to exploring the intersection of finance, mathematics, statistics, and computer science. The club aims to provide students with practical exposure to quantitative methods used in financial markets, investment management, risk analysis, and financial modeling. QF@FSU offers workshops, speaker events, and hands on projects covering topics such as financial modeling, programming for finance, statistics and probability, algorithmic trading, and portfolio optimization. Members gain opportunities to work on real world financial problems, collaborate on projects, and develop technical and analytical skills valued in careers such as quantitative trading, asset management, risk management, data science, and financial research.
summary: QF@FSU explores the intersection of finance, mathematics, statistics, and computer science.

name: Mu Epsilon Delta at FSU
description: https://mednational.org/
summary: Mu Epsilon Delta is a national co-educational, pre-health professional fraternity whose mission is to prepare members to succeed in pre-health careers through the development of scholarship, fraternity, and service.

name: Omega Chi Zeta at FSU
description:
Omega Chi Zeta at Florida State University is a professional social sorority dedicated to empowering women in technical and scientific fields. As the Alpha founding chapter of the Omega Chi Zeta, we support women pursuing majors in Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics, Pre-Health, and related majors.

Our chapter emphasizes academic excellence, professional development, leadership, and sisterhood. We provide a supportive and inclusive environment where members can grow personally and professionally while building long lasting connections with other women in technical studies.

Through academic support, professional workshops, networking opportunities, and mentorship, Omega Chi Zeta at FSU equips members with the tools and confidence to succeed in rigorous academic programs and future careers. Beyond academics, we strive to create a strong sense of sisterhood through social events, philanthropy, and service, while promoting diversity and inclusion. Omega Chi Zeta at FSU is more than a sorority, but rather a community that empowers women to thrive in STEM and beyond.
summary: Omega Chi Zeta is a sorority for Women in STEM and Pre-Health Studies. This includes women in Science, Technology, Engineering, Math, and more.

name: Glow Up Club at FSU
description:
At Glow Up Club at FSU, our mission is to redefine beauty and self-care by fostering confidence, creativity, and community among students. While traditional conversations about beauty often focus on trends or appearance, we aim to highlight the deeper meaning of self-expression—how taking care of yourself, inside and out, can empower your confidence and well-being.

 

Through interactive workshops, beauty and fashion events, and discussions centered on wellness and self-love, Glow Up Club at FSU encourages students to embrace their individuality and feel radiant in their own skin. We believe that beauty is not one-size-fits-all—it’s about confidence, growth, and authenticity. In the spirit of our name, we are dedicated to helping every student “glow up” by nurturing their style, self-care, and self-worth.
summary: Glow Up Club at FSU is all aspects of beauty and self-expression —cosmetics, skincare, fashion, self-care, and confidence. From product to confidence workshops to self-care events, Glow Up encourages students to embrace their personal glow in every way.

name: Chemical and Biomedical Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: The purpose of CBEGSA shall be to foster collaboration among graduate students in chemical and biomedical engineering through academic, professional, and social activities.

name: Grill Club at FSU
description:
Grill Club at FSU is a community built around bringing people together through food, friendship, and shared experiences. Our mission is to create a welcoming environment where students can connect, learn, and enjoy the art of outdoor cooking.

We aim to bring people together through food while teaching members how to grill safely and confidently, use grilling equipment properly, and develop practical cooking skills they can use beyond college. Whether you’re a beginner or experienced on the grill, Grill Club offers hands-on learning, social events, and opportunities to build community one meal at a time.
summary: Grill Club at FSU is a community built around bringing people together through food, friendship, and shared experiences. Our mission is to create a welcoming environment where students can connect, learn, and enjoy the art of outdoor cooking.

name: Tax Law Society of FSU
description: The purpose of the Tax Law Society is to bring students interested in the Tax Law together and connect them with the Tax Law community of Florida.
summary: The purpose of the Tax Law Society is to bring students interested in the Tax Law together and connect them with the Tax Law community of Florida.

name: Eta Sigma Delta at FSU
description: Eta Sigma Delta is a prestigious international honor society for superior hospitality administration students. The Florida State chapter offers many exciting opportunities to meet new people and become involved in department activities while increasing both your knowledge and exposure to the hospitality industry. Only Dedman College of Hospitality majors with an overall 3.0 or higher GPA will receive an email offering admission each semester.
summary: Eta Sigma Delta is an international honor society for hospitality administration students. The Florida State chapter offers many exciting opportunities to meet new people and increasing both your knowledge and exposure to the hospitality industry.

name: Medieval Studies Club at FSU
description: The Medieval Studies Club at FSU is a space for students interested in any aspect of the Middle Ages to share their interests with like-minded peers. It seeks to promote an accessible study of the Middle Ages across various disciplines, including history, art, and literature.
summary: The Medieval Studies Club at FSU is an organization seeking to provide an interdisciplinary, inclusive community among those interested in the Middle Ages.

name: CapitILL State a Dance Organization at FSU
description:
CapitILL State is a hip-hop dance team and an official affiliate of the Filipino Student Association (FSA). The organization is dedicated to strengthening and cultivating community through movement, creativity, and shared experiences. Through hip-hop dance, CapitILL State aims to bridge gaps, overcome adversity, and create a welcoming space where individuals from diverse backgrounds can connect, express themselves, and grow together.

As an affiliate of FSA, CapitILL State prioritizes the representation and celebration of Filipino culture and people. The team uses dance as a platform to honor cultural identity, amplify Filipino voices, and showcase the richness of Filipino heritage to both campus and broader audiences. By blending hip-hop foundations with cultural pride, CapitILL State strives to inspire confidence, unity, and artistic excellence while fostering a strong sense of belonging within the community.
summary: Hip-hop dance team affiliated with FSA.

name: Care Beyond the Chart Collective at FSU
description:
Care Beyond the Chart Collective is a pediatric-focused service organization dedicated to supporting children receiving hospital care, whether for long-term, chronic, or short-term medical needs, by addressing experiences beyond clinical treatment. We partner with pediatric hospital units to provide engagement, comfort, and connection through service initiatives, ethical fundraising, and interdisciplinary innovation. Our work focuses on improving patients' quality of life through creative programming, resource support, and impact-driven projects that address needs often overlooked in traditional healthcare settings. The organization is intentionally interdisciplinary and welcomes students from all majors. Members are encouraged to contribute their unique academic backgrounds to our mission. For example, healthcare students may support patient engagement, business and marketing students may lead fundraising and sponsorship initiatives, and engineering or technology students may contribute to innovation projects. However, members are not limited to these roles and are encouraged to explore any area of impact that aligns with their interests.

For questions or additional information, please contact our President, Braxton Keith, at bak24d@fsu.edu
summary: Care Beyond the Chart Collective supports pediatric patients through hospital engagement, ethical fundraising, and interdisciplinary innovation to improve quality of life beyond medical treatment.

name: Reach Campus Ministry at FSU
description:
The purpose of this organization is to meet students' spiritual needs in a deeper sense while providing fun interactions with other students.

Reach will meet in person on Wednesday nights at Dodd Hall. We host numerous bible studies in residence halls as well as off campus locations. A few events every semester are more activity and entertainment based.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to meet students' spiritual needs in a deeper sense while providing for fun interaction with other students. Reach will be meeting in Dodd auditorium. We meet spiritual needs by hosting Bible studies.

name: Cartoon Club at FSU
description:
This club is for people who love making and/or watching cartoons! At our meetings we will be watching animated TV shows/movies, making crafts, and having workshops to teach you how to make your own cartoons! In our meetings we will also have various minigames to keep it entertaining.

If you would like to stay updated on events or leave suggestions, join our GroupMe!

https://groupme.com/join_group/112924400/F5HOMqc5
summary: This club is for people who love making and/or watching cartoons! At our meetings we will be watching animated TV shows/movies, making crafts, and having workshops to teach you how to make your own cartoons!

name: P!NK!ND Club at Florida State University
description:
P!NKIND is a nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting breast cancer patients through fundraising and awareness initiatives. We are currently established in high schools such as Cypress Bay High School and Archbishop McCarthy High School, and we are expanding into universities—beginning with Florida State University. Our mission is simple yet powerful: to direct all funds raised toward the University of Miami’s Sylvester Comprehensive Cancer Center. Beyond fundraising, we strive to cultivate a supportive and optimistic environment for students who have been personally affected by breast cancer—whether through their own experiences or those of loved ones. By fostering awareness among young people, we aim to inspire future generations to seek proper care and resources to combat this illness. We are not just an organization—we are the future of this movement. In its very first year, P!NKIND was established by Ryen Bieber at Cypress Bay High School and Archbishop McCarthy High School. By the end of the 2023–2024 school year, Cypress Bay had welcomed over 250 members, while Archbishop McCarthy enrolled more than 150. With over 400 members in our founding year, we proudly sustained that momentum throughout 2025. Our leadership structure includes an executive board of 28 dedicated officers. Our fundraising model combines student engagement with community partnerships. We raise money through:

  • Membership dues ($25 annually)
  • Restaurant fundraisers (e.g., Chipotle, Chili’s, Pizza Heaven, Crumbl, Chillin’, Playa Bowls, BurgerFi, and more)
  • Competitive events such as pickleball and soccer tournaments
  • Donations from local businesses that allow us to sell their products


At the same time, P!NKIND builds meaningful connections through social events that strengthen community ties, including:

  • Guest speaker sessions
  • General meetings
  • Creative holiday-themed activities

Our officers work tirelessly to maintain a positive, inclusive environment, ensuring members feel both supported and inspired.
summary: P!NKIND is a nonprofit supporting University of Miami’s Sylvester Comprehensive Cancer Center. We fundraise, raise awareness, and create a supportive community for students impacted by breast cancer, inspiring future generations to seek care and resource

name: The Legal Edge Society
summary: The Legal Edge Society is a hands-on pre-law organization at FSU focused on building real legal skills through mock mediations, case breakdowns, and interactive workshops. We help students move beyond networking and start practicing like future attorneys

name: Uncensored America at Florida State University
description: This is a non-partisan organization focused on promoting free speech and civic/political engagement on college campuses. This is done by inviting high profile political speakers and influencers to host events on college campuses.
summary: Uncensored America is a youth-driven free speech organization dedicated to hosting honest and fun conversations with controversial figures to fight censorship and cancel culture.
